> `/shows/the-voice/season/the-finale?view=community`, `/u/e2e`,
> `/themes/best-villain-editing`) walks ran, desktop + mobile,
> deliberately targeting The Voice — the most recently fully
> drained show (29 seasons through its NBC finale), never
> critiqued before (same strategy as pass 78 → Shark Tank, pass
> 79 → Perfect Match). 4 findings filed (0 high, 2 medium, 2
> low) after re-rating the reader's one HIGH proposal down to
> MED at self-assess (a factual self-contradiction, not a hard-
> rule break — same severity class as the pass-79 villa/house
> row). Zero console errors, zero failed requests, zero mobile
> overflow on any capture; auth handshake confirmed genuine
> (`authState: authenticated:cloud`, `@e2e` chrome across
> header/profile/comment-composer attribution, real member-since
> date on `/u/e2e`). No spoiler leakage found on any capture (no
> winner/eliminated/crowned/runner-up keywords surfaced). No
> per-show SVG iconography violations found — only the shared
> brand mark + generic vote chevrons appear in page markup. The
> long-documented `/shows?_rsc=...` Next.js Link-prefetch-abort
> artifact resurfaced on the authed `/u/e2e` capture and was
> dropped again per the ~15-pass precedent (passes 6-11, 29-47+).
> Strongest finding: The Voice's own pages disagree on the show's
> tenure — the show page's live `{yearsWord}` token renders
> "fifteen years" while the finale season's static copy says
> "fourteen-year run" / "fourteenth year," and the show's
> `status: hiatus` sits alongside a finale season whose own lede
> narrates a permanent close ("marked the end of The Voice's
> run," "brought the show to a close") — compare
> `americas-next-top-model.md`, which pairs its finale season
> with `status: ended`. Root cause traced: `content-check.ts`'s
> `YEAR_TENURE_RE` only matches spelled-out tens (twenty through
> ninety), so teen-number tenure phrases like "fourteen-year" are
> structurally invisible to the drift gate. Second/third findings
> are both fresh content-voice gaps: `/themes/best-villain-editing`
> entry #01 opens with an unintentional "cast was cast" word-tic,
> and entry #05 closes by echoing the list's own hero-lede thesis
> almost verbatim (distinct from the already-closed #02/#04 "leans
> all the way in" echo, issue #462). Fourth is the recurring
> Section-02-vs-Section-03 duplication class (previously fixed on
> Bachelor S28 issue #464 and MAFS New York issue #478, currently
> also Pending unresolved for Perfect Match) now reproduced on The
> Voice's finale season: the season body and the canon.md
> rationale render two near-verbatim paragraphs back to back.

> are the same string twice on Shark Tank's season 1 page
> (`filming_caption` duplicates `location` verbatim) — and this
> reproduces on 133 season files across 22 shows catalog-wide, not
> just Shark Tank, making it a systemic content-authoring gap on
> the scale of the Big Brother/Love Island UK duplication classes
> already fixed. Filed MED, content-only, likely a multi-tick
> drain. Second finding: Shark Tank's CAST SIZE stat renders "5
> players" — `isCompetitionGenre()` in the season-page's
> `statsFor()` matches "Business competition" on the substring
> "competition" and defaults to the generic competition-show noun,
> which is wrong for a panel/judge-format show whose "cast" are
> investors, not competing players. Filed MED, single-function
> code fix. Three LOW findings: the best-comeback-seasons theme's
> "ENTRIES 6 / SHOWS 5" stat pair goes unexplained (mismatch is
> because Survivor appears twice, not a counting error); the
> `/shows` tier-filter control renders "ALL 1" with no separator
> between the tab label and its count; and Shark Tank's CAST SIZE
> stat tile lists the sharks in a different name order/format
> (surname-only, rotated) than the season body two sections down
> (full names, canonical order). One raw observation from the
> authenticated reader agent — a HIGH claim that the mobile
> comment thread showed the anonymous "Sign in to comment" stub
> despite a genuine authenticated session — was independently
> re-verified via a direct `scripts/critique-walk.mjs` re-run and a
> raw `curl` against `/api/comments` with the minted cookie: both
> confirmed `signedIn: true` and the real composer ("as @e2e / Tap
> to write") render correctly on mobile. Dropped as a false
> positive (likely a stale/incomplete capture in that specific
> agent run, not reproducible). A second raw observation — the
> season page's "Where it sits in the canon" paragraph matching
> the show page's canon-tab entry verbatim — was checked against
> source (`whereItSitsCopy()` in
> `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx`) and confirmed to
> be intentional, established architecture (the season page always
> quotes the show's own `canon.md` rationale by design, same
> pattern already engaged with favorably across many prior passes)
> — not filed.

> shows at once — same duplication class already fixed on
> Bachelor S28, Love Island UK, and Survivor 50, but this is the
> first time it's shown up as a structural risk: `whereItSitsCopy()`
> deliberately quotes the canon rationale verbatim whenever
> `canonTotal === 1`, and every freshly-scaffolded show enters the
> catalog with exactly one canon.md entry under the standing
> content-velocity mandate — so this collision is now guaranteed
> to recur on every new show's S1 page unless canon.md and the
> season body are deliberately written to argue different angles.
> Filed HIGH with a fix that differs from the existing
> compare-to-neighbors precedent (no neighbor exists yet for a
> single-season show) — argue the curatorial case in canon.md
> instead. Three MED findings: MAFS New York's stat rail is
> missing PREMIERED/EPISODES tiles that its RHOSLC sibling (same
> tick, same template) ships; MAFS's FORMAT tile caption is a
> canon-narrative claim instead of a format detail and breaks the
> site's sentence-case caption convention; and the CAST SIZE
> tile's hardcoded "player(s)" noun (`page.tsx:175`) is applied to
> every show regardless of genre, reading as competition
> vocabulary on docusoap/social-reality shows (RHOSLC, MAFS, and
> every Housewives/Vanderpump/Jersey-Shore-class show in the
> catalog) — a shared-function fix, not a content typo. One LOW:
> the B-tier band tag "Recent additions · under review" frames
> tier as recency when the `tier` field is documented as
> editorial-confidence — visibly contradicted by Dancing with the
> Stars (21 years, 34 seasons) sitting at B tier under a "recent"

> literally identical was investigated against the wider corpus —

> near-identical location/filming_caption pairs are the norm
> across dozens of season files and explicitly dropped the same
> class of finding on naked-and-afraid S19, so re-filing it here
> would contradict established precedent; and the
> `/themes/best-comeback-seasons` "SAVE (THIS DEVICE)" label
> reading the same whether authed or not was dropped — that
> device-scope qualifier was added deliberately (issue #377/#385
> history) to be honest about localStorage-only save state
> regardless of auth, not a bug to fix.

> restate the same ceremony/settled-grammar/provisional argument
> near-verbatim — same duplication class as the resolved Bachelor
> S28 finding, now on a third show. Authed pass confirmed the auth
> handshake works cleanly (`@e2e` chrome, live vote-pair and
> comment-composer state) and surfaced two component-level
> findings on `/shows/survivor?view=community`: `RankShiftPill`
> deltas render in `CommunityRankList` with no vote-count floor
> guard (same arithmetic-artifact class as the resolved #334
> mover-ticker fix — `MOVER_VOTE_FLOOR` was never applied to the
> full-list rows, so Survivor 50 shows a "↓38" pill against zero
> votes), filed HIGH; and the "No community votes yet" banner
> copy is misleading when the same table shows nonzero per-season
> vote counts (the source flag tracks the reorder threshold, not
> whether any votes exist), filed MEDIUM. A fifth finding: the
> pass-68 SEO title-stutter fix (`seasonDisplayTitle` in
> `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx`) only guards
> against the generic "Season N" title pattern, so a milestone
> season titled with the show name baked in ("Survivor 50")
> still renders "Survivor S50 — Survivor 50." Spoiler discipline
> P0 intact — no winner/elimination/finale exposure on any
> capture; `/themes/best-finales`'s 7 entries all describe finale
> quality without naming outcomes.
>

> S1 under the `e2e` bot account on production data, then reverted
> both. This violates critique.md §6 hard rule 1 ("never modify
> code, content, or data"). Independently re-verified post-hoc via
> anonymous `curl` against both season pages and `/u/e2e` — vote
> counts read `0` on both, and `/u/e2e` shows no vote record;
> production data is confirmed clean. No CRITIQUE row filed for
> this since it isn't a product defect, but noting it here so the
> next `/critique` pass's reader-agent prompt is explicit that
> click-simulation is out of scope entirely (read pre-interaction
> state only) until `critique-walk.mjs` grows a narrowly-scoped,
> auto-reverting click helper of its own — same recommendation the
> agent itself made.
>

## Pending

- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/married-at-first-sight/season/boston — the FORMAT stat tile caption calls Boston's expert swap the "Second consecutive panel change," but the season's own lede and body, plus the neighboring Chicago season's copy, contradict "consecutive." `content/shows/married-at-first-sight/seasons/06-boston.md` lede: "...for Rachel DeAlto — the second panel change in three seasons." Body: "...the panel's second shakeup in as many years." Neither says consecutive, and for good reason: aired order is New York, New York II, Atlanta, Miami (panel change #1: DeAlto/Roberson join), Chicago (no change — `05-chicago.md` lede: "...with a settled panel for three new Chicago couples"), Boston (panel change #2: Griffin replaces DeAlto). Chicago sits between the two panel-change seasons with a stable panel, so Boston's change is not consecutive to Miami's — a full season intervened. A reader who reads the stat tile alongside the body paragraph two inches below it, or who clicks back to the adjacent Chicago season, hits a factual disagreement on this page's own numbers. Fix: drop "consecutive" from `format_caption` — it's the only one of the three phrasings that claims back-to-back seasons, and it's the one that's wrong. Something like "Second panel change in three seasons" (matching the lede) keeps the stat tile terse and true. Content-only, one field. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (format-mechanics detail only, no outcome exposure). (URL: /shows/married-at-first-sight/season/boston, source: critique-pass-83) — issue: #516 — RESOLVED 896b366: reworded `format_caption` to "Second panel change in three seasons," matching the lede's framing and removing the false "consecutive" claim. Verify gate green: 194 test files / 2811 unit tests, content:check ok (58 shows/722 seasons), build (978 pages), e2e 3246 passed. Closes #516.

- [x] [MED] [anon+authed] /shows/shark-tank/season/season-11 — the season body (Section 02, "The shape of the season") and the canon.md rationale (Section 03, "Where it sits in the canon") render the same paragraph verbatim, differing only in their closing sentence — the recurring Section-02-vs-Section-03 duplication class already fixed on Bachelor S28 (#464), Love Island UK (#459), MAFS New York (#478), The Voice finale (#502), and Perfect Match (#501), now reproduced on Shark Tank's freshly-drained Season 11. Section 02 body (`content/shows/shark-tank/seasons/11-season-11.md`): "Season eleven keeps the classic six exactly as they've been for years, but the guest-shark rotation reaches its highest-profile run yet — familiar recurring guests alongside one-off names from well outside the usual entrepreneur-and-investor circuit. Twenty-four episodes lean on that contrast: a steady, unbothered core panel and a guest chair pulling louder names than any season before it. Nothing about the format itself moves; the marquee lineup is the story this year." Section 03 (`content/shows/shark-tank/canon.md`, "## 11. Season 11" entry) is identical through "Nothing about the format itself" and then diverges only for the last clause: "...changes, which is exactly why it slots into the canon's middle — a fun wrinkle, not a real swing." A reader scrolling from Section 02 to Section 03 hits the same paragraph twice. Fix: rewrite the canon.md rationale body to argue why the guest-lineup framing earns slot #7 of 11 specifically (e.g. contrast against neighboring slots #6/#8), rather than restating the season body's recap paragraph verbatim. Content-only, one field. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (no outcome exposure). - [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/the-voice/season/the-finale — the season body (Section 02, "The shape of the season") and the canon.md rationale (Section 03, "Where it sits in the canon") render two near-verbatim paragraphs back to back, the recurring Section-02-vs-Section-03 duplication class already fixed on Bachelor S28 (issue #464) and MAFS New York (issue #478) and currently also Pending unresolved for Perfect Match (row above/below), now reproduced on The Voice's freshly-drained finale season. Fix: rewrite one of the two closing clauses (recommend the canon.md rationale, since the season body's version reads more naturally as the recap-closer) to argue a different angle — e.g. why the crossover premise earns its canon slot rather than restating that it could have flopped. - [ ] [LOW] [anon] /shows/perfect-match — the hero stat reads "4 SEASONS AIRED" but only season 1 has been scaffolded and ranked (`content/shows/perfect-match/seasons/` contains only `01-season-1.md`; `/shows/perfect-match/season/season-2` 404s), and there's no in-page affordance explaining that the other three aired seasons simply haven't been ranked yet. A first-time visitor drawn in by "4 seasons" has no season-2/3/4 to reach and no signal that this is a content-coverage gap rather than a broken link. This is the expected shape for a freshly-added show under the site's standing content-velocity mandate (one show scaffolded per tick, seasons drained incrementally) — not a bug, but worth a small UX affordance. Fix: add a lightweight "seasons not yet ranked" note near the ALL/season selector (e.g. "3 more seasons aired — ranking in progress") so the gap reads as roadmap rather than missing content. - [x] [HIGH] [anon] /shows/big-brother/season/the-pilot (and, on spot-check, every Big Brother season — systemic across the whole show) — the season-file body and the `canon.md` rationale for the same slot restate the same argument near-verbatim, the same duplication class already fixed on Bachelor S28, Love Island UK (all 11 series), Survivor 50, RHOSLC, and Married at First Sight, but never applied to Big Brother. Confirmed on three separate seasons, not just the one walked: `content/shows/big-brother/seasons/01-the-pilot.md` body — "The pilot, and a strange piece of television to revisit. Ten strangers in a Studio City soundstage, cameras everywhere, and an eviction model lifted from the European original — the public voted, not the houseguests. CBS aired it nearly every weeknight, a pace the show would never try again." — versus `canon.md` "## 1. The Pilot" rationale — "The original, and a strange piece of television to revisit. Ten strangers in a Studio City soundstage, cameras everywhere, and an eviction model lifted straight from the European original — the public voted, not the houseguests. CBS aired it nearly every weeknight across a long summer, a pace the show would never attempt again." Same pattern reproduces on `content/shows/big-brother/seasons/06-summer-of-secrets.md` body ("Fourteen houseguests enter the Studio City compound, every one of them carrying a pre-show connection to someone else inside... The alliance math gets dense fast. The confessionals stay quotable.") versus `canon.md` "## 6. Summer Of Secrets" rationale ("Fourteen houseguests walk into the Studio City compound, every one of them carrying a pre-show connection to someone else inside... The alliance math gets dense almost immediately. The confessionals stay quotable across the full thirty-episode run."). Because the pattern reproduces on two unrelated seasons at opposite ends of the canon (bottom-ranked pilot, top-ranked fan-favorite), this reads as a show-wide authoring gap rather than a one-off — Big Brother is the one show in the catalog whose full 26-season canon predates the de-duplication convention now applied to every newer show. Fix: audit and rewrite `content/shows/big-brother/canon.md`'s rationale paragraphs (all entries with a matching season file) to argue comparative canon placement — why this slot vs. its neighbors — rather than re-describing the same premise/atmosphere facts the season body already owns, following the Love Island UK precedent (issue #459, all 11 series rewritten in one content-curator pass). Content-only; likely a multi-tick drain given the show's size (26 seasons), same shape as the Love Island UK full-show audit. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (no outcome/elimination/winner facts touched — rationale rewrites stay in premise/format/comparative-placement territory). (URL: /shows/big-brother/season/the-pilot, /shows/big-brother/season/summer-of-secrets, source: critique-pass-76) — issue: #488 — RESOLVED 4af4dd0: rewrote all 26 `canon.md` rationale paragraphs (via content-curator sub-agent) to argue comparative canon placement — why each slot sits where it does relative to its neighbors — instead of re-describing premise/atmosphere facts the season body already owns, following the Love Island UK precedent. One sentence in the "Veterans vs. Newbies" entry was hand-edited afterward to drop the phrase "measured against," which tripped the cliché-repetition content-check guard (4th corpus-wide occurrence against a threshold of 3). No outcome/elimination/winner facts touched anywhere; spoiler discipline P0 intact. Verify gate green: 194 test files / 2796 unit tests, content:check ok (53 shows/700 seasons/53 canons/12 themes/3 legal docs); build (936 pages); e2e 3098 passed (18.4m). Closes #488.

- [x] [HIGH] [authed] /shows/survivor?view=community — `RankShiftPill` trend deltas render in the full ranking table with no vote-count floor guard, reproducing the same arithmetic-artifact defect class already fixed for the movers ticker (issue #334, pass-37: "a 5- to 36-spot weekly swing on 1-2 ballots is an arithmetic artifact of a thin vote pool, not a community-driven shift"). `src/lib/community/live.ts`'s `pickMovers` enforces `MOVER_VOTE_FLOOR = 5` distinct voters before a season can show a delta on the movers ticker, but `src/components/canon/CommunityRankList.tsx` (the full-list row renderer, ~line 90) renders `RankShiftPill` whenever `entry.trend != null && entry.trend !== 0` with no equivalent floor check. Confirmed on `/shows/survivor?view=community`: the Survivor 50 row shows a "↓38" shift pill while its Votes column reads "—" (zero votes) — a confident-looking 38-spot slide with no vote data backing it, in the exact same table that (correctly) suppresses the pill for other zero-vote rows via the `entry.trend != null` check being false there. Fix: in `CommunityRankList.tsx`, gate the `RankShiftPill` render on `entry.voteCount >= MOVER_VOTE_FLOOR` (import the constant from `src/lib/community/live.ts`) the same way `pickMovers` does, falling back to the existing em-dash placeholder below the floor. Single component, no visual change to rows that already clear the floor. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (ranking mechanics only). (URL: /shows/survivor?view=community, source: critique-pass-73) — issue: #472 — RESOLVED 9396621: gated `RankShiftPill` on `entry.voteCount >= MOVER_VOTE_FLOOR` in `CommunityRankList.tsx`, mirroring `pickMovers`. Regression test added for the sub-floor non-zero-trend case (the exact Survivor 50 repro). Verify gate green: 194 unit test files / 2784 tests, 136 script tests, content:check ok (48 shows/695 seasons/48 canons/12 themes); build (911 pages); e2e 3028 passed (17.5m). Closes #472.
- [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/survivor?view=community — the live-status strip surfaces a raw internal snapshot version token with zero explanation: `V122 · open to anyone` sits directly beside plain-language chrome (`LIVE`, `LAST UPDATE · 2D AGO`, `NEXT UPDATE · THURSDAY 9PM ET`, `VOTERS THIS WEEK · 0`, `STATUS · MIRRORING THE CANON`). A first-time signed-in reader has no way to know what `V122` refers to — a vote-cycle counter? a build/deploy version? Confirmed at the source: `CommunityLiveStrip.tsx:56` renders `<b>{formatVersion(version)}</b> · <b>open</b> to anyone`, and `formatVersion()` in `src/lib/community/live.ts:187-189` is `version == null ? 'pending' : \`v${version}\`` — the literal Supabase snapshot row's numeric `version` column, surfaced verbatim with a bare `v` prefix and no gloss. Every other value in the same strip is either a plain-English label (`live`, `mirroring the canon`) or a self-explanatory count (`voters this week · 0`); this is the one token that reads as internal/engineering shorthand, breaking the bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain-spoken, never pretentious`. Fix: replace the literal `v${version}` with plain language, e.g. `Week {version}` or drop the token from user-facing chrome entirely and reserve it for an internal debug surface only. Single function edit in `formatVersion()` (or the call site in `CommunityLiveStrip.tsx` if the raw number still needs to reach a data attribute for testing). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome copy only). (URL: /shows/survivor?view=community, source: critique-pass-74) — issue: #477 — RESOLVED 93820d9: `version` is a raw `rank_snapshots` row id, not a week/vote-cycle counter — relabeling it "Week N" would misrepresent it, so dropped the token from user-facing chrome entirely rather than reword it. Removed `formatVersion()` and its call site; the raw value is still available as a `data-version` attribute on the strip for tests/debug. Verify gate green: 194 unit test files / 2787 tests, build (911 pages), 3028 e2e (17.4m). Closes #477.
- [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/survivor?view=community — the ranking-table header banner reads "No community votes yet — list mirrors the editor canon" (`src/components/canon/CommunityRankList.tsx:41`, fired whenever `source !== 'votes'`), but the same table's Votes column shows real nonzero per-season counts directly beneath it (e.g. Cagayan 3, Heroes vs. Villains 5, Borneo 3) — a reader sees "no votes yet" and "3 votes" in the same screen. Root cause: `source` reflects whether `votersThisWeek` clears the 5-distinct-voter reorder threshold (`VOTE_THRESHOLD` in `src/lib/community/ranking.ts`), not whether any votes exist at all — per-season counters are populated independently of that threshold (by design, per the code comment at `ranking.ts:124-134`). The banner copy just describes the wrong condition. Fix: reword the `source === 'canon'`/`'seasons'` banner text in `CommunityRankList.tsx` (and `sourceBannerCopy()` in `src/lib/community/rank.ts` if it feeds the same copy) to something like "Not enough votes yet to override the editor's order" rather than implying zero votes exist. Content/copy-only, one string. Spoiler discipline P0 intact. (URL: /shows/survivor?view=community, source: critique-pass-73) — issue: #475 — RESOLVED a3b4591: an already-tested `sourceBannerCopy()` helper in `src/lib/community/rank.ts` already produced the correct per-source copy (`canon`: "Mirrors the Editor's Canon until enough community votes land."; `seasons`: "Showing seasons in air order until enough community votes land.") but was never wired into the component — `CommunityRankList.tsx` hardcoded its own stale inline string instead. Swapped the hardcoded fallback for a call to the existing helper; the `votes`-state string is untouched (protected by the #256 voice regression test). Two new colocated tests pin the canon/seasons banner copy and assert the misleading "No community votes yet" phrase is gone. Verify gate green: 194 unit test files / 2786 tests, build (911 pages), 3028 e2e (17.6m). Closes #475.

Description/meta-template gap (missing `lede`) is out of scope for this row — tracked as a separate content-gap finding this pass. - [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/love-island-uk/season/summer-2015 (and likely other seasons in the show's canon — pattern repro'd on Series 3 too) — Section 02 "The Shape of the Season" and Section 03 "Where It Sits in the Canon" read as near-verbatim restatements of each other rather than two distinct editorial arguments, directly undercutting the site's own numbered-section design (each section is meant to carry its own argument — Section 02 the shape, Section 03 the ranking rationale). Section 02 (`content/shows/love-island-uk/seasons/01-summer-2015.md` body): "Series 1 is the rough draft that turned out to be the blueprint. Smaller cast, slower pace, a villa that feels almost private compared to the spectacle to come... Caroline Flack and Iain Stirling set the host-narrator tone immediately." Section 03 (`content/shows/love-island-uk/canon.md` Series 1 rationale, quoted verbatim into the page via `whereItSitsCopy()` at `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:262-264`): "It is the rough draft that turned out to be the blueprint: a smaller cast, a slower pace, and a villa that feels almost private next to the spectacle to come... Caroline Flack and Iain Stirling establish the host-narrator tone immediately." Same duplication pattern confirmed on Series 3 (canon.md rationale vs. `content/shows/love-island-uk/seasons/03-summer-2017.md` body — both share "electric"/"chemistry", "Casa Amor", and near-identical "Caroline Flack and Iain Stirling at full strength/height of their partnership" phrasing). Root cause: Section 03 is designed to quote the canon.md rationale directly (likely the fix for a prior "Section 03 reads as an empty stub" finding — see Done section), but the rationale prose itself was written to closely mirror the season body rather than argue the season's placement relative to its neighbors. Fix: rewrite `content/shows/love-island-uk/canon.md` rationale fields (Series 1 and Series 3 confirmed, worth auditing all 11) to focus on comparative placement — why this slot vs. the seasons immediately above/below it in the canon — rather than re-describing atmosphere and cast facts the season body already owns. Content-only. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (atmosphere/format commentary, no outcome exposure). (URL: /shows/love-island-uk/season/summer-2015, source: critique-pass-71) — 48ec163 — issue: #459 — RESOLVED 0b984a8: audited all 11 series (not just the two the pass flagged) and found the same restatement pattern on every entry — delegated to content-curator to rewrite all 11 `content/shows/love-island-uk/canon.md` rationale paragraphs to argue comparative placement (why this slot vs. the immediate neighbors above/below in canon rank), rather than re-describing atmosphere/cast/twist facts the season body already owns. `tag`/`slot_argument`/`community_rank_hint` lines, ranks, and tiers untouched — only the free-text rationale paragraph per entry changed. Spoiler discipline P0 intact. Verify gate green: leg1 194 unit test files, content:check ok (43 shows/680 seasons/43 canons/12 themes/3 legal docs); leg2 build (876 pages); leg3 2918 e2e. Closes #459.
- [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/love-island-uk/season/summer-2015 (and every season/show page with a comment composer — systemic, shared component) — the comment composer `<textarea>` has no accessible name at all: no `aria-label`, no `aria-labelledby`, no `id`/`<label>` pairing. It relies solely on the placeholder string "Say what you actually think.", which disappears once the user starts typing and is not a substitute for a real accessible name — a screen-reader user tabbing into the field after typing has begun gets no context for what the control is. Confirmed via live DOM probe on the deployed page: `ariaLabel: null, ariaLabelledby: null, id: ""`. Source: `src/components/composition/CommentInput.tsx:165-176` — the `<textarea>` carries `className`, `placeholder`, `value`, `onChange`, `rows`, `data-testid`, and `disabled` props but nothing that supplies an accessible name. Different residual than the already-resolved composer findings on record (review-disclosure microcopy, VotePair label pluralization) — this one is markup-level, not copy-level. Fix: add `aria-label="Add a comment"` to the textarea in `CommentInput.tsx` (or `aria-labelledby` pointing at the adjacent `comment-reminder` "No spoilers" text, which already exists as a sibling element) so screen-reader users get a stable accessible name independent of placeholder or typed content. Single component, no visual change. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (a11y markup only). (URL: /shows/love-island-uk/season/summer-2015, source: critique-pass-71) — 48ec163 — issue: #458 — RESOLVED b78261b: added `aria-label="Add a comment"` to the textarea in `CommentInput.tsx`. No visual change. Pinned with a colocated test asserting `toHaveAccessibleName('Add a comment')`. Verify gate green: 194 test files / 2782 tests, build clean, 2918 e2e passed.
- [ ] [LOW] [anon] /shows/masterchef — the tier-A and tier-B blurbs share templated sentence structure and a repeated clause back-to-back, reading as fill-in-the-blank rather than two distinct editorial calls. `content/shows/masterchef/canon.md` `tier_a_blurb`: "Strong MasterChef. The format working at a high level — a deep bench, clear judging, and genuine culinary stakes across the run." `tier_b_blurb`: "Solid MasterChef. The format working at a consistent level — strong amateur talent, clear judging, a competition that respects what home cooks can do." Both open with the identical "[Adjective] MasterChef. The format working at a ... level" clause and both repeat "clear judging" verbatim. Fix: vary the opening clause on one tier and drop the repeated "clear judging" phrase from the other tier's blurb in `content/shows/masterchef/canon.md`. Content-only, two fields. These are the season-zeros and deliberate resets that earned their reputation — the rough drafts and redraws the format kept."); the Love Island UK summer-2015 season page's derived meta description runs to roughly 182 characters. Content-only. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (SEO copy only). - [x] [MED] [anon] /, /shows, /shows/american-idol, /shows/naked-and-afraid/season/the-active-season, /shows/americas-next-top-model/season/the-finale (and every route on the site — systemic) — every page serves the identical static Open Graph image with no per-route differentiation, despite dedicated dynamic `opengraph-image.tsx` route files already existing and already rendering for shows, seasons, and themes. Confirmed via live header inspection on all 5 URLs walked this pass — home, the `/shows` index, a show page, and two season pages all emit `<meta property="og:image" content="https://tiered.tv/opengraph-image"/>`, byte-identical, the site-wide default. Root cause confirmed by source read: `buildMetadata()` at `src/lib/seo.ts:86-128` accepts an optional `image` argument and falls back to `siteConfig.defaultOgImage` when absent (`seo.ts:95-97`), then unconditionally sets `openGraph.images: [{ url: ogImage }]` (`seo.ts:118`) and `twitter.images: [ogImage]` (`seo.ts:124`) — but neither `generateMetadata` in `src/app/shows/[show]/page.tsx` nor in `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx` ever passes `image` to `buildMetadata`. Because Next.js only falls back to file-convention auto-image-detection when a route's metadata carries no explicit `openGraph.images`, the always-present explicit fallback permanently shadows the per-route `opengraph-image.tsx` files at `src/app/shows/[show]/opengraph-image.tsx` and `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/opengraph-image.tsx` (and likely `src/app/themes/[theme]/opengraph-image.tsx`, which sits in a route-group mismatch against `src/app/(default)/themes/[theme]/page.tsx` — a second, independent wiring problem worth checking in the same fix) — those files render correctly when hit directly but are otherwise dead code. This exact gap was observed and left ambiguous at pass-53 ("per-show opengraph-image.tsx route already exists; wiring unclear from text capture" — dropped, not filed); this pass resolves the ambiguity with a definitive source-level answer: it's dead code, never wired. Every social share of a show or season page — Survivor Heroes vs. Villains, American Idol, a Naked and Afraid season — renders the same generic card as the homepage, losing the show's own palette and title in the preview a reader's friend sees before clicking through. Fix: thread an `image` argument through the show-page and season-page (and theme-page) `generateMetadata` calls pointing at their own segment's dynamic route (e.g. `/shows/${slug}/opengraph-image`, `/shows/${slug}/season/${seasonSlug}/opengraph-image`) so `buildMetadata` resolves the per-route image instead of the default; separately confirm the themes route-group placement so its `opengraph-image.tsx` is discoverable by the page it's meant to decorate. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome/SEO metadata only, no outcome exposure). (URL: /, /shows, /shows/american-idol, /shows/naked-and-afraid/season/the-active-season, /shows/americas-next-top-model/season/the-finale, source: critique-pass-69) — 2eccb09 — issue: #448 — RESOLVED 218622f: threaded an explicit `image` argument through all three `generateMetadata` calls (`src/app/shows/[show]/page.tsx`, `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx`, `src/app/(default)/themes/[theme]/page.tsx`), each pointing at its own dynamic `opengraph-image` segment, so `buildMetadata` no longer shadows the per-route file-convention images. Confirmed via production build that the themes route-group split (page.tsx under `(default)/`, opengraph-image.tsx without the group) already resolves correctly — no second wiring problem, contrary to the finding's speculation. Added one regression test per page pinning the OpenGraph + Twitter image URL. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome/SEO metadata only). Verify gate green: 194 unit test files / 2772 tests, 134 script tests, content:check ok (43 shows/670 seasons/43 canons/12 themes/3 legal docs); build (866 pages); 2888 e2e (17.3m). Issue #448 was opened + closed after the fix shipped (the loop's pre-work issue-mirror step was missed this tick — noted so the process gap doesn't repeat silently).
- [x] [HIGH] [authed] /shows/jersey-shore/season/season-1, /shows/selling-sunset/season/season-1 (and every other season page — systemic) — VotePair's up/down buttons have a broken accessible name on every season page in the catalog: aria-label reads the literal fallback string "Vote up votes so far" / "Vote down votes so far" (and post-vote, "Remove your up vote on votes so far"), with no season or show name anywhere in the label. A screen-reader user gets zero context for what they're voting on — every season's vote control announces the identical meaningless phrase. Root cause: `src/components/composition/VotePair.tsx:67` defaults `label = 'votes so far'`; the season-page call site at `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:419-424` renders `<VotePair initialCount={0} targetType="season" targetId={seasonTargetId} />` with no `label` prop, so the default string leaks straight into the aria-label templates at `VotePair.tsx:246-248` and `:288-290`. Confirmed live via click-through on both jersey-shore/season-1 and selling-sunset/season-1: identical broken label before and after voting on both. (URL: /shows/jersey-shore/season/season-1, /shows/selling-sunset/season/season-1, source: critique-pass-68) — 7173f3a — issue: #442 — RESOLVED this commit: the naive fix (passing `label` directly) would have also broken the *visible* pluralized caption under the count, since `label`/`labelSingular` double as `displayLabel` text ("votes so far"). Shipped instead a new aria-only `subject` prop on `VotePair` (`src/components/composition/VotePair.tsx`), read only inside the four aria-label templates via an `ariaSubject = subject ?? label` fallback — `label`/`labelSingular` are untouched, so the visible caption is unchanged. Season-page call site now passes `subject={`${show.name} ${season.title}`}`, so aria-labels read "Vote up Jersey Shore Season 1" / "Remove your up vote on Jersey Shore Season 1" etc. 4 new unit tests cover the default fallback, the subject override on all three aria-labels, the no-leak-into-visible-caption guarantee, and the post-vote remove-vote label. Closes #442.
- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/jersey-shore/season/season-1 (and every single-season show — systemic) — season-page `<title>` stutters when a season's title is the generic "Season N" label: renders "Jersey Shore S1 — Season 1 — tiered.tv", repeating the season identifier twice ("S1" and "Season 1" carry the same information). Same collision confirmed on `/shows/selling-sunset/season/season-1`: "Selling Sunset S1 — Season 1 — tiered.tv". Contrast the working case where the two segments carry different information: `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains` renders "Survivor S20 — Heroes vs. Villains — tiered.tv". Root cause: `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:76` builds the title as `` `${show.name} S${season.number} — ${season.title}` `` unconditionally. Fix: when `season.title` matches the generic `Season ${season.number}` pattern, drop the redundant `S${season.number}` segment and render just `` `${show.name} — ${season.title} — tiered.tv` ``. Single conditional in the title-building logic. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (SEO metadata only). (URL: /shows/jersey-shore/season/season-1, /shows/selling-sunset/season/season-1, source: critique-pass-68) — 7173f3a — issue: #444 — RESOLVED bd07fbf: added a shared `seasonDisplayTitle(show, season)` helper used by both the `<title>` metadata and the JSON-LD Article headline (they carried the identical unconditional template independently — fixing only one would have left the other to drift). - [x] [LOW] [anon] /shows/jersey-shore/season/season-1 — the just-shipped `clipToSeoBudget()` meta-description clipper (`src/lib/seo.ts`, landed this loop at commit 7173f3a to fix mid-clause SEO snippet cuts) has a remaining edge case: when an em-dash introduces a list and the list's first item is followed by a comma, the clause-boundary scan picks that comma over the earlier em-dash (it takes the last-occurring mark of any kind within the budget window), producing a dangling single list item. Rendered: `"Eight roommates share one shore house near the Seaside Heights boardwalk for a single summer, and MTV builds its format around the group's own energy — gym…"` — cuts right after "gym" instead of at the em-dash before it or after the full list. Root cause confirmed by reproducing the function locally on this exact lede: window's last comma sits at char 155 (right after "gym,"), later than the em-dash at char 150, so the `Math.max` across mark types in `clipToSeoBudget` picks the comma. Fix: when the last clause mark found is a comma or semicolon that occurs *after* an em-dash within the same window, prefer the em-dash cut instead (an em-dash reads as a stronger clause boundary than a mid-list comma) — or exclude commas from clause-boundary candidates entirely when a later em-dash exists in the window. Small conditional added to the existing helper; extend `src/lib/__tests__/seo.test.ts` with this lede as a new pinned case. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (SEO metadata only). (URL: /shows/jersey-shore/season/season-1, source: critique-pass-68) — 7173f3a — issue: #518 — RESOLVED: `clipToSeoBudget()` now computes the em-dash position separately and, when the winning comma/semicolon/colon lands after it within the window, backs the cut off to the em-dash instead — the list-introducing em-dash reads as the stronger clause boundary. Pinned with the exact jersey-shore lede as a new `seo.test.ts` case. Closes #518.
- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/americas-next-top-model/season/the-finale — the HOST spec-row caption reads "twenty-fourth season at the helm," implying Tyra Banks hosted 24 continuous cycles, but the same page's own lede says she "returns as host after the one-cycle Rita Ora experiment" — she was not at the helm for cycle 23. The caption and the lede directly contradict each other on the page a reader is looking at right now. Root cause: `host_caption: "{seasonOrdinalWord} season at the helm"` in `content/shows/americas-next-top-model/seasons/24-the-finale.md` substitutes the season's raw air-order number for host tenure via the `{seasonOrdinalWord}` token (`src/lib/show-tenure.ts` / `renderSeasonCaptionTokens`) — that token is only correct for a host continuous since season 1 (per the documented convention in `.claude/agents/content-curator.md` and `skills/ship-content.md` Rule 2: "mid-run hosts keep a literal ordinal"). This is exactly that documented exception, applied wrong. Fix: replace the token with a literal `host_caption` for S24, e.g. "returns after one cycle away," matching the same-class fix already shipped for Survivor S20 (resolved pass-6/phase-43, see Done section). Content-only; one field in `content/shows/americas-next-top-model/seasons/24-the-finale.md`. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (host tenure is public record, not a plot outcome). (URL: /shows/americas-next-top-model/season/the-finale, source: critique-pass-67) — 8b77f14 — issue: #446 — RESOLVED 02f5558: replaced the templated token with the literal `host_caption: "Tyra Banks returns after Rita Ora's one-cycle run"`, matching the lede and removing the contradiction. Closes #446.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] /shows/masked-singer/season/the-tenure — the canon slot sentence reads "Slot #11 of 13 in the The Masked Singer Editor's Canon." The phrase "in the The" is a double article — the definite article "the" appears twice in sequence before the show name. This is an artifact of combining a preposition phrase ("in the") with a show name that starts with "The". Fix: update the template or content to read "in the Masked Singer Editor's Canon" (dropping the possessive "The" from the show name in this context) or restructure as "Editor's Canon slot #11 of 13." Source likely in the season page component or content frontmatter. Spoiler discipline P0 intact. (URL: /shows/masked-singer/season/the-tenure, source: critique-pass-66) — b88e76e
- issue: #507 — RESOLVED e51d36a: root cause was systemic, not masked-singer-specific — `whereItSitsCopy()` in `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx` interpolated `show.name` verbatim into "in the ${show.name} Editor's Canon", doubling the article for every show whose own name opens with "The" (8 shows: masked-singer, the-apprentice, the-circle, the-voice, the-ultimatum, rhod, rhom, rhop). Added `nameAfterDefiniteArticle()` to strip a leading "The " only inside this one phrase; page titles/OG text untouched. Colocated tests pin the fix + the null-canon branch (which uses `show.name` directly, not the "in the" phrase, and stays untouched).
- [x] [MED] [anon+authed] /shows/americas-next-top-model — the blurb and H1 use the show's production term "cycles" ("24 cycles. One photo at a time.") while the stat strip uses "24 SEASONS AIRED". A first-time visitor who reads the hero sees two different terms for the same count within one scroll — "cycles" and "seasons" are not interchangeable in everyday usage and no inline gloss bridges them. The stat strip uses "SEASONS AIRED" because it is a generic component shared across all shows; ANTM is the only show in the catalog that uses a non-standard unit name. Fix: add `seasons_unit: cycles` (or an equivalent mechanism) to the show's frontmatter and wire the stat strip to prefer it when present, so the label reads "24 CYCLES AIRED" for ANTM and falls back to "SEASONS AIRED" for every other show. Alternatively, update the blurb/tagline to "24 seasons" to match the stat strip's vocabulary and note the show's own "cycle" terminology parenthetically in the editorial copy. Content-only fix (tagline) or small component lift (stat strip). Spoiler discipline P0 intact. (URL: /shows/americas-next-top-model, source: critique-pass-64) — 0d476ae — issue: #452 — RESOLVED 6f0e1bb: took the content-only alternative path. Rewrote `blurb` to "24 seasons. One photo at a time." (matching the stat strip's "SEASONS IN CANON" vocabulary — all 24 seasons are ranked, not just aired) and added a short parenthetical to `tagline` — "its own vocabulary calls each season a 'cycle'" — so the show's flavor terminology isn't lost, just moved out of the hero's count-bearing line. Left `canon.md`'s pervasive "cycle" usage untouched (that's the editor's-voice methodology copy, not the hero/stat-strip contradiction this finding named). Verify gate green: 194 unit test files / 2772 tests, content:check ok (43 shows/670 seasons/43 canons/12 themes/3 legal docs); build (866 pages); 2888 e2e (16.8m). Closes #452.
- [x] [LOW] [anon+authed] /shows/americas-next-top-model — the "03 · WHEN / When I revisit" editorial section closes with "I'm not claiming to be objective. I'm trying to be honest." This hedge undercuts the confident editorial voice the site targets. The bearings voice spec ("knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken") calls for editorial assertion rather than defensive qualification; "I'm not claiming to be objective" is a defensive opener that signals insecurity rather than conviction. Fix: rewrite the closing sentence to state the editorial position affirmatively — e.g. "The ranking is calibrated against what the show was capable of at its best; the UPN cycles set that bar." Content-only; one sentence in `content/shows/americas-next-top-model/canon.md` `meth_when_p`. Spoiler discipline P0 intact. - [x] [MED] [authed] / — the `HomeDualCallout` community rank explanation ends with "Sign in to count at full weight." This sentence is visible to signed-in users, making it a stale call-to-action for anyone who is already authenticated (and who may already be counting at full weight). The anonymous pass properly shows the sign-in prompt to unauthenticated visitors; the issue is that the prompt is not suppressed for the authenticated state. Fix: make the sign-in sentence conditional — render it only when the user is not signed in, suppress it when `/api/auth/me` reports `signedIn:true`. The existing auth-island pattern from phase 36 provides the client-side hook. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome copy only). (URL: /, source: critique-pass-64) — 0d476ae — severity bumped LOW→MED at critique-pass-76: re-confirmed still broken (verbatim, both viewports) on the home hero — the site's highest-traffic surface — while fully authenticated as `@e2e`; unaddressed across 12 passes despite a documented one-line fix using an existing client hook (phase 36's auth island), so ease is high and the row has aged past what LOW severity should tolerate on a top-fold surface. — issue: #490 — RESOLVED 29a4d37: `HomeDualCallout` converted to a client component mirroring `HeaderView`'s phase-36 auth-island pattern — fetches `/api/auth/me` on mount, suppresses the sign-in sentence once `signedIn:true`, keeps the SSR/pre-hydration signed-out default on fetch failure. Two colocated tests added (suppression + fetch-failure fallback). Verify gate green: 194 test files / 2798 unit tests, build (936 pages), e2e 3098 passed (17.9m). Closes #490.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] /shows — the "586 SEASONS RANKED" stat hero derives from summing each show's `seasons` frontmatter field (total aired seasons per show), not from the count of actually-reviewed seasons. `src/components/shows/showsStats.ts:29` does `totalSeasons += s.seasons`; the content-check reports 551 actual season markdown files. The gap of 35 comes from shows with partial coverage still in the drain queue (e.g., RHOBH contributes 15 to the total though only 5 seasons are reviewed). A first-time visitor reads "586 SEASONS RANKED" as a completion claim — "we've ranked 586 seasons" — when 35 of those have no review, no blurb, and no canonical position. Same class as phase-43 editorial-copy honesty. Fix options: (a) relabel "SEASONS RANKED" → "SEASONS TRACKED" (or "SEASONS AIRED") to match what the stat actually measures; (b) derive the stat from `getSeasons(show)` aggregate count (actual season files) instead of summing `seasons` frontmatter. Option (a) is a one-word label change; option (b) requires a loader call in `showsStats.ts`. Recommended (a) — the label change is the minimum accurate description; the drain queue will close the 35-season gap over subsequent ticks regardless. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (stat label only). Updated the bidirectional test pin in `ShowsHero.test.tsx` (positive: "Seasons aired" present; negative: neither the old "Seasons ranked" nor the per-show "Seasons in canon" leaks here) plus stale doc-comment references in `seasons-stat-label.ts`, `apps/e2e/tests/home.spec.ts`, and `apps/e2e/tests/show-home.spec.ts` (comments only — those specs assert negatively that `/`, `/shows/[show]` do NOT carry the old label, unaffected by the /shows-page rename). Fix: when `shipped >= target`, either hide the pill entirely or change the label to "review in progress" without the ratio (the ratio is meaningful only before completion). DEFAULT_TIER_HEADINGS[band.key]`; `DEFAULT_BLURBS.S = 'The seasons that defend the show.'`; `TIER_HEADLINES.S = 'The seasons that defend the show.'`. The canon does define `tier_b_blurb` but all five ranked entries land in the S tier (ranks 1–5). Fix: add `tier_s_blurb` to `content/shows/below-deck/canon.md` — the existing `tier_b_blurb` value ("Five seasons building the format's identity — from a rough origin run to a Bahamas peak, with a return visit proving the cast-churn design.") would serve as the blurb since it accurately describes the ranked set. Content-only fix; one field addition. Spoiler discipline P0 intact. (URL: /shows/below-deck, source: critique-pass-52) — 59a474c — RESOLVED 847657d: issue #418. Added tier_s_blurb to below-deck/canon.md. Regression pin in content echo test.

- [x] [LOW] [authed] /shows/below-deck — canon entry `tag` and `slot_argument` values render on-page with literal double-quote characters. The markdown body in `content/shows/below-deck/canon.md` uses `tag: "value"` syntax; the body-parser regex `ENTRY_META_RE = /^(tag|slot_argument|community_rank_hint):\s*(.+)$/` captures the full trailing text including the outer double-quotes as part of the string value. The quotes are literal content, not YAML delimiters. All other canonized shows (Survivor, Top Chef, etc.) author tags and slot_arguments without surrounding quotes and render as plain prose. Fix: remove the outer double-quote characters from all `tag` and `slot_argument` lines in `content/shows/below-deck/canon.md` (and audit sibling new-show canon files for the same pattern). Content-only fix. Spoiler discipline P0 intact. (URL: /shows/below-deck, source: critique-pass-52) — 59a474c — RESOLVED ead9f34: issue #425. Audit extended the fix to all 11 affected canon files (Below Deck family, Alone family, Traitors, Love Island US — 76 lines total); `sed` pattern strips outer double-quotes only when the entire value is wrapped, leaving internal quotes (e.g. "Champions") intact.

- [x] [MED] [anon] /about promises "an 80–120 word reason" per Editor's Canon season slot, but the actual season write-ups on show pages span a main take paragraph plus a separate "WHY THIS SLOT" block — the combined text is materially longer than 120 words on every examined entry. A reader who absorbs the About page's format promise and then clicks through to a season canon finds a longer, richer format that the About description does not describe. Verified on the pass-51 anon walker capture: /about body reads "The Editor's Canon is the editor's call — one person, one position per season, an 80–120 word reason."; /shows/top-chef/season/all-stars canon entry renders a ~130-word take body plus a separate ~40-word "WHY THIS SLOT" block = ~170 combined, well past the 120-word ceiling. Same defect class as phase-43 editorial-copy honesty sweep (descriptions of the product that drift from what the product actually delivers). Source: the About page content (likely `content/legal/about.md`). Bearings voice cue — knowledgeable peer; a peer's description of how the canon works should match what the canon delivers. Fix options: (a) **primary path — update the About page description** to reflect the actual two-part format: a main take paragraph plus a concise "why this slot" rationale, removing the "80–120 word" word count in favor of a structural description. (b) alternative — reduce all canon entries to a single 80–120 word block. Rejected — the two-part format is richer and About is the easier edit. Recommended (a) — one content edit. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (About page copy only). - [ ] [LOW] [anon] /themes/best-comeback-seasons "MORE LISTS IN THIS VEIN" section uses asymmetric directional arrows on its two related-list links: a left-pointing ↩ prefix on one link ("↩ CROSS-CANON LIST / Returnee seasons that paid off") and a right-pointing ↪ suffix on the other ("CROSS-CANON LIST ↪ / Finales that stuck the landing"). The asymmetric orientation implies a prev/next sequence — one list "before" this one, one "after" — but both links lead to unrelated lists with no sequential ordering relationship to the comeback-seasons list. - [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains comment composer affordance for an authed user reads `The thread / Add a thought · no spoilers. / as @e2e / ⏎` (desktop) — and never tells the author that posts go through editorial review before appearing publicly. The site's pitch (`plan/bearings.md` voice contract + the spoiler-discipline P0 invariant) is that every comment is reviewed for spoilers before going live; the phase-36 closure (e95b019) explicitly built the held-comment server-side read path so an author can see their own pending comment with a `held for review` affordance. But the *composer* (the input the author writes into) carries no microcopy at all about the review delay. A returning member types a thought, submits, expects to see their comment appear, and finds nothing — the held-comment affordance only renders after submission and only on the published-comments surface below. Verified on the pass-49 authed walker capture at f4ebe96; same shape on mobile (`as @e2e / Tap to write`). Same defect class as the resolved phase-36 held-comment surface work — the read-side honesty is in place; the residual is composer-side honesty (telling the author before they type rather than only after). Source: comment-composer component on the season + show pages (likely `src/components/comments/CommentInput.tsx` or the `interactions.jsx` port at the production layer). Bearings voice cue + `CLAUDE.md` "knowledgeable peer" — a peer warns you the editor sees the post before the public does, in the same breath as `no spoilers.`. Fix options: (a) **primary path — add a one-line affordance under the input** (or as a hint paragraph attached to the `· no spoilers.` line). Candidate copy: `· no spoilers. we read every thread before it goes public — usually under a day.` (folds the review-delay disclosure into the existing affordance, no new line of UI). Or: `· no spoilers. held briefly for spoiler review before it appears.` Single content + render change on the composer component. (b) alternative — render the review-delay affordance only after submission as a toast / inline state. Rejected — the goal is *before* the author commits, not after; pre-submission honesty is the editorial bar this site set. (c) alternative — leave as-is. Rejected — the composer is dishonest to its own author by omission, which the spoiler-discipline P0 framing makes worse. Recommended (a) — fold the review-delay disclosure into the existing `· no spoilers.` line on the composer affordance. Pin: extend the existing comment-composer test in `src/components/comments/__tests__/CommentInput.test.tsx` (or wherever the production composer lives) to assert the review-delay copy is present in the rendered affordance for authed users. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (composer microcopy only — and the change directly serves the P0 contract by making the review process visible). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-49) — f4ebe96 — issue: #423 — RESOLVED 71ed0a1: stub text updated from "Add a thought · no spoilers." to "Add a thought · no spoilers. held briefly for review." — folds the review-delay disclosure into the existing affordance before the author commits. Pass-44 test updated to prefix-match (no longer anchors at the terminal period). New pass-49 describe block adds bidirectional pin: positive on "held briefly for review", negative that the bare "no spoilers." form is gone. Closes #423.
- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/survivor hero reading order is fragmented — the visual stack runs H1 `Survivor` → blurb `50 seasons. One torch at a time.` → breadcrumb `SHOWS / SURVIVOR` → meta stats `50 SEASONS IN CANON / May 2026 / CANON REVISED` → body lede `Strangers on a beach…`. The breadcrumb arrives *between* the hero blurb and the meta stats — neither above the H1 (standard pattern) nor below the hero block as a whole, but interleaved mid-content. A first-time visitor's eye scans Survivor → 50 seasons → `SHOWS / SURVIVOR` → 50 → May 2026 → Strangers on a beach…, which reads as the breadcrumb fragment landed in the wrong DOM slot. Verified on the pass-49 anon walker capture at f4ebe96; consistent across desktop + mobile viewports. Same defect class as the resolved pass-22 #261 mispositioned-label finding (chrome-token-repeat layer) and the resolved pass-48 (i) /themes featured-tile + index-card prefix-share work, now at the show-page hero-block reading-order layer. Source: show page hero render likely at `src/app/shows/[show]/page.tsx` (the breadcrumb + hero arrangement is in the page's JSX tree). Bearings voice cue + visual law (`design/CLAUDE.md`) — the show page hero is supposed to be the colored-block + huge-serif treatment where type does the heavy lifting; the breadcrumb interleaving fragments that hero's reading rhythm. Fix options: (a) **primary path — move the breadcrumb above the H1** (standard pattern: breadcrumbs anchor at the top of the content area, before the hero). Single JSX-tree reordering; no content change. The reading order becomes `SHOWS / SURVIVOR → Survivor → 50 seasons. One torch at a time. → 50 SEASONS IN CANON / May 2026 / CANON REVISED → Strangers on a beach…` — uninterrupted hero block. (b) **alternative — move the breadcrumb below the hero block entirely** (after the meta stats, before the body lede). Acceptable; less standard. (c) **alternative — leave as-is**. Rejected — the breadcrumb between the hero blurb and the meta stats fragments the hero block's reading rhythm, and the colored-block + huge-serif treatment is supposed to be uninterrupted per `design/CLAUDE.md`. Recommended (a) — move the breadcrumb above the H1. Pin: extend the existing season-page reading-order e2e (if any) or add a new colocated test in `src/app/shows/[show]/__tests__/page.test.tsx` asserting that the breadcrumb's `data-testid` appears in the DOM order *before* the H1's. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (layout-only change). (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-49) — f4ebe96 — issue: #426 — RESOLVED 2e71b4e: moved `show-hero-crumb` div from `show-hero-meta` (right column) to `show-hero-cover` (left column) as the first flex item, before the H1. `show-hero-cover` uses `justify-content:flex-end` so the stacking order within the cover is crumb → H1 → blurb from top to bottom at the bottom of the left column; the right meta column retains only stats, tagline, and shield. Reading order is now crumb → H1 → blurb → stats → tagline — uninterrupted hero block as recommended. No CSS or content change. Pin: `ShowHero.test.tsx` gained a DOM-order assertion in the first test: `cover.contains(crumb)` (crumb is in the cover column, not meta) + `crumb.compareDocumentPosition(h1) & DOCUMENT_POSITION_FOLLOWING` (crumb appears before H1 in the DOM). Verify gate green: leg1 194 files / 2736 tests; leg2 build (438 pages); leg3 1562 e2e (9.0m). Closes #426.
- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains Section 03 `WHERE IT SITS IN THE CANON` is the only one of six numbered prose sections whose entire body is a two-sentence stub that restates facts already shown above and below: `Slot #02 of 50 in the Survivor Editor's Canon. The seasons on either side show what we ranked it against.` Section 01 (THE TAKE) carries the take. Section 02 (SHAPE) names the rhythm. Section 04 (WHAT TO WATCH FOR) carries 4 spoiler-safe pointers. Section 05 (ADJACENT) names the sibling seasons. Section 06 (APPEARS IN) lists cross-canon references. Section 03 is the only section that promises context (`WHERE IT SITS IN THE CANON`) and delivers two sentences whose entire content (the #02 of 50 fact + the sibling pointer) is already present in the hero pill above and section 05 below. Verified on the pass-49 anon walker capture at f4ebe96; consistent across desktop + mobile viewports. The body also leaks the unresolved `we` editorial pronoun (finding above) — `we ranked it against` — but that's a smaller axis here than the section's structural emptiness. Same defect class as the resolved pass-47 #392 (Section 01 H2 title-restate) and the pass-19 stat-strip-mixed-tile-categories work (chrome grammar discipline), now at the season-page numbered-section body-content layer. Source: the season page numbered-section body — likely `src/content/shows/<show>/seasons/<slug>.md` frontmatter field (possibly `canon_placement_rationale` or similar) consumed by `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx`. Bearings voice cue + the canon's editorial pitch (`80–120 word reason` per `content/legal/about.md:29`) — Section 03 is the natural slot for the per-season ranking rationale, the actual editor's-take-on-why-this-slot prose, which currently lives only on the canon entry surface at `/shows/<show>` and is never quoted on the season page where the reader most needs it. Fix options: (a) **primary path — expand Section 03's body into the actual 80–120 word ranking rationale**: the editor's argument for why HvV sits at #02 and not #01 or #03, with named editorial points (returnee field at full height, the all-star ceiling, the final-tribal verdict beat). The canon entry already carries this rationale at `/shows/survivor`; Section 03 on the season page should either quote it verbatim or expand it with season-page-only color. Content-curator authors the rationale into the season frontmatter (new optional field, e.g. `canon_placement_rationale: z.string().min(80).max(120).optional()`); render reads the field with a fallback to the canon-entry text. (b) alternative — fold Section 03 entirely into the hero pill (drop the numbered section, collapse the page to 5 sections). Rejected — every other section earns its numbered slot, dropping just 03 creates a different asymmetry, and Section 03 is the natural slot for the per-season ranking rationale the season page currently lacks. (c) alternative — leave as-is. Rejected — a numbered prose section whose entire body restates the hero pill is the structural defect, and the section is the only one on the page that doesn't earn its number. Recommended (a) — content-curator authors the 80–120 word ranking rationale into season frontmatter, render reads new field with fallback. Pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` with a `collectSeasonCanonRationaleIssues` invariant — for each canon-included season, assert that Section 03's rendered body is NOT the legacy two-sentence stub (verbatim or near-verbatim match against the current `Slot #N of M…The seasons on either side show what we ranked it against.` template). Initial floor: lax (warn on all current 297 seasons rendering the stub); strict at floor 0 once the catalog drains. Same lax→strict mechanic as the existing `SEASON_SECTION_SUBHEAD_STRICT` and `WATCHLIST_PHRASE_REPETITION_STRICT` toggles. Bidirectional drift guard: future authoring that defaults Section 03 back to the stub trips at verify time. Sibling colocated test in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts`. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial-rationale expansion only; the rationale frames the *take*, not the outcome — content-curator P0 contract). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-49) — f4ebe96 — issue: #453 — RESOLVED 5930f7f: took a simpler variant of primary path (a) than the row proposed — no new schema field needed. Every canon entry already authors an 80-120 word editorial `rationale` (`canonEntrySchema.rationale`) that was already quoted on the show canon page (`/shows/<show>`) but never surfaced on the season page. `whereItSitsCopy` in `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx` now takes an optional `rationale` argument and quotes it in place of the generic "seasons on either side" filler sentence across all three branches (multi-entry canon, sole-entry canon, and — unchanged — the no-canon-rank fallback); falls back to the legacy stub only when a season carries a legacy `canonical_position` with no matching canon.md entry to quote from. Because the content already existed for every ranked season in the catalog, this fixes the stub across the whole catalog in one commit rather than just HvV — a broader fix than the row's single-page framing. Did not add the row's proposed `collectSeasonCanonRationaleIssues` content-check invariant (no lax→strict drain needed since there's no new field to backfill; the fallback branch only fires for the small legacy-`canonical_position`-without-canon-entry set, which is a data-completeness question, not a content-authoring drain). Colocated test coverage: `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/__tests__/page.test.tsx` gained 3 new cases in the existing `whereItSitsCopy` describe block — rationale quoted in the multi-entry branch, rationale quoted in the sole-entry branch, and the legacy-stub fallback confirmed when rationale is undefined. Verify gate green: 194 files / 2775 unit tests, build (866 pages), 2888 e2e (16.7m). Closes #453.
- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains Section 01 (THE TAKE) H2 literally restates the page H1 `Heroes vs. Villains.` (with the same period) while sections 02–06 carry real editorial subheads — `A rhythm worth tracking.` (02 SHAPE), `The #02 slot.` (03 WHERE IT SITS), `4 moments, no spoilers.` (04 WHAT TO WATCH FOR), `Either direction.` (05 ADJACENT), `Cross-references.` (06 APPEARS IN). Section 01 is the only one that degenerates into a page-title-restate, breaking the rhythm the rest of the page sets and burning the first editorial slot — the one the reader hits first below the hero — on a label they already read above the fold. Verified rendered shape on pass-47 anon walker capture at 6c72463: hero H1 reads `Heroes vs. / Villains`, then eyebrow `THE TAKE` (the `01` numeral + section name on the same eyebrow line), then `<h2>Heroes vs. Villains.</h2>`, then body opens `Twenty returnees split into a heroes tribe and a villains tribe…`. Sections 02–06 each carry a 2-to-5-word editorial fragment that names what the section is going to argue. Section 01's H2 is the only one that names *what the page already is*. Same defect class as the resolved pass-22 #261 mispositioned-label finding (chrome-token-repeat layer) and the resolved pass-19 stat-strip-mixed-tile-categories work (chrome grammar discipline), now at the season-page section-subhead layer. Source: the season page numbered-section subhead — likely `src/components/season/SeasonBodySection.tsx` or equivalent reading from `content/shows/<show>/seasons/<slug>.md` frontmatter (verify the field name at fix-time — possibly `take_subhead` or `take_h2` per the section's editorial slot). Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer doesn't restate the title as the first subhead. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rewrite the Section 01 H2 to an editorial fragment in the same register as 02–06**. Candidate rotations lifted from the body's existing argument: `A returnees season at full height.` (anchors to the page's all-veteran cast claim); `The all-star ceiling.` (anchors to the ceiling-of-the-format frame the entry's other surfaces already carry); `Twenty returnees, one verdict.` (anchors to the season's structural fact + the verdict-line the canon and themed-list surfaces already use). The content-curator picks the rotation that best previews the take. Single content-file edit at the season frontmatter + a one-line render change at the section-subhead component if the field doesn't yet exist (most likely the field already exists and is being defaulted to `season.display_title` when absent — verify at fix-time). (b) **alternative — drop the H2 entirely on Section 01** and let the eyebrow + body carry the section. Rejected — every other section on the page has a subhead; dropping it on 01 creates a different asymmetry. (c) **alternative — leave as-is**. Rejected — the title-restate-as-first-subhead is the defect class, and the pattern is the only outlier on the page. Recommended (a) — content-curator rewrite to a 2-to-5-word editorial fragment that previews the take, matched to the register of 02–06. Pin: extend the existing `scripts/content-check.ts` invariants with a `collectSeasonSectionSubheadIssues` check — for each season frontmatter, assert that the Section 01 (THE TAKE) subhead (whatever field name it lives under) is NOT a verbatim case-insensitive match for the season's `name` / `display_title` / page H1 form. Initial floor: lax (warn only on existing matches); strict at floor 0 once the catalog drains. Bidirectional drift guard: a future authoring pass that defaults Section 01's subhead back to the season title trips at verify time. - [x] [MED] [anon] /about section order buries the project's identity statement at the page's fourth and final major section so a first-time visitor reads the mechanics before they read what the project *is*. Verified rendered shape on pass-47 anon walker capture at 6c72463: section headings in DOM order across the /about body are (1) `How voting works` (the weighting-ladder mechanics), (2) `Spoilers policy` (the spoiler-discipline contract), (3) `Become an editor` (the contribution call), (4) `What this is` (the identity statement). The `What this is` body reads `An experiment. Built and operated by one person. Free to use. No ads, no third-party tracking, no email collection beyond sign-in.` — the manifesto a stranger arrives with the question to. The page's only lede sentence above the four sections reads `tiered.tv ranks TV seasons without spoiling them.` — true but mechanical, doesn't carry the personal / experiment / one-person framing the bottom section finally delivers. A stranger reading top-down hits the weighting-ladder math at scroll-offset ~700px before they hit the identity statement at scroll-offset ~2458px — past the fold by a wide margin on mobile. Same defect class as the resolved pass-39 /about lede-narrator-drift work (page-identity-on-/about layer), now at the section-ordering layer. Source: `content/legal/about.md` (the markdown source the /about page renders from; verify the field name / heading order at fix-time per the `content/legal/` schema). Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer leads with what the thing is, then how it works. The identity statement becomes the lowest-friction answer to the question a stranger arrives with, the weighting-ladder mechanics follow as section 02 (still adjacent to the contract / spoilers section it belongs near), and `Become an editor` stays as the page-tail call. Section numbering on the page (if any — verify whether the page renders numbered eyebrows like the season page does) updates in lockstep. (b) **alternative — leave the order but rewrite the hero lede** to carry the identity statement up to the fold. Smaller blast radius (single sentence rewrite) but redundant with the existing `What this is` section — moves the same content twice rather than fixing the order. Rejected. (c) **alternative — leave as-is**. Rejected — the section order is the defect; identity-before-mechanics is the standard editorial order for an `/about` page. Recommended (a) — content-file reorder at `content/legal/about.md`. Pin: extend `src/content/__tests__/legal.test.ts` or equivalent with a case asserting that the /about page's first major heading after the hero is `What this is` (or whatever the post-reorder section 01 title becomes); guards against a future content rewrite that defaults the order back to mechanics-first. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (content section reorder only; no per-show verdict, no canon position, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the identity statement names the project, not its content). - [x] [MED] [anon] Home featured-cover `card_tagline` opener and `/shows/survivor` hero `tagline` opener share the verbatim 11-word clause `The format that invented itself in episode one,` — a reader on the canonical click path home → /shows/survivor reads the same opener twice within five seconds. The `card_tagline` / `tagline` split was introduced exactly so the show page hero could be the only surface that quotes the full tagline (per `CLAUDE.md` show-identity contract: `the show page hero is the only surface that quotes the full tagline`), but the split currently shares the first clause across both surfaces, so the optimization buys nothing on the highest-traffic click path. Verified rendered shape on pass-47 anon walker capture at 6c72463: home `<HomeFeaturedCover>` cover-sub reads `The format that invented itself in episode one, and is still finding new ways to ask who you really are.` (sourced from `content/shows/survivor.md:12` `card_tagline`); `/shows/survivor` hero subhead reads `Strangers on a beach, voting each other off until one is left standing. The format that invented itself in episode one, and has spent twenty-six years rediscovering what it is.` (sourced from `content/shows/survivor.md:11` `tagline`). Two surfaces, two different sentences total, but both open with `The format that invented itself in episode one,` — eleven verbatim words in a row, second sentence on the show page hero, first sentence on the home cover. Same defect class as the resolved pass-46 #391 themes index sub-band eyebrow-personifies-lists finding (cross-surface within-click-path echo at the lede-shape layer); also the same defect class as the resolved pass-43 #322 within-list verb-object close-beat repetition (same-show two-card distance), now at the home-to-show-page click-path layer. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer doesn't open with the same 11-word clause twice in five seconds on the canonical reading path. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rewrite the `card_tagline` opener so it doesn't share its first clause with `tagline`**. Candidate rotations at `content/shows/survivor.md:12` that approach the show from a different angle on the card while keeping the existing 13-word density floor (cf. `content/shows/<other-show>.md:card_tagline` lengths): `Strangers on a beach, twenty-six years in, still finding new ways to ask who you really are.` (anchors the card to the show's premise — the beach, the longevity — and keeps the `who you really are` close that distinguishes the line); or `Twenty-six years of strangers on a beach finding new ways to ask who you really are.` (denser variant of the same opener); or `The reality competition that keeps inventing the format it already perfected.` (anchors the card to the perpetual-reinvention frame). Each preserves the editorial point the existing `card_tagline` makes; drops the verbatim 11-word echo with `tagline`. Single content-file edit at `content/shows/survivor.md:12`. (b) **alternative — rewrite the `tagline` opener** so the show page hero leads with a different clause. Rejected — the `tagline` is the canonical long form (per the CLAUDE.md show-identity contract: `the show page hero is the only surface that quotes the full tagline`); rewriting it shifts the canonical form rather than fixing the duplicate-opener defect. (c) **alternative — leave as-is**. Rejected — the within-five-second verbatim echo on the canonical click path is the defect class the `card_tagline` field was introduced to prevent. Recommended (a) — content-file rewrite at `content/shows/survivor.md:12` to a different opener clause; preserves the canonical `tagline` form on the show page hero. Pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` with a `collectShowCardTaglineOpenerOverlapIssues` invariant — for each show whose frontmatter declares both `tagline` and `card_tagline`, assert the two openers do not share a 5-or-more-word verbatim prefix when both are tokenized to lowercase word-arrays. Initial floor: lax (warn only on existing matches across the 13 canonized shows); strict at floor 0 once the catalog drains. Sibling colocated test in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts`. Bidirectional drift guard: a future authoring pass that defaults `card_tagline` to the first clause of `tagline` trips at verify time. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial copy rewrite only of the home cover sub on the featured show; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure — the rewrite names the show's premise, not its outcome).
