Pinned with a new `collectShowCardTaglineOpenerOverlapIssues` invariant in `scripts/content-check.ts`: for each show whose frontmatter declares both `tagline` and `card_tagline`, tokenize each to lowercase word-arrays (the existing `tokenizeForVerbatimEcho` helper) and assert no verbatim 5-gram appears in both. Threshold matches the `VERBATIM_PHRASE_ECHO_NGRAM = 5` precedent set by `collectThemedEntryVerbatimPhraseEchoIssues` for the pass-40 #365 closure — 5-gram is long enough that natural prose almost never produces an accidental match, every hit is a real cross-surface restate. Ships LAX (`CARD_TAGLINE_OPENER_OVERLAP_STRICT = false`) during the catalog drain — 5 sibling shows (bachelor, bachelorette, bake-off, dragrace, top-chef) authored `card_tagline` from the same source phrasing as their `tagline` and surface as warnings; subsequent /ship-content drain ticks rotate one show's `card_tagline` at a time, and the final drain tick flips the toggle to true. One-line toggle mirroring SEASON_SECTION_SUBHEAD_STRICT / WATCHLIST_PHRASE_REPETITION_STRICT (the lax→strict pattern). Colocated test coverage: `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` gained a `show card_tagline/tagline opener overlap (critique pass-47, issue #394)` describe block with 7 cases — (1) live Survivor `card_tagline` passes post-rewrite; (2) historical defect (the pre-rewrite Survivor shape) flags with the 5-gram `the format that invented itself` named in the message + issue #394; (3) clean shape (the post-rewrite Survivor `card_tagline`) passes; (4) show without `card_tagline` passes (the field is optional); (5) case-insensitive (catches a future TITLECASE drift on either field); (6) 4-gram overlap is tolerated (the threshold is 5, the floor of the precedent); (7) multi-shared phrases — the message lists every collision. The e2e fixture at `apps/e2e/tests/show-home.spec.ts:158` (the Survivor meta-description pin) was updated to expect the new `card_tagline` form, deriving the years word through the existing `derivedSurvivorTenureWord` helper so the pin survives the {yearsWord} substitution path; the rotation message also references the historical literal so a future reader can git-blame back to the rewrite. The pass-46 #389 S-tier subhead pin (`src/components/shows/__tests__/tierMeta.test.ts:151`) had a docstring citing the historical Survivor `card_tagline` as the agreement target — docstring updated to a) preserve the historical context for blame-readers and b) note that the format-as-agent S-tier subhead pin survives the #394 card_tagline rewrite (the pin is now an /shows tier-band voice regression guard independent of any one show's tile copy; the assertion's vocabulary commits are unchanged). No URL change, no schema field add, no page-family change. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial copy rewrite only of the home cover sub on the featured show; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the rewrite names the show's premise, not its outcome). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 193 unit files / 2669 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1362 e2e (8.0m). Closes #394.

- [x] [MED] [authed] Survivor canon S-tier entries #01 (Cagayan) and #02 (Heroes vs. Villains) both carry within-entry headline-to-body echoes of the entry's load-bearing claim. Verified rendered shape on pass-47 authed walker captures at 6c72463 of `/shows/survivor` canon pane S-tier expansion: entry #01 (Cagayan) lede reads `every modern Survivor season is, in some form, descended from it` and the WHY THIS SLOT body restates `Every season since 2014 borrows from Cagayan.` — same descent-from-Cagayan claim, twice, four-and-a-half-tokens apart in adjacent paragraphs of the same expanded card. Entry #02 (Heroes vs. Villains) lede reads `Samoa pushes physical play into a place earlier returnee seasons never reached,` then WHY THIS SLOT opens `Samoa's coast gives the season a setting the franchise had not yet handed a returnee cast.` — same Samoa-as-setting argument restated twice in the same expanded card. Both entries are S-tier, both sit at the top of the canon pane, both echo their headline thesis in the body without adding fresh evidence between the two statements. Same defect class as the resolved pass-46 #386 /themes/best-finales entries #05/#06 within-entry headline-to-body verbatim echoes — at the higher-traffic /shows/survivor canon-entry surface the prior closure didn't reach. Source: `content/shows/survivor/canon.md` — entry #01 (Cagayan) `lede` + `why_this_slot` fields, entry #02 (Heroes vs. Villains) `lede` + `why_this_slot` fields (verify the exact field names against `src/content/schemas.ts` canon entry shape at fix-time — likely `lede` + a body field like `slot_argument` or `why_this_slot`). Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer doesn't restate the same claim twice in one short card. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rewrite the WHY THIS SLOT body for each affected entry** to a different angle that the lede gestures at but does not own. For #01 Cagayan: rewrite the body to anchor on a specific structural beat the descent claim points to — the cast-archetype reset, the jury-craft template, the strategic-vocabulary expansion — rather than restating that every modern season borrows from it. For #02 HvV: rewrite the body to anchor on the casting-as-format argument (which the lede gestures at with `physical play into a place earlier returnee seasons never reached` but does not own as a body claim) rather than reprising the Samoa-setting note. Each preserves the entry's editorial point; drops the within-entry restate. Single content-file edit at `content/shows/survivor/canon.md` for both entries. (b) **alternative — rewrite the ledes instead** so the body keeps its current claim. Rejected — the lede is the entry's editorial frame the reader reads first; the body should defer to it, not the other way around (same precedent as the pass-46 #386 closure). (c) **alternative — leave as-is**. Rejected — same defect class as the resolved pass-46 #386 closure, on the higher-traffic canon-entry surface; the editorial voice should commit to one framing per entry. Recommended (a) — content-curator rewrite of both WHY THIS SLOT bodies, lede frames intact. Pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` with a `collectCanonEntryHeadlineBodyEchoIssues` invariant mirroring the pass-46 #365 `collectThemedEntryVerbatimPhraseEchoIssues` helper — for each canon entry across all `content/shows/*/canon.md` files, tokenize `lede` + `why_this_slot` (or whichever body field the schema declares) to lowercase word-arrays, compute 5-grams for each, assert the intersection is empty. Initial floor: lax (warn only on existing matches); strict at floor 0 once the catalog drains. Bidirectional drift guard: a future authoring pass that re-introduces a 5+ word verbatim echo at any canon entry trips at verify time. Sibling colocated test in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts`. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial body rewrite only; the helper tokens count words, not verdicts; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on either surface — the rewrites anchor structural claims, not outcomes). - [x] [MED] [authed] Cross-surface list-meta shape drift on the catalogue's list surfaces: the same theme list surfaces four different sub-eyebrow meta framings across one walk. Home list-row (`src/components/home/HomeListRow.tsx:33-34`) renders `{shows} shows · {entries} entries` → reads `6 SHOWS · 7 ENTRIES` for Finales-that-stuck-the-landing (CSS uppercase via the home list-row meta style). /themes featured-rail card (`src/components/lists/FeaturedCard.tsx:53` with showLabel resolution at `:21-23`: `isSingleShow ? shows[0].name : 'Cross-canon'`) renders `{showLabel} · {entryCount} entries` → reads `CROSS-CANON · 7 ENTRIES` for the same list. /themes index card (`src/components/lists/ListRow.tsx:38-40`) renders just `{entryCount} entries` + status on the next line → reads `7 ENTRIES / STABLE LIST` (no show-count or scope chip). /themes/best-finales detail hero (`src/components/lists/ListDetailHero.tsx:62-79`) renders a four-cell dl meta-strip → `ENTRIES 7 / SPANS 6 shows / CURATED BY tiered.tv editor / LAST REVISED June 2026` (the only surface that calls show-count `SPANS` and the only surface lowercased on the value side `6 shows` because the `meta-val` doesn't uppercase). Same list, same week, four different shapes. A reader who hops home → /themes → list-detail in two clicks reads four list-meta voices for one fact. Concrete observed shapes from the pass-40 walker captures (the same Finales-that-stuck-the-landing list rendered on each surface): home tile `Finales that stuck the landing / 6 SHOWS · 7 ENTRIES` ; /themes featured-tile `CROSS-CANON · 7 ENTRIES / Finales that stuck the landing / ... / STABLE LIST / read the list →` ; /themes index card `Finales that stuck the landing / ... / 7 ENTRIES / STABLE LIST / →` ; /themes/best-finales hero `ENTRIES / 7 / SPANS / 6 shows / CURATED BY / tiered.tv editor / LAST REVISED / June 2026`. Same defect class as the resolved pass-38 #340 `SHOWING · ALL 9 IN THE INDEX` mispositioned-label finding (catalogue-page eyebrow voice consistency) and the resolved pass-38 `stable list` casing drift (#342, `.feat-foot` vs `.list-row-meta` divergence — both renders ran through `formatThemeStatus()` but typeset differently per CSS); same root cause class — list-meta eyebrows render the same canonical facts (`theme.entries.length`, distinct shows covered) via independent ad-hoc literals across components rather than a shared helper. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer cites a list with one accounting voice across the surfaces the reader hops through. Fix options: (a) **primary path — extract a `formatListMeta(theme, shows)` helper to `src/lib/themes-format.ts`** returning a canonical shape `{ showCount, entryCount, showLabel, scopeLabel }` plus a `formatListMetaLine(theme, shows)` returning the canonical concatenation `{N} shows · {M} entries` (the home / `HomeListRow` form is the cleanest two-fact accounting — distinct shows AND distinct entries, both bare integers, separated by a middot). Re-wire `HomeListRow.tsx:33-34` (unchanged literal — it's the baseline), `FeaturedCard.tsx:53` (drop the `CROSS-CANON` / `Single-show` label conflation; cross-canon vs single-show is *coverage scope*, not list-meta, and it's already declared by the bullet-cluster above the line + the `/u/[handle]`-scoped `data-coverage` attr at `HomeListsStack.tsx:19`), `ListRow.tsx:38-40` (add the missing show-count: `{N} shows · {M} entries` to match home), and `ListDetailHero.tsx:62-79` (the hero meta-strip keeps its dl structure but renames `Spans` cell to `Shows` for consistency, drops the lowercase `shows` unit-word doubling — currently reads `SPANS / 6 shows`, becomes `SHOWS / 6` to match the bare `ENTRIES / 7` cell next to it). Single helper extraction + four call-site rewires; no schema change. (b) **alternative — let each surface keep its current literal but unify the casing + uppercasing** at the CSS layer (lift `text-transform: uppercase` from the catalogue-eyebrow base class). Rejected — the casing isn't the defect (uppercase is already consistent); the *content* of the eyebrow drifts across surfaces. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — accept the four voices. Rejected — same defect class as pass-38 #340 / #342 at a different layer; the catalogue's accounting voice should be one voice. Recommended (a) — single helper extraction in `src/lib/themes-format.ts`, four call-site rewires, brings home / /themes / /themes/[theme] to one shape (`{N} shows · {M} entries`). - [x] [LOW] [anon] /themes/best-finales #02 (Survivor S20 Heroes vs. Villains) within-entry headline-to-body phrase echo: the six-word phrase `verdict on the returnee era` appears in both the entry headline at `content/themes/best-finales.md:26` (`title: "The all-star format at its ceiling, closing on a final tribal that reads like a verdict on the returnee era."`) and the entry body close at `content/themes/best-finales.md:27` (`blurb: "Twenty returnees split into heroes and villains compress everything Survivor had been by 2010 into one cast. The jury reads like a court built from the show's own history, and the vote plays as a verdict on the returnee era it was about to enter."`). Same six-word phrase repeats inside one short entry. The headline already does the framing work — declaring what the final tribal "reads like" — and the body close repeats the same conclusion verbatim. A reader scanning the entry reads the same verdict twice in 80 words. Same defect class as the resolved pass-38 #05/#06 within-entry headline-to-body echoes closed a5c1de9 (Round Table tightening + crown coronation restated verbatim across headline + body of the same entries); #02 is the next instance carrying the pattern. Same defect class also as the resolved pass-23 LOW `final tribal` verb-object beat at the entry close (closed by a content-curator pass that rotated the verb-object form across the entries); that close addressed the *grammatical shape* at the entry close, this row addresses the *verbatim phrase repetition* at the within-entry headline-to-body layer. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer doesn't restate the same six-word frame inside one short entry. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rewrite the blurb's final clause** so the headline keeps `verdict on the returnee era` uniquely. The headline carries the editorial framing; the body should expand on the concrete beat that makes the verdict land, not restate the frame. Suggested rewrite for `content/themes/best-finales.md:27`: `Twenty returnees split into heroes and villains compress everything Survivor had been by 2010 into one cast. The jury reads like a court built from the show's own history, and the final seat sits a player whose run rewrites what the format would chase for the next decade.` (Concrete jury / final-seat beat preserved; the verdict frame stays in the headline alone.) The exact replacement copy is content-curator's call — the row's contract is "rewrite the body close so the six-word phrase appears once, not twice." (b) **alternative — rewrite the headline** to a different framing phrase so the body keeps `verdict on the returnee era` uniquely. Rejected — the headline is the editorial frame the reader reads first; the body should defer to it, not the other way around. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — accept the within-entry echo. Rejected — same defect class as the resolved pass-38 #05/#06 closures; the editorial voice should commit to one framing per entry. Recommended (a) — content-curator rewrite of the blurb's final clause keeping the headline's frame intact. Implementation: tokenize `title` + `blurb` to lowercase word-arrays, compute 5-grams for each, assert their set-intersection is empty. Bidirectional drift guard: catches future entries that develop the same echo. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial rewrite only of the rank-card blurb that doesn't expose the winner — the existing close hints at "the verdict" without naming who won; the rewrite preserves that discipline by shifting to a concrete jury / final-seat beat that names the structural fact without the show's outcome). The replacement preserves spoiler discipline P0 (no winner / elimination / finale beat named) and keeps the entry within the 50–70-word floor; the bigram-level pass-38 invariant above no longer fires on `returnee era` either, since the body's only mention is in the headline (which is exempt by construction). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial rewrite only of the rank-card blurb; the helper tokens count words, not verdicts; new invariant operates on `title` / `blurb` shape, never names a winner / elimination / finale beat). - [x] [MED] [authed] /themes filter-state label `SHOWING · ALL 9 IN THE INDEX` sits directly above the `Featured this month.` block which renders three featured tiles, not nine — the count + block-content immediately under the label mismatch by 6 tiles. The actual nine-tile All-lists grid doesn't begin until the `All lists.` subhead further down the page. A reader scanning the page hits the count label expecting nine tiles to follow, encounters three featured tiles instead, and either reads `9` as a wrong count or reads the featured block as a 3-of-9 reveal. The label and the immediately-under block disagree on what `the index` names. Concrete observed shape on the rendered page (verified across both desktop + mobile authed captures): filter-chip row `ALL / BY TONE / BY STRUCTURE / BY CRAFT / SINGLE-SHOW` → filter-state subhead `SHOWING · ALL 9 IN THE INDEX` → `Featured this month.` block-head → `EDITOR-SELECTED · FEATURED FOR JUNE 2026` → 3 featured tiles (Comebacks worth the swing, Finales that stuck the landing, Premieres that earned it) → `All lists.` subhead → `ORGANIZED BY WHAT THEY'RE ADMIRING` → `BY TONE · 2 / [2 tiles] / BY STRUCTURE · 4 / [4 tiles] / BY CRAFT · 2 / [2 tiles] / SINGLE-SHOW TIERS · 1 / [1 tile]` (= 9). So the label is correct ABOUT the All-lists block four sections down, but mispositioned ABOVE the Featured block which renders three other tiles disjoint from the nine. Same defect class as the resolved pass-22 #261 split-reveal closure (the chip semantics scope the All-lists grid below the Featured block, not the Featured block itself) — that closure addressed the chip *semantics*; this row addresses the *position* of the chip-state subhead which the closure didn't move. Source: filter-state subhead rendered between filter-chip row and Featured block — likely `src/components/lists/ListsFilterMode.tsx` or `ListsHero.tsx` rendering the `Showing · all N in the index` line at the wrong DOM position relative to the Featured block. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer announces what they're showing right before showing it, not in front of a different block. Fix options: (a) **primary path — move the `SHOWING · ALL 9 IN THE INDEX` filter-state subhead so it sits directly above the `All lists.` subhead** (which is the block it describes). Preserves the filter-chip behavior + the Featured block's standalone reveal pattern; aligns label position with block content. Single DOM-position edit. (b) **alternative — rename the label to cover both blocks**: `SHOWING · 3 FEATURED + 9 IN THE INDEX` or `SHOWING · 12 LISTS` so the label scope grows to the page-level total, matching the hero's `12 we'd defend in a group chat` math. Acceptable; preserves position but loses the chip-state semantics (the chips scope the All-lists grid only). (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — accept the label-vs-content mismatch. Rejected — the position drift is the defect. Recommended (a) — single DOM-position edit, mirrors the resolved #261 semantic close at the position layer. Pin: extend the colocated test on the /themes page (verify path — likely `src/app/(default)/themes/__tests__/page.test.tsx` or component-level `ListsFilterMode.test.tsx`) with a regression case asserting the `Showing · all N in the index` subhead sits as a sibling of the `All lists.` subhead in the rendered DOM, NOT a sibling of the `Featured this month.` block. Bidirectional drift guard against a future refactor reverting the position. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome position edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface). (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-38) — d6cf91f — issue: #340 — RESOLVED this commit: took the row's primary path (a). `src/components/lists/ListsFilterController.tsx` API rewritten from a single `children` prop to two named slots — `featuredRail` + `allLists`. The `<span data-testid="lists-filter-mode">` that previously rendered inside `<div class="lists-filter-bar">` (where it visually preceded the 3-tile Featured rail) moves out into a new sibling `<div class="lists-filter-mode-row" data-testid="lists-filter-mode-row">` rendered between `featuredRail` and `allLists` in the scope wrapper's child order. The bar now contains chips only; the mode-row's `nextElementSibling` is the All-lists section, so the `Showing · all 9 in the index` label sits as the eyebrow for the block it describes (nine tiles in the All-lists grid below) rather than for the Featured rail (three tiles, disjoint from the nine). `/themes` page updated to use the slot API. New CSS rule `.lists-filter-mode-row` carries the prior bar-padding (32px top → restored to 64px / 48px mobile to match the section-head's prior 64px/48px breathing room from featured rail) plus an adjacent-sibling selector tightening `.lists-section-head` margin-top to 12px/10px when preceded by the mode-row, so the mode-row + section-head reads as one logical block visually. Pin landed per the row's spec: three new colocated cases in `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListsFilterController.test.tsx` under a new describe block `filter-mode position (critique pass-38, issue #340)`: (1) mode-row is NOT a descendant of the filter-bar (asserts `bar.contains(modeRow) === false` — bidirectional drift guard against a future refactor reverting the mode-text into the bar); (2) mode-row sits between featuredRail and allLists in scope child order (asserts exact reading-order bar → featured → mode-row → all-lists by data-testid index); (3) mode-row is the immediate previous sibling of the allLists slot (asserts `modeRow.nextElementSibling === allStub`, the strongest sibling-position invariant). The existing six chip/mode-text tests + four empty-category suppression tests all migrated to the new slot API (`allLists={<div>children</div>}`) and kept their assertions unchanged. No e2e fixture row owed — `apps/e2e/src/fixtures/page-reads.ts:125` asserts `[data-testid=lists-filter-bar]` exists (still does, with chips); `apps/e2e/tests/themes.spec.ts:73` counts chips inside the bar (still works). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome position edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the DOM-position edit moves a count label, not its content). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 188 unit files / 2510 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1362 e2e (8.7m). Closes #340.

- [x] [LOW] [authed] /themes featured-tile lifecycle label `stable list` renders lowercase while the same string on All-lists tiles below renders uppercase (`STABLE LIST`). The CSS rules diverge: `.feat-foot` (the featured-tile footer) has no `text-transform` so it inherits the source string verbatim from `formatThemeStatus()` (`src/lib/themes-format.ts:43` → `'stable list'`); `.list-row-meta` at `src/styles/screens.css:344` applies `text-transform: uppercase` so the same string renders as `STABLE LIST`. Two tile templates on the same page surfacing the same lifecycle metadata in two typographic registers. Concrete observed shapes from the rendered /themes captures: featured tile #1 closes `... made the premise pay off. The comebacks the audience felt land. / [blank line] / stable list / read the list →`; All-lists tile (BY STRUCTURE, Reunion specials) closes `... ensemble television at its widest. / 7 ENTRIES / STABLE LIST / →`. Both render the same underlying `theme.status === 'stable'` value, both via the same `formatThemeStatus` helper, both ending up at chrome eyebrow-level micro-labels — but typeset differently. Source: `.feat-foot` rule at `src/styles/lists.css:272-281` (no `text-transform`); `.list-row-meta` rule at `src/styles/screens.css:344` (`text-transform: uppercase`); both render `formatThemeStatus()` literal output. The CSS comment at `src/styles/lists.css:353` (pass-33 mobile fix) names the uppercase form `STABLE LIST` as the All-lists shape — confirming the uppercase is the intended chrome treatment, not the lowercase. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer commits to one casing for the same metadata across analogous surfaces. Fix options: (a) **primary path — apply `text-transform: uppercase` to `.feat-foot`** to mirror `.list-row-meta`. Single CSS rule edit at `src/styles/lists.css:272-281`. Brings the featured-tile lifecycle label to `STABLE LIST` matching the All-lists template. Letter-spacing already aligned (both 0.06em / 0.1em range — `.feat-foot` letter-spacing 0.06em → match the All-lists 0.12em if alignment desired). (b) **alternative — drop `text-transform: uppercase` from `.list-row-meta`** to bring All-lists tiles down to lowercase. Rejected — the uppercase form is the established chrome treatment named in the pass-33 fix comment + matches the eyebrow grammar across the rest of /themes (`SHOWING · ALL 9 IN THE INDEX`, `BY TONE · 2`, `7 ENTRIES`, etc., all uppercase). (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — accept the casing drift. Rejected — same eyebrow-level surface, same source string, two casings reads as accident not intent. Recommended (a) — single CSS rule edit, brings the featured-tile chrome up to match the established All-lists casing. Pin: extend the colocated test on `FeaturedCard.tsx` (`src/components/lists/__tests__/FeaturedCard.test.tsx` if present, else create) with a regression case asserting the rendered `data-testid="lists-featured-status"` element's computed `text-transform` resolves to `uppercase` (or asserts the visible text matches `/^STABLE LIST$/` post-CSS apply via jsdom + getComputedStyle). Sibling positive on `ListRow.tsx` asserting `.list-row-meta` continues to render uppercase. Bidirectional drift guard against future refactors. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome CSS edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the casing edit runs on the lifecycle metadata, not the metadata's content). (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-38) — d6cf91f — issue: #342 — RESOLVED a0a0852: took the row's primary path. `src/styles/lists.css` `.feat-foot` rule gained `text-transform: uppercase` (line 299) so the lifecycle status label on featured tiles now renders `STABLE LIST` / `UPDATED THIS WEEK` / `REVISED <MONTH YYYY>` matching the All-lists `.list-row-meta` casing. To keep the CTA copy in its authored editorial register, `.feat-foot b` gained an override `text-transform: none` (line 313) so `read the list →` stays lowercase — the uppercase applies only to the lifecycle status (left child) the row scoped. CSS comment on the `.feat-foot b` override cites the #342 closure + the rationale so a future maintainer reading the rule understands why the override exists. - [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains canon-slot module prints two near-identical attribution eyebrows back-to-back on the same scroll region: `CANON ENTRY BY THE TIERED.TV EDITOR` (rendered from the SeasonHero `byline` prop at `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:379` literal `Canon entry by <span className="who">the tiered.tv editor</span>`) immediately followed by `EDITOR'S CANON` (rendered as the `RankScale` headLabel default at `src/components/composition/RankScale.tsx:28` `headLabel = "Editor's Canon"`, used by `SeasonInfoCard` for the canon-rank slot). The reader is told this is editor's canon twice in the same module head before reaching the actual #02 of 50 slot card. One label is doing the same job as the other — the byline names WHO authored the entry, the RankScale headLabel names WHAT the slot belongs to, and the two map to the same canonical fact (this is the tiered.tv editor's canon, this slot is in it). Concrete observed sequence from the rendered captures (both desktop and mobile): `... The pace doesn't slow, even for sleep. / CANON ENTRY BY THE TIERED.TV EDITOR / EDITOR'S CANON / 50 SEASONS / #02 / of 50 / #02 / #01 · CANON PEAK / #50 · THE TAIL`. The two attribution eyebrows sit in adjacent lines of the linearized DOM — desktop renders them in the right-aside RankScale block + left-column byline side-by-side; mobile renders them stacked vertically. Either way, the reader reads `editor's canon` twice in immediate succession. Same defect class as the resolved pass-31 #306 (cross-surface editorial-byline drift — plural `tiered.tv Editors` vs `/about` singular admission, closed by aligning to singular `the tiered.tv editor`), now at the **within-module double-attribution** layer rather than the cross-surface plural-vs-singular layer. The #306 close updated `SeasonHero` byline to `Canon entry by the tiered.tv editor` (singular, post-close); the RankScale `Editor's Canon` headLabel was not touched at that close because its job was naming the slot's container, not the entry's author. The two surfaces now agree on grammar (both singular) but stack the same attribution claim twice. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer doesn't lead with `Canon entry by the tiered.tv editor` and then immediately follow with `Editor's Canon / 50 seasons / #02 of 50`. Either the byline carries the attribution and the RankScale carries just the slot context (`50 seasons / #02 of 50` without `Editor's Canon` label), OR the RankScale headLabel carries the attribution and the byline drops as redundant. Fix options: (a) **primary path — drop the `CANON ENTRY BY THE TIERED.TV EDITOR` byline on the SeasonHero** when the season is canon-ranked (canonRank != null). The RankScale headLabel + the slot card (`#02 of 50`) carry the attribution implicitly; the byline becomes redundant. The byline stays for non-canon-ranked seasons (canonRank == null) where the RankScale renders the `not yet ranked` state and the byline carries the only attribution. Single conditional render at `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:378-380`. (b) **alternative — drop the RankScale `Editor's Canon` headLabel** when the SeasonHero byline is present. Loses the slot-card eyebrow that pairs with `50 seasons / #02 of 50` as a clean info-card head; the info card would lose its top-line context. Rejected — the RankScale head pairs with the slot count in a clean grammar (`Editor's Canon / 50 seasons / #02 of 50`); dropping it loses the info-card grammar. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — accept the doubled attribution. Rejected — the within-module repetition reads as the same defect class as pass-31 #306 at a different layer. Recommended (a) — single conditional render edit, preserves both labels at the surfaces they each do work (RankScale carries attribution when canon-ranked; byline carries attribution when not yet ranked), drops the within-module double-attribution. Pin: extend the colocated test on the SeasonHero / season page (likely `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/__tests__/page.test.tsx`) with two cases — (1) canon-ranked season (`canonRank: 2`): rendered DOM contains exactly one match for `/editor'?s canon|canon entry by/i` (the RankScale headLabel); (2) non-canon-ranked season (`canonRank: null`): rendered DOM contains exactly one match (the byline). Sibling positive on `RankScale.test.tsx` asserting headLabel stays `Editor's Canon` (no change there). Bidirectional drift guard against a future refactor adding a third attribution surface or removing the conditional. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome attribution edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the conditional gates on canonRank presence, not on canonRank value). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-38) — d6cf91f — issue: #339 — RESOLVED 74065be: took the row's primary path (a). The literal `<span>Canon entry by <span className="who">the tiered.tv editor</span></span>` at `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:378-380` was extracted into a pure named export `seasonHeroBylineFor(canonRank: number | null)` that returns the JSX node only when `canonRank == null` and returns `null` when the season is canon-ranked. The `byline` prop of SeasonHero now reads `byline={seasonHeroBylineFor(canonRank)}`; SeasonHero's existing `byline ? ... : null` render guard at `src/components/composition/SeasonHero.tsx:143` drops the `.hero-byline` element entirely when null, so canon-ranked seasons render the RankScale headLabel `Editor's Canon` as the sole attribution eyebrow in the module head while non-canon-ranked seasons (the not-yet-ranked state) keep the byline as the only attribution surface. Pinned per the row's pin clause: three new colocated cases in `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/__tests__/page.test.tsx` under a new describe block `seasonHeroBylineFor — within-module double-attribution gate (issue #339)`: (1) canon-ranked (`seasonHeroBylineFor(2)` / `(50)` / `(1)`) returns null; (2) not-yet-ranked (`seasonHeroBylineFor(null)`) returns a valid ReactNode whose rendered text contains `Canon entry by the tiered.tv editor` AND a `.who` element with text `the tiered.tv editor`; (3) negative pin asserting no branch of the helper re-introduces a verbatim `Editor's Canon` eyebrow (belt-and-braces against a re-stack). The sibling positive at `src/components/composition/__tests__/SeasonHero.test.tsx` was left untouched — its existing `hides eyebrow and byline when not provided` + `renders eyebrow and byline when provided` cases continue to pin the conditional-render contract at the SeasonHero component layer. No change to RankScale's `Editor's Canon` headLabel default per the row's option (b) rejection (the RankScale head pairs with the slot count in a clean `Editor's Canon / 50 seasons / #02 of 50` grammar; dropping it would lose the info-card grammar). No URL / schema / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — page-side conditional edit only; the smoke walker only pins 200 / H1 / no console errors on `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains` so it inherits the new render cleanly. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome attribution edit only; the conditional gates on canonRank presence, not on canonRank value — no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 188 unit files / 2507 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1362 e2e (8.0m).

- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/survivor S-tier slots #01 (Cagayan) and #02 (Heroes vs. Villains) both close with the identical sentence frame `tiered.tv's canon places it [Nth] because no other [X]...` — two adjacent rationales running the same closing formula at the very top of canon reads as a CMS template tell rather than the knowledgeable-peer voice the rest of the catalog holds. Concrete observed shape on the live `/shows/survivor` canon ladder: slot #01 ends `tiered.tv's canon places it first because no other season has shaped what the show is allowed to be more decisively.`; slot #02 ends `tiered.tv's canon places it second because no other all-star attempt in the format's...`. The frame `tiered.tv's canon places it [ordinal] because no other [noun]` appears verbatim in both rationales, with the only difference being the ordinal slot and the noun specifying what gets compared. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) is `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken, never pretentious. No exclamation points unless quoted. Plain sentences over clever ones.` — a peer wouldn't use the same closing formula twice in a row at the top of the most-visited canon page, especially when the closer is a self-naming brand attribution (`tiered.tv's canon places it`) that doubles as a vanity stamp. The same defect class as pass-31 #306 (cross-surface editorial-byline drift — plural `tiered.tv Editors` everywhere vs `/about` singular admission), but at a within-canon rather than cross-surface scope. Source: `content/shows/survivor/canon.md` entries[0] (`canonical_position: 1`, Cagayan) + entries[1] (`canonical_position: 2`, Heroes vs. Villains) rationale fields — grep the canon-content fileset for the literal `tiered.tv's canon places it` to scope the spread of the frame across this and other shows' canons. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rotate one of the two closers off the `tiered.tv's canon places it [N] because no other [X]` frame**. Recommended target is #02 (HvV) — pass-20 #246's closure for the same finale list already shipped `A closing run that pays off a finale built on real artistry.` for Drag Race, so the surface has surface-native rotational vocabulary to draw on. Candidate rotation for HvV: drop the self-naming closer and let the rationale's own editorial verdict close the entry (e.g. `Decades in, this is the season Survivor will still be measured against.` — already present elsewhere on the HvV-adjacent surfaces). Keep #01's closer if it carries the editorial weight pass-3 #03 / pass-4 #04 / pass-20 #244 closed on, or rotate it instead — the constraint is breaking the formula adjacency, not preserving any particular slot. (b) alternative — sweep the whole `content/shows/<show>/canon.md` corpus for the same `tiered.tv's canon places it` literal, since the frame may be a curator template that propagated across more than just survivor #01 / #02. If the literal recurs across show-canon files (e.g. amazing-race #01 + #02 close the same way, top-chef #01 + #02 close the same way), the right scope is a curator drain across the catalog rather than a one-canon fix. (c) leave-as-is — accept that the closing-formula attribution is a deliberate brand stamp, accept the adjacent doubling as a small voice tic the reader will pass over. Recommended (a) first to break the adjacency at the highest-traffic canon (Survivor is the project's anchor show), then (b) as a follow-up sweep to lift the pattern at corpus scope if the literal recurs elsewhere. Pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` with a `collectCanonRationaleClosingFormulaIssues` invariant — for every `content/shows/<show>/canon.md`, scan each entry's `rationale` field for the closing-sentence shape `tiered.tv's canon places it [ordinal] because no other [noun]`; if two adjacent ranked entries (by `canonical_position`) carry the same closing formula, fail with both `canonical_position`s + the offending phrase. Cheap, catches the editorial-template class at author-time, and surfaces propagation across other shows' canons without requiring a manual sweep. Sibling test pin per the #242 closure pattern: `src/components/canon/__tests__/ShowRanking.test.tsx` gains a fixture case with two adjacent rationales running the same closing formula → assert the rendered surface does not present the same closer twice in a row (bidirectional drift guard against a regression re-introducing the formula at adjacent positions). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (rationale-prose edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination exposure on either surface). (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-32) — b003420 — issue: #312 — RESOLVED 7d10999: took the row's primary path (a). `content/shows/survivor/canon.md` entry #02 (Heroes vs. Villains, canonical_position 2) rationale closer rotated off the self-naming brand-stamp frame — `tiered.tv's canon places it second because no other all-star attempt in the format's history operates at this much sustained pressure across this many recognizable faces.` swapped for `The second slot is its by default. No other all-star attempt sustains this much pressure across this many recognizable faces, and decades on, the rest of the format is still measured against this stretch of TV.` The new closer drops the self-naming `tiered.tv's canon places it [ordinal] because no other [noun]...` literal entirely, preserves the editorial verdict the original closer carried (all-star ceiling, sustained pressure across recognizable faces), and lands `decades on, still measured against this stretch of TV` as the closing beat — rephrased from the HvV `pull` quote to avoid verbatim cross-surface drift. Entry #01 (Cagayan, canonical_position 1) keeps its closer (`tiered.tv's canon places it first because no other season has shaped what the show is allowed to be more decisively.`) — the row's constraint is breaking the formula adjacency, not preserving any particular slot, and #01 carries the editorial weight pass-3 #03 / pass-4 #04 / pass-20 #244 already closed on. New HvV rationale word count: 114 (in-band, 80–120 per `rationaleBody` in `src/content/schemas.ts:288`). Corpus sweep at fix-time confirmed the literal `tiered.tv's canon places it` recurs at only one other surface (`content/shows/project-runway/seasons/01-new-york-2004.md:22` season frontmatter, single occurrence) — single-instance cross-show case outside the row's adjacency scope, left untouched per the row's option (b) sweep threshold ("if the literal recurs across show-canon files (e.g. amazing-race #01 + #02 close the same way), the right scope is a curator drain at corpus scope rather than a one-canon fix"). Pin landed per the row's spec: `scripts/content-check.ts` gained `collectCanonRationaleClosingFormulaIssues` + `CANON_CLOSING_FORMULA_RE = /tiered\.tv's canon places it (?:[a-z-]+) because no other/i` — for every show's canon, scan entries in `entry.rank` order; if two adjacent entries both close with the self-naming brand-stamp frame, fail with both `canonical_position`s + titles. Ships strict at floor 0 (`CANON_CLOSING_FORMULA_STRICT = true`) since the HvV rotation drains the only adjacency pair in the corpus. The non-self-naming `The canon places it...` form (used across every other show's canon — Project Runway, The Challenge, etc.) is deliberately out of scope per the row's editorial framing ("the closer is a self-naming brand attribution... that doubles as a vanity stamp"); only the brand-stamp version is flagged. Single-occurrence is fine; only adjacency trips the gate. The sibling `src/components/canon/__tests__/ShowRanking.test.tsx` test pin proposed in the row was reconsidered at fix-time and deferred — the content-check invariant catches the class at the earlier author-time layer (mechanical floor on every canon file, every push), so a render-level guard on a component that doesn't transform its rationale input would be redundant. Eight new colocated cases at `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` (new describe block `content-check — canon-rationale closing-formula adjacency (critique pass-32, issue #312)`): (a) passes at the live catalog post-rotation (asserts `setContentRoot(null)` reads zero issues); (b) passes when only one entry carries the formula (single occurrence is fine); (c) passes for non-self-naming closers (the `The canon places it...` form across other shows' canons is fine); (d) flags two adjacent entries that both carry the self-naming frame (asserts message contains `canonical_position 1 (One) and 2 (Two)` + `closing-formula adjacency drift` + `issue #312`); (e) does not flag two carriers separated by a non-carrier (only adjacency trips); (f) flags every adjacent pair when three carriers run in a row (n=2 issues for n=3 adjacent); (g) case-insensitive coverage (catches a future authoring pass titlecasing the literal); (h) tolerates a show without a canon (no-op rather than crash). No URL / schema / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — rationale-prose edit + invariant only; the smoke walker only pins 200 / H1 / no console errors on `/shows/survivor` so it inherits the new copy cleanly. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (rationale-prose edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on either surface). - [x] [MED] [anon] / home Community Rank card commits the product to a self-attestation rule the formal /about voting policy never substantiates. Home (`src/components/home/HomeDualCallout.tsx:18-20`, the form shipped at fa9dcf3 closing pass-27 #287, after the earlier pass-26 #282 fix at a0d7cd5) reads `Signed-in voters self-attest they watched the season end to end; new accounts count at 0.25×, anonymous votes at 0.1×.` /about's `How voting works` section (`content/legal/about.md:31-40`) details the 0.1× / 0.25× / 1.0× weighting ladder + the 72-hour edit window + weekly recompute, but never mentions an end-to-end-watched attestation: `Every season asks one question — does it belong in the community top 10? — and your upvote or downvote answers it. One vote per reader per season. Change your mind within 72 hours; after that the vote locks. ... Long-standing accounts carry the most weight. Anonymous-guest votes count at 0.1×. Accounts under 7 days old count at 0.25×. Accounts 7+ days old count at 1.0×. Rate-limits run behind the scenes to catch coordinated voting campaigns, and should never bite a normal reader.` Same defect class as pass-26 #282 (home flat claim `Every voter has watched` contradicted by the weighting ladder on /about) + pass-27 #287 (home two-tier framing vs /about three-tier ladder), now sliding one tier deeper. The two prior closures brought the *weights* into agreement; the attestation rule was added unilaterally on home as part of #282's fix without a matching addition to /about's source-of-truth voting policy. A reader who follows the natural home → about path to verify the attestation can't find it; the home commits to a mechanic the formal policy never substantiates. Bearings voice rule is `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken` (`plan/bearings.md:370`); a peer would either name the attestation on both surfaces or on neither. Source: `src/components/home/HomeDualCallout.tsx:18-20` (the `Signed-in voters self-attest they watched the season end to end` literal, pinned by `src/components/home/__tests__/HomeDualCallout.test.tsx`'s `names the weighting mechanic, not a flat watched-every-voter claim` case) vs `content/legal/about.md:31-40` (the `How voting works` paragraph, no attestation language anywhere in the section). Fix options: (a) **primary path** — add the self-attestation line to /about's `How voting works` paragraph so the formal policy is the source of truth. Candidate insertion right before the weighting ladder: `Signed-in voters self-attest they watched the season end to end before casting.` (single sentence, ~80 chars, names the same mechanic the home Community Rank card already commits the product to). The placement makes the weighting ladder read as the second layer of trust (rather than the only layer), which the home now claims. (b) alternative — drop the self-attestation clause from the home Community Rank card so the home stops promising a mechanic /about doesn't carry: revert `Signed-in voters self-attest they watched the season end to end; new accounts count at 0.25×, anonymous votes at 0.1×.` → `Signed-in voters carry the most weight; new accounts count at 0.25×, anonymous votes at 0.1×.` Reads honestly but loses the "we trust watched voters" editorial substance pass-26 #282 was trying to preserve. (c) reframe both surfaces in a unified third form (e.g. weight + intention + log) — larger blast radius, not a one-tick fix. Recommended (a) — preserves the trust-mechanic the home already commits to, brings /about into agreement, single-sentence /about edit, same closure pattern as #282 + #287 (name the mechanic on the page that owes the truth). Pin: extend `src/app/(default)/about/__tests__/page.test.tsx` with a cross-surface parity case asserting that whenever the rendered `/about` body contains the weighting ladder (matches `/0\.1×|0\.25×|1\.0×/`), the same page MUST also contain the self-attestation language (matches `/self.attest|watched the season end/i`) — bidirectional drift guard so a future authoring pass that drops one but keeps the other trips at unit time. The existing pass-26 #282 + pass-27 #287 pins on `HomeDualCallout.test.tsx` (regression against flat `Every voter has watched`, positive on `/0\.25|new account|under 7/i` + `/0\.1/`) hold unchanged. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (legal-doc prose addition only; no per-season verdict, no canon position change, no winner / elimination exposure). - [x] [LOW] [anon] /themes/best-finales #02 (Survivor S20 Heroes vs. Villains) doubles `final tribal` + a `[verb]s like a [noun]` simile inside ~50 words. Title (`content/themes/best-finales.md:26`) reads `The first all-returnee season closing on a final tribal that reads like a verdict.` Body opener (line 27) reads `The endgame compounds — every conversation freighted, every move read as both move and statement. The final tribal lands like a summary statement for the entire returnee era the show was about to enter.` Two `final tribal` noun phrases AND two `[verb]s like a [noun]` similes (`reads like a verdict` in the title + `lands like a summary statement` in the body's third sentence) all land within one ~50-word card. Same within-entry repetition class as the remaining pass-31 Pending row (#05 `table` x3 in Traitors S02) + pass-31 closed siblings #01 (`live sprint` x2 in Amazing Race S07) + #06 (`artistry/artists` cognate in Drag Race S06); the cross-page voice elsewhere on the same list finds a fresh angle per entry without re-using its own framing noun within itself. Fix options: (a) **primary path** — curator-only edit in `content/themes/best-finales.md` entry #02. Keep the title's `final tribal` (it's the structural beat the entry is built around — the verdict-read frame is load-bearing for the entry's editorial claim about HvV defining the modern returnee finale form), rotate the body's second occurrence + drop the simile redundancy. Candidate rotation: `The endgame compounds — every conversation freighted, every move read as both move and statement. The jury vote reads as a summary statement for the entire returnee era the show was about to enter.` (swaps `final tribal lands like` → `jury vote reads as`, which (1) drops the second `final tribal` noun phrase + (2) drops the second `[verb]s like a [noun]` simile + (3) keeps the editorial point intact — the jury vote IS what the entry is reading as a verdict; the simile structure was carrying redundant load). The entry now reads with three distinct framings (the title's `final tribal that reads like a verdict`, the body's `endgame compounds` opener, the body's `jury vote reads as a summary statement`) instead of two repeats of one + two simile structures. (b) alternative — keep both nouns, rotate one of the two similes off `[verb]s like a [noun]` to a plain declarative: `The final tribal IS a summary statement for the entire returnee era the show was about to enter.` (preserves the noun, drops the simile redundancy by stating the equivalence rather than gesturing at it). Recommended (a) — the larger rotation drops both repetition axes at once (noun + simile structure) without losing the editorial point. Pin: this is the third within-entry repetition finding on /themes/best-finales this critique cycle (pass-31 #309 #310 #308 + this #02 + #05 still pending). The `collectThemeBodyPhraseRepetitionIssues` within-entry stem-pair extension proposed in pass-31 #309 + #308 + #310 is the right pin for all of them — should ship alongside whichever of #05 (`table` x3) or this #02 lands first so the catch-everything invariant lands once across the three findings rather than three times. Recommend deferring the pin to the resolution that picks up two findings in one tick. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial prose only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on either surface — the rotation preserves the entry's editorial framing without naming the winner or the final-vote count). Moment 1 carries the label `OPENER · COLD OPEN` (`content/shows/survivor/seasons/heroes-vs-villains.md` `watch_list[0]` — the literal `cold-open` framing is structural for the season's first-episode beat). Don't blink.` — re-using `cold-open` for the LATE structural slot collides with the label sense of the same word three cards earlier. The two structural slots aren't the same kind of cold open (one is a literal first-episode cold open, the other is a colloquial use to mean "the first minute of the late-game stretch"), so the doubled vocabulary muddles the read at the same time the moments are supposed to be reading as four distinct beats. Bearings voice cue is `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken, never pretentious. No exclamation points unless quoted. Plain sentences over clever ones.` — the doubled word is a small voice tic on a section that gets the season's "what to watch for" job done in four small editorial gestures and shouldn't repeat its own vocabulary within itself. Same within-entry repetition class as the /themes/best-finales pending rows but on a different page family (season-page `watch_list` callouts rather than themed-list entries). Source: `content/shows/survivor/seasons/heroes-vs-villains.md` `watch_list` array — moment 1's `caption: OPENER · COLD OPEN` + moment 4's `body: The cold-open of the late-game stretch is doing real work…`. The label `cold-open` for moment 1 is load-bearing (it names the first-episode structural beat the page is teaching the reader to look for); the body use in moment 4 is the rotatable instance. Fix options: (a) **primary path** — curator-only edit to moment 4's body opener. Candidate rotation: `The first minute of the late-game stretch is doing real work — staging, blocking, who's eating where. Don't blink.` (swaps `cold-open of the late-game stretch` → `first minute of the late-game stretch`, drops the term collision, keeps the staging-blocking-eating beat intact). Alternative: `The opening of the late-game stretch is doing real work…` (similar effect, slightly less specific). Recommended (a) — the more concrete framing (`first minute` over `cold-open`) reads cleaner against the LATE · THIRD ACT label that introduces it. (b) alternative — rename moment 1's label from `OPENER · COLD OPEN` → `OPENER · FIRST HOUR` and let moment 4 keep `cold-open`. Larger blast radius (the `cold-open` label may be load-bearing for the structural beat the page is teaching), worse trade. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial prose only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure — the rotation is at the third-act watch-for callout, which already discloses the LATE structural position the page sanctioned by the phase-26a `watch_list` discipline). Pin: extend `src/components/composition/__tests__/WatchList.test.tsx` (or wherever the `watch_list` rendering is tested colocated) with a within-callout-list vocabulary-collision case asserting no two callouts on the same `watch_list` share the same content-bearing 2-word phrase (excluding label tokens like `EP N` / `OPENER` / `LATE` that are structural). Cheap, catches the class at unit time, scopes to a single page family. (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-32) — b003420 — issue: #325 — RESOLVED this commit: took the row's primary path (a). `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-vs-villains.md` `watch_list[3].body` rotated from `The cold-open of the late-game stretch is doing real work — staging, blocking, who's eating where. Don't blink.` to `The first minute of the late-game stretch is doing real work — staging, blocking, who's eating where. Don't blink.` — drops the term collision with moment 1's structural `OPENER · COLD OPEN` label, keeps the staging-blocking-eating beat intact, and reads cleaner against the LATE · THIRD ACT label that introduces it. The label on moment 1 (`Opener · cold open`) is left untouched — it's load-bearing for the first-episode structural beat the page is teaching the reader to look for. Pin landed per the row's prescription but lifted to content-check rather than the WatchList unit test (the component is a passthrough renderer, so the data-layer scanner catches the class at author-time across all 161 watch_list-bearing seasons rather than once per fixture). New `collectWatchListPhraseRepetitionIssues` in `scripts/content-check.ts` walks every season; for each `watch_list` with ≥2 items, scans content-bearing 2-word phrases from each item's body (using a hyphen-splitting tokenizer so `cold-open` ↔ `cold open` collisions catch — the standard `extractContentBigrams` keeps hyphenated tokens whole and would have missed the finding). Flags when (1) two bodies share a bigram OR (2) one body shares a bigram with another item's label. Label-vs-label collisions are exempt (structural label tokens like `Opener · cold open` / `Late · third act` are supposed to carry recurring scaffolding vocabulary). Ships LAX (warnings, not failures) during the corpus drain — the live catalog surfaces ~40 incidental warnings across Survivor / The Challenge / Top Chef / Traitors (load-bearing show terminology like `blood vs water`, `new era`, `road show`, `round table` shows the format-name-class needs editorial judgment per case); the HvV literal that triggered this finding is the only known offender warranting per-tick rotation, and subsequent /ship-content drain ticks rotate each remaining offender. Final drain tick flips `WATCHLIST_PHRASE_REPETITION_STRICT = true`, one-line toggle mirroring `SEASON_EYEBROW_CALENDAR_STRICT` and the other lax→strict invariants above. Six colocated tests at `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` pin the class: (a) flags the HvV-class case (body re-uses a label noun); (b) flags two bodies sharing a 2-word phrase; (c) does NOT flag label-vs-label collisions (the structural-label exemption); (d) passes a watch_list with distinct vocabulary; (e) skips a season without watch_list; (f) skips stopword-only repetitions. No URL / schema / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — editorial prose edit + corpus invariant only; the smoke walker only pins 200 / H1 / no console errors on the HvV season page so it inherits the new body text cleanly. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial prose edit + corpus invariant only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure — the rotation preserves the third-act watch-for callout's structural framing without naming any in-season outcome). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2445 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs; the new lax invariant emits ~40 warnings, none promote to failures); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1362 e2e (7.8m).
- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains top eyebrow `AIRED SPRING 2010 · FILMED IN SAMOA` contradicts the PREMIERED meta cell three sections later (`Feb 11, 2010`) on the same page. Feb 11 is winter, not spring — HvV aired Feb 11 through May 16, 2010, so the season straddled winter and spring but the eyebrow simplification calls it `spring` while the meta block names the actual premiere date. A reader scanning the page sees `SPRING 2010` at the top and `Feb 11, 2010` three sections down and either flags one as wrong or trusts neither. Same factual-collision class as the resolved `Probst tenth season at the helm` (HvV `host_run` stat error, addressed) but at the season-label layer rather than the per-stat-cell layer. Source: `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-vs-villains.md:12` `eyebrow: "Aired spring 2010 · Filmed in Samoa"` (the literal that renders at the SeasonHero eyebrow slot, pinned by `src/components/composition/__tests__/SeasonHero.test.tsx:112` `eyebrow: 'Aired spring 2010'`). Fix options: (a) **primary path** — swap the eyebrow's season-label clause to a span that includes the premiere date: `Aired winter–spring 2010 · Filmed in Samoa` (smallest diff, accurate to the Feb 11–May 16 run; matches the way the season's own structural beats — premiere on a winter night, finale on a spring night — actually played out). (b) alternative — drop the season-label entirely and let the eyebrow lead with the calendar fact: `Aired Feb–May 2010 · Filmed in Samoa` (more precise, but reads less editorial than the season-label form). (c) **alternative** — keep `spring 2010` but only if the PREMIERED meta cell is also corrected to a spring-coherent date (e.g. surfacing the merge or the finale date instead of premiere); larger blast radius, worse trade. Recommended (a) — single-frontmatter edit, preserves the editorial reading-of-the-season cadence (the `winter–spring` span reads as a story arc), aligns with the page's own PREMIERED meta. Pin: extend `src/components/composition/__tests__/SeasonHero.test.tsx` with a within-page consistency case asserting that whenever the rendered eyebrow contains a single-season-label string (e.g. `spring|fall|summer|winter`), the page's PREMIERED meta date must fall within that calendar season. Cheap, catches the class at unit time, generalizes to every season eyebrow. Alternative pin: a `scripts/content-check.ts` invariant — for every `content/shows/<show>/seasons/*.md`, if `eyebrow` matches a single-season-label regex AND `premiered:` date is present, fail if the date does not fall within that season (Northern Hemisphere convention: spring = Mar 1–May 31, summer = Jun 1–Aug 31, fall = Sep 1–Nov 30, winter = Dec 1–Feb 28/29). Catches the class corpus-wide at author-time. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (eyebrow-label edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure — the new label `winter–spring 2010` discloses only the calendar span of the air-window already published on the page's PREMIERED meta cell). HvV literal: `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-vs-villains.md:12` `eyebrow: "Aired spring 2010 · Filmed in Samoa"` → `"Aired winter–spring 2010 · Filmed in Samoa"` (45 chars, within the 80-char eyebrow schema cap; the `winter–spring` span discloses both halves of the Feb 11–May 16 run on the page's own surface). The SeasonHero unit-test fixture (`src/components/composition/__tests__/SeasonHero.test.tsx:112-117`) swapped to the same literal in lockstep so the synthetic eyebrow-and-byline render case carries the post-fix text (no behavior change — the test pins HTML rendering of any provided eyebrow string). Pin landed per the row's alternative pin clause: new `collectSeasonEyebrowCalendarIssues` in `scripts/content-check.ts` — for every season with both an `eyebrow` carrying a single calendar-season label (spring/summer/fall/autumn/winter; `\b(...)\b` matcher; `autumn` synonymized to `fall`) AND a `premiere_date`, flags when the premiere date's month falls outside that calendar season under the Northern Hemisphere meteorological convention (spring=Mar–May, summer=Jun–Aug, fall=Sep–Nov, winter=Dec–Feb). Multi-label spans like `winter–spring 2010` opt out (`labels.length !== 1` short-circuit — the editor has already disclosed both halves of the run). The drift message names the eyebrow's label + the premiere's calendar season + suggests an adjacent-pair span rewrite (winter–spring for a `spring` label drifting into winter; falls back to a non-specific "Rewrite as a span across the run" suggestion when the label is 180° off, e.g. winter↔summer) AND a calendar-fact alternative (`Aired Feb–May 2010`). Ships LAX (warnings, not failures) during the corpus drain — a walk of the live catalog finds ~38 offenders (Bachelorette May-premiere `summer` runs, Survivor Feb-premiere `spring` runs, three Project Runway / Challenge Aug-premiere `fall` runs, Drag Race S12 Feb 28 `spring 2020`, Love Island UK Summer 2016 May 30 `summer 2016`) that do not warrant the per-tick scope of an /iterate fix; subsequent /ship-content drain ticks rewrite each offender's eyebrow and the final drain tick flips `SEASON_EYEBROW_CALENDAR_STRICT` to true (same one-line toggle pattern as `CROSS_SHOW_STRICT` during phase 41 and the other lax→strict invariants in content-check.ts). Pin tests landed at `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts`: new describe block with 7 cases — (a) flags the HvV literal class (synthetic Feb 11 + `spring` → flag); (b) passes when premiere inside the named season; (c) skips span eyebrows; (d) skips eyebrows without any season-label; (e) skips seasons without `premiere_date`; (f) treats `autumn` as a synonym for `fall`; (g) flags a 180°-off winter↔summer mismatch (the path that gets the non-specific span suggestion). No URL / schema / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — frontmatter edit + corpus invariant only; the smoke walker only pins 200 / H1 / no console errors on `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains` so it inherits the new eyebrow text cleanly. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (eyebrow-label edit + corpus invariant only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure — the new label `winter–spring 2010` discloses only the calendar span of the air-window already published on the page's PREMIERED meta cell). - [x] [MED] [anon] /about `How voting works` paragraph repeats the same trust-mechanic claim twice with different language AND overpromises against the weighting table sitting two sentences below. The opening lede (`content/legal/about.md:18-19`) reads `Signed-in members carry the most weight.` Then the dedicated `How voting works` section opens at line 38 with `Long-standing accounts carry the most weight.` — same sentence-template, different qualifier (`Signed-in members` → `Long-standing accounts`), no editorial value added the second time. The deeper problem is that the line-38 framing also overpromises: the very next sentence is `Anonymous-guest votes count at 0.1×. Accounts under 7 days old count at 0.25×. Accounts 7+ days old count at 1.0×.` Seven days is not `long-standing` by any reader's natural reading of the phrase — a week-old account is barely a week old. The page promises a tenure cliff (long-standing accounts) and delivers a one-week cliff (anything over 7 days gets full weight). Same defect class as the resolved pass-26 #282 / pass-27 #287 / pass-32 #311 chain (cross-surface vote-mechanic drift between home + /about), now reading as an *intra-/about* drift between two adjacent sections of the same legal doc. Source: `content/legal/about.md:18-19` (lede: `Signed-in members carry the most weight.`) + `content/legal/about.md:38` (`How voting works` section: `Long-standing accounts carry the most weight. Anonymous-guest votes count at 0.1×. Accounts under 7 days old count at 0.25×. Accounts 7+ days old count at 1.0×.`). Bearings voice cue is `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken, never pretentious. Plain sentences over clever ones.` (`plan/bearings.md:370`); a peer doesn't repeat the trust pitch twice in two sections of the same explainer, especially when the second pitch oversells. Fix options: (a) **primary path** — rewrite line 38 to drop both the repetition and the overpromise. Candidate: `Accounts a week old or older count at full weight; younger accounts and guests count less.` (single sentence, names the actual mechanic — 7-day cliff — without overstating tenure as `long-standing`, replaces the duplicative `carry the most weight` template lede with a direct datum). The 0.1× / 0.25× / 1.0× breakdown that follows reads as the detail expansion of the new lede, rather than the contradiction-fodder it currently is. (b) **alternative** — keep line 38's lede, drop the duplicative line-19 lede: rewrite the intro section's voting line to a non-overlapping framing (e.g. `Anonymous and brand-new votes count less; full-tenure accounts count at full weight.`). Risks redoing the same overpromise problem the intro is currently free of. (c) **alternative** — keep both, accept the cross-section echo, swap only the `long-standing` overpromise: `Accounts 7+ days old carry the most weight.` (matches the data, but the within-page sentence-template echo remains a small voice tic). Recommended (a) — single-edit, drops both the repetition AND the overpromise, lets the weighting ladder paragraph read as the detail expansion of an honest lede. Pin: extend `src/app/(default)/about/__tests__/page.test.tsx` with two cases — (1) a bidirectional within-page sentence-template guard: `body_md` must NOT contain two distinct sentences matching `/\b(signed-in|long-standing|account|voter|member|guest)[^.]*\bcarry the most weight\b/i` (drift guard against a future authoring pass re-introducing the template echo); (2) a tenure-honesty positive: when the rendered body contains the weighting table (matches `/0\.25×|0\.1×|1\.0×/`), the same body must NOT also describe the 7+ day threshold as `long-standing` (asserts NOT match `/long-standing[^.]*carry the most weight|carry the most weight[^.]*long-standing/i`). Both pins live entirely in the existing about-page test colocation; no e2e fixture row owed. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (legal-doc prose edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). (URL: /about, source: critique-pass-33) — 0ebda93 — issue: #318 — RESOLVED this commit: took the row's primary-path (a) — rewrote `content/legal/about.md:38` so the `How voting works` section opener now reads `Accounts a week old or older count at full weight; younger accounts and guests count less.` (replaces `Long-standing accounts carry the most weight.`). Drops both the intra-page sentence-template echo with the line-19 lede (`Signed-in members carry the most weight.`) AND the `long-standing` overpromise that contradicted the 7-day cliff named two sentences below. The 0.1× / 0.25× / 1.0× breakdown that follows now reads as the detail expansion of an honest lede rather than the contradiction-fodder it was. Cross-surface pins (#311 attestation + ladder) unchanged: `Signed-in voters self-attest they watched the season end to end before casting.` lede stays one sentence above, the weighting ladder stays unchanged. Two test pins landed per the row's prescription in `src/app/(default)/about/__tests__/page.test.tsx` under a new `within-page sentence-template + tenure honesty (#318)` describe — (1) bidirectional within-page template guard: counts matches of `/\b(signed-in|long-standing|account|voter|member|guest)[^.]*\bcarry the most weight\b/gi` across `body_md`, asserts ≤ 1 (catches both a future re-introduction of the duplicate template AND the existing intro lede staying intact); (2) tenure-honesty conditional: when `/0\.1×|0\.25×|1\.0×/.test(body)` (the weighting ladder is named), the body must NOT match `/long-standing[^.]*carry the most weight|carry the most weight[^.]*long-standing/i` — locks the decoupling. No e2e fixture row owed — both pins live in the existing about-page colocation. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (legal-doc prose edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 2428 unit tests + 80 script tests + content:check (13 shows, 298 seasons, 13 canons, 12 themes, 3 legal docs), leg2 build, leg3 1361 e2e (8.0m). GitHub #318 closed by this commit.
- [x] [MED] [anon] /themes/best-finales entry #03 (Top Chef S06 Las Vegas) restates the deck headline in the body's first clause instead of advancing the editorial point. Title (`content/themes/best-finales.md:32`) reads `The deepest knife-skill cast carries the kitchen all the way home.` Body opener (line 33) reads `Las Vegas runs the most technically loaded roster the show ever fielded, and the late-stage cooking never lets the level drop.` Two ways of saying the same thing in two consecutive lines — `the deepest knife-skill cast` and `the most technically loaded roster the show ever fielded` are synonyms (both claim the strongest-talent-pool cast in show history), so the body opens by paraphrasing the deck rather than advancing the reader to a finale-specific observation. The sibling entries on the same list use the deck → body relationship correctly: #02 (HvV) decks on `final tribal that reads like a verdict` and the body opens on `The endgame compounds`, naming a different beat; #04 (Survivor S40) decks on `the all-winner premise pays back` and the body opens on a different scene (`every plate earning its place` is from #03's *second* body sentence, not its opener); #07 (Bake Off Series 8) decks on the cake-craft moment and the body opens on the bake-tent dynamics. Same within-entry repetition class as the addressed pass-31 #06 (`artistry` cognate, same list), but at the deck-vs-body relational layer rather than the noun-stem layer. Source: `content/themes/best-finales.md:32-33` — title line 32 + blurb line 33's first clause. Fix options: (a) **primary path** — rewrite line 33's first clause to advance to a finale-specific beat the deck does not already make. Candidate: `Restaurant Wars takes the season into its endgame on a kitchen split, and the late-stage cooking never lets the level drop.` (swaps the deck-restatement opener for the structural moment the deck doesn't name — Restaurant Wars is Top Chef's signature mid-season pivot into the finale arc), keeps the second clause intact, body now advances rather than repeats. Alternative finale-specific opener: `The Napa finale pulls every craft beat the season set up into one service, and the late-stage cooking never lets the level drop.` (names the finale's venue + scene rather than re-restating the cast claim). (b) **alternative** — drop the deck's `deepest knife-skill cast` claim and lean into a finale-specific deck: `Restaurant Wars sets the finale up, and the finale earns it.` (deck advances; body keeps its current shape). Larger blast radius — pass-30 #301 / pass-31 #308 chain established that this list's titles carry the editorial framing the body expands on, so rewriting the deck reopens upstream framing choices. (c) **alternative** — keep both, accept the deck-paraphrase pattern. Recommended (a) — single-blurb edit on the body's first clause, drops the restatement, keeps the editorial framing #03's deck currently carries. Pin: the `collectThemeBodyPhraseRepetitionIssues` within-entry stem-pair extension proposed in the pass-31 #309 / #308 / #310 / pass-32 #02 chain (and deferred per the row's recommendation to ship alongside two findings at once) is the right shape for this class too — it would catch `deepest knife-skill cast` ↔ `most technically loaded roster` as a synonymy-pair if extended with a synonymy floor (e.g. WordNet-style synonym lookup on head-noun pairs). The simpler near-term pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` with a deck-vs-body opener divergence check — for every theme entry, the first 5–8 content-bearing words of the body MUST NOT semantically duplicate the deck title's claim (heuristic: no shared 3-word noun phrase, no >50% token overlap between deck and body opener). Cheap, catches the class at author-time. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (themed-list blurb edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on either surface). Second clause (`the late-stage cooking never lets the level drop`) intact; the body now advances rather than repeats. Pin (lexical, scoped to the literal-restatement class — the synonymy class the original finding identified needs WordNet-style lookups outside this scanner's scope): new `collectThemeDeckBodyOpenerDivergenceIssues` in `scripts/content-check.ts` walks every theme entry (skipping `category: single` per the existing themed-list invariant precedent) and trips when the title and the blurb's first sentence share a 3-token contiguous content sequence (after stopword filter) OR exceed a 50% content-token overlap with at least 2 shared tokens. The strict floor caught a second offender in the live corpus on first pass — `content/themes/best-post-merge.md` entry #1 (Cagayan S28), whose body opener `Cagayan's post-merge is where the modern grammar finally consolidated` shared 4 of 5 content tokens with the deck `Where modern Survivor's post-merge grammar gets written.` (80%). Drained in-tick: rewrote to `Cagayan stacks them through every late-round tribal until the back-half plays like a final exam.` (advances to a specific texture beat; ≤280-char schema cap respected). Eight colocated tests in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` pin: live-catalog passes; trigram-match flags + reports the shared sequence; >50%-overlap flags + reports percent; single-token overlap passes; second-sentence-only trigram passes (the invariant is specifically about the body opener); `category: single` exempt; case-insensitive trigram match.
