- [x] [MED] [authed] /themes at 375px drops both the per-list `N ENTRIES` count and the `STABLE LIST` / `REVISED <Month YYYY>` status tag from every All-lists row, leaving mobile readers with zero scale or freshness signal on the catalog. Desktop tile reads `Seasons that live in their loudest arcs · Seasons whose most-discussed moment lands in the back half... · 7 ENTRIES / STABLE LIST · →`. Mobile tile reads `Seasons that live in their loudest arcs · Seasons whose most-discussed moment lands in the back half... · →` — the entire `.list-row-meta` column (entry count + status) is hidden. Source: `src/styles/screens.css:1247-1248` — at the mobile breakpoint, `.list-row { grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto; gap: 14px; padding: 18px; }` AND `.list-row .list-row-meta { display: none; }` (the meta column is dropped entirely rather than restyled). Mobile catalog readers therefore can't tell at a glance whether a list has 3 entries or 30, or whether it was revised last week or six months ago — both of which are the data the desktop affordance was teaching them to expect. Sibling design surfaces don't do this: `src/components/lists/FeaturedCard.tsx:47` renders `{showLabel} · {entryCount} entries` inline within the card body (not in a meta column), so the featured strip's `N entries` survives the mobile breakpoint because it isn't in `.list-row-meta`. The All-lists grid is the surface that loses the signal. Bearings doesn't formalize a mobile-parity rule for stats specifically, but the smoke walker invariant (`scrollWidth === innerWidth` at 375px) is the only mobile guardrail currently shipped, so this class slips through the gate. Same mobile-parity class as the resolved 375px-nav-drops-with-no-hamburger finding, but at the index-tile metadata layer rather than the chrome layer. Fix options: (a) **primary path** — preserve the entry count on mobile by moving it into the row body. Drop the `display: none` on `.list-row-meta` at mobile width, AND restyle `.list-row-meta` at mobile to render inline below the blurb rather than as a fourth grid column. Candidate CSS at `src/styles/screens.css:1247-1248`: keep the simplified 3-column grid (`auto 1fr auto`), drop the `.list-row-meta { display: none }` line, add `.list-row .list-row-meta { grid-column: 2 / 3; font-size: 12px; color: var(--show-ink-2); margin-top: 4px; }` so the meta lives below the blurb in the body column at mobile width. Smaller diff to the visual cascade than restructuring the row layout. (b) **alternative** — render only the count on mobile, drop the status tag: smaller diff but loses the freshness signal which is arguably the more useful of the two (a `REVISED 2 weeks ago` status tells a returning reader whether to re-engage with a list they've seen before). (c) **alternative** — restructure `.list-row` at mobile to a stacked card (vertical layout, meta below body) and let the existing `.list-row-meta` block render naturally; larger refactor but matches the way the FeaturedCard mobile shape already works. Recommended (a) — smallest CSS diff that restores both signals, preserves the desktop 4-column layout, and matches the visual hierarchy the desktop already has (meta as a secondary affordance below the body). Pin: extend `apps/e2e/tests/themes.spec.ts` (or the equivalent /themes spec) with a mobile-viewport case asserting that for every rendered list-row at 375px width, the row contains visible text matching `/\d+ entries?/i` AND visible text matching `/(stable list|revised\s+\w+)/i` — bidirectional drift guard, catches both signal drops at e2e time. Sibling pin: extend `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListRow.test.tsx` with a snapshot/JSDOM case asserting the rendered `.list-row-meta` block contains both the count and the status string (the unit test catches the data-shape; the e2e catches the CSS visibility — both are required since the current bug is purely CSS-side). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (mobile CSS edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-33) — 0ebda93 — issue: #320 — RESOLVED 4435310: took the row's primary path (a). Both mobile breakpoints flipped — `src/styles/lists.css:351-389` (@media max-width 720px) and `src/styles/screens.css:1247-1271` (@media max-width 767px) — drop `display: none` on `.list-row-meta` and re-place it into row 2 of column 2 (`grid-template-rows: auto auto`, meta at `grid-column: 2 / 3; grid-row: 2 / 3`, left-aligned, no margin). Bullets + arrow span both rows (`grid-row: 1 / span 2`, `align-self: center`) so the row visually stays one beat. The 721-767 window (where lists.css doesn't fire) inherits the screens.css fix. Pin landed per the row's e2e prescription: new `themes.spec.ts:207` `mobile @ 375px: every list-row preserves the N entries + status meta (closes #320)` — sets viewport 375x800, walks every `[data-testid=lists-row]`, asserts `.list-row-meta` is visible AND text matches both `/\d+\s+entr(y|ies)/` AND `/stable list|growing|updated|started/`. Bidirectional drift guard — a regression to `display: none` OR a regression that hides either signal alone trips this case. The colocated `ListRow.test.tsx` `"renders the entry-count + status meta"` data-shape guard stays intact (the unit test already validated the markup; the CSS visibility was the regression — pinning at e2e is the right surface). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (mobile CSS edit only; no per-season verdict, no canon position change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2435 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1362 e2e (8.0m, includes the new mobile-meta pin).
- [x] [LOW] [authed] global footer SHOWS column lists exactly three shows — `RuPaul's Drag Race / Survivor / The Amazing Race` — with `ALL SHOWS →` beneath, and no header signaling this is a partial set. With 13 shows tracked in the catalog (`content/shows/*.md`, surfaced as `13 SHOWS TRACKED` on `/shows`), the unlabeled three reads as the complete index until the reader spots the catch-all link. The footer is the navigation surface a reader hits when they've finished reading and want to keep exploring — landing on three shows when 13 exist is small but real navigation-honesty drift. The three shows shown aren't even the S-tier set (`/shows` S-tier renders Survivor + The Amazing Race + one more, not three including Drag Race) — they appear to be alphabetical-cluster-into-three or curator-pick-three with no labeling either way, so the reader can't infer the selection rule. Same nav-honesty class as the resolved breadcrumb `Tiers` → `Shows` rename (`/breadcrumb` + footer `Tiers` vs design files using `Shows`, addressed) and the home-page `13 shows tracked / 9 tiles surface / 4 unreachable` finding (addressed), but at the per-show footer-column layer rather than the index-level layer. Source: site-wide footer component (likely `src/components/chrome/Footer.tsx` or `src/components/layout/SiteFooter.tsx` — wherever the `SHOWS` column literal is rendered, with the three slugs `rupauls-drag-race / survivor / the-amazing-race` hardcoded or sourced from a curator pick). Fix options: (a) **primary path — label the column to match what it actually shows**. Two candidates: `FEATURED SHOWS` (the marketing framing; matches the home `CURRENTLY FEATURED` eyebrow above the Survivor hero) or `EDITOR'S PICKS` (the editorial framing; matches the bearings `knowledgeable peer` voice). Single string swap on the column header — the catch-all `ALL SHOWS →` link below already implies more shows exist, the header rename completes the contract. Recommended `FEATURED SHOWS` — matches the existing home `CURRENTLY FEATURED` framing the user has already met above the fold. (b) **alternative** — change the data source to the actual S-tier shows (two: Survivor + The Amazing Race) so the column matches the editorial tier the rest of the site teaches: keeps the column header `SHOWS` but drops the count to two and they're the right two. Smaller header diff but a content/data change rather than a label change; risks reading as a too-small footer column. (c) **alternative** — surface 5–6 shows in the column so the partial-set framing fades and `ALL SHOWS →` reads more like a "see all" link than an "actually there's a lot more" link. Larger blast radius (footer grows visually) and the underlying mismatch (3 of 13 unlabeled) still exists in a slightly less obvious form. Recommended (a) — single label edit, smallest diff, restores navigation honesty without changing the data the footer shows. Pin: extend the colocated footer test (`src/components/chrome/__tests__/Footer.test.tsx` or wherever the footer is tested) with a case asserting the SHOWS column header reads `FEATURED SHOWS` OR `EDITOR'S PICKS` (NOT bare `SHOWS`) whenever the rendered list count is less than the catalog's `getShowsCount()`. Bidirectional drift guard, catches both directions of regression. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (footer label edit only; no per-season verdict, no canon position change). The `<nav aria-label>` flipped from `Shows` → `Featured shows` in the same edit so the screen-reader landmark name stays in lockstep with the visible h2 — both name the column as the *featured subset* (3 of N) rather than the complete index. Closure pattern matches #242 (bidirectional drift guard with positive + negative pins): `FooterTiersCol.test.tsx` gained a new `does NOT render the bare "Shows" head literal — bidirectional drift guard` case asserting (1) `head.textContent` does NOT equal `Shows` AND matches `/featured/i`, and (2) the same bidirectional pair on `nav.getAttribute('aria-label')`. The two existing tests that pinned the old `Shows` literal updated in lockstep (`renders the "Featured shows" column head` + the empty-list graceful-count case's heading-text assertion). The alternative-path `getShowsCount()`-conditional pin from the row's prescription was reconsidered at fix-time and folded into a simpler always-true pin (`featured` is always the right framing while the column caps at the top-3 tier-sorted slice — the conditional adds complexity without strengthening the guard since the cap-at-3 logic is the actual contract). The two outer `Footer.test.tsx` test-name strings `renders three columns: brand + Shows + tiered.tv (about)` + `renders a bullet next to each show in the Shows column` were left intentionally untouched — they describe the column-as-domain (the column *about* shows) and assert via testid not label, so the test names stay semantically correct without churning the test surface. No URL / schema / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — chrome label edit only; the smoke walker only pins 200 / H1 / no console errors on every route so it inherits the new chrome literal cleanly across every page in the catalog. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome label edit only; no per-season verdict, no canon position change, no winner / elimination exposure). - [x] [LOW] [authed] /themes/<theme> hero stat strip doubles the noun in the first tile only — `ENTRIES / 7 entries` repeats `entries` in label + value, while the three sibling tiles avoid the doubling. Concrete observed shape on /themes/best-finales (the pattern renders on every `/themes/<slug>` detail page since the component is shared): the four-tile strip reads `ENTRIES / 7 entries / SPANS / 6 shows / CURATED BY / tiered.tv editor / LAST REVISED / May 2026`. Three tiles read cleanly — `SPANS / 6 shows` (label-as-verb + plural noun; the verb names the dimension, the value names a distinct object), `CURATED BY / tiered.tv editor` (verb-phrase + name), `LAST REVISED / May 2026` (adjective-phrase + date). The entries tile breaks the pattern: label and value both carry the same noun. Source: `src/components/lists/ListDetailHero.tsx` — the `Entries` `<dt>` paired with `{entryCount} {plural(entryCount, 'entry', 'entries')}` `<dd>`. The sibling `Spans / 6 shows` cell derives its noun (`show/shows`) from `showCount` so the value lands on a different object than the label names — that's the pattern that makes the strip read. Bearings voice cue is `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken, never pretentious. Plain sentences over clever ones.` — a peer hero-stat strip wouldn't say `Entries: 7 entries`; they'd say `Entries: 7` (matches `LAST REVISED / May 2026` shape exactly) or rephrase the label to make the noun-doubling resolve the way `SPANS / 6 shows` does. Fix options: (a) **primary path** — drop the noun from the value cell. Final: `ENTRIES / 7` (matches the `LAST REVISED / May 2026` shape: label names the dimension, value carries only the datum). Smallest diff, single-helper change. (b) rename the label to a verb so the doubling resolves the way `SPANS / 6 shows` does — e.g. `HAS / 7 entries`. Reads slightly more conversational but `HAS` is a weak label; less editorial value. (c) keep both nouns, accept the small stutter — leaves the asymmetry. Recommended (a) — single-character change in the `<dd>` template (drop the trailing `{plural(...)}` call), matches the surface-native `LAST REVISED / May 2026` shape, restores label/value distinctness across all four tiles. Pin: extend `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListDetailHero.test.tsx` with a `meta-cell entries` case asserting the rendered value cell text matches `/^\d+$/` (just the integer) and does NOT contain the literal `entries` or `entry` — bidirectional drift guard, keeps the doubling out at unit time. The other three tile shapes are already implicit through the component's existing tests; the new case only needs to lock the entries-tile fix. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (UI-component label edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change). Cross-surface: the fix flows through every `/themes/<slug>` detail page since `ListDetailHero` is the shared component — 12 themed-list detail surfaces inherit the cleaner hero strip in one edit. (URL: every `/themes/<slug>`, source: critique-pass-32) — b003420 — issue: #313 — RESOLVED this commit: took the row's primary-path (a) — dropped the noun from the entries value cell in `src/components/lists/ListDetailHero.tsx:65-66`, the `<dd className="meta-val">` now reads `{entryCount}` only (was `{entryCount} {plural(entryCount, 'entry', 'entries')}`). The three sibling tiles unchanged — `SPANS / N shows` keeps its `plural()` call (the noun names a distinct object from the label, so the doubling reading the bearings voice cue calls out doesn't apply), `CURATED BY` + `LAST REVISED` unchanged. Cross-surface: the fix flows through every `/themes/<slug>` detail page in one edit since `ListDetailHero` is the shared component across 12 themed-list detail surfaces. Pin landed per the row's prescription: `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListDetailHero.test.tsx` gained `entries meta value is a bare integer (no noun-doubling against the ENTRIES label)` — renders a 7-entry theme, reads `.meta-val` textContent inside `[data-testid=list-meta-entries]`, asserts it matches `/^\d+$/` AND does NOT match `/entr(y|ies)/i` — bidirectional drift guard, catches both the regression direction (re-adding the noun) and the over-correction direction (dropping the integer entirely). The existing four-cell positive-render case + the singular-meta single-entry case were updated in lockstep so the test file's three entries-tile assertions all read against the new bare-integer shape. e2e `apps/e2e/tests/themes.spec.ts:219-221` `list-meta-entries` regex was `/entries|entry/` (case-sensitive, would not have matched the bare label `Entries` either, so the prior test was actually asserting on the now-dropped value-cell noun) — swapped to `/Entries\s*\d+/` so the e2e asserts the new shape (label + integer with optional whitespace between them) and stays load-bearing. No other call sites — `apps/e2e/src/fixtures/page-reads.ts:142` asserts only the cell's visibility, not its text content; `src/lib/__tests__/themes-format.test.ts:171,175,176` still tests the `plural()` helper directly (still used by the Spans cell). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (component label edit only; no per-season verdict, canon position, winner, elimination, finale beat exposure). - [x] [HIGH] [anon] cross-surface editorial-byline drift: every editorial attribution on the site is plural (`tiered.tv Editors` / `tiered.tv editors` / `editors who have rewatched`) while `/about` admits the opposite twice in plain prose. Concrete observed surfaces: (1) season-page byline — rendered HTML at `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains` reads `CANON ENTRY BY TIERED.TV EDITORS` (source: `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:379` literal `Canon entry by <span className="who">tiered.tv Editors</span>` + matching `author: 'tiered.tv Editors'` JSON-LD at line 311); (2) themed-list curated-by — `content/themes/<slug>.md` `curator` frontmatter defaults to `'tiered.tv Editors'` via `src/content/schemas.ts:235` and renders on every `/themes/[theme]` detail page hero; (3) featured-themes strip on `/` home — `src/components/featured-themes/FeaturedThemes.tsx:34` literal `Cross-canon · curated by tiered.tv editors` (lowercase); (4) show-page canon intro — `src/components/canon/ShowRanking.tsx:98` literal `One ranking, written by editors who have rewatched every season at least twice. We argue with the community out loud — but we ship one number.` (plural verb + plural subject). Then `/about` flatly contradicts the plural framing: `content/legal/about.md:29` reads `The Editor's Canon is the editor's call — one person, one position per season, an 80–120 word reason.` (singular possessive, explicit `one person`) and line 78 reads `An experiment. Built and operated by one person. Free to use.` A first-time reader on the natural season → /about path (or themed-list → /about, or featured-strip → /about) hits the contradiction on a single click — the bylines promise a team, /about admits a solo operation, the reader's trust takes the hit on whichever surface they read second. Bearings voice is `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken, never pretentious` (`plan/bearings.md:370`); plural-editor framing on every editorial surface and a one-person admission on /about reads as ad-copy plurality undercutting the page that owes the truth, same defect class as pass-26 #282 (`Every voter has watched` flat claim contradicted by the weighting ladder on /about). The `/about` `Become an editor` section (lines 67-74) does anticipate a future plural editorship `New editors come in by invitation after a draft round` — but the current state is one person, and the bylines should match the current state, not the aspirational one. Fix options: (a) **primary path** — singularize the attribution everywhere the rendered chrome reads it. (a1) `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:379` swap `tiered.tv Editors` → `the tiered.tv editor` (rendered: `CANON ENTRY BY THE TIERED.TV EDITOR`); matching JSON-LD line 311 swap `author: 'tiered.tv Editors'` → `author: 'tiered.tv editor'` (the JSON-LD `author` field is a single Person/Organization name — singular reads more honestly). (a2) `src/content/schemas.ts:235` change the `curator` default from `'tiered.tv Editors'` → `'tiered.tv editor'` (the singular form), then propagate to all 12 themed-list frontmatter files via a single sed-style content drain (`content/themes/*.md` `curator: "tiered.tv Editors"` → `curator: "tiered.tv editor"`). (a3) `src/components/featured-themes/FeaturedThemes.tsx:34` swap `curated by tiered.tv editors` → `curated by the tiered.tv editor`. (a4) `src/components/canon/ShowRanking.tsx:98` swap `written by editors who have rewatched every season at least twice. We argue with the community out loud — but we ship one number.` → `written by an editor who has rewatched every season at least twice. The community argues back; this is one number.` (singularizes the subject + verb + first-person plural — `We argue / we ship` becomes the community-vs-editor framing the rest of /about uses). Keep `/about`'s `Built and operated by one person` line as the anchor; keep `Become an editor` as the aspirational pluralization path. (b) alternative — drop the `one person` admission on /about and lean into a small editorial collective (requires a second editor in practice; content cost real, not a one-tick fix). Recommended (a) — preserves the editorial honesty /about already commits to, brings every byline into agreement with the page that owes the truth, single editorial pass across ~5 file edits + 12 frontmatter substitutions. Pin: `scripts/content-check.ts` invariant — every `content/themes/*.md` `curator` value and every rendered editorial-byline literal in source must NOT match `/editors\b/i` (plural form) until `/about` removes the `one person` line; a sibling positive guard asserts the singular `editor` form is present on the same surfaces. Bidirectional, catches both directions of drift at content-check time. Pin 2 (test-level): `src/app/(default)/about/__tests__/page.test.tsx` gains a cross-surface case asserting that whenever the rendered `/about` body contains `Built and operated by one person`, the canon-page byline literal must also be singular (or vice versa) — fails at unit time if either surface diverges. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (cross-surface attribution prose only; no per-season verdict, winner, elimination, finale beat, or canon position exposure). (URL: /about, source: critique-pass-31) — 3755f50 — issue: #306 — RESOLVED 63a711d: took the row's full primary-path (a) — singularize attribution everywhere the rendered chrome reads it, keep /about's `Built and operated by one person` line as the anchor. Source-file bylines: `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:311,379` swapped JSON-LD `author: 'tiered.tv Editors'` → `'tiered.tv editor'` and the season hero literal `Canon entry by <span className="who">tiered.tv Editors</span>` → `the tiered.tv editor` (renders `CANON ENTRY BY THE TIERED.TV EDITOR`); `src/components/featured-themes/FeaturedThemes.tsx:34` flipped `curated by tiered.tv editors` → `curated by the tiered.tv editor`; `src/components/canon/ShowRanking.tsx:98` rewrote `One ranking, written by editors who have rewatched every season at least twice. We argue with the community out loud — but we ship one number.` → `One ranking, written by an editor who has rewatched every season at least twice. The community argues back; this is one number.` (singular subject + verb + drops the first-person plural). Catalog frontmatter (25 files): `src/content/schemas.ts:235` `curator` default `'tiered.tv Editors'` → `'tiered.tv editor'`; 12 `content/themes/*.md` `curator:` fields flipped; 13 `content/shows/*/canon.md` `editor:` fields flipped. Pin 1 (content-check invariant) landed: new `collectEditorialBylineSingularIssues` in `scripts/content-check.ts` reads `/about` and — while it carries either of the two singular-admission anchors (`Built and operated by one person` or `the editor's call — one person`) — asserts every theme `curator` and every canon `editor` field must NOT match `\btiered\.tv\s+(?:E|e)ditors\b`. Bidirectional: goes silent if `/about` removes both anchors (a future plural editorship rewrites the admission first). Wired into `main()` strict at floor 0 (`EDITORIAL_BYLINE_SINGULAR_STRICT = true`), one-line toggle mirroring the twelve existing invariant patterns. Test pins per the #242 closure pattern (bidirectional): `src/components/featured-themes/__tests__/FeaturedThemes.test.tsx` gained a positive case on `curated by the tiered.tv editor` + negative regex pin against `/tiered\.tv editors\b/i`; `src/components/canon/__tests__/ShowRanking.test.tsx` gained a positive pin on `written by an editor who has rewatched` + three negative pins against `editors who have rewatched`, `we argue with the community`, `but we ship one number`; `src/app/(default)/about/__tests__/page.test.tsx` gained a cross-surface parity guard — when `/about` body contains the singular admission, the body must NOT contain `tiered.tv Editors` or `by editors` (a divergence between /about and the chrome trips here AND in the colocated component tests, not silently in only one); `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` gained a new describe block with 7 cases on the new invariant (live-catalog passes, theme `curator` regression flags, canon `editor` regression flags, lowercase-plural form catch, silent-when-/about-rewrites, no-/about tolerance, named-editor carve-out). `src/content/__tests__/schemas.test.ts` default expectation updated to the singular form; fixture-only literals in `src/app/(default)/themes/[theme]/__tests__/page.test.tsx`, `src/components/home/__tests__/HomeListRow.test.tsx`, `src/components/lists/__tests__/fixtures.ts` swapped in lockstep. No URL / schema field / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — the byline text was not pinned anywhere in `apps/e2e/`; smoke + spec walks inherit the new copy cleanly. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (cross-surface attribution prose only; no per-season verdict, winner, elimination, finale beat, or canon position exposure). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2397 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e (6.7m).
- [x] [MED] [authed] /themes "By tone" group head label mis-classifies four of its six entries — `Reunion specials that closed the loop` (best-reunion-specials) is a format slice, `The back-half at full volume` (best-post-merge) is a structural region of a season, `Returnee seasons that paid off` (best-returnees) is a casting structure, `Firsts that hold up` (firsts) is a chronological position. Only `Seasons that live in their loudest arcs` (best-comeback-seasons) and `Rookie casts walking in fluent` (best-newbie-casts) are genuinely tonal. The taxonomy label promises one kind of cut and delivers another, which makes the index hard to scan for a reader trying to use the `BY TONE / BY CRAFT / SINGLE-SHOW` toggles. (Note: the section heading currently renders as `By tone · 6` — the 7th `tone`-categoried list `best-non-winning-runs` lives in the featured strip above, so the All-section grouping shows 6.) Source: `src/lib/themes-format.ts` `GROUP_HEAD_LABELS = { tone: 'By tone', craft: 'By craft', era: 'By era', single: 'Single-show tiers' }` (the label literal) + the per-theme `category` frontmatter in `content/themes/*.md` (the categorization data). Fix options: (a) **primary path — rebalance via frontmatter**: move `best-reunion-specials`, `best-post-merge`, `best-returnees`, and `firsts` from `category: tone` to a new `category: structure` (or extend the existing `era` slot into `era-or-structure`); add `structure: 'By structure'` to `GROUP_HEAD_LABELS` and add `'structure'` to the `ThemeCategory` Zod enum + the `ORDERED_CATEGORIES` array in `src/components/lists/ListsAllSection.tsx`. End-state buckets: `By tone · 2` (comeback-seasons, newbie-casts), `By structure · 4` (reunion-specials, post-merge, returnees, firsts), `By craft · 4` (finales, location-reveals, villain-editing, premieres), `Single-show · 1` (survivor-pillars). Smallest data-truth change, taxonomy label finally matches contents. (b) alternative — rename `tone` → `cut` / `angle` / `reading angle` (rename the label only, keep the data shape); `By cut · 6` reads honestly because `cut` is broad enough to subsume both genuine tone and structural slice. Smaller diff (one label literal swap + matching test), but `cut` is less expressive than the split (a) provides. (c) leave-as-is — accept that `tone` is loosely defined and includes structural slices. Recommended (a) — the right honest taxonomy + the index toggles finally do what they say. Pin: extend `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListsAllSection.test.tsx` with a per-category content-shape case — for each rendered group, assert the rendered ListRow titles include only entries whose semantic match the label (e.g., the `By tone` group must NOT include `Reunion specials`, `Back-half`, `Returnee`, or `Firsts` keywords). Bidirectional regression guard. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (taxonomy + frontmatter only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-31) — 3755f50 — issue: #307 — RESOLVED e172e50: took the row's primary path (a). `src/content/schemas.ts` `themeCategorySchema` gains `structure` between `tone` and `craft` with a rationale comment naming the pass-31 split; `src/lib/themes-format.ts` extends `FilterKey` / `FILTER_KEYS` / `FILTER_LABELS` / `GROUP_HEAD_LABELS` / `FILTER_MODE_LABELS` in editorial reading order (tone → structure → craft → era → single); `src/content/loaders.ts` `getThemesByCategory` initializer + `THEME_CATEGORIES` updated so every category key is present even when empty; `src/components/lists/ListsAllSection.tsx` `ORDERED_CATEGORIES` matches `FILTER_KEYS` lockstep with a cross-reference comment; `src/components/lists/AdjacentLists.tsx` `CATEGORY_LABELS` gains `structure`; `src/styles/lists.css` filter cascade adds the structure rule; `src/app/(default)/themes/page.tsx` `byCategoryRest` + `counts` initializers add the structure slot; `scripts/content-check.ts` `CROSS_SHOW_CATEGORIES` now includes `'structure'` so the >=3-distinct-shows floor (phase 41 `CROSS_SHOW_STRICT`) applies symmetrically — structural cuts inherently cross shows. Content: `best-reunion-specials`, `best-post-merge`, `best-returnees`, `firsts` all flipped from `category: tone` to `category: structure`; each already carries entries from 5-6 distinct shows so the strict cross-show invariant stays green at content-check time. End-state buckets at the live `/themes` index now read: `By tone · 2` (comeback-seasons, newbie-casts), `By structure · 4` (reunion-specials, post-merge, returnees, firsts), `By craft · 4` (finales, location-reveals, villain-editing, premieres), `Single-show · 1` (survivor-pillars) — `best-non-winning-runs` stays in the featured strip and remains tone-categoried. Pinned per the #242 closure pattern: `src/lib/__tests__/themes-format.test.ts` gains a positive `By structure` label case + a `GROUP_HEAD_LABELS` key-order pin asserting `['tone','structure','craft','era','single']` so a future reorder must touch both files together; `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListsAllSection.test.tsx` order case extends to include the structure column AND a new bidirectional case asserts that when `byCategory.structure` has entries the rendered head reads `By structure` and does NOT read `By tone` — regression guard against a future merge-back; `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListsFilterController.test.tsx` chip-count + filter-order assertions updated to 6 chips including structure with a symmetric `hides the structure chip when counts.structure === 0` empty-suppression case mirroring the era zero-count guard; `src/content/__tests__/loaders.test.ts` `getThemesByCategory` case asserts every category key is present including structure; `src/content/__tests__/schemas.test.ts` new positive case accepts `category: structure` without an `era_range`; `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` two new cases on the cross-canon suite — a structure list at 2 distinct shows fails, a structure list at >=3 passes — mirroring the existing tone/craft/era coverage; `apps/e2e/tests/themes.spec.ts` filter-bar loop covers `structure` alongside the existing keys. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (taxonomy + frontmatter only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). - [x] [MED] [anon|authed] /themes/best-finales #06 (RuPaul's Drag Race S6) carries a residual `artist*` cognate pair after pass-30 #301 stripped the `real artists` repetition. Concrete observed shape: `content/themes/best-finales.md:50` title reads `A final lip-sync that pays off a finale built on real artistry.` and `content/themes/best-finales.md:51` body opener reads `Season 6 spends its run building toward a finale of working drag artists, and the last episodes deliver — runway moments still replayed, a lip-sync stretch with genuine stakes.` Two `artist`-stem cognates (`real artistry` in the title + `working drag artists` in the body's first clause) within one entry's six rendered lines — the pass-30 fix patched at one node (`real artists` → `real artistry`) rather than across the entry. The cross-page voice elsewhere on the same list finds a fresh angle per entry without re-using its own framing noun within itself (#02 `endgame compounds` + `final tribal`; #04 `all-winner premise` + `final tribal`; #07 `final stretch` + `closing run`); reading two `artist*` cognates back-to-back in #06 reads as a small word-tic the curator would catch on a re-read. Same within-entry repetition class as findings (iv) and (v) on this same critique pass (#01 `live sprint` x2, #05 `table` x3 in Traitors). Fix options: (a) **primary path** — rewrite either the title or the body opener to drop one of the `artist`-stem words while preserving the editorial point. Title-side: `A final lip-sync that pays off a finale built on craft.` (lands the same `built on X` framing with a less-overlapping noun) or `A final lip-sync that pays off a finale built on real performance.` (keeps the `real` adjective tonal anchor pass-30 left in place, swaps the noun). Body-side: `Season 6 spends its run building toward a finale of working performers, and the last episodes deliver — runway moments still replayed, a lip-sync stretch with genuine stakes.` (swaps `drag artists` → `performers` — neutral, performance-language adjacent, drops the `artist`-stem). (b) alternative — drop `real artistry` from the title and rotate to the body's strongest beat: title `A final lip-sync that pays off a finale built on a season's craft.` Recommended title-side swap to `built on craft.` (smallest diff, single-word substitution, preserves rhythm + meter, drops the cognate without recasting the body). Pin: extend the existing `scripts/content-check.ts` `collectThemeBodyPhraseRepetitionIssues` invariant (shipped at 1bc71ca for pass-28 #297) with a within-entry stem-pair check — for each theme entry, scan title + blurb tokens for two tokens sharing the same stem (Porter-stem or naive last-4-char prefix-stem match) within a 50-word window; if any stem-pair fires, soft-warn with the offending pair + entry. Catches the next instance of this class at author-time; same shape as the existing cross-entry floor, narrower scope. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial prose only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure). (URL: /themes/best-finales, source: critique-pass-31) — 3755f50 — issue: #308 — RESOLVED f38c17b: took the row's primary path (a) title-side, smallest diff. `content/themes/best-finales.md:50` title swapped `A final lip-sync that pays off a finale built on real artistry.` → `A final lip-sync that pays off a finale built on craft.` — single-word substitution; the `built on X` framing + rhythm + meter all preserved; body left intact since the cognate is gone at the title side (no second edit owed). The body's `working drag artists` clause now reads as the entry's sole `artist*`-stem occurrence, framing the season's editorial point without doubling back on the title. The companion within-entry stem-pair invariant the row proposes is a separate concern carried over to pass-31 #01 (`live sprint` x2) and #05 (`table` x3) — both LOW siblings still Pending on this page; the invariant is the right shape to ship alongside one of those resolutions in a future tick so the catch-everything pin lands once across three findings rather than three times. - [x] [LOW] [anon] /about voting-mechanics paragraph closes with `Brigade rate-limits run behind the scenes and should never bite a normal reader.` (`content/legal/about.md:39-40`). `Brigade` here is a verb-as-noun referring to vote brigading — the practice of coordinated upvote / downvote campaigns — but the term lands cold for a first-time visitor who has not seen it in the wild. The rest of the /about page reads in plain language (`one vote per reader`, `change your mind within 72 hours`, `flagged comments enter a human-reviewed mod queue`), so `Brigade rate-limits` is the one piece of community-moderation jargon on the page. Bearings voice rule is `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken` (`plan/bearings.md:370`); a peer would unpack the term once before deploying it. The clause is also load-bearing for trust — it's the sentence that promises the rate-limit will not catch the reader by accident — so the obscurity costs the page exactly the line it needs the reader to absorb. Fix options: (a) **primary path** — unpack the term inline so the trust line lands plainly: `Rate-limits run behind the scenes to catch coordinated voting campaigns, and should never bite a normal reader.` (adds ~9 words, names the function in plain language, removes the cold jargon). (b) drop `Brigade` entirely and lean on function: `Rate-limits catch coordinated vote campaigns behind the scenes; they should never bite a normal reader.` (terser, same plain framing). (c) keep `Brigade` but bridge to it once: `Vote-brigading rate-limits — protections against coordinated campaigns — run behind the scenes and should never bite a normal reader.` (introduces the term with an inline gloss). Recommended (a) — preserves the trust-line role, removes the cold jargon, single-sentence edit with no new term introduced. Pin: extend `src/app/(default)/about/__tests__/page.test.tsx` with a copy-shape case asserting the rendered `/about` body does NOT contain the bare standalone token `/\bBrigade\b/` unless paired with a `coordinated` / `campaign` / `brigading` bridge in the same sentence (bidirectional drift guard against a regression back to the bare-jargon form, AND against accidentally introducing the same jargon elsewhere on /about). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (legal-doc prose only; no per-season content). Anonymous-guest votes count at 0.1×. Accounts under 7 days old count at 0.25×. Accounts 7+ days old count at 1.0×. Brigade rate-limits run behind the scenes and should never bite a normal reader.` → `Long-standing accounts carry the most weight. Anonymous-guest votes count at 0.1×. Accounts under 7 days old count at 0.25×. Accounts 7+ days old count at 1.0×. Rate-limits run behind the scenes to catch coordinated voting campaigns, and should never bite a normal reader.` The trust-line role is preserved (`should never bite a normal reader` lands intact); the cold jargon token `Brigade` is dropped; the rate-limit function is named in plain language (`coordinated voting campaigns`). Pin landed at `src/app/(default)/about/__tests__/page.test.tsx` as a new `content/legal/about.md rate-limit trust line voice (#310)` describe block with two bidirectional cases: (1) the bare-jargon regression guard — walks every line containing `\bBrigade\b` and asserts each such line also names `coordinated|campaign|brigading` so the term lands unpacked (catches a regression back to the prior form AND catches the same jargon migrating into a different /about paragraph); (2) the plain-language positive — pins the rendered body contains `coordinated voting campaigns` so the function-naming clause cannot drift away silently. - [x] [HIGH] [anon|authed] /shows Amazing Race A-tier tagline outputs broken grammar: `a cross-continent route mechanic that has held its shape across twenty-five of starting lines.` The phrase `twenty-five of starting lines` is ungrammatical — a word is missing between `twenty-five` and `of`. Confirmed source: `content/shows/amazing-race.md:11` `tagline:` reads `... has held its shape across {yearsWord} of starting lines. ...`. The `{yearsWord}` token (defined in `src/lib/show-tenure.ts:129`) substitutes a spelled-out years number ONLY — it does NOT inject the word `years` after itself. Every other corpus use of `{yearsWord}` pairs the token with an explicit `years` word in the literal (see `src/lib/__tests__/show-tenure.test.ts:117,126` — `spent {yearsWord} years rediscovering what it is`). The Amazing Race tagline omits that pairing, so the rendered output is the bare number + `of` + noun — broken grammar on the main catalog surface every visitor sees when browsing shows, and on the longest A-tier blurb on that page. Fix: edit `content/shows/amazing-race.md:11` `tagline:` to insert `years` after `{yearsWord}` — final reads `has held its shape across {yearsWord} years of starting lines.` (matches the canonical `{yearsWord} years` pattern the tenure helper was designed for). Pin: a regression class-check in `scripts/content-check.ts`'s existing `collectYearTenureIssues` invariant — every show frontmatter `tagline` containing the literal `{yearsWord}` must also contain the literal substring `{yearsWord} years` (i.e., the token must be followed by ` years`), with the same `TENURE_ANCHOR_ALLOWLIST` escape hatch if a future tagline needs a different construction (e.g., `{yearsWord}-year run`). The check is cheap, catches the entire class, and lifts at content-author time rather than at production render. Pin 2: add a colocated case to `src/lib/__tests__/show-tenure.test.ts` documenting the contract that `{yearsWord}` substitutes the number only and authors must supply the surrounding noun explicitly — currently the tests show the pattern by example but don't pin the contract. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (tagline-prose-only edit; no per-season verdict). (URL: /shows, source: critique-pass-28) — 5101ab6 — issue: #292 — RESOLVED ac2dafe: took the row's full path on raw frontmatter rather than the rendered-string surface. `content/shows/amazing-race.md:11` tagline edited to insert `years` after `{yearsWord}` — final reads `... has held its shape across {yearsWord} years of starting lines. ...`, matching the canonical pairing every other corpus use (Survivor) already carries. The pin landed as a new sibling helper `collectYearTokenPairingIssues` in `scripts/content-check.ts` (rather than extending `collectYearTenureIssues` literally — the existing helper reads loader-materialized text where the token is already substituted, so it cannot see the raw `{yearsWord}` literal). The new helper reads each show's source markdown directly (via `showFile()` + `gray-matter`) and asserts: any `tagline` or `card_tagline` field containing `{yearsWord}` must also contain `{yearsWord} years`. Wired into `main()` strict at floor 0 (`YEAR_TOKEN_PAIRING_STRICT = true`, mirroring the eight existing one-line strict toggles). Future taglines that need a different construction (`{yearsWord}-year run` as the row notes) extend the helper with a per-show allowlist — the failure message names the path. Pin 2 landed at `src/lib/__tests__/show-tenure.test.ts` as a new `{yearsWord} substitutes the number only — author supplies the noun` describe block carrying three cases: (1) bare-form regression — `across {yearsWord} of starting lines` substitutes to `across twenty-five of starting lines` (no `years` injected, reproduces the pre-fix Amazing Race shape); (2) paired-form canonical render — `across {yearsWord} years of starting lines` → `across twenty-five years of starting lines`; (3) hyphen-compound — `a {yearsWord}-year run` → `a twenty-five-year run` (proves the token never injects a noun, even when the next character is a hyphen, supporting the future `{yearsWord}-year run` construction the row anticipates). Sibling pin at `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` exercises `collectYearTokenPairingIssues` against a temp content tree across five cases: clean tagline, paired form, the Amazing Race bare regression, multi-occurrence tolerance (one paired form anywhere satisfies the check), and a `card_tagline` regression case proving the helper scans both fields independently. No URL / schema / route change. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (tagline-prose-only edit; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). - [x] [MED] [authed] /u/e2e empty-state owner self-view reads as an admin screen rather than an editorial product. Rendered body (signed-in as @e2e, account fresh, zero votes): `Your record / @e2e / Member since May 2026 / 0 SEASONS VOTED / 0 COMMENTS / 0 SHOWS VOTED ON / Nothing on your record yet. Vote on a season and it will land here. / Start with Survivor → / Browse all shows →`. Six pieces of zero or absence stack before two CTA arrows. The bearings voice cue is `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken`; this surface is plain but neither warm nor knowing. Every other first-paint surface (home hero, /shows tier intros, season ledes, themed-list ledes) leads with a one-sentence editorial line that earns reader attention before showing structure. The empty owner profile owes the same — it's a new account's first impression of the product and currently the only surface that lets the editorial voice down. Source: `src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx` (the empty-state component the route renders at zero counts) and the surrounding stat-tile block (likely `ProfileStats.tsx`). Fix options: (a) **primary path** — add a one-sentence editorial lede above or in place of the three-zero stat row for the zero-counts state. Voice match attempts: `New here. Cast one vote and your record starts writing itself.` or `Nothing on record yet — that's the whole point of starting somewhere.` or `A clean slate. Pick a season, weigh in once, watch the record fill.` Reserve the stat tile row for accounts with at least one vote so the structure earns its space; (b) drop the three-zero stat row when all three are 0 — render only the editorial line + the two CTAs; the tiles return the first time the user votes; (c) keep the tiles but pair each with a one-clause editorial lead so the page reads as voice + structure rather than structure alone. Recommended (a) — minimum change (one new component slot), keeps the existing CTA pair, restores the editorial voice on the only surface currently missing it. Pin: extend `ProfileEmpty.test.tsx` with a case asserting the empty-state body contains the editorial lede sentence and does NOT lead with a stat-tile row when all three counts are 0; and a positive case for the non-empty state (any count > 0) that the stat tiles render and the editorial lede does not. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (UI-component prose, no per-season content). - [x] [LOW] [authed] /themes/best-finales body copy leans on a single noun phrase — `closing run` / `closing stretch` / `closing hour` — across the tagline plus four of seven entry bodies. Concrete counts (verified against `content/themes/best-finales.md`): tagline (line 4) opens `The closing run is where a season either pays off its promise...`; entry #01 The Amazing Race S07 title (line 20) reads `A final leg that proves the format's closing run is built right.` and blurb (line 21) `The closing run is where this season turns...`; entry #03 Top Chef S06 blurb (line 33) `... and the closing run never lets the level drop.`; entry #04 Survivor S40 blurb (line 39) `The milestone framing earns itself in the closing run.`; entry #05 The Traitors US S02 title (line 44) `The Round Table tightening into the closing run...` and blurb (line 45) `The closing stretch is where the US adaptation proves...`; entry #06 RuPaul's Drag Race S06 title (line 50) `A closing run that pays off a finale built on real artistry.` and blurb (line 51) `The closing hour lands a season...`; entry #07 Big Brother S07 blurb (line 57) `... a closing run sharp enough to hold against every era that followed it.` That's nine `closing X` constructions in roughly 500 words of editorial body across one list — the load-bearing noun phrase repeats in the tagline + 5 of 7 entry bodies + 2 of 7 entry titles. Reads as templated rather than written; the voice elsewhere on the site finds a fresh angle per entry. Fix: pass back through the four most repetitive entry bodies (#01, #03, #05, #06) and vary the metaphor — `final leg`, `endgame`, `final service`, `banishment table`, `lip-sync stretch` are already in the surrounding editorial vocabulary on the same page and can carry the same beat without leaning on one phrase. Keep one `closing X` as the tagline's load-bearing phrase if it best frames the category; rotate the rest. Pin: a colocated `scripts/content-check.ts` invariant — for every themed-list `content/themes/<slug>.md`, count occurrences of the most-repeated 2-word noun phrase across all entry blurbs + titles + tagline; if any one phrase appears in more than 50% of entry bodies, fail with a soft warning naming the phrase + count. Cheap, catches the editorial-template class, lifts at author-time. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial prose only; no per-season verdict change). (URL: /themes/best-finales, source: critique-pass-28) — 5101ab6 — issue: #297 — RESOLVED 1bc71ca: took the row's full path. The four most-repetitive entry bodies rotated their metaphor using surface-native vocabulary already in the surrounding editorial body: entry #01 (amazing-race S07) title swapped `closing run` → `live sprint` and blurb swapped `The closing run is where this season turns…` → `That final leg is where this season turns…` (`final leg` already in the title; `live sprint` + `last pit stop` already in the blurb body); entry #03 (top-chef S06) blurb opener swapped `closing run` → `late-stage cooking` (paired with `final service` already in the second sentence); entry #05 (traitors S02) title swapped `closing run` → `final banishment` (paired with the `banishment` beat already in the blurb) and blurb closer swapped `closing stretch` → `last seat at the table` (preserves the round-table framing); entry #06 (dragrace S06) title swapped `closing run` → `final lip-sync` and blurb closer swapped `closing hour` → `crown coronation` (paired with `runway moments` + `lip-sync stretch` already in the blurb body). Tagline kept as the row prescribed; entries #04 + #07 also kept their single in-entry `closing run` line — final state is 2 of 7 entries carry the phrase (28.6%), well below the 50% structural-templating floor the pinned invariant enforces. The pin landed as a new `collectThemeBodyPhraseRepetitionIssues` helper in `scripts/content-check.ts` (sibling to the cliche-repetition / about-list-title invariants): for every themed list with >= 5 cross-canon entries (`category: single` exempt — natural show-name repetition), scan all entry titles + blurbs for the most-repeated 2-word phrase (alphabetic bigrams, both words content-bearing — stopwords excluded). If any phrase appears in more than 50% of entries (entry counts as containing it iff its title or blurb does), fail naming the phrase + entry count. Tagline occurrences fold into the reported total but the floor is measured strictly against entries. Wired into `main()` strict at floor 0 (`THEME_BODY_PHRASE_STRICT = true`, mirroring the ten existing one-line strict toggles). Seven-test fixture suite in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` locks the behavior — 50%-floor trigger (5 of 7 entries), title-or-blurb counting (4 of 5 entries — phrase in title only vs blurb only), `single`-category exemption, <5-entry skip (statistical floor), stopword filter, case-insensitivity, live-catalog pass. No URL / schema field / e2e fixture row owed (content edit + invariant only). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial prose only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / final-vote names). - [x] [LOW] [anon|authed] /themes lede vs hero stats requires mental arithmetic to reconcile. Rendered body: lede `12 lists we'd defend in a group chat. Some span the catalog, one lives inside one show. None of them spoil what they rank.` immediately followed by stat tiles `3 FEATURED / 9 IN THE INDEX / 10 SHOWS COVERED / May 2026 INDEX LAST REVISED`. The reader has to compute `3 + 9 = 12` to tie the lede number to the visible stats — and `9 IN THE INDEX` is louder than `3 FEATURED` (the same numeric size, but the index tile is the second tile in reading order and tagged with the more concrete count), so the first impression is "there are 9 lists" before the eye reaches the 3 and adds. Distinct from pass-23 #263 (which pinned that the lede already derives from `stats.total` rather than hardcoding `12` — that fix holds, the source-of-truth wiring is correct) and from pass-22 #261 (which split the stat tile into FEATURED + INDEX to avoid the prior 12=total/9=visible mismatch — also correct). The remaining nit: two derived counts (lede total + stat-tile split) describe the same underlying set across one screen, and the relationship between them is implied rather than shown. Same comprehension class as pass-26 #282 / pass-27 (ii) (voting-tier reconciliation across home and /about) — adjacent numbers about the same set should agree without arithmetic. Source: `src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx` (lede opener line) + the adjacent stat-tile block (likely `ListsStatRow.tsx` or inline). Fix options: (a) **primary path** — re-word the lede to make the math explicit: `9 in the index, 3 featured this month — 12 we'd defend in a group chat.` Same total, same voice, the relationship is shown rather than computed; (b) collapse to a single `12 LISTS / 10 SHOWS COVERED / May 2026 LAST REVISED` tile row so the lede and stats agree at first glance, and let the body grid headers (`BY TONE · 6 / BY CRAFT · 2 / SINGLE-SHOW · 1` plus the Featured strip cards) carry the split downstream; (c) merge the stat tiles into a single `12 LISTS · 3 FEATURED · 9 INDEXED` summary line so the split is named in the same place the total is. Recommended (a) — single-prose-line edit, smallest blast radius, preserves the stat-tile shape, makes the math visible. Pin: extend the existing `ListsHero.test.tsx` pass-23-#263 case — `lede === featuredVal + indexVal` already pinned (the math is correct); add a copy-shape assertion that the lede mentions both component counts (`featuredVal` and `indexVal`) in the rendered prose when both > 0, so a future regression that drops the split-reveal phrasing fails at unit time. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (lede prose only; no editorial content change). (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-28) — 5101ab6 — issue: #295 — RESOLVED b531ba4: took the row's recommended Option (a). `src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx` opener now branches on `stats.featuredCount`: when the split tiles render (`featuredCount > 0`) the lede is `${indexCount} in the index, ${featuredCount} featured this month — ${stats.total} we'd defend in a group chat.` — the math is spelled, not computed. When `featuredCount === 0` the original single-total opener (`${total} ${plural} we'd defend in a group chat.`) stays so the no-split branch keeps reading as one sentence about a flat catalog (the existing `^N lists\b` regression pins in `falls back to a single "N LISTS" stat`, `singularizes one lives inside one show`, `keeps "some live inside one show"`, `swaps the accent and lede to single-canon-honest copy`, etc. all hold). The wording mirrors the on-page label vocabulary verbatim — `Featured this month.` already heads the rail (`ListsFeaturedRow.tsx:42`); `In the index` is the stat-tile key (`ListsHero.tsx:68`) — so the lede now quotes the structures it summarises rather than introducing new prose. Pinned per the row's spec (and bidirectionally): the prior pass-23 #263 `lede number matches featured + index sum` case rewrote as `lede shows the math inline when featured + index split — names both component counts and the total (critique-pass-23 #263 + pass-28 split-reveal)`. The new case asserts (a) `featuredVal + indexVal === stats.total` (the underlying math wiring carried over from #263), (b) the lede's `${total} we'd defend in a group chat` still tracks `stats.total` (regex `(\d+)\s+we'd defend in a group chat`), (c) both component counts appear in the lede prose alongside their on-stat labels (regexes `\b${indexVal}\s+in\s+the\s+index\b` and `\b${featuredVal}\s+featured\s+this\s+month\b`) — bidirectional, so a regression that hardcodes the opener literal, drops either component, or returns to silent arithmetic fails at unit time. No URL / schema / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — `apps/e2e/tests/themes.spec.ts:38` only pins `not.toContainText(/every show/i)` on the lede (the overclaim guard from a prior pass), unchanged by the new shape. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (lede prose only; no editorial content change, no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2345 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e (8.1m).
- [x] [LOW] [anon] /themes `Featured this month` eyebrow carries no per-strip date receipt — the subhead reads `Editor-selected · refreshed monthly` (source: `src/components/lists/ListsFeaturedRow.tsx:25`) but no date label sits next to the cadence claim. The page-level hero tile `May 2026 INDEX LAST REVISED` stamps the catalog index, not the featured strip. On 2026-06-03 a reader looking at the strip can't tell from the surface whether they're seeing the May refresh (now month-stale) or the June refresh (current); the monthly-cadence promise has no date receipt at the strip that makes the promise. Distinct from pass-23 #265 (which was about a different surface). The fix is cheap and tightens an existing voice promise. Source: `src/components/lists/ListsFeaturedRow.tsx:22-27` (`<h2>Featured this month.</h2>` + `<span className="lists-section-meta">Editor-selected · refreshed monthly</span>`). Fix options: (a) **primary path** — derive a per-strip stamp from the `today` prop already threaded into the component: render `Editor-selected · Featured for {monthName} {year}` (e.g., `Editor-selected · Featured for June 2026`). Replaces the abstract cadence claim with a concrete current-month receipt; no schema work, no content edit, the `today` value is already in scope; (b) supplement rather than replace: keep `Editor-selected · refreshed monthly` and add a small label `· {monthName} {year}` after it; (c) flip the heading itself to name the month — `Featured for {monthName} {year}.` instead of `Featured this month.` — collapsed cadence promise into the heading, eyebrow drops. Recommended (a) — cleanest single-component edit, the cadence claim becomes self-evident on every render. Pin: extend `ListsFeaturedRow.test.tsx` (the existing pass-#254 case at line 16 pins the literal subhead) with a `today` case asserting the rendered subhead names the current month + year when `today` is supplied (e.g., `today: new Date('2026-06-15')` → subhead matches `/June 2026/`), and a `today: undefined` fallback case preserving the original literal `Editor-selected · refreshed monthly` so e2e and unit tests that don't supply `today` keep passing. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (UI-eyebrow prose only). (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-28) — 5101ab6 — issue: #294 — RESOLVED a456a0e: took a deterministic variant of the row's primary path. The finding's option (a) recommended driving the stamp from a `today` prop "already threaded into the component" — `today` is in the component signature but not actually passed by the page (it threads through to `<FeaturedCard>` for its own `formatThemeStatus` use, not to the subhead). Rather than thread `today = new Date()` from `/themes/page.tsx` (which would re-introduce the build-time `new Date()` mismatch class pass-24 #269 closed out for canon-revised labels), `ListsFeaturedRow` now derives the stamp from the max `last_revised` ISO across the rendered featured themes — a source-of-truth date the curator already stamps on every themed list, deterministic across day/month boundaries, and unaffected by deploy timing. New helper `latestFeaturedRevised(featured)` returns the max ISO (or undefined when empty); `canonRevisedLabelFromIso` formats it to "Month YYYY". Final subhead: `Editor-selected · Featured for ${stamp}` when computable; falls back to the original literal `Editor-selected · refreshed monthly` when the helper returns null (malformed `last_revised`, bounded by Zod at content-check time but the runtime fallback keeps the regression class clamped). The same `formatCanonRevisedLabel` chain that the home + /shows + show-page hero stats use ties this surface into the catalog's shared UTC-anchored date pipeline. Pinned per the row's spec (and bidirectionally): `ListsFeaturedRow.test.tsx` "subhead names the latest last_revised month + year (critique pass-28: #294 — stamp the strip with its cadence)" passes a 3-theme featured set with `last_revised` values `[2026-04-15, 2026-06-01, 2026-05-20]` and asserts the rendered subhead equals `Editor-selected · Featured for June 2026` exactly (max picks 2026-06-01, formatter renders June 2026); the prior pass-#254 literal-subhead regression guard is preserved as the fallback case "subhead falls back to the literal cadence claim when no derivable date is available" — passes `last_revised: 'not-a-date'`, asserts the subhead collapses to the original literal `Editor-selected · refreshed monthly` so a future malformed-date regression doesn't render a broken stamp. The bidirectional `not.toMatch(/every 1st/i)` regression guard (pass-#254) is carried into the new positive case. No URL / schema field / page-family change; the `today` prop signature is preserved (still threads to `<FeaturedCard>` for status-formatter use, just no longer drives the subhead). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (UI-eyebrow prose only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2345 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e (7.3m).
- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/survivor `What changed this week` carousel leads with `One World — Slid 36 spots since the last weekly update. was #08 now #44` against three sibling cards moving in single digits (`The Australian Outback -9`, `New Era I -7`, `Heroes vs. Villains +5`). The carousel sits under a `COMMUNITY RANK · UPDATED THURSDAY` header — a per-week cadence promise. A 36-position swing from the top tier (#08) into the tail (#44) inside one weekly tick reads as noise, not a measured editorial signal. The underlying data is probably honest (small vote volumes on lower-traffic seasons amplify single-week swings), but the rendered presentation has no context for the reader to distinguish a 36-spot move from a 5-spot one — both render as cards of the same visual weight. A casual reader who sees that magnitude under the weekly-cadence frame stops trusting the rank as a measured editorial output and reads it as algorithmic chatter. Pass-26 #283 fixed the "binary vote → ordinal answer" comprehension gap with a bridge sentence; this is the next-tier integrity question: even with the bridge, does the magnitude on display match the trust frame? Source: `/shows/survivor` ShiftsRow card cluster (`src/components/composition/ShiftCard.tsx` + `src/components/canon/ShiftsRow.tsx`), rendered values verifiable against the live `compute_weighted_rank()` snapshot for survivor at the current Thursday tick. Fix options: (a) **primary path** — expose the underlying vote count alongside the delta (e.g., `Slid 36 spots since last week · 12 votes`), so the reader can judge the signal-to-noise ratio themselves; the magnitude is honest if grounded in volume; (b) cap the carousel to single-digit moves and surface larger swings under a separate `Biggest realignments this month` header — frames the magnitude with the right cadence; (c) hide individual movers when the season's total vote count for the week is below a floor (e.g., `< 10 votes` → suppress from the carousel) — preserves the per-week frame but trades coverage for reliability. Recommended (a) — minimum semantic change (one new field on the card), highest editorial honesty, the reader sees both the delta and the volume that produced it. Pin: extend `src/components/composition/__tests__/ShiftCard.test.tsx` with a positive case asserting that when `voteCount` is supplied, the card renders it (matches `/\d+ vote/`); bidirectional case asserting the card without vote count doesn't render `vote` in its text. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (ranking-shift surface only, no per-season verdict edit). (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-27) — 9d07409 — issue: #289 — RESOLVED 00d7750: took the row's primary path. `CommunityMover` gained an optional `voteCount?: number` field; `pickMovers` threads it from `CommunityRankRow.voteCount` (already-populated, no upstream change). `moverNote` drops the trailing period and appends `· N votes` when the count is supplied, preserving the existing period-ended form when absent so older fixtures keep compiling; "1 vote" singular / "N votes" plural mirrors the existing "one spot" / "N spots" pattern. The 36-spot One World card now renders `Slid 36 spots since the last weekly update · 12 votes` — the reader can judge magnitude against volume directly; a small-volume swing reads distinctly from a large-volume one. Pinned per finding contract: `ShiftCard.test.tsx` gained three new cases — positive (mover with `voteCount: 12`, delta -36 → card text matches `/\d+ vote/` AND contains literal `· 12 votes` AND the cadence beat), singular-form (`voteCount: 1` → `· 1 vote`), and bidirectional negative (mover without voteCount → card text MUST NOT match `/vote/i`, AND must keep the period-ended form). `live.test.ts` gained a `pickMovers` voteCount-passthrough case (proves the source-row `voteCount: 12` flows through to the emitted mover), three new `moverNote` cases (vote-count-supplied form, pluralization 1/n, period-ended-form-preserved bidirectional pin). No URL / schema / page-family change. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (ranking-shift surface only, no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2335 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains vote block renders to a signed-in viewer who has not voted as `YOUR VOTE / CAST VOTE / Does this belong in the community top 10? / +1 / COMMUNITY · NET VOTE` — two adjacent labels with the `+1` value sandwiched between them. The signed-in viewer's actual state (zero votes cast, verifiable on the matching /u/e2e profile self-view showing `0 SEASONS VOTED`) is not stated anywhere in the block; the `+1` is the community net but the layout puts it directly under the `YOUR VOTE / CAST VOTE` heading, where the first-read interpretation is that the +1 is the viewer's own vote. The `COMMUNITY · NET VOTE` label sits below the +1, not above it, so the reading order doesn't disambiguate. A signed-in member who lands on a season they haven't voted on can't tell at a glance whether they've already voted, which is the single piece of state the block exists to communicate. Pass-26 (vi) noted this class but dropped without a click-through capture; this finding is the pre-click state, separately observable. Source: the season VotePair + community-net stack (likely `src/components/composition/VotePair.tsx` + `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx` around line 318 where the top-10 prompt is rendered — verify with `grep -RE "COMMUNITY .{0,3}NET" src/`). Fix options: (a) **primary path** — render an explicit `YOU HAVEN'T VOTED YET` micro-affordance in the YOUR VOTE column for signed-in non-voters, separated from the COMMUNITY block by a visible divider or column rule so the +1 reads as community-only; (b) merge YOUR VOTE and COMMUNITY into a single two-column block with explicit `YOU` / `COMMUNITY` headers so the +1 sits unambiguously in the community column; (c) suppress the `+1 COMMUNITY · NET VOTE` block until after the viewer casts a vote — trades community visibility for clarity. Recommended (a) — preserves the community signal, adds the missing self-state affordance, smallest blast radius. Pin: colocated VotePair test asserting that for a signed-in user who has not voted, a "haven't voted" or "CAST VOTE" call-to-action renders in the YOUR VOTE column distinct from the COMMUNITY NET VOTE rendering — bidirectional regression guard. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged. (URL: /shows/<show>/season/<slug>, source: critique-pass-27) — 9d07409 — issue: #290 — RESOLVED 8db4bfd: took the row's recommended primary path (a). Reopened pass-12 #189's earlier scope — the no-vote pill had been dropped on the theory that `<VoteRowHead>`'s "cast vote" head meta owned the channel; pass-27 reframed the channels as state-vs-action and restored the no-vote shape descriptively (the head meta calls the action, the cap declares state — together they finish the three-state triad). `src/components/composition/VotePair.tsx` now renders a three-state cap for every signed-in viewer: `value 0` → "you haven't voted yet" (`data-vote-state='none'`); `value 1` → "you voted higher" (`up`); `value -1` → "you voted lower" (`down`). Anon viewers still see no cap (the affordance is viewer-identity bearing — pass-14 #199 / pass-15 #207 invariants preserved). The pre-existing `noVote ? 'community · '` label-prefix logic at line 195 stays — the cap and the label work together: the cap declares "you haven't voted yet"; the label declares "community · net votes" — both channels disambiguate, neither acts alone. The source/CSS/test comments are rewritten in lockstep to name pass-27 + the state-vs-action framing; the pass-12 #189 rationale is preserved historically inside the new comment block so a future author understands the channel reframing rather than the literal-string flip. Pinned per the row's spec (and broader than the row asked — bidirectional + spectrum-of-counts): `src/components/composition/__tests__/VotePair.test.tsx` `state pill (signed-in viewers — three-state triad)` describe-block gained the new shape (asserts `cap.textContent === "you haven't voted yet"` AND `data-vote-state === 'none'` for signed-in-no-vote, across the count spectrum [-9, -1, 0, 1, 9] so a regression on the cap declaration flipping by community-net sign is caught at unit time). The "cap flips on click" test now covers signed-in-no-vote → signed-in-with-vote in both directions (pre-click cap declares no-vote; post-click cap declares the optimistic post-vote state). `apps/e2e/tests/vote-state-pill.spec.ts` flipped its no-vote assertion from `toHaveCount(0)` to the new triad shape; the "clicking up" e2e case now asserts the retract path lands on "you haven't voted yet" instead of an absent cap (the anon-no-cap path at line 194 is unchanged — the affordance stays viewer-identity bearing). Test totals 187 unit files / 2335 tests / 127 script tests / 1361 e2e. No URL / schema field owed (component-internal copy + a11y-friendly `data-vote-state` extension; the `data-testid="vote-state-cap"` selector predates this fix). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (cap describes the viewer's own vote, never the season's verdict; the season's canon position is untouched). VoteRowHead's "cast vote" meta unchanged — it remains the action affordance for the signed-in-no-vote state. Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2335 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e (7.4m).

- [x] [LOW] [anon] /about prose names example themed lists in scare quotes as `"best premieres"` and `"best post-merge runs"` to illustrate the cross-show pattern the site indexes. Neither title exists on /themes — the actual lists are `Premieres that earned it` and `The back-half at full volume` (verified against `content/themes/` slugs and the live /themes index rendering). A reader who follows the example over to /themes to skim those exact lists finds nothing by those names. The /about page is the editorial trust-frame document; example fidelity matters here more than anywhere — a hand-waved "best premieres, best post-merge runs" reads as generic SEO labels, not as the warm, specific list titles the curator actually wrote. Source: `content/legal/about.md` (the example parenthetical — grep `best premieres` or `post-merge`); /themes index for the real list titles. Fix options: (a) **primary path** — quote two real list titles directly: `Or skim the themed lists for cross-show patterns ("Premieres that earned it", "The back-half at full volume", etc.).` — preserves the editorial flow, the link from /about example to /themes list is now true; (b) drop the example parenthetical entirely and rely on the link to /themes to carry the catalog promise; (c) generalize to a category rather than naming specific lists: `cross-show patterns (premieres, finales, back-half runs, etc.).` — vague but harder to drift. Recommended (a) — names two real titles the curator wrote, keeps the warm voice, simple content edit, no code change. Pin: `scripts/content-check.ts` invariant asserting any quoted list-title inside `content/legal/about.md` matches a real theme's `title` frontmatter — catches the drift class at content-check time, so a future theme rename auto-flags this paragraph. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (legal-doc prose only). (URL: /about, source: critique-pass-27) — 9d07409 — issue: #296 — RESOLVED f1cac03: took the row's recommended Option (a). `content/legal/about.md:23` swapped the two illustrative quotes from `"best premieres"` / `"best post-merge runs"` (neither was a real themed-list title) to `"Premieres that earned it"` / `"The back-half at full volume"` (both verbatim from `content/themes/<slug>.md` `title` frontmatter); a reader who follows the example over to /themes to skim those lists now finds them. The pin landed at `scripts/content-check.ts` as a new `collectAboutListTitleQuoteIssues()` helper — scoped to the paragraph(s) referencing `(/themes)`, it reads `content/legal/about.md` raw via `legalFile()`, strips frontmatter with `gray-matter`, splits paragraphs, and asserts every double-quoted phrase inside a `/themes`-linking paragraph matches a real theme `title` exactly. Other quoted strings elsewhere in about.md (the voting-rationale example list at lines 54-57: `"the season was shortened"` / `"the cast had great chemistry"` / `"darker than usual"` / `"filmed in Fiji"` — illustrative example reasons that are NOT meant to name list titles) live in a different paragraph block that does NOT reference `/themes`, so they pass through unaffected. Interior whitespace collapsed before matching so an authored line wrap mid-quote (e.g. `"The back-half\nat full volume"`) matches the title the reader sees once markdown joins the lines. Wired into `main()` strict at floor 0 (`ABOUT_LIST_TITLE_STRICT = true`), the one-line toggle mirroring the nine existing strict invariants (STRICT, CROSS_SHOW_STRICT, YEAR_TENURE_STRICT, TAGLINE_TAIL_STRICT, THEME_COUNT_TAIL_STRICT, THEMED_ENTRY_SPOILER_STRICT, WATCH_ORDER_CLASSIFICATION_STRICT, CLICHE_REPETITION_STRICT, YEAR_TOKEN_PAIRING_STRICT). Pinned per the row's spec (and bidirectionally): six new cases in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` — (1) passes when the /themes paragraph quotes real titles verbatim, (2) reports the pass-27 regression shape (both `"best premieres"` + `"best post-merge runs"` flagged, message names a real theme title as a remediation hint), (3) ignores quoted strings in paragraphs that do NOT reference `/themes` (voting-rationale examples), (4) passes when about.md never references `/themes` (link removed), (5) tolerates a missing about.md as a no-op rather than crash, (6) tolerates a line-wrapped quoted title, (7) reports a drift even when one of several quotes is real (bidirectional partial-match guard). No URL / schema field / e2e fixture row owed — /about is a long-shipped legal-doc page family and no e2e pins the prior literal. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (legal-doc prose only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). - [x] [LOW] [anon] /themes `<meta description>` reads `Themed lists across every tiered.tv canon — best premieres, best finales, cross-canon and single-show tiers, organized by what they admire.` Two drifts: (a) `every tiered.tv canon` overclaims — the same page's visible stat reads `10 SHOWS COVERED` against 13 shows tracked (3 shows have no theme presence); (b) `best premieres / best finales` are generic SEO labels, not the actual editorial list titles on the page (`Premieres that earned it`, `Finales that stuck the landing`).
