- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/survivor mixes two mutually-exclusive mental models of the Community Rank on the same scroll. The site-wide vote question — `Does this belong in the community top 10?` (home `src/components/home/HomeDualCallout.tsx:17-18`, every season's vote block default `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:318`, /about "How voting works") — is a binary yes/no top-10 ballot. But the "What changed this week" shift cards on the same page render ordinal positions across the full N-season set (e.g. `was #08 / now #44 / Slid 36 spots since the last weekly update.` — verbiage from `src/components/composition/__tests__/ShiftCard.test.tsx:45,62`), and the community ranking pane below renders every season's ordinal position 1..N. A first-time reader who reads "does it belong in the top 10?" then sees a shift card saying "now #44 of 50" cannot reconcile the two — is the question binary or ordinal? Technically both are accurate descriptions of different planes of the same data (the yes/no question is the prompt the reader responds to; the ordinals are derived from approval-percentage rollups across all "in"/"out" votes), but the page doesn't give the reader a bridge between them. The page jumps from a top-10 question to a #44-of-50 answer with no sentence connecting the two. Source: `src/components/home/HomeDualCallout.tsx:17`, `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:318` (top-10 framing); `src/components/composition/__tests__/ShiftCard.test.tsx:45,62` ("Slid N spots" / "Climbed N spots" ordinal vocabulary). Fix options: (a) **primary path** — add one sentence to the community pane intro (or the show-page ShiftsRow header) that bridges the two models, e.g. `Each season carries a yes/no vote — does it belong in the community top 10? — and the share of "in" votes orders them 1..N below.`; (b) **alternative** — retire absolute ordinals below the top-10 floor and switch the shift-card vocabulary to relative-position language ("fell out of the top 10", "climbed into the top 25") so the page never mixes binary-question framing with absolute-ordinal answers. Recommended (a) — preserves the existing community-data display and resolves the comprehension gap with one editorial sentence. Pin: extend the colocated test for whichever component owns the bridge sentence (ShowRanking community-pane intro or ShiftsRow header) with a positive assertion that the intro contains the bridge phrase (matches `/(top 10|in[- ]votes|approval|share of)/i`) AND a negative regression that it does NOT carry the top-10 question alone without a follow-on ordinal sentence. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged. (URL: /shows/survivor, every /shows/<show>, source: critique-pass-26) — 215deb5 — issue: #283 — RESOLVED 28edf1d: took the row's recommended primary path (a) on the `ShowRanking` community-pane intro surface (the row named both `ShowRanking` and `ShiftsRow` as eligible — the community-pane intro sits inside the `?view=community` visual scope right above the ordinal rank list it explains, the strongest visual coupling for the bridge). `src/components/canon/ShowRanking.tsx:102-108` `cp-community-only` span gained a leading bridge sentence: `Each season carries a yes/no vote — does it belong in the community top 10? — and the share of "in" votes orders every season 1..N below.` immediately before the existing cadence copy. The redundant `Approval is the share of voters who would keep a season in its slot or move it up.` sentence in the `hasLiveVotes` branch was dropped — the bridge's `share of "in" votes` wording is tighter to the actual binary mechanic, removes the second framing the old copy introduced (slot/move-up), and avoids re-stating "share of" in adjacent sentences. Empty/mirror branch preserves its `Until enough votes land — be the first to move it.` closer untouched. Pinned per the row's "Pin:" spec: `src/components/canon/__tests__/ShowRanking.test.tsx` gained a new `ranking-intro community meta bridges the binary top-10 question to the ordinal rank (pass-26 #283)` case asserting (1) positive that the intro text matches `/top 10/i` AND `/(in[- ]?votes|share of|orders|1\.\.N)/i`, (2) negative drift guard that if "top 10" ever appears alone in the intro, a follow-on share/orders/1..N sentence must appear with it — bidirectional, so a future authoring pass that drops the bridge clause trips the unit gate at verify time. The pre-existing #256 cadence pin (`Updated every Thursday at 9pm ET`) continues to assert against the preserved cadence copy. No URL / schema field / e2e fixture row owed — `apps/e2e/` + `src/**` grep clean against the prior `Approval is the share` literal; the smoke walker only pins 200/H1/no console errors so it inherits the new copy cleanly. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (intro copy edit only, no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2310 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e (7.9m).

- [x] [MED] [anon] / home `<HomeDualCallout>` Community Rank card closes with `Every voter has watched the season end to end.` (`src/components/home/HomeDualCallout.tsx:18-20`) — stated as a flat fact. But `/about` "How voting works" admits explicitly that there is no watched-it gate at the input layer: `Long-standing accounts carry the most weight. Anonymous-guest votes count at 0.1×. Accounts under 7 days old count at 0.25×.` (`content/legal/about.md:37-38`). Anonymous guests can vote without signing in, without any watched-it attestation, and without any review of their history; the weighting ladder is the only thing keeping their vote from carrying full force. So the home claim "Every voter has watched the season end to end" is not true at the input layer — it is only approximately true at the *weighted* layer (an anonymous guest's "have not watched" vote counts at 0.1×). The home page is the editorial trust surface; stating a falsifiable claim that the /about page immediately disqualifies undercuts the brand promise of plain-spoken honesty. The two reads collide on a single navigation step (home → about) and the contradiction is one a reader will notice before they cast their first vote. Source: `src/components/home/HomeDualCallout.tsx:18-20` ("Every voter has watched the season end to end.") vs `content/legal/about.md:37-38` (the 0.1× / 0.25× / 1.0× weighting ladder). Fix options: (a) **primary path** — soften the home claim to acknowledge the weighting mechanic, e.g. `Signed-in voters self-attest they watched the season end to end; anonymous votes count at 0.1× of a signed-in vote.` (names the mechanic the /about page details, ~150 chars); (b) drop the line entirely and let the weighting be the editorial signal at the surface that explains it (the /about page already carries the mechanic); (c) reframe as an aspirational stance rather than a flat fact, e.g. `We weight votes so the people who watched carry the most weight.`. Recommended (a) — preserves the editorial substance (we trust watched voters), matches the actual mechanic, and reads as plain-spoken rather than overpromised. Pin: extend `src/components/home/__tests__/HomeDualCallout.test.tsx` with a negative regression case asserting the live-pane blurb does NOT match `/Every voter has watched/i` AND a positive case asserting it mentions the weighting mechanic (matches `/(weight|0\.1|signed-in|self-attest|attest)/i`). Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged. (URL: /, /about, source: critique-pass-26) — 215deb5 — issue: #282 — RESOLVED a0d7cd5: took the row's recommended primary path (a). `src/components/home/HomeDualCallout.tsx:18-20` swapped `Every voter has watched the season end to end.` → `Signed-in voters self-attest they watched the season end to end; anonymous votes count at 0.1×.` — preserves the editorial substance (we trust watched voters) by framing it as self-attestation rather than a flat fact, and names the weighting mechanic the /about page already details (the 0.1×/0.25×/1.0× ladder at `content/legal/about.md:37-38`). The home and /about now agree on the same trust mechanic, end-to-end. Pinned per the sister pass-26 #281 closure pattern: `src/components/home/__tests__/HomeDualCallout.test.tsx` gained a new `names the weighting mechanic, not a flat watched-every-voter claim` case carrying the row's two assertions verbatim — (1) negative pin `expect(live).not.toMatch(/Every voter has watched/i)` so a regression back to the flat-claim form trips at unit time, (2) positive pin `expect(live).toMatch(/(weight|0\.1|signed-in|self-attest|attest)/i)` so a future authoring pass that drops the mechanic without restoring it also trips. Bidirectional drift guard, mirroring the cadence pin the same file gained one commit earlier (#281). No URL / schema field / e2e fixture row owed — no e2e pinned the prior literal (`apps/e2e/` + `src/**` grep clean against `every voter` / `has watched the season` before the edit). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (home callout copy edit only, no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2309 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e (8.0m).

- [x] [MED] [authed] /u/[handle] profile stats render three counter cells: `SEASONS VOTED`, `COMMENTS`, and `SHOWS FOLLOWED` (`src/components/profile/ProfileStats.tsx:40-43` — `label: '${plural(votedShowCount, "show", "shows")} followed'`). The third cell is labeled as a Follow stat but the underlying value `votedShowCount` is a count of *distinct shows the user has voted on*, not a count of shows the user has followed. There is no Follow / Track / Subscribe feature anywhere in the product — no show page, no chrome control, no profile setting — that a signed-in reader can use to "follow" a show (verified via `grep -rE 'follow' src/components/` — the only `followed` reference is this stat label and its colocated test). So the stat advertises a feature that doesn't exist, and the label silently miscategorizes the underlying voting data as following. The label was likely chosen because "shows-with-votes" doesn't read warmly, but "followed" is a stronger promise than the data carries; a reader who sees `0 SHOWS FOLLOWED` on their own profile reasonably asks where the follow control is, and there isn't one anywhere on the site. Source: `src/components/profile/ProfileStats.tsx:40-43` (the `followed` label literal); `src/components/profile/__tests__/ProfileStats.test.tsx:25` (the test pins `shows followed`). Fix options: (a) **primary path** — relabel the third cell to match the underlying data: `${plural(votedShowCount, "show", "shows")} voted on` (or `${plural(...)} with votes`) so the label is honest about what `votedShowCount` actually counts; (b) **alternative (broader)** — ship a Follow Show feature on /shows/[show] (a signed-in-only control + a `follows` table + a participation tally) so the stat is grounded by a real mechanic — a phase-candidate-shaped change, not a critique fix; (c) drop the third cell entirely and render a two-cell stat grid until/unless a Follow feature ships. Recommended (a) — the data already exists, the label just needs to describe it honestly; matches the editorial honesty register the rest of the site holds. Pin: extend `src/components/profile/__tests__/ProfileStats.test.tsx` with a negative regression case asserting the third-cell label does NOT match `/followed/i` (regression pin against the ungrounded-counter framing) AND a positive case asserting it contains either `voted` or `with votes`. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (participation aggregate only, no per-ballot detail). (URL: /u/e2e, source: critique-pass-26) — 215deb5
  - issue: #284 — RESOLVED 00538dc: took the row's primary path (a). `src/components/profile/ProfileStats.tsx:42` flipped `${plural(votedShowCount, 'show', 'shows')} followed` → `${plural(votedShowCount, 'show', 'shows')} voted on` — the label now describes what `votedShowCount` actually counts (distinct shows the user has voted on), no longer promising a Follow / Track / Subscribe mechanic the product doesn't carry. Comment block at lines 11-22 swept to name the new cell order (`SEASONS VOTED → COMMENTS → SHOWS VOTED ON`) and cite the critique pass + the verified absence of a follow mechanic in the codebase. Pinned per the #277/#278 closure pattern: `src/components/profile/__tests__/ProfileStats.test.tsx` gains a new `labels the third cell against the data it counts, not a missing follow mechanic` case asserting the cell text matches `/voted/i` AND does NOT match `/followed/i` (bidirectional regression guard against the ungrounded-counter framing). Existing first-case labeled-text assertion swept from `'shows followed'` → `'shows voted on'`. No e2e fixture row owed — pre-flight grep across `apps/e2e/{tests,src}/` for `shows followed`/`SHOWS FOLLOWED`/`followed` returned no production code refs (only Playwright SDK internals). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (participation aggregate only, no per-ballot detail, no canon position change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2310 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e (7.9m).

- [x] [LOW] [authed] /u/[handle] owner self-view empty-state renders exactly one outbound CTA: `Start with Survivor →` (`src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx:50-55`, the `selfView`-branched link that points at the featured show). For a signed-in reader with zero activity, the whole 13-show catalog and the 12 themed lists are the real answer to "what do I do next" — pointing only at the featured show reads as a stub. A reader who has already watched Survivor and signed up for a non-Survivor reason (a Top Chef season they want to weigh in on, a themed list they want to argue against) gets one CTA that is wrong for them and no secondary path to the catalog from the empty state. The featured-show CTA is fine for first-time readers; the gap is the absence of a "browse all shows" / "browse themed lists" sibling link beside it. The home page's own intro pane already pairs `Browse all shows` with `How it works` as a two-CTA hero pattern, so the convention exists in the product. Source: `src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx:36-58` (the `selfView` branch renders `<ProfileStats>` + the second-person prompt + the single `Start with {showName} →` link). Fix: add a single secondary link beside `Start with Survivor →` — either `Browse all shows →` linking to `/shows` or `Browse themed lists →` linking to `/themes`. Two links, not five — keep the empty state quiet but offer the catalog. Pin: extend `src/components/profile/__tests__/ProfileEmpty.test.tsx` with a positive case asserting BOTH the Survivor CTA and the secondary catalog link render in the `selfView` branch (matches `/Start with Survivor/i` AND `/(Browse all shows|Browse themed lists)/i`); the stranger branch (no `selfView`) stays untouched — no CTA leakage on someone else's record. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged. Stranger branch (no `selfView`) untouched: neither CTA renders, so no door opens on someone else's record. - [x] [MED] [anon] `/about` ships a flat fallback `<meta name="description">` reading `A spoiler-free home for ranked TV seasons. How it works.` — the homepage tagline with three terse words tacked on. The page body itself is genuinely substantive (the canon spec, the vote-weighting bands 0.1x/0.25x/1.0x, the spoilers policy, the AI pre-filter explanation, the become-an-editor invitation) but none of that substance reaches search snippets, social-card previews, or any indexing surface that consumes the description. `/about` is the editorial honesty page — the surface a curious reader follows when they want to know how the canon is made and how vote weight works — so a meta description that says nothing about either is a missed acquisition surface. Compounded by the `<title>About tiered.tv — tiered.tv</title>` brand-repetition: `buildMetadata({ title: doc.title })` reads `title: About tiered.tv` from `content/legal/about.md:3` and `buildMetadata` appends the global ` — tiered.tv` suffix, producing the doubled wordmark. Source: `content/legal/about.md:3-4` (the frontmatter description literal that flows through to `src/app/(default)/about/page.tsx:14`). Fix options: (a) **primary path** — rewrite the description in `content/legal/about.md` to name the page's substance, e.g. `Two rankings side by side: an editor's canon and a weekly community vote, with a spoilers policy that defines what's fair game and what isn't.` (~150 chars, ledes the substantive content the body delivers); (b) **stretch sub-fix on the title** — change the frontmatter `title:` to bare `About` (the suffix appender produces `About — tiered.tv`, no brand repetition), or rewire `buildMetadata` to skip the suffix when the title already contains `tiered.tv` (broader change, deferrable). Pin: extend `src/app/(default)/about/__tests__/page.test.tsx` (or add the colocated test if absent) with two cases — positive assertion that the description does NOT match `/How it works\.$/` (regression pin against the flat-fallback drift) AND a positive assertion that the description contains a substantive editorial keyword the body owns (`spoilers` or `canon` or `vote`). Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (metadata edit only, no body-content change). (URL: /about, source: critique-pass-25) — issue: #276 — RESOLVED cd7c7e4: took the row's primary path (description rewrite + colocated test pin). `content/legal/about.md:4` swapped `A spoiler-free home for ranked TV seasons. How it works.` → `Two rankings side by side — an editor's canon and a weekly community vote — with a spoilers policy that defines what's fair game and what isn't.` (~150 chars) — the new copy ledes with the editorial substance the body delivers (canon spec + weight ladder + spoilers policy) and reaches every consumer of `buildMetadata({ description })`: `<meta name="description">`, OG/Twitter description, JSON-LD. The "stretch sub-fix on the title" (`title: About tiered.tv — tiered.tv` brand-repetition) was deferred per the row's "broader change, deferrable" note — that surface is index-blocked anyway (the route stays open to indexing but the title-suffix change touches every legal-doc title at once, a wider blast radius than this row warrants). Pinned per the #242/#257/#258 closure pattern: `src/app/(default)/about/__tests__/page.test.tsx` gained a new `content/legal/about.md frontmatter description (#276)` describe block with three cases — (1) positive that the description names at least one of {canon, vote, spoilers} (matches the body substance the finding called out); (2) negative that the description does not match `/How it works\.\s*$/` (regression pin against the flat-fallback closer drift); (3) negative that the description does not match `/A spoiler-free home for ranked TV seasons/` (regression pin against the home-tagline duplicate opener that produced the prior drift). Bidirectional, so a future authoring pass cannot silently revert either direction without tripping the unit gate. No URL / schema field / page-family change; no e2e fixture row owed — `/about` is a long-shipped legal-doc page family and no e2e pins the prior description literal. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (metadata edit only, no body-content change, no per-season verdict change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2296 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] On `/` (home) the `HomeMoreShows` section renders a section divider that reads `+ 9 more in the index / Browse all →` immediately above a grid of 9 compact show tiles — the same 9 shows the divider's `+ 9 more` framing teases. A first-time reader scanning vertically reads the `+ N more in the index` pill as the established UI affordance it imitates ("there are N additional items behind a click; tap to expand") and then immediately encounters all N of those items rendered right below, with the `Browse all →` CTA leading to `/shows` where the same 9 shows reappear with longer blurbs. The pill stops being honest signage and becomes a vestigial header for a section it's not actually gating. Two clean reads (one of these): (a) the pill is intended as a section header for the rest-of-the-index grid, in which case the `+ N more` framing is misleading — the rest of the index is not "more" hidden behind a click, it's *right there* on the same scroll — and the label should rename to a flat sectioning header like `The rest of the index` (preserves the `Browse all →` CTA as a path to fuller editorial blurbs on `/shows`); (b) the intent is genuinely to hide those 9 tiles behind a click, in which case `HomeMoreShows` should render only the pill, not the children, and clicking `Browse all →` should expand or navigate. Recommended: (a) — the home is meant to surface coverage, not hide it; the editorial blurbs on `/shows` are richer than the compact tiles, so the CTA stays useful. Source: `src/components/home/HomeMoreShows.tsx:14-15` (the `+ {count} more in the index` literal); the structure-comment at `src/lib/home/show-partition.ts:14` documents the divider/grid contract. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (copy/label edit only). Call site `src/app/(default)/page.tsx:89` drops `count=`. Structure-comment at `src/lib/home/show-partition.ts:14` updated to name the new label form. Pin: colocated `src/components/home/__tests__/HomeMoreShows.test.tsx` gains a renamed first case asserting `label.textContent === 'The rest of the index'` AND a negative regex pin `expect(label.textContent).not.toMatch(/\+\s+\d+\s+more\s+in\s+the\s+index/)` (the exact pattern from the row's pin spec), bidirectional so a regression back to the teaser framing fails at unit time.
