- [x] [MED] [authed] On `/themes` the lede reads `12 lists we'd defend in a group chat. Some span the catalog, one lives inside one show.` (derived from `stats.total`, ListsHero.tsx:20 `const opener = \`${stats.total} ${plural(stats.total, 'list', 'lists')} we'd defend in a group chat.\``) — but four rows below, the filter scope chip reads `SHOWING · ALL 9 LISTS` (derived from `counts.all`, which `src/app/(default)/themes/page.tsx:61-66` computes as the sum of NON-featured categories: `byCategoryRest.tone + byCategoryRest.craft + byCategoryRest.era + byCategoryRest.single`). The two numbers are both internally honest — `12` is the catalog total, `9` is the index-grid count excluding the 3 featured-this-month entries rendered above — but the word `ALL` on the chip naturally reads as "every list in the catalog" not "every list in the index-grid scope". So the reader sees `12 lists` in the lede, the hero stat strip splits to `3 FEATURED · 9 IN THE INDEX` (which reconciles correctly — 12 = 3 + 9), then the filter chip says `ALL 9 LISTS` and the index grid renders 9 cards. The chip's `ALL` quantifier silently shadows the lede's `12`. The pass-23 #263 fix specifically wired the lede to read from `stats.total` to prevent drift between lede and stats; this pass-25 finding exposes the next drift link — between the lede and the filter chip. Two clean fixes: (a) **fold the featured into the index total** — let `counts.all = stats.total` and re-render the featured cards inside the grid (visually the featured rail above stays; the filter scope total now reads `ALL 12 LISTS` and clicking `ALL` shows everything including featured); this requires letting the grid render the featured-card slugs alongside their non-featured siblings (the pass-20 dedupe to prevent double-render needs the inverse decision); (b) **honest scope on the chip** — change `filterModeText(filter, counts)` at `src/lib/themes-format.ts:88` from `showing · all ${counts.all} lists` to `showing · all ${counts.all} in the index · ${stats.featured} featured above` (or shorter — `showing · all ${counts.all} in the index`), so the word `ALL` is qualified by `in the index` and the catalog total no longer silently disagrees with the chip total. Recommended: (b) — smallest blast radius (single-string edit + a `featured` count plumbed through to `counts`), preserves the editorial intent that featured cards are a curatorial spotlight distinct from the index, fixes the comprehension drift on the chip without re-litigating the pass-20 dedupe decision. Source: `src/lib/themes-format.ts:83-88` (`filterModeText` body — needs the qualifier) and `src/app/(default)/themes/page.tsx:104` (the `<ListsFilterController counts={counts}>` call site — needs `counts` to also carry `featured` for the qualifier copy). Pin: extend `src/lib/__tests__/themes-format.test.ts` `filterModeText` cases — positive assertion that `filter === 'all'` mode includes a qualifier substring (`/in the index/i` or `/indexed/i`) AND a negative pin that the literal does NOT match `/^showing · all \d+ lists$/` (regression guard against the bare-quantifier drift). Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (label-copy edit only). (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-25) — issue: #278 — RESOLVED this commit: took the recommended short form of path (b). `src/lib/themes-format.ts:88` `filterModeText('all', counts)` flipped from `showing · all ${counts.all} lists` to `showing · all ${counts.all} in the index` — the qualifier `in the index` scopes `ALL` to the filter-grid the chip drives, so the lede's catalog total (`12 lists`) no longer silently disagrees with the chip's index total (`ALL 9 IN THE INDEX`). The shorter shape (no `· ${featured} featured above` tail) avoids plumbing a new `featured` count through `counts`, keeps `ListsFilterController`'s `counts: Record<FilterKey, number>` prop shape unchanged, and preserves the editorial split the hero stat strip already names (`3 FEATURED · 9 IN THE INDEX`). The chip and the stat strip now read the same scope label, end-to-end. Pinned per the #263 closure pattern: `src/lib/__tests__/themes-format.test.ts` `filterModeText` describe gains (1) updated positive `'showing · all 12 in the index'` exact-match, (2) a `/in the index/i` substring assertion under a non-default count (9), and (3) a five-count negative pin (`0, 1, 9, 12, 100`) asserting the literal does NOT match `/^showing · all \d+ lists$/` — bidirectional drift guard. `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListsFilterController.test.tsx` swapped the default-mode assertion from `'all 12 lists'` → `'all 12 in the index'` and added a render-level negative pin against the bare-quantifier shape — so a regression in either the formatter OR the controller wiring fails at unit time. No source change to `ListsFilterController.tsx` or `src/app/(default)/themes/page.tsx`; no schema field; no e2e fixture row owed (`apps/e2e/{tests,src}/` grep against `lists-filter-mode`/`showing.*all` returned no matches before this commit). Spoiler P0 intact (label-copy edit only, no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2299 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] The editorial phrase `measures itself against` / `measured against` / `the season the franchise measures itself against` appears as the fallback canonical-claim closer across at least 10 distinct surfaces in the content corpus and reaches at least 4 in a single reader's first walk: `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-vs-villains.md:14` pull-quote (`Decades in, this is the season Survivor will still be measured against.`), `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-vs-villains.md:43` body closer (`The season the franchise measures itself against.`), `content/shows/survivor/canon.md:53` slot #02 tag (`The bar every all-star season measures itself against.`), `content/themes/best-finales.md:26` entry #02 title (`A closing stretch the franchise still measures itself against.`), `content/themes/best-returnees.md:20` entry #01 title (`The ur-returnee season the rest get measured against.`), `content/themes/best-post-merge.md:21` entry #01 blurb (`Cagayan's post-merge is the template … set the bar later back-half runs measure against.`), `content/themes/best-villain-editing.md:21` entry blurb (`The reference any later villain stretch is measured against.`), `content/shows/amazing-race.md:11` tagline (`still the format every other race show is measured against`), `content/shows/top-chef.md:11` tagline (`still sets the bar every culinary competition gets measured against`), `content/shows/big-brother/canon.md:11` tier-S blurb (`The seasons the franchise's later runs are still measured against.`) + entry body line at :109 (`The reference point every later modern alliance gets measured against.`), `content/shows/project-runway/seasons/04-new-york-2007.md:12` pull (`Every later season measures itself against this one.`). Across one casual walk (home → /shows/survivor → /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains → /themes/best-finales) the phrase lands 4 times, with two of them on the SAME page (HvV's pull-quote AND body closer). Bearings voice rule is explicit: `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken, never pretentious. Plain sentences over clever ones.` The phrase is genuinely a clever closer — it pulls "measure," a numeric/measurement metaphor, onto an editorial claim — and it lands the first time, but reused 10+ times across the corpus it becomes the editor's tic. By the third encounter it stops reading as a considered claim and starts reading as a refrain the writer reaches for whenever they need to say "this season matters." Fix: scope the phrase to ONE high-leverage surface (recommendation: HvV's THE TAKE pull-quote at `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-vs-villains.md:14`, where it has the most editorial weight and lands the cleanest) and rewrite the other 9+ surfaces with material specific to the work each entry is doing — HvV's body closer can lean on the post-merge density it already names; the Survivor canon slot #02 tag can foreground the casting-as-format observation; the best-finales #02 entry can name what specifically lands in the closing run; the best-returnees #01 title can name the format-defining returnee dynamic; the per-show tagline literals (Amazing Race, Top Chef, Big Brother) can name what specifically makes the show the format leader (the airport-backpack rhythm, the Restaurant Wars reckoning, the alliance-as-format engine). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (content-curator edit only, no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). Bonus invariant: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` with a `collectClicheRepetitionIssues` pass that counts cross-surface occurrences of high-leverage editorial phrases (`measures? itself against`, `measured against`, candidates for future additions like `set the bar`, `the bar every`) and flags >3-occurrence drift — the same guard pattern `collectYearTenureIssues` uses for `est_year` math, but for phrase-reuse. (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-25) — issue: #280 — RESOLVED 92ebd74: took the row's full path (content rewrite + the bonus content-check invariant). Inventory at HEAD was 32 occurrences across 22 files (the row's "at least 10" undercount surfaced once the grep was run repo-wide). `content-curator` rewrote 31 occurrences and kept the single HvV pull-quote — the highest-leverage surface, exactly per the row's recommendation. Each rewrite installs a *local* fact in place of the meta-claim rather than reaching for another shared closer: tier-S blurbs (Bachelor / Bachelorette / Big Brother / Bake Off) each foreground what that show's S-tier is specifically about; canon entry tags / slot_arguments install the local structural fact (Survivor #2 casting-as-format, Big Brother Brigade alliance-lore, Bachelor #13 single-dad-lead + eleven-episode runway, Top Chef #6 Las Vegas brothers-on-veteran-roster); season pulls/bodies close on the named texture (Project Runway S04 Klum/Gunn/Garcia/Nina-as-lockstep panel, Bachelor S25 the twenty-four-prior-winters precedent, Fiji 2024 three-production-swings-in-one-summer); themed-list titles / blurbs land on the entry's own observation (best-finales #02 retitled around HvV back-half volume, best-post-merge Cagayan closed on "where the modern grammar finally consolidated"). Bachelorette S08 + Project Runway S04 + HvV body trimmed back into the 50-80 word envelope after the rewrite expanded their close (curator over-wrote each by 3-4 words; tightened in main-agent pass). All `canonical_position`, tier slots, and placement ordinals unchanged. Spoiler discipline P0 held — no winners, no eliminations, no finale beats; brand promise intact. Bonus invariant per the row: `scripts/content-check.ts:566` added `collectClicheRepetitionIssues` mirroring `collectYearTenureIssues`'s cross-surface scan (every editorial text field on every show, season, canon entry, and theme entry); closed-list `CLICHE_PATTERNS`; threshold per pattern. The "measures/measured against" pattern uses threshold 3 — one kept high-leverage surface plus a small margin. Wired strict (`CLICHE_REPETITION_STRICT = true`) — the rewrite drained the corpus to 1 occurrence, well under threshold. A future drain that wants to add another pattern (e.g. "set the bar", "the reference point") appends to `CLICHE_PATTERNS` once the corpus has been cleaned to fit under its threshold — adding a pattern without a paired rewrite would break verify (same lax->strict pattern the year-tenure / tagline-tail / theme-count-tail invariants ship). Pinned per the #277/#278 closure pattern: `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts:1997` 7-case describe block — live catalog passes (post-rewrite 1 < threshold 3), threshold-3 passes, threshold+1 flags every hit, multiple hits in a single field, all three forms (measures itself against / measured against / measure against), case-insensitive, no false-positives on incidental phrases ("the measure of the room", "measured response"). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2307 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (298 SSG season slugs); leg3 1361 e2e (8.1m).

- [x] [MED] [anon] `/sign-in` page `<meta name="description">` reads `Sign in to tiered. Magic link by email.` — the brand has been truncated to `tiered.` (dropping the `.tv` suffix). CLAUDE.md hard rule 6 is explicit: "The brand name is `tiered.tv` — always lowercase, including the `.tv` suffix. Never capitalize the T. The dot is part of the wordmark; never stylize, color, or kern it apart." A description of `Sign in to tiered.` puts a fullstop where the `.tv` belongs, which reads as if the brand name ends mid-word — the literal opposite of the brand-mark contract. The route is `noIndex: true` so search engines won't surface this in SERP snippets, but the description still ships in the rendered head and is visible to (a) any social/preview crawler that ignores `noIndex` for inspection, (b) a reader using View Source, (c) the OG/Twitter card fallback path that pulls from `<meta description>` when an explicit OG description is absent. Source: `src/app/(default)/sign-in/page.tsx`'s `buildMetadata({ description: 'Sign in to tiered. Magic link by email.', path: '/sign-in', noIndex: true })`. Fix: change the description literal to `Sign in to tiered.tv. Magic link by email.` — the dot before `tv` is part of the brand wordmark, not the sentence-ending fullstop. Pin with a colocated test in `src/app/(default)/sign-in/__tests__/page.test.tsx` asserting the description string includes the literal substring `tiered.tv` (the unit gate would catch any future authoring pass that re-truncated the suffix); bonus invariant — extend the existing `scripts/content-check.ts` brand-spelling check (if any; otherwise add one) that scans every `description` string returned from a `buildMetadata` call across the route tree and rejects any value matching `\btiered\.\s*(?!tv)` (a stray `tiered.` not followed by `tv`). Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (metadata text edit only). (URL: /sign-in, source: critique-pass-24) — issue: #270 — RESOLVED eafe126: took the row's primary path (description literal swap + colocated test pin). `src/app/(default)/sign-in/page.tsx:12` swapped `'Sign in to tiered. Magic link by email.'` → `'Sign in to tiered.tv. Magic link by email.'` — the dot before `tv` is now part of the brand wordmark, not the sentence-ending fullstop. Pinned per the #256/#265/#266 closure pattern: `src/app/(default)/sign-in/__tests__/page.test.tsx` `generateMetadata` describe block gained a new case asserting the description contains the literal `tiered.tv` AND does NOT match `/\btiered\.(?!tv)/` (a stray `tiered.` not followed by `tv` — covers whitespace, end-of-string, or any non-`tv` continuation). Bidirectional, so a future authoring pass that re-truncates the suffix trips the unit gate at verify time. The "bonus invariant" extension to `scripts/content-check.ts` (a build-wide `buildMetadata` description scan) was scoped down to the colocated test for this tick — every other `buildMetadata` description in the route tree was grep-verified clean (`pnpm exec rg "tiered\\." src/app --type tsx` returns only the just-fixed surface), so the wider invariant is a stretch follow-up if a second surface ever surfaces. No URL / schema field / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — `/sign-in` is a long-shipped auth-funnel page family and `apps/e2e/src/fixtures/canonical-urls.ts` + `page-reads.ts` already carry its path; no e2e pinned the prior description literal so the walk inherits the new copy cleanly. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (metadata text edit only, no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2290 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e (8.1m).

- [x] [MED] [anon] `/shows/survivor` "What changed this week" panel renders position diffs (`was #08 now #44`, `36 this week`) under an eyebrow that reads `UPDATED THURSDAY` — but the panel never labels which ranking is shifting. The panel sits **above** the canon/community tab pair, and is followed by the editorial copy block that names the canon as "one ranking, written by editors … revised quarterly when a new season changes the math." A first-time reader scanning vertically reads: weekly-shifting movers panel → canon/community tab toggle → "revised quarterly" promise — and is left to infer that a panel showing 36-slot weekly moves belongs to the community side. The visual proximity to the canon eyebrow invites the wrong inference (an editorial canon that moved 36 slots in a week would directly contradict the "revised quarterly" promise that renders one screen below). The previous pass-23 finding on this surface trimmed `SENTIMENT-TAGGED` from the eyebrow but didn't add the source-of-truth label the panel still owes. Fix options: (a) extend the eyebrow to read `WHAT CHANGED IN THE COMMUNITY RANK · UPDATED THURSDAY` (or `COMMUNITY RANK · UPDATED THURSDAY`) — names the source explicitly without lengthening the panel; (b) move the existing `community rank updates weekly` microcopy from the season-page VotePair into this panel's footer, so the source-of-truth caption sits inside the same visual scope as the deltas; (c) restructure so the panel sits *below* the tab pair on the community-active pane and is absent on the canon-active pane (largest blast radius — touches the tab-toggle state machine). Recommended: (a) — single-component eyebrow string edit, lowest cost, pays off the editorial honesty the canon page promises. Source: `src/components/composition/ShiftsRow.tsx:24` (the recently-trimmed eyebrow string; second related surface at `src/components/canon/CommunityMovers.tsx:22`). Pin with the same closure pattern pass-23 #265 used: a colocated test asserting the panel eyebrow contains `community` (case-insensitive) AND a negative pin against the prior orphan-clause-only form (so a future edit can't silently drop the source label back to bare `UPDATED THURSDAY`). Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (eyebrow copy edit only, no per-season verdict change). (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-24) — issue: #271 — RESOLVED 1c3f293: took the row's recommended path (a). `src/components/composition/ShiftsRow.tsx:24` swapped `Updated Thursday` → `Community rank · Updated Thursday` (CSS continues to uppercase the literal to `COMMUNITY RANK · UPDATED THURSDAY` via `.sec-meta { text-transform: uppercase }`). The eyebrow now names the source-of-truth ranking in the same visual scope as the position diffs, so the panel's editorial intent reads correctly even when scanned in isolation against the canon's "revised quarterly" promise one screen below. The sister surface `src/components/canon/CommunityMovers.tsx:22` was left untouched — that eyebrow (`Top changes`) already sits inside the `?view=community` pane visual scope, so the source-of-truth is unambiguous from context; the finding's primary mention names `ShiftsRow.tsx:24` as the recommended fix surface. Pinned per the #265/#266/#270 closure pattern: the existing `ShiftsRow.test.tsx` regression-guard case is upgraded — drops the prior `^Updated Thursday$` strict match + the `·` exclusion (now intentional separator), adds a positive `/community/i` assertion + a verbatim `^Community rank · Updated Thursday$` match, keeps the `/sentiment/i` negative pin so a future edit cannot silently reintroduce the orphan clause #265 removed. Bidirectional: a regression that drops the source-of-truth label back to bare `UPDATED THURSDAY` trips the verbatim match; a regression that re-adds the sentiment clause trips the negative pin. No URL / schema / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — `/shows/[show]` is long-shipped and the only e2e pin on `shifts-row` is the empty-state count check in `show-home.spec.ts:64-66`, unchanged by this edit. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (eyebrow copy edit only, no per-season verdict change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2290 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e (7.9m).

- [x] [MED] [authed] On `/themes/[theme]` detail pages a signed-in member clicks `Save list` and the button flips to `Saved` — but there is no return-path surface for the member to find their saved lists later. The chrome has no `Saved lists` nav item; `/u/[handle]` self-view exposes a three-cell stat row (SEASONS VOTED / COMMENTS / SHOWS FOLLOWED) with no `LISTS SAVED` row and no link to a saved-lists index; and the localStorage key (`tiered_saved_lists`) means a returning member loses every save the next time they switch device or clear browser data. The action implies a member-record affordance (the button claims authoritative state — "Saved" — not transient state — "Saved on this device"), and the profile that should hold it doesn't. Two clean fixes, scoped differently: (a) **honest cap** — add a small `saved on this device` caption next to the post-click `Saved` label so the localStorage scope is transparent; existing tools row stays; no new pivot owed (smallest blast radius, fully scoped to `ListDetailTools.tsx`); (b) **wire the member record** — add a `LISTS SAVED` cell to `ProfileStats` reading the same localStorage key client-side and a `Saved lists →` link on `/u/[handle]` that lists the saved set; sync to Supabase deferred to a separate phase. Recommended: (a) for this iterate-sized tick — the honest-cap path closes the comprehension gap without taking on the larger member-record wiring that belongs in a phase. Source: `src/components/lists/ListDetailTools.tsx` (lines 5–53 for the localStorage handling, the visible CTA copy state). Pin with colocated test cases on `ListDetailTools.test.tsx`: (i) the unsaved label reads `Save list` exactly; (ii) the post-click label reads `Saved` AND the rendered surface includes a caption containing `this device` (regression pin against re-introducing a member-record claim without backing it). Bonus: a stretch `Saved lists →` row on `ProfileStats` can land in a follow-up tick once the localStorage key is reachable from the profile route boundary. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (UI caption edit only). (URL: /themes/best-premieres, source: critique-pass-24) — issue: #272 — RESOLVED 25b42d2: took the row's recommended path (a) "honest cap". `src/components/lists/ListDetailTools.tsx` gains a sibling `<span className="tool-caption" data-testid="list-save-caption">saved on this device</span>` rendered immediately after the save button when `saved === true`; absent when `saved === false`. The Saved button's literal copy + aria/data attributes stay identical (no claim-side change, just the new scope caption next to it). `src/styles/lists.css` gains a `.list-tools .tool-caption` rule — same mono 11px / 0.08em letter-spacing as the surrounding `.tool-btn` family, dimmer ink (`var(--color-ink-3)`), lowercase, `align-self: center` so it sits on the button baseline. The caption is read by AT users alongside the pressed button via DOM order. Pinned with the two cases the finding's "Pin:" section requests: (i) `unsaved label reads "Save list" exactly and no caption is rendered` (positive label + `queryByTestId('list-save-caption')` null pin); (ii) `post-click label reads "Saved" and surface includes a "this device" caption` (positive label + `getByTestId('list-save-caption')` text `/this device/i`). The pre-existing `save toggles localStorage and aria-pressed` case is preserved unchanged (no break in the localStorage round-trip contract). The bonus `Saved lists →` row on `ProfileStats` is deliberately deferred — it owes a member-record wire-up that belongs in a phase, not an iterate tick. No URL / schema / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — `apps/e2e/tests/themes.spec.ts:261` `Save list toggles aria-pressed + data-saved on click` pins the data-attrs not the visible label, so it inherits the new surface cleanly (verified green). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (UI caption add only, no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + unit tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build; leg3 1361 e2e (8.0m).

- [x] [LOW] [anon] `/themes` subhead `ORGANIZED BY WHAT THEY'RE ADMIRING` uses a curly right-single-quote (U+2019) for the apostrophe in `THEY'RE`. Every other apostrophe on this page — and across the rest of the cross-page capture set (7 anon URLs, 7 authed URLs, 28 captures total) — is a straight ASCII apostrophe (U+0027) including `EDITOR-SELECTED`, `we'd defend in a group chat`, `aren't claiming`, `we are trying`. A second matching instance lives on `/themes/best-finales`'s `RANKED · EDITOR'S PICK` ribbon. Standing alone in an uppercased label string, the smart-quote reads as the kind of typographic inconsistency a careful reader catches before they consciously name it — slightly more so on the `/themes` overview because the all-caps subhead invites visual scanning across multiple labels. Fix: replace the U+2019 with U+0027 in the two component surfaces (`src/components/lists/AllListsHeading.tsx` for the `/themes` subhead and `src/components/lists/ListEntryStack.tsx:32` for the entries-meta ribbon — pass-18 #236 closed `Editor's Canon` → `Editor's pick` on this surface; the apostrophe inherited the smart-quote at that edit). Pin with a colocated assertion on each component's literal that the rendered text contains no U+2019 codepoint; bonus invariant in `scripts/content-check.ts` (or a new `scripts/check-smart-quotes.mjs` gate) that scans rendered route output for stray U+2019 in label literals (carve out narrative editorial body copy where curly quotes are sometimes intentional). (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-24) — issue: #275 — RESOLVED d794db9: took the row's primary path. Two literals flipped to straight ASCII apostrophes: `src/components/lists/ListsAllSection.tsx:29` `Organized by what they&rsquo;re admiring` → `Organized by what they're admiring` (the AllListsHeading the row named is the lists-section-meta surface inside `ListsAllSection`, where this literal lives today); `src/components/lists/ListEntryStack.tsx:32` `Ranked · Editor&rsquo;s pick` → `Ranked · Editor's pick`. Both surfaces sit under CSS `text-transform: uppercase` + monospace (`src/styles/lists.css:180-185` `.lists-section-meta` and `:649-654` `.entries-meta`), where the U+2019 reads stark against the rest of the family. Pinned per the #270/#274 closure pattern: `ListEntryStack.test.tsx`'s existing entries-meta case updated in lockstep — `toBe("Ranked · Editor's pick")` (straight) + new bidirectional negative `expect(meta?.textContent).not.toMatch(/’/)` so a regression back to U+2019 fails at unit time; the prior `Editor's Canon` reservation pin retained in both straight + curly forms (defensive against either authoring style). `ListsAllSection.test.tsx` gained a new colocated case (`lists-section-meta renders the subhead with a straight ASCII apostrophe`) asserting `toBe("Organized by what they're admiring")` + the same negative regex pin. Narrative editorial body copy across the codebase (`SeasonHero` decodeTitleEntities, `HowTiersMove`, `CommunityWeeklyQuestionCard`, `ProfileHeader`, `ShowRanking`, `layout.tsx` metadata, `feed.xml`/`feed/[slug]` descriptions, `(default)/page.tsx` metadata) keeps its intentional `&rsquo;` typography — only the two CSS-uppercased label surfaces flipped, matching the row's explicit carve-out for body copy. The "bonus" content-check gate is intentionally deferred — the colocated bidirectional pins already trip on the two surfaces that were regressing; a broader rendered-route scan belongs in a separate row once a third uppercased-label surface exists to test the carve-out logic against. No URL / schema field / e2e fixture row owed — no e2e pinned either literal; the smoke walker only pins 200/H1/no console errors. Spoiler P0 intact (typography edit only, no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2278 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e (7.6m).

- [x] [LOW] [authed] `/themes/[theme]` `Suggest an entry` CTA opens the user's mail client addressed to `editors@tiered.app` — but the customer-facing brand is `tiered.tv`. An authed reader who clicks `Suggest an entry` sees an off-brand domain in their compose window that reads like a typo of the brand wordmark. The `tiered.app` namespace is the internal auth-tenant identifier (`https://tiered.app/permissions` claim in `src/lib/auth0/permissions.ts:12`, `e2e@tiered.app` test-user inbox in `plan/bearings.md:37`, `https://api.tiered.app` audience in `plan/bearings.md:99`) — internal infrastructure, never customer-facing. The customer-facing mailto should match the customer-facing brand. Source: `src/components/lists/ListDetailTools.tsx:71` — `const mailto = \`mailto:editors@tiered.app?subject=...\``; the existing colocated test at `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListDetailTools.test.tsx:59` pins the current literal so a fix needs to land in lockstep with the test. Fix: change the mailto target to `editors@tiered.tv` (set up an editors@ forwarder on the customer-facing domain if not already in place — this is a single-line code edit + one test assertion swap; the inbox-routing infrastructure work can land in a separate phase if needed). Update both colocated tests (`ListDetailTools.test.tsx:59` and the sister assertion in `ListDetailHero.test.tsx:80`). Pin with a new content-check invariant scanning all user-facing mailto / href attributes for `tiered\.app` and flagging any match outside the auth-tenant infrastructure paths (Auth0 permissions claim, e2e test user, api audience). Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged. (URL: /themes/best-premieres, source: critique-pass-24) — issue: #274 — RESOLVED this commit: took the row's primary path. `src/components/lists/ListDetailTools.tsx:71` swapped the mailto target literal `editors@tiered.app` → `editors@tiered.tv`; the `encodeURIComponent('Suggest entry: …')` subject + the `<a>` rendering are unchanged. Both colocated tests updated in lockstep per the #270/#271 closure pattern: `ListDetailTools.test.tsx`'s `suggest is a mailto link with subject including the theme title` case now asserts `/^mailto:editors@tiered\.tv/` (positive) AND `not.toMatch(/tiered\.app/)` (negative, bidirectional regression pin); `ListDetailHero.test.tsx`'s `shows tools row with save/share/suggest + shield` case got the same positive + negative pair. The remaining `tiered.app` references in the codebase (`src/lib/auth0/permissions.ts:12` PERMISSIONS_CLAIM + its test, `src/app/api/mod/action/__tests__/route.test.ts:90` claim comment, `src/app/(default)/mod/__tests__/page.test.tsx:24` PERMISSIONS_CLAIM constant) are all the auth-tenant namespace the row explicitly carved out — internal infrastructure, never customer-facing. The bonus content-check invariant is intentionally deferred — the colocated bidirectional pins already trip on the only customer-facing surface that was regressing; a broader scan belongs in a separate row once a second customer-facing mailto exists to test the carve-out logic against. No URL / schema field / e2e fixture row owed — the `list-suggest` testid's only e2e references in `apps/e2e/tests/themes.spec.ts` pin tool presence + aria-label, not the mailto literal. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (mailto href edit only). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + unit tests + script tests + content:check; leg2 build; leg3 e2e.

- [x] [HIGH] [anon] `/themes` lede prose still reads `12 lists we'd defend in a group chat. Some span the catalog, one lives inside one show.` while the hero stat block immediately below now reads `3 FEATURED · 9 IN THE INDEX` (the just-shipped fix for #261 at 973989e). The lede was written when the page rendered 12 lists in a single grid; the post-#253 dedupe filters the 3 featured-this-month entries out of the index grid, and the post-#261 stat fix splits the hero number into FEATURED + INDEX — but the lede sentence was not updated in either fix. A first-time reader sees `12 lists` in the lede, then `3 FEATURED · 9 IN THE INDEX` in the stats, then a chip below reads `SHOWING · ALL 9 LISTS`, then the grid renders 9 list cards. The "12" appears only once on the page and is not labeled — it reads as the catalog total but contradicts everything below. This is a real promise/delivery drift on a brand-central editorial surface (the only home for the themed-list voice) and undoes part of the just-shipped #261 fix that was supposed to bridge exactly this gap. Source: `src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx` lede literal (the number is hardcoded in the prose, not derived from `getThemeStats().total`). Fix: derive the lede number from `stats.total` instead of the hardcoded literal — change the lede to read from `${stats.total} lists we'd defend in a group chat. Some span the catalog, one lives inside one show.` so the prose stays in lockstep with whatever the catalog ships next. (The `total` value is already computed and passed to the hero per #261's split, so no new plumbing is needed.) Pin: extend the `ListsHero.test.tsx` cases #261 added — assert the lede text contains the same `total` the FEATURED+INDEX stats sum to (parse both `.lists-stat-val` numbers + the lede number, assert lede === featured + index), so a regression that re-introduces a literal divergent number fails at unit time. Also add a negative pin that the lede never contains the substring `12` while the stat row sums to a different total. Stretch: if the lede needs to scan as "x lists" only when total > 1, branch on that — the singular `1 list we'd defend...` reads fine; the current pluralized template at total=9 reads `9 lists we'd defend...` which scans naturally. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (lede prose edit only). (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-23) — issue: #263 — RESOLVED this commit: took the **pin-only** disposition. Pre-flight inspection of `src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx:20` confirmed the lede opener already derives from `stats.total` — `const opener = \`${stats.total} ${plural(stats.total, 'list', 'lists')} we'd defend in a group chat.\``; the finding's "hardcoded in the prose" diagnosis was a misread by pass-23 (the rendered surface looks hardcoded at stats.total=12, but the source already plumbs through the data). The arithmetic also reconciles cleanly: with stats.total=12 and featuredCount=3, the page renders 12 in the lede / 3 + 9 in the stat row / 3 featured cards + 9 index cards — 12 = 3 + 9, all surfaces self-consistent. So no prose change owed; the gap the finding identified is the **missing test pin** the "Pin:" section requests: no existing test asserted the lede number ↔ stat-sum lockstep, so a future regression that hardcodes the opener literal would not fail at verify time. Pinned both directions in `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListsHero.test.tsx` (alongside the pass-22 #261 "split invariant" case at line 65): (1) positive lockstep — parses both `.lists-stat-val` numbers + the lede opener number, asserts `lede === stats.total` AND `lede === featuredVal + indexVal` (closes the exact assertion the finding's "Pin:" section requests); (2) negative drift — at stats.total=7, asserts the lede reads `^7 lists\b` AND does NOT contain `\b12\s+lists?\b` (regression pin against a stale hardcoded literal — the exact substring the finding flagged). Both cases use the same `plural()` helper the source uses so a future singular branch (`1 list we'd defend`) stays in scope. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (test additions only, no editorial content change, no UI/copy/schema shift). No URL, schema field, or e2e fixture row owed; no e2e pinned the lede literal before this commit (`apps/e2e/` grep clean against `we'd defend in a group chat`). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2263 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1360 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon+authed] On `/shows/[show]/season/[slug]` the community-vote block shows the bare number `1` under the label `COMMUNITY · NET VOTE` with no sign, no unit, and no positional context. A first-time reader (anon) and a signed-in member who has not yet voted (authed) both read the same display: the literal numeral `1` floating between the `CAST VOTE` button above and the `one vote per reader. community rank updates weekly.` clause below. The label `NET VOTE` implies signed math (upvotes minus downvotes), but the rendered value has no leading `+` or `-`. A reader has three reasonable guesses: (a) `1` means one total vote has been cast, (b) `+1` net (one more "yes" than "no"), (c) the season's current community rank position (top-of-mind because the show page's tab pair is "Editor's Canon" and "Community" by *position*, not by net). The widget itself answers none of them. Source: the VotePair / VoteRowHead component on the season page. Fix: format the net value with explicit sign — `+1` when positive, `-3` when negative, `0` when even — and lean on CSS for the `+` glyph if it's awkward in the data (the component already renders the prefixed `↑` / `↓` direction glyphs on the show-page mover strip, so the syntactic precedent is in-house). A signed integer reads unambiguously as a net delta and removes the rank-vs-count confusion. Bonus: consider moving the `one vote per reader. community rank updates weekly.` rule above the figure (or breaking it into an eyebrow above + the figure below) so the meaning of the number is established before it's shown. Pin: VotePair (or VoteRowHead) colocated test cases — positive value renders with leading `+`, negative renders with leading `-`, zero renders without sign (or as `0`); update e2e fixture on `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains` to assert the rendered numeric carries an explicit sign character. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (UI-format edit only). Filed once though both passes hit it — see reader.md "Coverage is union, not intersection." (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-23) — issue: #264 — RESOLVED 0d4dd79: took the row's "explicit sign" path. Added `signedCountText` helper in `src/components/composition/VotePair.tsx` that prefixes `+` when `count > 0`, falls through to `toLocaleString()` (which already prepends `-`) when negative, and renders the bare `'0'` when zero. The count cell at `VotePair.tsx:248-255` now renders `signedCountText(state.count)` instead of `Math.round(state.count).toLocaleString()`. Pinned per the #242 closure pattern: new `explicit-sign count rendering` describe block in `VotePair.test.tsx` (9 cases — positive prefix, larger positive prefix, negative free from toLocaleString, zero bare, click flips optimistic positive, click flips optimistic negative, regression guard that an unsigned positive must never render, server-reconciled positive POST, server-reconciled negative POST). The existing 14 assertions in the file updated in lockstep (`'10'`→`'+10'`, `'6'`→`'+6'`, `'4'`→`'+4'`, `'42'`→`'+42'`, `'9'`→`'+9'`, `'3'`→`'+3'`, `'1'`→`'+1'`; `'-1'` unchanged). No URL / schema / page-family change; no e2e fixture row owed — `vote-state-pill.spec.ts` and `vote-backend.spec.ts` pin label patterns + state-cap copy + persistence, not the rendered numeral, so the e2e walk inherits the new format cleanly. Spoiler P0 intact (UI-format edit only, no per-season verdict change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2272 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1360 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] `/shows/survivor` "What changed this week" panel eyebrow reads `UPDATED THURSDAY · SENTIMENT-TAGGED`, but every rendered mover row carries only the direction glyph (↑ / ↓), the numeric delta, the season title, the "was #NN now #NN" frame, and the season number — no sentiment tag is visible anywhere in the row. The word `SENTIMENT-TAGGED` reads as a promise the surface does not deliver on. This is the same class of voice violation the bearings cue warns against ("plain sentences over clever ones") — the eyebrow earns rhetorical weight by naming a feature that doesn't render. Two ways the design can pay it off honestly: (a) remove `· SENTIMENT-TAGGED` from the eyebrow so the eyebrow reads just `UPDATED THURSDAY` (matches the existing "What changed this week" / "since last week's update" rhythm post-#242), or (b) ship the sentiment tag as a visible small chip on each row (e.g., `fans disagree`, `editor concurs`, `consensus shift`) so the eyebrow earns it. Recommended: drop the `· SENTIMENT-TAGGED` clause — sentiment tagging is meaningful product work that should land as a deliberate phase (file as a candidate in PHASE_CANDIDATES.md), not ornamental jargon in the eyebrow. Source: the mover-strip eyebrow in `ShiftsRow.tsx` or the parent show-page component that composes the panel header. Fix: locate the eyebrow literal (`Updated Thursday · sentiment-tagged` CSS-uppercased) and trim the second clause. Pin with a colocated test asserting the eyebrow no longer contains `/sentiment/i` and never re-renders the orphan separator dot, plus a negative `content-check` assertion on any future mover-strip surface. (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-23) — issue: #265 — RESOLVED 5dd7e56: took the row's recommended path (a). Two surfaces swapped: `src/components/composition/ShiftsRow.tsx:24` `Updated Thursday · sentiment-tagged` → `Updated Thursday`; `src/components/canon/CommunityMovers.tsx:22` `Top changes · sentiment-tagged` → `Top changes` (same orphan clause on the sister surface in the `?view=community` pane, scrubbed in the same sweep). Pinned per the #256 closure pattern: `ShiftsRow.test.tsx` + `CommunityMovers.test.tsx` each gained a regression-guard case asserting the eyebrow renders verbatim AND that no `/sentiment/i` or `·` separator survives — bidirectional, so a future edit cannot silently reintroduce the orphan clause without tripping the unit gate. Engineering identifiers (`RankSentiment`, `data-sentiment` on the pill, `--s-*` CSS aliases, `sentiment` props) all kept — only the user-facing eyebrow strings flipped. No URL / schema field / e2e fixture row owed (no e2e pinned the prior literal; `community-page.spec.ts` only pins the unchanged `Thursday 9pm ET` cadence label). Spoiler P0 intact (voice edit only). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2274 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1360 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] `/shows/survivor` carries two numbered enumerations in close vertical proximity that collide for a scanning reader: the canon/community tab pair labeled `01 Editor's Canon CURATED / 02 Community LIVE` (positioned as the show-page primary surface), then immediately below, the methodology block restarts at `01 · WHO / Who ranks? / ... / 02 · HOW / How do we weigh it? / ... / 03 · WHEN`. A reader scanning vertically reads `01 Editor's Canon` then `01 · WHO` — two `01`s separated by only a few hundred pixels, with no visible parent grouping that explains they belong to different lists. The visual register difference (the tab pair uses lockup labels, the methodology block uses an eyebrow `·` separator) is too subtle to make the structural separation obvious on first read. Two clean fixes: (a) drop the `01 / 02` numbering on the canon/community tab pair (use the labels `Editor's Canon / Community` directly — the position prefixes don't carry navigation value when there are only two items), or (b) change the methodology block to a non-numeric scheme (`WHO · HOW · WHEN` without the digit prefixes, since the section's editorial work is to *name* the three lenses, not to *order* them). Recommended: (a) — drop the canon/community numbering. The tab pair already enjoys typographic weight as the page's primary navigation; the `01 / 02` prefixes add visual noise and create the collision. Locate: `CanonTabSwitch.tsx` (the `01 Editor's Canon` / `02 Community` labels). Pin with a colocated test asserting the tab labels render without the leading digit prefix and that the methodology block's `01 · WHO` is the only `01` on the page. Bonus regression guard: a content-check invariant flagging any page surface where two `01` strings render within N vertical pixels of each other (over-engineered for now; revisit if other surfaces grow numbered duals). (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-23) — issue: #266 — RESOLVED this commit: took the row's recommended path (a). `src/components/canon/CanonTabSwitch.tsx` dropped the `marker` field from the `TABS` array entries and removed the `<span className="cp-tab-marker">` span from each rendered tab button — the canon/community pair now reads serif `Editor's Canon` (curated cap) / `Community` (live cap) with no leading digit prefix. The methodology block under `<CanonMethodology>` keeps its `01 · WHO / 02 · HOW / 03 · WHEN` numbering, so on the canon pane `01 · WHO` is now the only `01` rendered — the visual collision is resolved without flattening the methodology block's editorial ordering. `src/styles/canon.css` dropped the orphaned `.cp-tab-marker` rules (the base style + the `.cp-tab.on .cp-tab-marker` selected-state color) — kept only for the now-removed span. Pinned per the #256/#265 closure pattern: `CanonTabSwitch.test.tsx` gained a new case asserting positive `cp-tab-name` text per tab (verbatim `Editor's Canon` / `Community`) AND three negative pins per tab — no `cp-tab-marker` span in the DOM, and the tab button text does not begin with `0?1` or `0?2` — so a regression that reintroduces either the marker span or the leading digit prefix fails at unit time. The pass-16 (#215) aria-label test stays in place (the `aria-label` attribute is unchanged — `Editor's Canon — the curated ranking` / `Community — the live ranking`), and the pass-8 (#158) `.cp-tab-cap` curated/live span pin stays in place (the cap is the on-page differentiator the markers never carried). The header comment in `CanonTabSwitch.tsx` documents the divergence from `design/tiered.tv · Survivor.html` — the design file shows the markers, but the collision with the directly-below methodology block (also in the design file) was the rejected read; the production resolution drops the lower-value half. No URL / schema / page-family change. e2e fixtures unchanged — `canon-tabs` / `canon-tab-canon` / `canon-tab-community` testids are the only stable anchors the suite uses (verified: `show-home.spec.ts:211/216`, `community-page.spec.ts:48`, `canon-page.spec.ts:72`, `page-reads.ts:77`). Spoiler P0 intact (typography edit only, no per-season verdict change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2275 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1360 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [authed] Mobile chrome at 375px renders the full nav row inline — `tiered.tv / Shows / Lists / About / ⌕ Search / @e2e / Sign out` — with no hamburger collapse and `Sign out` exposed as a top-bar text link on every authed page. The text technically fits within the 375px viewport (scrollWidth === innerWidth, no horizontal scroll, mechanical reflow gate stays clean), but it puts a one-tap-away destructive action immediately beside the user handle on a small touch target, on every page. A user mis-tapping `@e2e` (intending to land on their own profile) and hitting `Sign out` instead is a real and recoverable-but-annoying error class — they re-auth via magic link, which takes a minute and may interrupt a vote or comment in flight. Two reasonable mobile UX fixes: (a) move `Sign out` behind the `@e2e` handle (the handle becomes a tap-to-reveal small menu with `Your record` and `Sign out` as the two items), so the top bar holds nav links only and signing out requires a second deliberate tap; or (b) collapse all secondary chrome behind a hamburger menu at < 480px breakpoint, which is the broader pattern but a bigger styling shift. Recommended: (a) — scoped to the user-island, no broader chrome refactor. The desktop chrome can keep `@e2e / Sign out` inline because the touch target is larger and the visual hierarchy is clearer. Locate: the auth-island component the phase-36 work hydrated (header right-side user controls). Pin: e2e assertion at 375px that `Sign out` is not rendered as a direct top-bar text link when the auth state is `authed` (it should be inside an expanded panel on tap of the handle). Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (chrome composition only). (URL: / + every authed page, viewport: mobile, source: critique-pass-23) — issue #268 — RESOLVED 5435b96: took the row's recommended path (a). `src/components/chrome/HeaderView.tsx` gained a mobile account-menu disclosure scoped to the authed branch — desktop (>720px) keeps the inline `@handle / Sign out` pair (the existing `site-header-user-link` + `site-header-signout-link` testIDs unchanged so all existing colocated + e2e specs pass); mobile (≤720px) collapses the pair behind a tap-to-reveal panel whose trigger is the handle (a `<button>` with `aria-haspopup="menu"`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, and an `aria-label="Account menu for @<handle>"`) and whose items are `Your record` (→ `/u/<handle>`) and `Sign out` (→ `/auth/logout?returnTo=...`, the same href the desktop pair carries). The menu pins to bottom-right of the trigger, closes on Escape (returning focus to the trigger), outside pointer (mousedown/touchstart on document outside both refs), and item selection (each `<Link>`'s `onClick` flips state). Both DOM variants live in the markup; `src/styles/chrome.css` swaps `display:none` at the existing 720px breakpoint (matches the precedent set by the pass-7 nav-row reflow + phase-29 cmd+K kbd hint) — the desktop pair stays in DOM for getAttribute reads (the `user-profile.spec.ts` 375px reflow test discovers `/u/<handle>` from the link's `href` before resizing the viewport, so hidden-not-removed preserves it). Pinned per the row's "Pin:" section + the #265/#266 closure pattern: `HeaderView.test.tsx` gained a `mobile account menu (≤720px disclosure)` describe block — 8 cases covering trigger render alongside the desktop pair, open-on-click w/ verbatim item text + hrefs + role/aria wiring + `aria-controls` ↔ menu `id` lockstep, close-on-second-click, Escape + outside-pointer dismissal, item-selection close, "does not close when pointer lands inside the menu" (regression guard against a menu-item click being eaten before navigation), and "trigger absent when signed out" (regression guard against the disclosure leaking into the signed-out branch). `apps/e2e/tests/authed-example.spec.ts` gained the row's exact 375px assertion — at mobile viewport on the authed `/` walk: desktop pair `toBeHidden()` (still in DOM, not user-visible), trigger `toBeVisible()` with `aria-expanded='false'`, menu has zero count pre-click, post-click menu surfaces `Your record` + `Sign out` with the right hrefs. The e2e walk runs against the same Playwright chromium the rest of the suite uses, so the CSS swap is exercised end-to-end. The header's existing phase-36 `/api/auth/me` hydration loop is unchanged — the menu disclosure layers on top. No URL / schema field / page-family / data shape / sitemap change owed. Spoiler P0 intact (chrome composition only, no per-user/per-show/per-season content surface change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2294 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e (8.1m).

- [x] [LOW] [authed] On `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains` the authed comment-thread empty state reads `No comments yet. Weigh in on the season itself.` (rendered below the compose box for a signed-in user who has just opened the page and no comments have been published yet). The qualifier `on the season itself` reads as if there is somewhere else one could weigh in on something other than the season (the show? the canon slot? the franchise?). On a season page where the entire thread is scoped to that season, `itself` does not earn its place — it reads slightly arch, in the same voice-violation class as the pass-23 `SENTIMENT-TAGGED` finding ("plain sentences over clever ones"). Two clean trims: (a) `No comments yet. Be the first to weigh in.` (positive CTA, peer voice), or (b) `No comments yet. Open the thread.` (action-oriented, terse). Recommended: (a) — the "be the first to" frame echoes how the authed user is already invited to participate elsewhere on the page (the CAST VOTE button reads as the same kind of invitation), and matches the welcoming-peer register the bearings voice cue names. Locate: `CommentThreadLive.tsx` or the comment-thread empty-state composition that renders alongside `CommentInput` in the authed branch. Pin: colocated test asserting the empty-state text contains `Be the first to weigh in` and does not contain `/itself/i` (regression pin against the awkward qualifier). Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (copy edit only). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-23) — issue: #279 — RESOLVED e3e6b81: took the row's recommended path (a). `src/components/composition/CommentThread.tsx:50` empty-state literal swapped from `No comments yet. Weigh in on the season itself.` → `No comments yet. Be the first to weigh in.` — positive CTA in peer voice, echoing how the authed viewer is already invited to participate elsewhere on the page (the CAST VOTE button reads as the same kind of invitation). The composer placeholder (`Add a thought · no spoilers, please.`), the sign-in stub, and the rubric continue to carry the no-spoilers policy unchanged. Pin per the row's spec: colocated `src/components/composition/__tests__/CommentThread.test.tsx` exact-string assertion flipped to the new literal in lockstep, AND a new third case (`empty-state copy avoids the arch "itself" qualifier (critique pass-23 regression pin)`) adds a negative `/itself/i` regex pin so a regression back to the awkward qualifier fails at unit time. The pre-existing soft-spoiler-shape negative-pin case (`the result`/`the winner`/`the twist`/`the elimination`) stays — the two pins now guard the empty-state surface against both the spoiler-leak class and the voice-arch class. `CommentThread.test.tsx` grows 4 → 5 cases. No URL, schema field, prop, or page family change. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (copy edit only). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2300 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e.

- [x] [LOW] [authed] On `/u/[handle]` self-view the zeroed stat row renders in the order `0 COMMENTS / 0 SEASONS VOTED / 0 SHOWS FOLLOWED` — comments lead, votes are second. tiered.tv's brand mechanic is one-vote-per-season (the home Community Rank pane, every season page's vote prompt, and the empty-state CTA on this same page all foreground voting), so leading the stat row with COMMENTS misorders the participation hierarchy on the member's own record page. The empty-state line directly below the stat row reads `Nothing on the public record yet. Vote on a season and it will land here. / Start with Survivor →` — voting-first framing in the prose, comments-first in the stats two paragraphs apart. The contradiction is small but visible reading top-down. Suggested fix: reorder the three stat cells in `src/components/profile/ProfileStats.tsx` (or wherever the row is composed) to `SEASONS VOTED → COMMENTS → SHOWS FOLLOWED`. Aligns with the page's own voting-first empty-state and the site-wide voting-first mechanic. Single-component edit; update the colocated `ProfileStats.test.tsx` order assertion in the same commit. Spoiler P0 unchanged (UI-order edit only). (URL: /u/e2e, source: critique-pass-22) — issue: #267 — RESOLVED 8bce079: took the row's recommended path. `src/components/profile/ProfileStats.tsx`'s `cells` array reordered to render in the order SEASONS VOTED → COMMENTS → SHOWS FOLLOWED, so the dl now leads with the voting-first cell that matches the brand mechanic and the page's own empty-state CTA ("Vote on a season and it will land here. / Start with Survivor →"). A short comment above the array names the rationale (voting-first hierarchy, critique pass-22) so a future authoring pass sees why the order matters. Pinned per the closure pattern: `ProfileStats.test.tsx` gains a new case that reads the children of `[data-testid="profile-stats"]` and asserts the testid order is exactly `['profile-stat-seasons', 'profile-stat-comments', 'profile-stat-shows']` — a regression back to comments-first fails at unit time. Existing pluralization + zero-state cases hold unchanged (no prop change, no testid rename). No URL / schema / page-family change; no e2e fixture row owed — `user-profile.spec.ts:106` only asserts the seasons cell contains '1' (single-cell content, not relative order), which the new order keeps green. Spoiler P0 intact (UI-order edit only, never per-ballot detail). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2276 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1360 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [authed] `/u/[handle]` is a publicly addressable URL (the handle is public per CLAUDE.md, and the route renders for anyone), but the self-view ships unconditionally second-person copy — eyebrow reads `Your record`, empty-state reads `Nothing on the public record yet. Vote on a season and it will land here.`, CTA reads `Start with Survivor →`. There is no UX that distinguishes "owner viewing own record" from "stranger viewing it". Two read failures stack: (1) when a stranger lands at `/u/e2e` they get second-person prose that reads as if addressed to them ("Vote on a season and it will land here" — but the comment lands on e2e's record, not the stranger's), and (2) when the owner lands at their own page they get no signed-in-as confirmation, no settings/account-management affordance, no pivot to "your votes" / "threads you're in" once populated — so the self-view earns its second-person framing in the eyebrow but spends none of the surface that framing promises. Source: `src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx` already accepts a `selfView` prop (Done #238 narrowed the self-view prose; the prop exists, gating exists), but the copy strings are not gated on it for the second-person vs third-person split. Suggested fix (scoped to the achievable iterate-sized edit, not the larger structural one): gate the second-person copy on `selfView`. Owner branch reads what's there today (`Your record` / `Nothing on your record yet. Vote on a season and it will land here.`); stranger branch reads third-person — eyebrow `@{handle}'s record`, empty `@{handle} hasn't voted yet.` (or `No votes on the public record yet.`). Stretch: add a small signed-in-email confirmation under the handle in the self-view (single line, mono, low-weight). Pin: ProfileEmpty colocated tests already pin verbatim copy in both branches per #238/#247 — extend with two negative pins: stranger branch never matches `/your record/i`, self branch never matches `/the public record/i`. The "manage account" / "your votes" / "threads you're in" pivots are larger and stay a phase candidate, not an iterate fix. Spoiler P0 unchanged. (URL: /u/e2e, source: critique-pass-22) — issue: #262 — RESOLVED bf069c7: took the finding's recommended scoped path. `src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx` splits the empty-state prose on `selfView`: owner branch reads `Nothing on your record yet. Vote on a season and it will land here.` (second-person, alongside the existing CTA), stranger branch reads `No votes on the public record yet.` (third-person status, no CTA — no door to open on someone else's record). `src/components/profile/ProfileHeader.tsx` adds a stranger-branch eyebrow `@{handle}'s record` (testid `profile-stranger-eyebrow`) so the stranger view carries a positive ownership cue balanced against the owner's `Your record` (testid `profile-self-eyebrow` unchanged). Both eyebrows precede the H1 in document order so screen readers announce the ownership cue before the handle. Pinned per the #238/#247 closure pattern: `ProfileEmpty.test.tsx` swapped the stranger verbatim assertion to `No votes on the public record yet.` and the self verbatim assertion to `Nothing on your record yet. Vote on a season and it will land here.`, then added two negative pins — stranger branch never matches `/your record/i` and self branch never matches `/the public record/i` — so a regression that flipped either copy string fails at unit time. `ProfileHeader.test.tsx` gains four positive cases on the stranger eyebrow (default → testid present + `@dave's record` text + precedes the H1; `isSelfView={false}` → same; `isSelfView` → stranger testid absent; handle quoted verbatim with `someone-else`) plus one self-xor-stranger invariant via `rerender` so a regression that double-renders both eyebrows fails at unit time. Stretch (`signed-in-email confirmation` / `your-votes` / `threads-you're-in` pivots) deliberately deferred to a phase candidate per the row's own scoping. Page meta description on empty profiles (`Nothing on the public record yet.`) untouched — that's tiered-perspective third-person on an SEO surface, not viewer-addressed. e2e `user-profile.spec.ts` line 121's `profile-self-eyebrow → count 0` stranger assertion holds (new stranger eyebrow uses a distinct testid), and line 173's owner-view `Your record` assertion holds. Spoiler P0 intact (copy + eyebrow only, no per-season verdict change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2256 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1360 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] `/shows` A-tier renders Top Chef as the visual outlier among 11 A-tier tiles. Its tile body — sourced from `content/shows/top-chef.md`'s `tagline` field — reads "22 seasons of professional cooks in unfamiliar kitchens. Ranked by people who actually liked the food." (1 premise sentence + 1 wink half-sentence, ~117 chars). Every A-tier neighbor renders 2–3 sentences with a structural beat (year/network/location anchor + format-claim): Bachelorette ("21 seasons of one woman with the calendar, the keys, and the final say. The flip-the-script sibling that turned out to be its own show — warmer, sharper, and more willing to let the lead drive."), Bake Off ("15 seasons of amateur bakers in a white tent on a country estate, racing the clock through signature, technical, and showstopper. The gentlest competition on television, and somehow the most watchable."), Project Runway ("20 seasons of designers at sewing machines in a Manhattan workroom, racing a clock toward a runway show that judges everything. The format that made fashion competition a genre, still the one to beat."), The Challenge ("40 seasons of reality veterans hurling themselves off cliffs, into water, and at each other..."). This is the same density-mismatch class as pass-18 #235 (Drag Race vs Survivor in S-tier, RESOLVED a2f3571 by adding a tile-form `card_tagline` to Drag Race) — the issue has migrated from S-tier to A-tier and Top Chef is now the visual outlier. Curator decision: either lengthen Top Chef's `tagline` so the show-page hero matches A-tier density (a year/network anchor — Bravo, 2006 — plus a one-line editorial claim about the format, in the same shape as Bake Off / Project Runway), OR author a tile-form `card_tagline` for Top Chef that gives the A-tile a 2-sentence body without touching the show-page hero (mirrors the a2f3571 path for Drag Race). The CLAUDE.md `card_tagline` contract supports either path; the issue is the tile-density mismatch within the same tier, not the field itself. Suggested fix: take the show-page-hero path (lengthen `tagline`) — Top Chef's current `tagline` is shorter than is editorially natural even on the show page, so the fix improves both surfaces rather than only patching the tile. (URL: /shows, source: critique-pass-21) — issue: #252 — RESOLVED: `content-curator` rewrote `content/shows/top-chef.md`'s `tagline` from the 117-char "22 seasons of professional cooks in unfamiliar kitchens. Ranked by people who actually liked the food." (1 premise + 1 wink half-sentence) to a 38-word A-tier-density rewrite: "22 seasons of professional cooks in unfamiliar kitchens, racing Quickfires and Eliminations toward the dining-room reckoning of Restaurant Wars. The format that took food seriously first, and still sets the bar every culinary competition gets measured against." Sentence one borrows Bake Off's three-round anchor and recasts it for Top Chef's Quickfire / Elimination / Restaurant Wars trinity; sentence two lands the editorial claim in the Project Runway "still the one to beat" register. Density now matches Bachelorette / Bake Off / Project Runway / The Challenge across the A-tier row; the show-page hero copy also reads cleaner. Took the show-page-hero path (lengthen `tagline`) rather than the `card_tagline` carve-out so both surfaces improve in one stroke. Stale comment in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts:1407-1409` updated — the "Top Chef's actual closer" fixture comment now notes it's historical; the synthetic fixture string stays to pin the templated-tail regex's behavior on non-templated "Ranked by …" constructions. Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2209 unit tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1358 e2e.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] `/themes` Featured-this-month section subhead reads `EDITOR-SELECTED · REFRESHED EVERY 1ST` (sourced from `src/components/lists/ListsFeaturedRow.tsx:25` — the literal `Editor-selected · refreshed every 1st`, CSS-uppercased via `.lists-section-meta`). The fragment "every 1st" is grammatically incomplete — the natural English wants a noun ("every 1st of the month", "on the 1st", "monthly on the 1st") or a stand-alone adverb ("monthly"). Reads like a placeholder string that got truncated rather than the plain-spoken house voice ("knowledgeable peer · plain sentences over clever ones"). Adjacent meta strings on the same `/themes` page do this correctly — `ListsAllSection.tsx:29` reads `Organized by what they're admiring` (complete phrase), not `Organized by what`. Suggested fix: in `ListsFeaturedRow.tsx:25`, rewrite the literal to `Editor-selected · refreshed monthly` (shortest, voice-clean) or `Editor-selected · new on the 1st` (preserves the "1st" anchor but completes the phrase). No new test row owed if existing `ListsFeaturedRow.test.tsx` already pins the subhead string — update that assertion in the same commit. (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-21) — issue: #254 — RESOLVED this commit: took the finding's first recommended path. `src/components/lists/ListsFeaturedRow.tsx:25` swapped `Editor-selected · refreshed every 1st` → `Editor-selected · refreshed monthly` — the shortest voice-clean form that drops the incomplete "every 1st" fragment for a stand-alone adverb. Density now matches the sibling `/themes` meta strings (e.g., `ListsAllSection.tsx:29` `Organized by what they're admiring`) — every meta string on the page now reads as a complete phrase rather than a placeholder truncation. The original `ListsFeaturedRow.test.tsx` did not pin the subhead literal (it had three structural cases — null-on-empty, big-flag, no-padding), so this commit adds a new colocated case `subhead reads "Editor-selected · refreshed monthly" (regression guard for #254)` that pins both directions: positive verbatim assertion on the `.lists-section-meta` text + negative pin against `/every 1st/i` so a regression back to the truncated form fails at unit time. No URL, schema field, or e2e fixture row owed; no e2e pinned the prior literal (`apps/e2e/` grep clean). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (copy edit only). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2236 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1359 e2e.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] `/themes` filter bar renders a mode-status text element to the right of the filter pills (ALL / BY TONE / BY CRAFT / SINGLE-SHOW). When the ALL filter is active, this element reads `VIEW · ALL 12 LISTS` (sourced from `src/lib/themes-format.ts:88` — the `filterModeText()` helper returns `view · all ${counts.all} lists` for the all-filter case, CSS-uppercased via `.lists-filter-mode`). The string opens with the verb "view" which reads as an imperative CTA — a reader sees "VIEW · ALL 12 LISTS" sitting on the chip row and parses it as a clickable action ("view all 12 lists"), but it's a passive status indicator describing what the page is already showing. The mismatch makes the chip row feel like it has a dead control. Suggested fix: drop the leading verb in `filterModeText()` — change `view · all ${counts.all} lists` → `showing · all ${counts.all} lists` (or `${counts.all} lists`) and the per-category branch `view · ${counts[filter]} ${FILTER_MODE_LABELS[filter]}` → `showing · ${counts[filter]} ${FILTER_MODE_LABELS[filter]}` (or `${counts[filter]} ${FILTER_MODE_LABELS[filter]}`). The `filterModeText()` tests at `ListsFilterController.test.tsx:47` ("shows 'all 12 lists' mode text by default") will need the new string — update in the same commit. (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-21) — issue: #255 — RESOLVED 61ff81d: took the finding's recommended path (the "showing" variant, not the bare-count variant — `showing` preserves the meta-strip register the chip row had been carrying). `src/lib/themes-format.ts` `filterModeText()` swapped `view · all ${counts.all} lists` → `showing · all ${counts.all} lists` (line 88) and the per-category branch `view · ${counts[filter]} ${FILTER_MODE_LABELS[filter]}` → `showing · ${counts[filter]} ${FILTER_MODE_LABELS[filter]}` (line 90). The chip row now reads `SHOWING · ALL 12 LISTS` (and `SHOWING · 4 TONE LISTS` etc. when a category chip is active) — a status label, not a dead control. Updated the three `filterModeText` unit assertions in `src/lib/__tests__/themes-format.test.ts:100-115` to pin the new strings (all-filter case + tone + single-show category cases). Scope kept narrow per the critique row — sibling `view ·` strings in `src/components/composition/FilterBar.tsx:84` (composition-demo FilterBar — internal route, not flagged) and `src/components/canon/CanonEraToolbar.tsx:101` (canon era pill — different surface, not flagged) remain untouched. The `ListsFilterController.test.tsx:42-44,53-55` substring assertions (`.toContain('3 craft lists')`, `.toContain('all 12 lists')`) still pass — they match on the noun-phrase tail, which the rewrite preserves. Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2236 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1359 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] `/shows` S-tier presents two shows side-by-side and the editorial weight collapses because they render at different densities: Survivor's tile reads its short, focused `card_tagline` ("The format that invented itself in episode one, and is still finding new ways to ask who you really are.") while RuPaul's Drag Race renders its full two-clause `tagline` ("17 seasons of queens at a Los Angeles workroom, sewing through mini-challenges and lip-syncing for their lives on a pink-lit runway. The format that built a global drag economy and still sets the bar every other franchise chases.") because no `card_tagline` is set in `content/shows/dragrace.md`, so the home-tile / shows-tile fallback at `HomeHero.tsx:51` and the equivalent `/shows` tile path uses the longer `tagline`. The two S-tier tiles read as a one-line punchy frame next to a two-clause descriptive paragraph — same tier, asymmetric density. Curator decision: either author a tile-form `card_tagline` for Drag Race that matches Survivor's punchy single-line density (recommended — preserves the "the format that…" pattern across S-tier), OR remove Survivor's `card_tagline` so both fall back to their full `tagline`. The CLAUDE.md `card_tagline` contract supports either path; the issue is the inconsistency, not the field itself.
