Survivor's existing `card_tagline` untouched; Drag Race's full `tagline` (shown on the show page hero) untouched. Surfaces that fall back to `tagline` when `card_tagline` is absent (`HomeHero.tsx:51` + the equivalent `/shows` tile path) now read the punchy form for both S-tier shows. No e2e/schema row owed — no test pins the live `card_tagline` string at `/shows` (the existing tile-path tests assert on the resolved field, not on either show's value), and the CLAUDE.md `card_tagline` contract (frontmatter spec) already documents the field as optional. Verify gate green: leg1 typecheck + 186 unit files + 127 script + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1357 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] `/themes/[theme]` detail pages render the entries header as `<h2>The {count}, in order.</h2><span class="entries-meta">Ranked · Editor's Canon</span>` via `src/components/lists/ListEntryStack.tsx:32`. The phrase **"Editor's Canon"** is brand-load-bearing as the per-show canon name — it labels the `<button role="tab">` on `/shows/[show]` ("01 Editor's Canon — the curated ranking" from `CanonTabSwitch.tsx:21,23` after the pass-16 #215 fix), it titles the home dual-callout (`HomeDualCallout.tsx`), it appears in `RankTag` / `AppearsInList` / `SeasonInfoCard` meta as the per-show ranking source. Reusing it on a themed-list detail page (where the list is editor-ranked but NOT part of any show's Editor's Canon) bleeds the two concepts into one term — a reader arriving at `/themes/best-finales` from `/shows/survivor` reads "Ranked · Editor's Canon" as a claim that this list is part of an Editor's Canon, when it's actually a separately-curated themed list. Fix: scope the term — change the `<span class="entries-meta">` literal in `ListEntryStack.tsx:32` to `Ranked · Editor's pick` or `Editor-ranked` or just `Ranked`, and reserve "Editor's Canon" for the per-show canon. Pin with a `ListEntryStack.test.tsx` assertion on the new entries-meta text + a negative assertion that "Editor's Canon" no longer renders in that surface; the existing per-show canon tests (`CanonTabSwitch`, `RankTag`, `AppearsInList`) still hold on their unchanged surfaces. (URL: /themes/best-finales, source: critique-pass-18) — 16ea88f — issue: #236 — RESOLVED 5df0a36: took the finding's recommended path — flipped the `<span class="entries-meta">` literal in `src/components/lists/ListEntryStack.tsx:32` from `Ranked · Editor's Canon` → `Ranked · Editor's pick`, scoping the term so the themed-list surface no longer claims the brand-load-bearing per-show canon name. "Editor's Canon" now exclusively appears on the per-show surfaces (`CanonTabSwitch`, `HomeDualCallout`, `RankTag`, `AppearsInList`, `SeasonInfoCard` meta, the `/shows/[show]/season/[slug]` JSON-LD ranking name + OG eyebrow + meta), where it is the load-bearing label. Pinned with a new colocated `ListEntryStack.test.tsx` case asserting both directions: `entries-meta` text content reads `Ranked · Editor's pick` AND the rendered tree no longer contains the literal `Editor's Canon` (a regression that re-introduced the term in the themed-list surface fails). No URL, schema field, or page-family change; no e2e fixture row owed (no e2e pinned the prior literal). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2209 unit tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1357 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] Home page mobile source order at `src/components/home/HomeHero.tsx:36-117` renders `<div class="home-hero-cover">` (Currently Featured / Survivor — eyebrow + name + cover-sub + stats + Go-to-show CTA) BEFORE `<div class="home-hero-copy">` (the brand block: eyebrow `tiered.tv · est. 2026` + H1 `The seasons, ranked. no spoilers.` + brand blurb + Browse-all / How-it-works CTAs). On desktop the two sit side-by-side via grid/flex, so visual hierarchy doesn't lean on source order. On mobile (375px) they stack, and the source order makes Currently Featured / Survivor the first beat — the brand H1 sits as the second beat, below the fold for many devices. A first-time visitor's first impression is the featured show, not what tiered.tv *is*. Fix: at mobile breakpoint, reverse stack order via `flex-direction: column-reverse` on `.home-hero` (or apply `order: 1` to `.home-hero-copy` and `order: 2` to `.home-hero-cover` inside the mobile media query). Keep desktop side-by-side unchanged. Pin with an e2e at 375px asserting the H1 text-content precedes the "Currently featured" eyebrow in vertical DOM order via `boundingClientRect.top`. (URL: /, viewport: mobile, source: critique-pass-18) — 16ea88f — issue: #237 — RESOLVED 2f432f5: took the finding's recommended second path — grid-item `order:` rather than `flex-direction: column-reverse` (`.home-hero` is `display: grid` per `src/styles/screens.css:111`, not flex). Inside the `@media (max-width: 767px)` block (`screens.css:1188`), `.home-hero-copy { order: 1 }` + `.home-hero-cover { order: 2 }` swap the mobile single-column stack so the brand H1 ("The seasons, ranked. no spoilers.") leads. Moved the inter-section separator from the cover's `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line-soft)` to its `border-top` (with the old `border-bottom: 0`) so the visual seam stays between copy-above and cover-below after the reorder. Desktop side-by-side (`grid-template-columns: 1.05fr 1fr`) and the 980px intermediate breakpoint are untouched — both still render cover-then-copy in their original column order. Pinned with a new `home.spec.ts` test `mobile @ 375px: brand H1 sits above the featured-show cover` that asserts `boundingClientRect.top` of the main `h1` is strictly less than that of the `home-hero-eyebrow` testid — uses rect.top, not visual occlusion, so it survives layout shape changes that keep the contract intact. No URL, schema field, or page-family change; no `apps/e2e/src/fixtures/canonical-urls.ts` / `page-reads.ts` row owed (the path is already there). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 184 unit files / 127 script + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1359 e2e (incl. the new mobile DOM-order test).

- [x] [MED] [authed] On `/shows/[show]/season/[slug]` the comment-input affordance gives no signal of which identity the comment will publish under. The signed-in state of `CommentInput` renders the placeholder "Add a thought · no spoilers, please." with a `⏎` return-key glyph — no `as @e2e` attribution, no avatar, no caption naming the active user. The chrome header correctly reads `@e2e / Sign out` so the system knows the user; the input itself doesn't echo it. A returning user with multiple browsers or shared devices loses the signal that *this* tab is signed in as *this* handle before they hit Enter. The corresponding signed-out stub (the `<Link href="/sign-in">` "Sign in to comment" — pass-17 #220 finding's context) had this implicit (you weren't anyone), so the un-acted authed state is the surface where the gap shows. Fix: add an unobtrusive `as @{handle}` label inside the input wrapper (e.g., a small caption above the textarea or a placeholder-prefix on the textarea itself) when authed; gate on the same `authStatus === "authed"` branch the existing component uses. Pin with a colocated `CommentInput.test.tsx` case asserting the `as @e2e` text renders only when authed, and the existing un-authed test stays clean. (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-18) — 16ea88f — issue: #239 — RESOLVED 8a57e87: extended `/api/comments` route to include `handle` (derived via the existing `headerUserFromSession` so the nickname → email → sub fallback chain stays shared with the chrome auth island), wired `CommentThreadLive` to capture and forward it as a new `handle?: string | null` prop on `<CommentInput>`, and rendered an unobtrusive mono `as @{handle}` caption in two complementary surfaces: (1) the un-acted **stub** — wrapped the `⏎` glyph + new `as @{handle}` chip in a `.comment-stub-right` flex group so the caption sits to the left of the return glyph while the placeholder copy stays in its original space-between position; (2) the open-state **comment-foot** — caption to the left of the existing Cancel/Post pair (via `margin-right: auto` so the buttons keep their flex-end alignment) so the user sees the attribution at the moment they commit. The whole affordance is gated on the prop so the component's no-handle shape stays intact for legacy callers and a hypothetical handle-less session. Pinned with three colocated `CommentInput.test.tsx` cases (no-handle → no `as @` text anywhere; `handle="e2e"` → exact `as @e2e` text in both `comment-stub-as` and `comment-foot-as`; empty string treated as absent), two `CommentThreadLive.test.tsx` cases (response `handle: "e2e"` flows through to the stub caption; response `signedIn: true, handle: null` renders the stub without attribution), and four new `api/comments/route.test.ts` cases (signed-in returns `handle: "tester"`, anon returns `handle: null`, getSession-reject returns `handle: null`, sub-only session yields a non-empty derived handle — pinning that the route doesn't inline its own heuristic). No URL, schema field, e2e fixture row, or page-family change. Spoiler/P0 unchanged (handle is already public via `/u/[handle]`). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2219 unit tests + 127 script + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1358 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [authed] `/u/[handle]` self-view empty state in `ProfileEmpty.tsx` advertises three reader verbs in the copy — "Vote on a season pair, weigh in on a thread, and it will land here." — but offers a single CTA "Start with Survivor →". The two voting/thread verbs are doors; the CTA only opens one of them. After pass-16 #217 resolved the "thin profile" finding by adding the zeroed `ProfileStats` row (3 stat cells: comments / seasons voted / shows followed), the empty-state now has three doors named in the copy AND three door-shaped cells in the stat row — but still only one CTA wiring an action. The thread/list verbs read as advertised, not supported. Fix: either narrow the empty-state prose to match the single CTA ("Vote on a season pair and it will land here. / Start with Survivor →") OR add a thread/list CTA after the Survivor link (e.g., a secondary `Browse themed lists →` that surfaces `/themes`, the actual thread surfaces being season-scoped). Recommended: narrow the prose — adding two more CTAs would cost the editorial spareness the empty state earns by being short. Pin via the existing `ProfileEmpty.test.tsx` cases on the new copy. (URL: /u/e2e, source: critique-pass-18) — 16ea88f — issue: #238 — RESOLVED 51a491e: took the finding's recommended **narrow-the-prose** path. `src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx` swaps the empty-state sentence from `Nothing on the public record yet. Vote on a season pair, weigh in on a thread, and it will land here.` → `Nothing on the public record yet. Vote on a season pair and it will land here.` — the prose now names exactly the surface the single CTA opens, and the zeroed `ProfileStats` skeleton above (pass-16 #217) remains as the comprehensive activity scaffold. Two-CTA path was rejected as the finding itself recommends: a secondary `Browse themed lists →` would cost the editorial spareness the empty state earns by being short. Stranger-view (no `selfView` prop, no stat skeleton, no CTA) unchanged — still gets the same narrowed sentence; the consistent voice across both views is a feature. Pinned with three colocated `ProfileEmpty.test.tsx` updates: the canonical-copy verbatim assertion in both the default and self-view branches now shifts to the narrowed sentence, plus a new negative pin asserting the rendered text contains neither `thread` nor `weigh in` so a regression re-introducing the second door fails at unit time. No e2e fixture row owed — the populated e2e path doesn't reach the empty branch; the unit pins are the right surface. No page-side change (`src/app/(default)/u/[handle]/page.tsx` already routes through `<ProfileEmpty selfView={…}>` and its colocated page test mocks the component). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (copy edit only, no per-season verdict). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2210 unit tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1358 e2e.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] On `/shows/[show]/season/[slug]` mobile, the season hero meta reads `1 MIN READ` (the read-time estimate) immediately above an `<nav>` table-of-contents labelled `ON THIS PAGE / 6 SECTIONS`. The two are inconsistent length signals to a first-time visitor: one minute next to six section anchors reads as either undersold (the actual canonical-season reading time is longer than a minute given the body + canon entry + adjacent + appears-in blocks) or as the read-time only covering "The Take" (which would be honest but isn't disclosed). The TOC is already a length signal on its own. Fix: drop the `1 MIN READ` chip from the season hero meta (cleanest — TOC carries the signal), OR compute the estimate from the actual section body word count rather than just The Take and label it accordingly (e.g., `~3 MIN READ — full section`). Recommended: drop — the TOC is sufficient and the chip is the only place a numeric word-count estimate appears, so removing it doesn't lose any other surface its information. Locate the chip in the season-page `SeasonHero` or `SeasonMeta` component. (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, viewport: mobile, source: critique-pass-18) — 16ea88f — issue: #249 — RESOLVED this commit: took the finding's recommended **drop the chip** path. (1) `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx` — removed the `computeReadMinutes` import + the `const readMinutes = computeReadMinutes({...})` block; the `<SeasonHero>` byline now reads just `<span>Canon entry by <span class="who">tiered.tv Editors</span></span>`, dropping the orphaned dot separator + read span. (2) `src/styles/screens.css` — removed the now-unused `.hero-byline .dot` + `.hero-byline .read` rules (the `.hero-byline` byline is the only consumer of `.hero-byline .dot`; the other `.dot` selectors — `SeasonInfoCard.tsx`, `ListDetailTools.tsx`, `.shield-stack .line .dot`, `.list-tools .tool-btn.shield .dot` — are scoped to their own parents and stay). (3) Deleted `src/lib/season/read-minutes.ts` + its colocated test `src/lib/season/__tests__/read-minutes.test.ts` — the module had exactly one production caller (the page.tsx site above) and is now dead. (4) `src/components/composition/__tests__/SeasonHero.test.tsx` byline-prop fixture swapped from `7 min read` → `Canon entry by Editors` so the test no longer suggests a read-time mode on the component (the test verifies any byline child renders, not the production string). The TOC carries the length signal alone now — `<details class="toc-mobile">` summary reads `On this page / N sections` (per `SeasonTOCMobile.tsx:13-16`). Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (no editorial content shifted). No URL, schema field, or e2e fixture row owed; no e2e pinned the prior `min read` literal (`apps/e2e/` grep clean before the change), and the next critique pass re-verifies the absence per the precedent on the sibling rows (#246, #248). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2226 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1358 e2e.

- [x] [HIGH] [anon] The home page explains Community Rank as **pairwise** ("Two seasons at a time, voted by readers. Pairwise. Restless. Honest. The numbers shift each week.") in the `02 · LIVE` explainer block (likely `HomeDualCallout.tsx` or an adjacent home-explainer component), but every season-page vote widget delivers a **single-binary** "does this belong in the community top 10?" tap with `one vote per reader. community rank recomputes weekly.` This is a real promise/delivery mismatch on a brand-central product claim — the home explainer is the primary place a first-time visitor learns *how* tiered.tv ranks community votes, and the actual interaction on every season page contradicts the explanation. A reader who reads the home then clicks through to vote expects an A/B pair-up and gets a thumbs-up instead; a reader who votes first and then reads the home explainer concludes the explainer is wrong. Phase 11's `votes` table + `cast_vote()` RPC and phase 35's `compute_weighted_rank` are the canonical schema — the live mechanic is single-binary up-down, not A/B pairwise. Either ship pairwise voting on season pages (phase-level work — file a candidate), OR rewrite the home explainer to describe the actual single-binary mechanic that v1 ships. Recommended: rewrite the home blurb (single-line content edit) — pairwise voting is a bigger system shift that should land as a deliberate phase, not as a silent contract fulfilment. New copy direction: "One reader, one vote per season. Does it belong in the community top 10? The numbers shift each week." Pin with a unit assertion on the home explainer copy + a content-check rule ensuring the home Community Rank blurb does not contain the word "pairwise" while the season widget remains single-binary. (URL: / + /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-19) — issue #240 — RESOLVED at 5c07076: `src/components/home/HomeDualCallout.tsx:17-19` rewrote the `02 · LIVE` blurb to match the live single-binary mechanic — "One reader, one vote per season — does it belong in the community top 10? Plain. Restless. Honest. The numbers shift each week. Every voter has watched the season end to end." The new copy preserves the original three-staccato-qualifier rhythm ("Pairwise. Restless. Honest." → "Plain. Restless. Honest."), preserves the "every voter has watched it" assurance (singular "season" replaces the now-wrong plural "both seasons"), and drops every clause that promised pairwise (`Two seasons at a time` + `Pairwise` + `both seasons`). Pairwise voting stays a deliberate-phase choice rather than a silent contract change on the home page. Pin: the colocated test (`src/components/home/__tests__/HomeDualCallout.test.tsx`) gains a `describes the single-binary community vote mechanic, not pairwise` case that asserts the live cell (a) names the mechanic — `/one vote per season/i` AND `/community top 10/i` — and (b) does NOT contain `/pairwise/i`, `/two seasons at a time/i`, or `/both seasons/i`. Five assertions in one test pin both directions: a regression back to pairwise copy fails, and dropping the new copy entirely also fails. No e2e fixture pinned the prior literal (grep across `src/` + `apps/e2e/` returned only the source file before the change). The "content-check rule" the row also suggests is unnecessary — `HomeDualCallout` is a React component (no `content/` MDX), so the colocated component test is the right pin and `scripts/content-check.ts` stays focused on content-tree invariants. Spoiler/P0 unchanged. Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 173 unit test files (incl. new HomeDualCallout case) + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 static pages), leg3 1358 e2e.

- [x] [HIGH] [anon] On `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains` the season hero's watch-order chip reads `Watch order — start here, no prerequisites`. Heroes vs. Villains is Survivor Season 20 — an all-returnees season — and the same page's body argument is that recognition does half the work ("Twenty returnees split into two tribes by reputation... recognizable players placed opposite each other in a way that lets old grudges and old alliances do half the work."). Telling a first-time viewer "start here, no prerequisites" on a returnees season is editorially false and would actively misguide them — they'd lose the inter-season recognition the season is built on. The bug is the chip being either hardcoded as "start here, no prerequisites" across all seasons or auto-derived from season number without respecting returnee/standalone format. Locate the chip: likely in `SeasonHero.tsx` / `SeasonMeta.tsx` or a `season-watch-order` derivation. Fix: read the season's frontmatter `format` (or equivalent — `cast_size`/`format_caption` likely already encode this) and gate the chip text on returnee-vs-original — returnee seasons should read something like `Watch order — better after a couple of original seasons` or `Watch order — relies on prior-season recognition`; only legitimately standalone seasons (Borneo, premiere seasons of each show) get `start here, no prerequisites`. Pin with colocated `SeasonHero.test.tsx` cases on both the returnee branch (asserts the chip does NOT read "no prerequisites") and the standalone branch (asserts the original copy still holds), plus a content-check invariant flagging any all-returnees season whose computed chip resolves to the standalone copy. Spoiler/P0 unchanged (no winner/elimination/outcome named — the chip just labels watch-order). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-19) — issue #241 — RESOLVED f739f96: derived the watch-order shield line from the season's frontmatter via new `src/lib/season/watch-order.ts` helper (`seasonWatchOrderLine()`). Three branches: (1) returnee-flavored → `Watch order — relies on prior-season recognition` (detected by scanning `format_changes` + `format_summary` + `format_caption` + `eyebrow` + `lede` for tokens `returnee`/`returnees`/`all-?stars?`/`veteran`/`veterans`/`vets?[- ]`/`all-winners` — the eyebrow+lede sweep catches the rare cases where the structural fact only lives in editorial copy, e.g. The Challenge S29's "Staged returnee arrival" eyebrow); (2) premiere (`number === 1`) → preserves the original `start here, no prerequisites`; (3) mid-run original → drops the misleading "start here" qualifier for `Watch order — no prerequisites`. Season page now passes the computed `shieldLines` to `<SeasonInfoCard>`; the component's hardcoded default stays as a safety fallback for non-season call sites. Pin: 14 colocated `watch-order.test.ts` cases covering all three branches × four detection vectors (format_changes token, format_summary, format_caption, eyebrow, lede) plus two negative invariants (returnee text never names the outcome, never says "no prerequisites" or "start here"). New `<SeasonInfoCard>` case asserts the component honors a custom `shieldLines` override. Regression guard: `collectWatchOrderClassificationIssues()` in `scripts/content-check.ts` ships strict at floor 0 — if `title`/`eyebrow`/`lede` carries an explicit returnees/all-stars/veterans signal but the helper resolves to the non-returnee chip, fail content-check. First run caught two real-corpus misses (The Challenge S12 + S29) and prompted the helper's eyebrow/lede sweep + `vets?` (singular Vet- abbreviation) fix. Spoiler P0 unchanged. Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 188 unit files / 2238 unit tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1358 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] On `/shows/survivor` the "What changed this week" module describes each weekly canon shift as `Slid 36 spots since the last weekly recompute. / Slid 9 spots since the last weekly recompute. / Slid 7 spots since the last weekly recompute. / Climbed 5 spots since the last weekly recompute.` — the word **recompute** is engineering vocabulary, not editorial. tiered.tv's voice from `plan/bearings.md` is "knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken, never pretentious. Plain sentences over clever ones." A peer would say "since last week's update" or "this week" — not "the last weekly recompute". The slip repeats four times in the visible mover-strip on desktop and twice on mobile, so the procedural word is loud enough to land. The footer of the season-page vote block has the same pattern: `community rank recomputes weekly.` Fix: replace `recompute` → `update` in both surfaces (the mover-strip caption + the vote-block footer), OR drop the trailing `since the last weekly recompute` clause entirely from the mover-strip captions since the eyebrow already reads "What changed **this week**" and the time stamp is doubled. Recommended: replace with `update` (single-word search-replace; preserves the implicit "this week" cue). Locate: `ShiftsRow.tsx` / `pickMovers` caller for the mover-strip; the season-page vote-block footer in `VotePair.tsx` or `VoteRowHead.tsx`. Pin both with colocated cases asserting the captions no longer contain "recompute". (URL: /shows/survivor + /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-19) — issue: #242 — RESOLVED 17ef819: swapped `recompute` → `update` at `src/lib/community/live.ts:70-71` (moverNote) and `src/components/composition/SeasonInfoCard.tsx:50` (voteHelp default). Updated existing assertions in `live.test.ts` + `ShiftCard.test.tsx` + `SeasonInfoCard.test.tsx`; added two new colocated pins (`moverNote` never emits `/recompute/i` across climb/slide × singular/plural × any delta; `SeasonInfoCard` default voteHelp never contains `/recompute/i`) so the editorial-voice swap is enforced at the test gate, not just at this commit. Verify gate green: leg1 typecheck + 188 unit files + 127 script + content:check, leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1358 e2e. No source/data drift required; the internal `/api/internal/recompute` route + its colocated tests keep the technical term where it belongs (operator-facing infra), the user-facing copy is now in editorial voice.

- [x] [MED] [anon] On the home page the show-grid tile for The Amazing Race reads `38 seasons. Eleven teams, one route marker at a time.` — words for the team count. Every sibling tile in the same grid uses **numerals**: Bachelor `28 seasons. One rose at a time.`, Bachelorette `21 seasons. She holds the roses now.`, Survivor featured `50 seasons. One torch at a time.`, etc. The numerals/spelled-out swap is the only inconsistency in the row, and a fresh reader's eye snags on it as a one-off voice slip. The CLAUDE.md show-identity contract does not mandate either form, but the visible siblings all settled on numerals, so The Amazing Race is the outlier. Fix: in `content/shows/amazing-race.md`, change the home blurb's `Eleven teams` → `11 teams` so the home grid reads in one register. Single-word content edit; no schema, no e2e fixture row owed. Curator pass. (URL: /, source: critique-pass-19) — issue: #243 — RESOLVED this commit: edited `content/shows/amazing-race.md:10` `Eleven teams` → `11 teams`; the `ShowTile` (`src/components/home/ShowTile.tsx:48`) consumes `show.blurb` directly, so the home grid now reads in one numeric register across every tile. Scope kept narrow to the home-grid surface per the finding — long-form prose references to "Eleven teams" inside season bodies, canon rationales, and themed-list entries (`content/themes/*`, `content/shows/amazing-race/canon.md`, season files) are editorially natural English in paragraph context and stay untouched.

- [x] [MED] [authed] On `/shows/[show]/season/[slug]` the signed-in view of the comment thread suppresses the empty-state confirmation line. Anon visitors see three clear beats — `The thread` heading, the sign-in stub, then `No comments yet. Weigh in on the season itself.` — so they know the surface, the action, and the current state. Authed visitors see only `The thread / Add a thought · no spoilers, please. / as @e2e / ⏎` and then the page ends — no signal whether the thread is genuinely empty, still loading, suppressed by the spoiler shield, or failed to fetch. The composer placeholder is a prompt to act, not a state confirmation, so a returning member with a quiet thread or a slow connection can't disambiguate "no comments yet" from "load failed silently". Source: `src/components/composition/CommentThreadLive.tsx:113` passes `hideEmpty={state.signedIn}` to `<CommentThread>`, which suppresses the `comment-thread-empty` line for any signed-in viewer regardless of count. The colocating comment in `CommentThread.tsx:24-28` justifies the suppression by claiming the composer placeholder doubles the message — but `Add a thought · no spoilers, please.` is a prompt to act, not a confirmation that the list is empty. Fix: drop `hideEmpty={state.signedIn}` (or narrow it to only when `count > 0`) so the `No comments yet. Weigh in on the season itself.` line renders for the authed-zero-comments case the same way it does for the anon case. The composer's policy nudge and the empty-state confirmation are doing different jobs and should both render. Pin with a new colocated case in `CommentThreadLive.test.tsx` asserting the empty-state testid is present when `signedIn=true` and `count=0`. (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-19) — issue: #244 — RESOLVED 33cc695: dropped `hideEmpty={state.signedIn}` from `CommentThreadLive.tsx`; deleted the `hideEmpty` prop from `CommentThread.tsx` entirely (no other caller used it) so the `comment-thread-empty` line renders for any zero-children case (anon or authed). Flipped existing colocated case in `CommentThreadLive.test.tsx` to assert `comment-thread-empty` IS present at `signedIn=true, count=0`; removed the three `CommentThread.test.tsx` cases that pinned the prop's old contract. Verify gate green: leg1 188 unit files / 2236 tests + 127 script tests + content:check, leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1358 e2e.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] The `/themes` hub aggregate metric reads `INDEX LAST REVISED / 2026` — just the year, no month. The sibling hub `/shows` uses the same meta-stats column but reads `LAST REVISION / May 2026` — year plus month. Density and specificity don't match across the two IA hubs, and the year-only stamp on `/themes` is less honest about cadence than the year+month stamp on `/shows` (it suggests the lists were revised "sometime in 2026" when the actual revision is much fresher). Fix: render the lists-index `last_revision` as `<Month> 2026` on `/themes` to match the `/shows` shape — likely a content edit in the index frontmatter or a one-line stamp-format change in the page component. Locate: `src/app/(default)/themes/page.tsx` meta-stats block, or wherever the `INDEX LAST REVISED` literal is rendered. Pin with a colocated assertion that the rendered text matches a `^[A-Z][a-z]+ \d{4}$` (month-year) shape, not a bare-year shape. (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-19) — issue: #245 — RESOLVED ca7dc7a: `src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx` swapped `formatRevisedYear` → `canonRevisedLabelFromIso` (the same `Month YYYY` formatter the `/shows` hero, home hero, and per-show heroes already share for chrome-wide consistency), so the `/themes` index-revised stat now reads `May 2026` instead of `2026`. The drop-back-to-empty-string fallback (`?? ''`) preserves the prior empty-input behavior; `lastIndexRevision` is an ISO date that `getThemeStats` derives from the freshest theme's `last_revised`. Updated the existing `<ListsHero>` colocated case to assert `May 2026` and added a new case pinning the calendar shape — value matches `/^[A-Z][a-z]+ \d{4}$/` and never a bare-year — so a regression back to year-only fails verify, not the next reader pass. No e2e fixture row owed: `themes.spec.ts:15-17` already asserts `/\d{4}/` (any 4-digit year), which holds for both `2026` and `May 2026`. No URL/schema/page-family change. Verify gate green: leg1 188 unit files / 2237 tests + 127 script + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1358 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] On `/themes/best-finales` the RuPaul's Drag Race S6 entry headline reads "A closing run that pays off **two fully-formed queens at full height.**" — in the same blurb that frames the finale as "a lip-sync stretch with genuine stakes." Naming "two" of the finalists for the closing lip-sync narrows the finale's spotlight in a way no other entry on the list does — every sibling keeps the cohort vague ("the final tribal lands like a summary statement", "the closing run", "the Round Table tightening before the last vote"). The Drag Race finale narrows to a top 3, not a top 2 — claiming "two fully-formed queens" carries the moment lands on two players, which is a placement-narrowing leak class adjacent to the spoiler-discipline P0 promise the rest of the list holds. Fix: rewrite the headline to keep the editorial admiration without the cohort count — e.g. "A closing run that pays off a finale built on real artistry." — and let the body's "lip-sync stretch with genuine stakes" carry the climactic framing. Curator-only edit in `content/themes/best-finales.md` (no schema/page/e2e change owed; no test pins the literal). Pin via the same `scripts/content-check.ts` channel that already enforces themed-list discipline if a structural rule emerges; otherwise the next critique pass re-verifies the absence. (URL: /themes/best-finales, source: critique-pass-20) — issue: #246 — RESOLVED this commit: edited `content/themes/best-finales.md:50` `entries[5].title` from "A closing run that pays off two fully-formed queens at full height." → "A closing run that pays off a finale built on real artistry." Scope kept curator-only per the finding — no schema/page/e2e/test change owed, the body's "lip-sync stretch with genuine stakes" already carries the climactic framing, and `themes.spec.ts` only asserts shape/rank-order/`Month YYYY` cadence (no literal pin to remove). The blurb body (`entries[5].blurb`) stays unchanged: it speaks to the season-level artistry without naming a cohort. No structural invariant landed — the leak class is editorial judgement, not a derivable rule. The verify gate held green: leg1 188 unit files / 2237 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1358 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] On `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains` the "WHAT TO WATCH FOR · LATE · THIRD ACT" card body reads `The cold-open of the final eight is doing real ethnographic labor — staging, blocking, who's eating where. Don't blink.` Naming "the final eight" tells a viewer three episodes into the season that the cast contracts to exactly 8 players by the late phase — a structural attrition signal the brand's "scroll without losing the season you're three episodes into" promise rules out. This is the same vector the issue #152 / 08c121d fix closed when it swapped episode numbers for phase labels (Opener/Early/Mid/Late) on the four `watch_list` cards; the cards now carry phase labels in the eyebrow but the body still slips a cast-count milestone in the LATE card. Fix: replace "the final eight" with a non-numeric phase reference — e.g. "The cold-open of the late-game stretch is doing real ethnographic labor — staging, blocking, who's eating where. Don't blink." — matching the OPENER / EARLY / MID / LATE register the other three cards already carry in their bodies. Single-string content edit in `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-villains.md` (the `watch_list[3].body` field). Sweep the other shows' `watch_list` bodies in the same tick for any "final N" / "final M" cast-count leaks; the rule should be: phase labels in the eyebrow AND in the body. Spoiler discipline P0-adjacent (no winner/eliminee named — the leak is structural attrition pace, not outcome). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-20) — issue: #248 — RESOLVED this commit: took the recommended single-string content path on the originating row and ran the explicit cross-show sweep the finding called for. (1) `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-vs-villains.md:33` `watch_list[3].body` swapped `The cold-open of the final eight…` → `The cold-open of the late-game stretch…` — a non-numeric phase reference matching the OPENER / EARLY / MID / LATE register the other three cards already carry. (2) Sweep of all 161 season files with `watch_list:` against `\b(final|last|jury of) (one|two|three|four|five|six|seven|eight|nine|ten|eleven|twelve|[0-9]+)\b` surfaced one other hit — `content/shows/survivor/seasons/32-kaoh-rong.md:33` `watch_list[3].body` — same class of leak; swapped `The final four cycle is one of the densest…` → `The final-stretch cycle is one of the densest…` (the "final stretch" register matches the season's existing `episode_label: "Ep 12 · final stretch"`, so the body now mirrors the eyebrow's phase framing). No other cast-count leaks found across the corpus. Curator-only edits — no schema/page/component/test change owed; no source file pinned the prior literal (`apps/e2e/` + `src/**` grep clean for both phrases), and the content layer's published `body` is rendered verbatim. Spoiler discipline P0-adjacent unchanged — both replacements drop the cast-count milestone without naming outcome. The leak class is editorial judgement (an authored body slipping a numeric attrition milestone); the rule the finding implies ("phase labels in the eyebrow AND in the body") is not yet derivable into a `scripts/content-check.ts` invariant without false positives against legitimate uses of those words elsewhere in editorial copy, so the next critique pass re-verifies the absence per the precedent on the sibling row #246.

- [x] [MED] [authed] On `/u/[handle]` self-view (member's own profile, empty state), the prose still reads `Nothing on the public record yet. Vote on a season **pair** and it will land here.` (`src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx` per issue #238's RESOLVED note). But the live vote mechanic on `/shows/<show>/season/<slug>` is single-binary — `Does this belong in the community top 10?` with `one vote per reader. community rank updates weekly.` — not pairwise. Pass-19 #240 scrubbed the pairwise framing from the home explainer (`HomeDualCallout.tsx` "One reader, one vote per season…") but missed this profile-empty surface, which is the one place that explicitly tells a member **how** to populate their profile. A returning member reads "vote on a season pair", clicks through to a season page, finds a single up/down tap, and lands on the same promise/delivery gap that #240 closed at the home level. Same class, different surface. Fix: drop the word "pair" — `Vote on a season pair and it will land here.` → `Vote on a season and it will land here.` Curator-style single-word edit in `src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx`. Pin: update the existing `ProfileEmpty.test.tsx` canonical-copy assertion (the verbatim-string check that pass-18 #238 added when it narrowed the two-door prose to one door) so the new text is locked, plus a negative pin asserting the rendered text does not contain `/pair/i` so a regression back to pairwise framing fails at unit time. Stranger view (same component, no `selfView` prop) inherits the same fix — the consistent voice across views remains. Spoiler P0 unchanged (copy edit only). (URL: /u/e2e, source: critique-pass-20) — issue: #247 — RESOLVED 8f65770: took the finding's recommended single-word path. `src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx:36-38` swapped `Vote on a season pair and it will land here.` → `Vote on a season and it will land here.` — the prose now matches the live single-binary mechanic the season page actually ships ("Does this belong in the community top 10? — one vote per reader."). Stranger view (no `selfView` prop) inherits the same fix; the consistent voice across both views stays a feature. Pinned with two complementary contract changes in `src/components/profile/__tests__/ProfileEmpty.test.tsx`: (a) both verbatim-string assertions (the stranger-view default-copy case + the self-view-with-CTA "keeps the canonical sentence" case) flipped to the new text so a regression back to the old copy fails at the verbatim contract; (b) new dedicated negative pin `does not promise a pairwise vote in the prose (regression guard for #247)` asserts `.textContent` does not match `/pair/i` — bidirectional with the new positive copy assertion so a future content edit can never silently reintroduce "pair" without tripping the gate, the same closure pass-19 #240 applied to `HomeDualCallout`. Dev-internal comments at `src/lib/profile/context.ts:106-111` + `src/lib/profile/__tests__/context.test.ts:148-153` (both quoting the old "vote on a season pair" prompt as the rationale for `pickFeaturedSeason`'s contract) updated to match — the helper still picks the show's #1 canon entry's season so the CTA lands on a vote affordance above the fold; only the rationale comment shifted. No URL, schema field, or e2e fixture row owed — no e2e pinned the prior literal (`apps/e2e/` grep clean) and the populated-profile e2e path doesn't reach the empty branch, so the unit pin is the right surface. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (copy edit only). Verify gate green: leg1 188 unit files / 2240 tests (incl. the new negative pin) + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1358 e2e.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] On `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains` Section 03 "WHERE IT SITS IN THE CANON" body reads `Slot #02 of 50 in the Survivor Editor's Canon. The neighbors below frame what we ranked above and below it.` The sentence collides two senses of "below" — "**neighbors below**" (referring to the on-page adjacent strip immediately under this heading) and "**above and below it**" (referring to canon positions). A first-time reader parses "neighbors below" as lower-ranked, then immediately sees `← #01 IN CANON Cagayan` on the left of the adjacent strip — which is canon-above, contradicting the parse. Brand voice asks for plain sentences over clever ones; this sentence asks the reader to disambiguate before parsing. Fix: drop the "below" overload by losing the positional reference — `Slot #02 of 50 in the Survivor Editor's Canon. The seasons on either side show what we ranked it against.` Single-string edit in the section-03 helper (likely `src/lib/season/canon-context.ts` or wherever the "neighbors below frame…" template is constructed; grep for `neighbors below` should find it). Pin with a colocated unit test asserting the new copy AND a negative pin against the literal `neighbors below`. Spoiler-safe (no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-20) — issue: #251 — RESOLVED e6ddf5a: took the finding's recommended path. The helper lived at `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:240` (not `src/lib/season/canon-context.ts` — the brief's location guess; grep for `neighbors below` found the actual site, a single occurrence). Replaced `The neighbors below frame what we ranked above and below it.` → `The seasons on either side show what we ranked it against.` — drops the "below" overload and the positional reference both, so the sentence reads cleanly against either neighbor regardless of canon-above-or-below. Exported `whereItSitsCopy` from the page module to enable colocated unit pinning rather than leaning on the e2e walk for a copy regression. Added a five-case suite in the existing `__tests__/page.test.tsx`: (a) renders the ranked-slot framing with the new copy verbatim; (b) zero-pads single-digit canon ranks per the `pad2` contract; (c) a negative pin across five branches (`canonRank=1`, `=2`, `=50`, `null`, `total=1`) asserts the rendered string contains neither `neighbors below` nor `above and below` — the bidirectional gate the iterate brief asks for, so a future edit re-introducing the overload trips at unit time; (d) draft-in-progress fallback when `canonRank == null`; (e) sole-entry fallback when `canonTotal === 1`. The two fallback branches were already correct (didn't carry the overload) but pinning them locks the contract — copy now never drifts back to the overloaded phrasing on any branch. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (copy edit only, no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). Verify gate green: leg1 187 unit files / 2234 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1358 e2e.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] On `/themes` three lists (Comebacks worth the swing, Finales that stuck the landing, Premieres that earned it) appear twice on the same page — once in the "Featured this month" rail and again in the "All lists" grid under their BY TONE / BY CRAFT category — with identical blurbs verbatim both times. On a 12-list index this is a visible 25% repetition rate; a first-time visitor scrolls past the same paragraph twice within 1000px. Fix: either (a) filter the featured-rail's lists out of the all-lists grid so each list appears exactly once on the page (cleanest — the all-lists grid becomes "the rest of the catalog"), or (b) shorten the all-lists tile copy for items already featured above (the rail keeps the full blurb, the grid drops to a one-line eyebrow). Recommended path: (a) — filter the featured set out of the all-lists grid; the featured rail is the curator's spotlight, the grid is the exhaustive index, and excluding featured from the index reads as deliberate cadence rather than missing. Locate the filter site in `src/app/(default)/themes/page.tsx` / `ListsAllSection` (the consumer that calls `getThemesByCategory()`). Pin with an e2e at `/themes` asserting no list slug appears in both `lists-featured-rail` and `lists-all-section`, and the BY TONE/CRAFT/SINGLE counts drop by however many featured lists were filtered out. (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-20) — issue: #253 — RESOLVED 1344e77: took option (a) from the row — `src/app/(default)/themes/page.tsx` now derives `byCategoryRest` (the full `getThemesByCategory()` grouping with the featured slugs filtered out) and passes that to `<ListsAllSection>` instead of the unfiltered `byCategory`. The featured-rail stays the curator's spotlight; the grid below is now the rest of the catalog. Chip counts also drop accordingly (`counts.all` is the sum of the four filtered groups, not `themes.length`), so each chip describes the grid it actually filters — `all 9 lists` instead of `all 12 lists`, with the chip-row math staying internally consistent. Today's 4-featured / 12-total catalog (`getFeaturedThemes(3)` caps at 3 → best-comeback-seasons, best-finales, best-premieres; 4th featured best-villain-editing stays in both surfaces) trims tone 7→6 and craft 4→2; single (1) and era (0) unchanged. JSON-LD `numberOfItems` still reflects the full catalog count (12) so structured-data clients see every list. E2e pin: `apps/e2e/tests/themes.spec.ts` "no list slug appears in both the featured rail and the all-lists grid" collects `data-slug` from `[data-testid=lists-featured-card]` and `[data-testid=lists-row]` and asserts an empty intersection — a future regression reintroducing duplicates trips at e2e time. Existing filter-bar / group / featured-row / chip-mirror tests stay green: chips still mirror populated categories, all-lists still renders ≥1 group, featured row still renders 0–3 cards. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (page-layout change, no per-season verdict, no canon position). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 188 unit files + 127 script + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1359 e2e (the new `/themes` overlap pin is test #1331).

- [x] [LOW] [anon] On `/themes` the hero subtitle reads `Some span the catalog, some live inside one show.` — plural "some" applied to single-show lists — but the page's own "SINGLE-SHOW TIERS" section header carries `· 1`, exactly one list (Survivor: the load-bearing seasons). "Some" reads as ≥2 in plain English; the page's own count contradicts the hero one screen later. The plural-drift class previously closed at #133 (`/themes` hero "Some span every show" vs 10/13 shows covered) recurs here on the other clause of the same hero lede. Fix: either (a) pluralize the catalog by shipping a second single-show list before the next critique pass (likely the existing `survivor-pillars` could split, or a second show's pillars list could be authored — content-curator call), or (b) singularize the hero copy — `Most span the catalog; one lives inside a single show.` (or similar) — matching the live `getThemeStats().singleCount` value. Recommended: (b) — coverage-aware copy mirrors the #98 / #129 / #130 / #133 precedent (data-derived from `getThemeStats()`, not hardcoded). Make the helper render the single-vs-plural form based on the actual count, so the lede stays honest as the catalog grows. Pin with a `ListsHero.test.tsx` case asserting the singular phrasing at single-count=1 + a plural-phrasing case at single-count=2. Spoiler-safe — hero copy only. (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-20) — issue: #250 — RESOLVED at e0e84c0: took option (b) from the row — `<ListsHero>` (`src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx:9-39`) now derives the lede's middle clause from `stats.crossCanonCount` + `stats.singleShowCount`, picking singular vs plural per clause in mixed mode. Today's 11-cross / 1-single catalog reads `Some span the catalog, one lives inside one show.` (singularizes the second clause to match the SINGLE-SHOW · 1 header one screen later). Symmetric singularization on the cross clause: `crossCanonCount === 1` → `One spans the catalog`. Cross-canon-only mode (`singleShowCount === 0`) now drops the dangling "some live inside one show" clause and reads `Some span the catalog.` alone — defends against the same plural-drift class flipping direction as the catalog grows. Single-canon branch unchanged. Refactored the lede assembly into an `opener` / `middle` / `closer` triple so the branch logic is one IIFE instead of two parallel string literals. §5a: three new `ListsHero.test.tsx` cases pin (a) singular `one lives inside one show` at single-count=1 with negative against `some live`, (b) plural form at single-count=2 with negative against `one lives`, (c) symmetric singular `One spans the catalog` at cross-count=1. Existing tests still hold — the `/Some span the catalog/` regex matches both old and new copy on cross-only and mixed-10+ paths. Spoiler discipline P0 intact — hero copy only, no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change. Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2229 unit tests + 127 script + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 pages), leg3 1358 e2e.

- [x] [LOW] [authed] `/shows/survivor` hero `tagline` and the home/tile `card_tagline` word the same idea two ways: the show-page lede reads "The **genre** that invented itself in episode one, and has spent {yearsWord} years rediscovering what it is" (`content/shows/survivor.md:11`) while the home featured cover + `/shows` tile read "The **format** that invented itself in episode one" (`:12`). Same construction, different noun for the same claim — and "genre" is the looser word (Survivor is a show/format within the reality-competition genre, not a genre itself; its `genre_tag` is literally "Reality competition"). A reader moving home → show page meets the idea twice, worded inconsistently. Fix: align the `tagline` noun to "format" (or deliberately keep both and note why) in `content/shows/survivor.md`; the `{yearsWord}` token stays untouched. Curator copy edit — no schema/e2e row owed. (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-17) — 31ca474 — issue: #225 — RESOLVED: took the finding's suggested path. `content-curator` swapped the `tagline` noun `genre` → `format` in `content/shows/survivor.md:11` (single-word edit; the rest of the sentence and the runtime `{yearsWord}` tenure token untouched), so the show-page hero lede and the home/`/shows`-tile `card_tagline` now share the accurate construction "The format that invented itself in episode one." "format" is also the correct term — Survivor is a format *within* the reality-competition genre (`genre_tag` is "Reality competition"), not a genre itself. No e2e/schema row owed: no test pins the live `tagline` string — `card_tagline`'s pins (`show-home.spec.ts:146` + the home/tile component tests) are on the unchanged field, and `content-check.test.ts:1425`'s "genre that invented itself" is a synthetic `makeShowWithRawTagline` fixture, not an assertion against the real file. Verify gate green (fast gate + build + 1350 e2e).

- [x] [LOW] [anon] `/shows/survivor` canon entries carry a community-state marker rendered as "◆ hold" ("COMMUNITY ◆ HOLD #NN"; `src/components/canon/CanonHeroEntries.tsx:33` `trendSymbol` → `'◆ hold'`, with ▲/▼ siblings for climb/slide) but no legend glosses the ◆ glyph or the hold/climb/slide vocabulary anywhere on the page. The word "hold" carries most of the meaning, so this is genuinely LOW — but a first-time visitor scanning the canon meets the diamond + state word cold, and the sentiment color (the part distinguishing climb from slide) is invisible to anyone not perceiving color, so color is the sole carrier of that distinction. Fix: add a one-line legend above the canon ranking (or a `title`/`aria` gloss on the marker) defining the three community-state markers so the glyph reads on first paint and color isn't load-bearing alone. Pin with a colocated assertion on the legend/gloss. (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-17) — 31ca474 — issue: #226 — RESOLVED this commit: took the finding's `title`/`aria` gloss path rather than a visible legend — the binding design file (`design/tiered.tv · Survivor.html:618-710`) ports the `↑ N` / `◆ hold` / `↓ N` marker verbatim with **no legend**, so adding one above the ranking would invent UI against the faithful-port visual law. Added a `trendGloss(sentiment, delta)` helper in `CanonHeroEntries.tsx` returning plain language ("Climbing N spots / Sliding N spots / Holding steady in the community ranking", singular "spot" at delta 1) and applied it as BOTH `title` (hover tooltip → sighted comprehension of the cryptic ◆/arrows) AND `aria-label` (screen-reader name → the climb/slide/hold state is spelled out, so the sentiment **color** is no longer the sole carrier of that distinction, WCAG 1.4.1). The visible text content (`↑ 3` / `◆ hold` / `↓ 2`) and `.cp-mini-trend ${sentiment}` color class are untouched — the styled lockup is preserved exactly, the existing `↓ 3` text-content assertion still holds. Pinned with a new colocated `CanonHeroEntries.test.tsx` case asserting the gloss `title` + `aria-label` for all three sentiments + the singular-noun branch (6 cases total). No source layout change; not a new URL so no e2e fixture row owed. Verify gate green: leg1 186 unit files (incl. the +1 case) + 127 script + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes), leg2 build, leg3 1350 e2e.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] The `/shows/[show]` canon/community tab control (`src/components/canon/CanonTabSwitch.tsx`) renders each tab as `<span class="cp-tab-marker">01</span><span><span class="cp-tab-name">Editor's Canon</span><span class="cp-tab-cap">curated</span></span>` — the name and the state cap are adjacent inline spans with no whitespace or separator between them, and the `<button role="tab">` carries no `aria-label`. On desktop the two read as a single run-together token (`Editor's Canoncurated`, `Communitylive`); on mobile CSS line-breaks the cap so it separates. The button's accessible name therefore computes to `01Editor's Canoncurated` / `02Communitylive`, which a screen reader announces as one mashed token. Evidence: desktop capture text `01 / Editor's CanonCURATED / 02 / CommunityLIVE` vs mobile `01 / Editor's Canon / CURATED`. Fix: give each `<button>` an explicit `aria-label` (e.g. `aria-label="Editor's Canon — the curated ranking"` / `"Community — the live ranking"`) so the accessible name is clean regardless of the visual span packing; the visible run-together is acceptable as a styled lockup but the a11y name should not be. Pin with a colocated assertion in `CanonTabSwitch.test.tsx` on the new `aria-label`. (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-16) — 987754d — issue: #215 — RESOLVED: took the finding's suggested `aria-label` path. Added a `label` field to each `TABS` entry (`"Editor's Canon — the curated ranking"` / `"Community — the live ranking"`) and wired it onto the `<button role="tab">` via `aria-label`, so the screen-reader name is clean regardless of the visual `.cp-tab-name`/`.cp-tab-cap` span packing — the styled run-together lockup is preserved untouched (visible text, `data-tab`, `data-testid`, `aria-selected`, and the `.cp-tab-cap` curated/live chips all unchanged, so the pass-8 #158 mobile-stacking contract still holds). Pinned with a new colocated `CanonTabSwitch.test.tsx` case asserting the exact `aria-label` on both tab buttons.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] The themed list `best-non-winning-runs` carries the title/tagline "Non-winning runs that defined the season" / "Seasons whose most-discussed arcs lived outside the title-taking … without needing to end up at the final outcome." It frames its members by negation — telling a reader that the standout arc in each listed season did NOT win. On the list page this is the list's established editorial premise (a deliberate phase-24 category), but the same title also renders as an "Also appears in" cross-ref on individual season pages (e.g. Heroes vs. Villains), where it becomes a soft placement-outcome leak about that specific season's most-discussed player — adjacent to the P0 no-spoilers promise. This is leak-by-inference, not by naming a winner/eliminee, hence LOW; the structural question (whether a "non-winning runs" category should exist at all) is out of scope for a copy tick and is an editorial-direction call, not an iterate fix. The actionable narrow fix: reframe the **tagline** to admire the run without asserting placement (e.g. "arcs that gave a season its shape, whatever the final tally") so the cross-ref on a season page doesn't telegraph a non-win; leave the list title as the known category name. Locate in `content/themes/best-non-winning-runs.md` (`tagline`). (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-16) — issue: #216 — RESOLVED: broadened the finding's narrow tagline-only fix after verifying the code — `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:206` builds each "Also appears in" cross-ref row with `name: t.title`, so the list **title** (not the tagline) is the field that surfaces verbatim on a season page; the finding's tagline-only scope was premised on the tagline rendering there. Reframed all three list-level fields in `content/themes/best-non-winning-runs.md` to admire the seasons as distributed-ensemble texture with zero placement assertion: title `"Non-winning runs that defined the season"` → `"Seasons that live in their loudest arcs"` (the season-cross-ref leak vector, now outcome-free); tagline's `<b>`-emphasized clause `"the runs that didn't reach the final outcome"` → `"lasting shape lives in their ensemble texture"` (one `<b>` span preserved for the detail-hero); description `"arcs lived outside the title-taking … without needing to end up at the final outcome"` → ensemble-texture framing. Also swept the one genuine placement leak among the entry blurbs — Survivor S07 Pearl Islands `"most of them don't make the final vote"` → a character-texture clause — keeping the file internally consistent (the other entries' "shape lives in X, not in one closing line/service/team" constructions describe narrative locus, not placement, and stay). Slug, category, sentiment, and the entry set unchanged; `last_revised` bumped 2026-05-28. `content:check` green (12 themes); no e2e/fixture pinned the old strings so no harness row owed. Curator-authored (content-curator), spoiler discipline P0.

- [x] [LOW] [authed] The signed-in self-view of `/u/e2e` is very thin: eyebrow `Your record`, `@e2e`, `Member since May 2026`, an empty-state sentence, and one CTA — with no zeroed activity scaffold. Capture (515 chars): `Your record / @e2e / Member since May 2026 / Nothing on the public record yet. Vote on a season pair, weigh in on a thread, and it will land here. / Start with Survivor →`. The empty-state sentence ("Nothing on the public record yet … and it will land here") already does most of the disambiguation work (it reads as "empty record," not "unbuilt page"), so this is purely additive polish — a zeroed stat row (e.g. `0 votes · 0 threads`) above the empty-state copy would give the page a scannable activity skeleton so a new authed reader sees the *shape* of what will populate, not just a sentence promising it. Locate in `src/app/(default)/u/[handle]/page.tsx` / `src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx`; the populated-profile path presumably already renders these counts, so the fix is rendering them at zero rather than suppressing the row on an empty profile. Pin with a `ProfileEmpty.test.tsx` case asserting the zeroed row renders. Verify the stranger-view (non-self) empty path stays appropriately sparse (a stranger doesn't need the owner's zeroed scaffold). (URL: /u/e2e, source: critique-pass-16) — issue: #217 — RESOLVED: reused the existing `<ProfileStats>` rather than inventing a compact `0 votes · 0 threads` mini-row — rendering it with all-zero counts inside `ProfileEmpty`'s self-view branch gives the empty owner profile the *exact* visual treatment (3-cell bordered grid) the populated profile uses, so the reader sees the true shape of what will populate (`0 comments / 0 seasons voted / 0 shows followed`), not just a sentence promising it. Gated on the existing `selfView` prop so the stranger (non-self) empty path stays sparse — no owner scaffold for a visitor. The skeleton sits above the empty-state copy (outer `gap-3`→`gap-6`, copy+CTA wrapped in an inner `gap-3` so their original tight spacing is preserved). No page-side change — the page already routes self-view-empty through `ProfileEmpty(selfView=…)`, and its colocated page test mocks `ProfileEmpty`, so it's unaffected. Pinned with 3 new `ProfileEmpty.test.tsx` cases (self-view → zeroed `profile-stats` with all cells reading 0; stat skeleton precedes the empty-state copy in the DOM; stranger view → no `profile-stats`). Not a new URL (shipped phase 10, drained phase 38), so no e2e fixture row owed; the empty self-view path is unreachable by the populated e2e user, so the unit test is the correct pin.

- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/survivor hero stat eyebrow reads `SEASONS AIRED` (`/shows/survivor` capture: `50 / SEASONS AIRED / May 2026 / CANON REVISED`) while the home featured stamp under the same Survivor block reads `SEASONS RANKED` (home capture: `50 / SEASONS RANKED`) and the `/shows` index total reads `SEASONS RANKED` (capture: `298 / SEASONS RANKED`). The home → index → show-page reading sequence brags `RANKED, RANKED, AIRED` on the same data substrate — every aired Survivor season has a canon slot (the page below renders all 50 in tier bands), so the show page is under-claiming relative to its own contents and to what the two upstream surfaces just promised the reader. A first-time visitor arriving from home → show-page reads it as a step back ("the home page said 'ranked,' this page says 'aired' — did they only rank some of them?"). Fix: change the `/shows/[show]` hero stat label from `SEASONS AIRED` to `SEASONS RANKED` so the brag holds across the three surfaces. Verify the data substrate first: the label is only honest when `canon.entries.length >= seasons` for that show (i.e. every aired season has a canon slot). For shows where the canon is mid-drain (a future possibility), either keep `AIRED` only when `canon.entries.length < seasons`, or render `N RANKED / M AIRED` honestly. Survivor is fully drained today; Amazing Race / Top Chef / Drag Race / The Challenge are too (per the phase-31b drain final tick). Add a unit assertion in `src/components/show/ShowHero.test.tsx` or sibling that pins the chosen label and a content-check invariant (lax → strict) that fails when `SEASONS RANKED` is rendered on a show with `canon.entries.length < seasons`. (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-15) — faf0767 — issue: #208 — RESOLVED: implemented the derived-label option (the finding's "N RANKED only when canon >= seasons" branch). New pure helper `src/lib/canon/seasons-stat-label.ts` returns `'seasons ranked'` only when `canonEntryCount > 0 && canonEntryCount >= seasonsAired`, else `'seasons aired'`; wired into `src/app/shows/[show]/page.tsx` (the value stays `show.seasons`, only the label is derived from `canonEntries.length`). Fully-drained Survivor now reads `50 / SEASONS RANKED`, matching the home featured stamp + `/shows` index total; a mid-drain or canon-less show still reads `SEASONS AIRED`. The separate content-check invariant the finding suggested is **unnecessary** — the label is computed from data at render, not authored, so it structurally cannot over-claim (no stored value can drift). Pinned instead with 6 colocated cases in `src/lib/canon/__tests__/seasons-stat-label.test.ts` (fully-drained → ranked; canon > aired edge → ranked; mid-drain → aired; zero-canon → aired; one-short → aired; degenerate 0/0 → aired). The test-colocation gate (phase 42) enforces the helper carries its test.

- [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains authed-and-unvoted VotePair block eyebrow reads `YOUR VOTE / CAST YOURS THIS WEEK`, but the explainer line directly below clarifies `one vote per reader. community rank recomputes weekly.` — the vote act is a one-time per-reader event; only the rank recompute is weekly. The eyebrow `CAST YOURS THIS WEEK` conflates the two cadences and tells a small lie about vote frequency (an authed-unvoted reader could reasonably interpret it as "the vote window closes weekly" or "I can only vote during this week's window"). A peer wouldn't say "cast yours this week" if the action is a one-shot lifetime vote per season; they'd say "cast yours." The two-rankings explainer next to it (`community · NET VOTE` after the pass-14 #201 widening) already carries the recompute-vs-vote framing in subtext, but the dominant typographic element (the eyebrow stamp) is louder and contradicts it. Fix: drop the `THIS WEEK` qualifier from the authed-unvoted VotePair eyebrow — `CAST YOURS` alone is honest (the rank still recomputes weekly per the explainer; the vote itself is not time-windowed). Locate in `src/components/composition/VotePair.tsx` (or `SeasonInfoCard.tsx` if the eyebrow string is hoisted there). Pair with a unit case pinning the new eyebrow on the authed-no-vote branch and an e2e assertion in the authed vote-state-pill spec. The anon branch already renders a different eyebrow (`SIGN IN TO WEIGH IN`) and is unaffected. The string was loop-authored (#177), not design-mandated — `design/tiered.tv · Heroes vs. Villains.html:563` only renders the voted-state meta `change within 72h`, so no design conflict. Pinned with a VoteRowHead unit case (positive `cast yours` + negative `not 'this week'` assertion on the signed-in-no-vote branch) and an e2e assertion in `vote-state-pill.spec.ts`'s authed-no-vote (`api.value === 0`) branch: head `data-vote-head-state` is `signed-in-no-vote` and `.meta` reads exactly `cast yours`. - [x] [LOW] [authed] /u/e2e self-view profile page leads with an all-caps eyebrow `YOUR RECORD` directly above a warm conversational empty-state body (`@e2e / Member since May 2026 / Nothing on the public record yet. Vote on a season pair, weigh in on a thread, and it will land here.`). The caps stamp reads as a generic CMS section-header against the peer voice immediately below it — same editorial drift class the cloud loop just closed for `BE THE FIRST` on the comment thread (pass-14 #205, resolved at 29a66e8). The handle line `@e2e / Member since May 2026` already orients the viewer to whose record they're looking at; the caps eyebrow above it is doing the same naming work in a louder register that's off-voice from the bearings standard (`knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken, never pretentious. Plain sentences over clever ones.`). Fix: either drop the `YOUR RECORD` eyebrow entirely (the handle + member-since line carry the orientation) or recast it title-case as `Your record`. Recommend drop — the empty-state sentence below is doing the work the eyebrow tries to set up. Locate in `src/app/(default)/u/[handle]/page.tsx` or the empty-state component (likely `src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx`). Unit-pin the chosen copy with a colocated test. The stranger-view (non-self) of a profile is a separate render path; check whether the same eyebrow renders there too and apply consistently. (URL: /u/e2e, source: critique-pass-15) — faf0767 — issue: #209 — RESOLVED: took the finding's **recast** path, not the **drop** path. Dropping (the `BE THE FIRST` #205 precedent) would have regressed pass-13's deliberate intent — this eyebrow carries a *unique* self-vs-stranger ownership cue (nothing else differentiates the two views, and on a populated profile there is no empty-state sentence to carry it) and sits before the H1 for screen-reader ordering. `BE THE FIRST` was dropped because it was *redundant*; `Your record` is not. So `src/components/profile/ProfileHeader.tsx` keeps the eyebrow element, string, and DOM position but swaps its treatment from the all-caps mono stamp (`font-mono text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.18em] text-ink-3`) to the quiet muted-label register already used by the member-since line directly below it (`text-sm text-ink-3`) — sheds the CMS caps-stamp register the finding flagged while preserving the cue + a11y ordering. The stranger-view path renders no eyebrow (`isSelfView=false`), so nothing changes there. Pinned with a new assertion in `ProfileHeader.test.tsx`'s `isSelfView=true` case (`eyebrow.className` no longer contains `uppercase`, alongside the existing textContent + DOM-position pins); the e2e `user-profile.spec.ts:146` `toHaveText('Your record')` is unaffected (it compares textContent, not the CSS transform).

- [x] [LOW] [anon] / home labels the two canon types `01 · CURATED / Editor's Canon` and `02 · LIVE / Community Rank` — the numeric `01 / 02` prefix implies a 1→2 sequence or hierarchy, but the immediately-preceding prose explicitly frames the two as parallel views of the same show: `Two rankings for every show. One written by an editor with the spoilers stripped; one voted by the readers as they watch.` The numbering walks back the parallelism the prose just established. A reader scanning chrome-first (the dominant pattern on a home page) reads `01 · CURATED` then `02 · LIVE` and infers Editor's Canon is primary and Community Rank is secondary — but the page's editorial intent (and the broader project's two-rankings frame) is genuinely parallel: both are first-class views, the user picks which to read. The numbering is a small visual lie. Fix: drop the `01 · / 02 · ` prefix on the two ranking-type cards on the home page and keep `CURATED` / `LIVE` as the eyebrow labels. The cards are already differentiated by their tinted background, headline, and body — the number adds nothing the existing chrome doesn't already do, and removes the ordering connotation. Locate in the home-page component (likely `src/app/(default)/page.tsx` or a `RankingTypeCards` / `TwoRankingsBlock` child). Unit-pin the absence of the `01 ·` / `02 ·` prefixes. (URL: /, source: critique-pass-15) — faf0767 — DROPPED on self-assessment (not a fix): the `01 · Curated` / `02 · Live` `dual-tag` labels are an **exact faithful port** of the binding design file `design/tiered.tv · Home.html:634,639` (`<div class="dual-tag">01 · Curated</div>` / `<div class="dual-tag">02 · Live</div>`), rendered by `src/components/home/HomeDualCallout.tsx`. The visual law (`design/CLAUDE.md`, supreme on anything visible) makes the design file authoritative over an external observer's editorial opinion; this is the observer disagreeing with the design itself, not flagging a port miss. Per the standing pattern for design-conflicting reader findings, dropped rather than shipped. If the numbering is to change, it changes in the design file first and the component follows — no code change filed.

> ids — all four resolve to the same scroll position. (4)

> `/themes` renders `CROSS-CANON · 6 ENTRIES`) — real
