- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains anon VotePair head still renders bare `1 / NET VOTE` on the no-vote state — pass-13 #198's `community · ` qualifier prefix was deliberately scoped to the **authed-and-not-yet-voted** branch in `src/components/composition/VotePair.tsx` (the resolved row reasoned that anon "has no personal vote to confuse with"). The walker captures the anon SeasonInfoCard rendering `SIGN IN TO WEIGH IN / Does this belong in the community top 10? / 1 / NET VOTE / one vote per reader. community rank recomputes weekly.` — the helper line below carries the `community` framing in subtext, but the dominant typographic element (the big number + uppercase `NET VOTE`) is unqualified, and the anon stranger has not yet been taught the two-rankings frame the home + show pages spend real estate separating. A first-paint reader scanning the season page meets `1 NET VOTE` next to `EDITOR'S CANON #02` and has no syntactic cue distinguishing the two frames. Fix: extend the `community · ` prefix to the anon-and-no-vote branch as well — `authedNoVote` is the gating condition today; widen it to `state.value === 0 && !state.cast` (or equivalent) so the qualifier renders for both anon and authed in the unacted state. The original concern (anon has no personal vote to confuse with) is real, but the dual concern (anon has no canon-vs-community frame yet) is louder for a first-time visitor; both branches benefit from the same disambiguation. Add a unit case to `VotePair.test.tsx` pinning the anon-no-vote branch carries `community · ` and a matching e2e assertion on the anon `vote-state-pill` spec. (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-14) — 9e2ac61 — issue: #201 — RESOLVED efd7a3f: widened `VotePair.tsx`'s no-vote condition from `signedIn && state.value === 0` to `state.value === 0` and renamed the variable `authedNoVote → noVote`; the displayLabel ternary now emits `community · ${baseLabel}` for any unacted reader (anon or authed). Pinned with new unit cases (anon-no-vote prefix in plural + singular form) and the SSR-fallback pin flips to expect the qualifier (state.value=0 default applies to all viewer classes pre-hydrate). Three pluralize-aware label assertions that incidentally exercised the no-vote state widened to a suffix match (`labelText.endsWith(' net votes')`) to keep their concern focused on unit agreement. E2e: the anon branch of `vote-state-pill.spec.ts` flips from `not.toHaveText(/^community · /)` to `toHaveText(/^community · /)`; the authed branch's `if (api.value === 0)` guard already matches the new behavior unchanged. Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 1990 unit + 127 script + content:check (13 shows/298 seasons/13 canons/12 themes/3 legal docs), leg2 build, leg3 1350 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows declares a three-tier confidence ladder (S / A / B) in its legend strip — "B tier means we haven't watched everything twice yet — when we have, the show moves up" — but the rendered ladder contains zero shows in B. The page flows S (2 shows) → A (11 shows) → footer, with the B band absent entirely. A first-time visitor reading the legend then scrolling the ladder sees a named tier vanish from the surface that introduced it; the editorial-confidence framing the page is built on reads either as a missing render or as over-promised legend copy. Root cause is content-shape, not page code: every show in `content/shows/<slug>.md` currently carries `tier: S` or `tier: A` — no show is authored at `tier: B`, so the tier-grouping renderer correctly emits an empty band and the page elides it. Fix (two paths): (i) **content path** — promote at least one show to `tier: B` (or demote one from A) so the legend's promise is delivered; the `tier` enum in `CLAUDE.md` is explicitly an editorial confidence statement, and a B-tier show is not a content defect, it's an honest editorial signal; (ii) **page path** — render an explicit empty-band placeholder (`B — nothing here yet.`) when no shows occupy the tier, so the ladder structure honors the legend even when a band is empty; the placeholder reads as honesty about the editorial process the legend describes. The page path is the lower-effort fix; the content path is the truer fix. Recommend page path now + content path as a content-curator backlog item. (URL: /shows, source: critique-pass-14) — 9e2ac61 — issue: #202 — RESOLVED f304756 (page path): `TierSection.tsx` now always renders the `<section>` wrapper + `<TierHead>` (with `count={shows.length}` so the count line reads "0 Shows" honestly) plus a new `.tier-empty` placeholder (`"Nothing here yet."`) in place of `.shows-grid` when the tier holds no shows; the previous `if (shows.length === 0) return null` early-return is gone. The legend in `<HowTiersMove>` now has a B band to refer to on every render. Hero lede + SEO meta description stay honest about the populated subset — the critique-pass-11 #181 invariant holds (they only name tiers that carry shows). New `.tier-empty` styles in `src/styles/shows.css` (italic serif, dashed `--color-line-soft` border, centered, 48px vertical padding desktop / 32px mobile) deliberately read as a deliberate placeholder, not a CSS bug. `TierSection.test.tsx` flips the "renders nothing" case to pin the placeholder render (section wrapper + `data-tier` + count "0" + `.tier-empty[data-empty=true]` body + no `shows-grid`). E2e `shows-index.spec.ts` three updates: (1) `tierOrder` is now always `['S','A','B']`; (2) hero-lede `data-tier-coverage` matches the *populated* subset (sections containing a `shows-grid`), not the rendered subset; (3) B-tier status-pill assertion gates on the section containing a `shows-grid` rather than on the section itself. Comments in `tierLede.ts` + `tierMetaDescription.ts` updated to clarify they describe the populated subset while the page renders every tier with a placeholder. The content path (promoting a show to tier B) stays a content-curator backlog item — no content change in this fix. Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 1990 unit + 127 script + content:check (13 shows/298 seasons/13 canons/12 themes/3 legal docs), leg2 build, leg3 1350 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] / footer `TIERED.TV` column lists four destinations — `About the canon` (`/about`), `How voting works` (`/about#voting`), `Spoilers policy` (`/about#spoilers`), `Become an editor` (`/about#editors`) — but `/about` carries none of the `voting`, `spoilers`, `editors` anchor ids. All four links resolve to the same scroll position on `/about`. A first-time visitor reading the footer sees four distinct destinations and clicks two or three to compare; each click lands at the page top with no visible difference, which reads as broken links or as four labels for one undifferentiated page. This is a navigation-honesty defect — the footer is making promises the destination does not keep. Fix: add `<section id="voting">`, `<section id="spoilers">`, `<section id="editors">` wrappers around the existing `/about` content sections. No copy changes needed — the sections already exist as headed editorial blocks; only the anchor ids are missing. Lowest-effort fix in the queue. Verify the four footer links scroll to distinct positions with a tiny e2e assertion (compare `window.scrollY` after clicking each). (URL: /, source: critique-pass-14) — 9e2ac61 — RESOLVED ca9ca8d (#200): extended the Prose markdown parser with backward-compatible `{#anchor-id}` heading syntax (`HEADING_RE` widened to capture an optional trailing `\{#([a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*)\}`; `MdBlock.heading` gains an optional `id` field), rendered the captured id as the heading's `id` attribute in `src/components/prose/Prose.tsx`, and restructured `content/legal/about.md` to carry three new H2 sections matching the footer's promises — `## How voting works {#voting}` (voting weights + per-session rule, lifted from the page's FAQ JSON-LD that crawlers already see), `## Spoilers policy {#spoilers}` (rename of the existing "What 'no spoilers' means" block with the AI-pre-filter line folded in), `## Become an editor {#editors}` (new tiny section pointing readers to GitHub for invitation-round drafts). Tests: `src/lib/__tests__/markdown.test.ts` +8 cases for the `{#anchor}` parser (all 4 heading levels, kebab + digit ids accepted, uppercase + leading-hyphen ids rejected with the suffix preserved as text, trailing whitespace tolerance, id field omitted when absent); `src/components/prose/__tests__/Prose.test.tsx` +2 cases for the id-attribute emission (present vs absent); `apps/e2e/tests/seo.spec.ts` +1 spec walks `/about` and asserts each of `#voting`, `#spoilers`, `#editors` exists exactly once on a heading element. Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 173 unit test files + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows/298 seasons/13 canons/12 themes/3 legal docs), leg2 build, leg3 1350 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [authed] /u/e2e owner-view empty-state body reads `Nothing on the public record yet. Vote on a season pair, weigh in on a thread, and it will land here.` paired with a CTA `Start with Survivor →` that routes to `/shows/survivor`. The body's literal promise is "vote on a season pair" but `/shows/survivor` renders the 50-season canon ladder, not a vote-pair affordance. The user has to (a) recognize that a "vote pair" lives inside a season page, (b) scroll the ladder to find a season they've watched, (c) click into the season, (d) scroll to the vote block, (e) cast a vote. The empty state does not foreshadow that chain. A returning member taking the CTA at face value clicks into a list and stalls. Fix (two paths): (i) **destination path** — route the CTA directly to a season page that exposes VotePair above the fold (e.g. `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains`, which is the canonical Phase-26a reference) so the next click is literally the vote the empty state promised; (ii) **label path** — change the CTA copy to match the destination ("See the Survivor canon →") so the promise narrows to what the click actually delivers. Destination path is the truer fix — the empty state is selling the vote, not the canon — but label path is the lower-risk one. Pair either fix with a unit/e2e check that the empty-state CTA's `href` matches a promise the link copy makes. (URL: /u/e2e, source: critique-pass-14) — 9e2ac61 — issue: #203 — RESOLVED this commit (destination path): the `/u/[handle]` page now derives the self-view CTA destination from the featured show's #1 canon entry's season slug (Survivor → Cagayan, the rank-1 entry), so the CTA `Start with Survivor →` lands on `/shows/survivor/season/cagayan` — a page that renders `<VotePair>` above the fold in the season hero. New pure helper `pickFeaturedSeason(canon, resolveSeason)` lives in `src/lib/profile/context.ts` and is wired into `src/app/(default)/u/[handle]/page.tsx`; it picks the canon entry with the lowest `rank` (not lowest `season` number), looks the season up by number, and returns null if either the canon is absent or the rank-1 season isn't in the catalog — the page falls back to the bare show page in that case. Five new colocated unit cases in `src/lib/profile/__tests__/context.test.ts` pin: null canon → null; lowest-rank entry wins (not first-listed); resolver-returns-null → null; rank-keying (a low-numbered season at rank 5 is NOT picked); single-entry canon round-trip. The label "Start with {showName} →" stays — Survivor is still the brand-level entry point, but the click now delivers what the empty state copy promised. Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 173 unit test files (incl. +5 cases on `pickFeaturedSeason`) + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows/298 seasons/13 canons/12 themes/3 legal docs), leg2 build, leg3 1350 e2e.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains comment empty-state reads `No comments yet. Weigh in on the season, not the result.` The `not the result` clause is well-intended spoiler-policy nudging, but for a first-time visitor who has not yet watched HvV, the phrase itself signals that the season has a notable "result" worth not-spoiling — a soft spoiler-shape leak in the very copy meant to enforce the no-spoilers brand promise. The rubric directly above the input (`No plot, no winners, no twists →`) already carries the policy unambiguously, so the empty-state clause is redundant with a slightly leaky reformulation. Fix: drop the `not the result` clause and let the empty state read `No comments yet. Weigh in on the season itself.` — the rubric above carries the policy, the empty state stays terse. Optionally widen `scripts/content-check.ts`'s spoiler-tail rule to flag the canonical-result phrasing set (`the result`, `the winner`, `the twist`, `the elimination`) on empty-state strings in `src/components/composition/` so future drafts don't reintroduce the soft-leak pattern. (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-14) — 9e2ac61 — issue #204 — RESOLVED this commit: `src/components/composition/CommentThread.tsx:55` empty-state copy flipped `No comments yet. Weigh in on the season, not the result.` → `No comments yet. Weigh in on the season itself.` — the rubric in the composer placeholder (`No plot, no winners, no twists →` from `CommentInput.tsx`) carries the spoiler policy unambiguously, so the empty-state line no longer needs the `not the result` reformulation that softly leaked "this season has a notable result." The fix is one literal swap; no component shape, schema field, prop, or call-site change. §5a: `CommentThread.test.tsx` 7→8 — the existing empty-state assertion tightened from a permissive `/no comments yet/i` regex match to an exact-string `toBe('No comments yet. Weigh in on the season itself.')` so a regression back to the previous copy fails loudly; a new `empty-state copy avoids the soft-spoiler-shape "the result" phrasing` case pins negative assertions against the canonical-result phrasing set the row called out (`the result`, `the winner`, `the twist`, `the elimination`) at the empty-state surface so the soft-leak pattern can't reappear here without flipping a test. The optional `scripts/content-check.ts` rule the row suggested (widen the spoiler-tail rule across `src/components/composition/`) is deferred per skill rule 1 (one fix per tick) — the unit-test pin enforces the same invariant on the surface that actually shipped the leak, and the broader sweep stays available as a future tick if other composition surfaces drift into the same pattern. The sibling `CommentInput.tsx:143` composer placeholder (`No spoilers — past or future. Talk about the season, not the result.`) is left intact this tick — it's a different surface (composer rubric, not empty-state) and the critique row scoped specifically to the empty-state line; touching the composer in the same commit would couple two unrelated copy decisions. Spoiler discipline P0 intact — fix tightens the no-spoilers brand promise. Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 173 unit test files (incl. CommentThread 7→8) + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 static pages), leg3 1350 e2e.

- [x] [LOW] [authed] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains comment thread stacks three label-shaped strings vertically: `The thread` (section title) → `BE THE FIRST` (caps eyebrow) → `Add a thought · no spoilers, please.` (input placeholder). The middle line `BE THE FIRST` reads as a generic CMS empty-state stamp, off-register from the bearings voice ("knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken, never pretentious. Plain sentences over clever ones."). The section title + the placeholder together already carry the empty state; the caps eyebrow is doing the same work in a louder register. Fix: either drop the caps eyebrow entirely and let the placeholder carry the state, or rewrite it as a quiet sentence in mixed case ("No one's weighed in yet.") that matches the editorial voice of the rest of the page. (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-14) — 9e2ac61 — issue: #205 — RESOLVED at 29a66e8 (drop path): `src/components/composition/CommentThread.tsx` `aside-meta` span now renders only when `count > 0` — the live "42 comments" / "1 comment" reading still surfaces where it has a real count to display, but the empty state stops shouting an all-caps `BE THE FIRST` stamp at every signed-in or signed-out viewer with zero published comments. The section title `The thread` keeps naming the surface; the empty-state sentence below (`No comments yet. Weigh in on the season itself.`) keeps the editorial prompt; the composer placeholder keeps the "no spoilers" policy. CSS (`src/styles/screens.css` `.aside-meta`, `text-transform: uppercase`) untouched — when the strip does render at `count > 0`, it keeps the existing uppercase numeric reading. §5a: three count=0 cases in `CommentThread.test.tsx` (default empty / held-only / hideEmpty) flipped from asserting the eyebrow text `Be the first` to asserting `comment-count` testid is absent; two count=0 cases in `CommentThreadLive.test.tsx` (held-only / signed-in-zero) flipped to assert the meta eyebrow is absent; the two count>0 cases (42 comments, 1 comment) and the signed-in count=1 case stand unchanged. No URL, schema field, prop addition, or page family change. Spoiler discipline P0 intact — voice/copy edit only. Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 173 unit test files (1996 tests) + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 static pages), leg3 1350 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] /themes/best-finales body-hero `tagline:` field still closes on `...across six different franchises.` — the count-of-shows tail the recent drain (commits 9dc9418 + 1241040, audit #191) removed from 10 themed-list `description:` / `blurb:` fields. The body-hero `tagline:` is a separate frontmatter field that wasn't covered: `content/themes/best-finales.md:5` reads `tagline: "The closing run is where a season either pays off its promise or quietly admits it didn't have one. These finales <b>land the season they were always making</b> — texture intact, stakes earned, the last hour built at the size it needed, across six different franchises."` A first-time reader scanning the hero meets the exact pattern (`across <N> different franchises`) the recent fix flagged as an AI-counting tic. Fix: extend the drain to the `tagline:` field across all 10 cross-canon themed lists — `grep -l "across.*franchise\|across.*shows" content/themes/*.md` to enumerate the survivors, then rewrite the closing clause in-voice (the description rewrites in commits 9dc9418 / 1241040 are the reference shape). Optionally widen `scripts/content-check.ts`'s template-tail rule to also scan `tagline:` so future drains catch the field. (URL: /themes/best-finales, audit every /themes/<theme> page, source: critique-pass-13) — 1241040
  - issue: #196
  - RESOLVED this commit: widened `collectThemeDescriptionCountTailIssues` to scan both `description` AND `tagline` (one issue per offending field; same helper, same strict floor). Drained 6 tagline offenders — `best-finales`, `best-location-reveals`, `best-returnees`, `best-villain-editing` closed on `across <N> different franchises.`; `best-newbie-casts` and `best-non-winning-runs` opened on `Across six franchises,`. Each rewrite preserved the single `<b>...</b>` emphasis span and closed on the editorial observation the line actually carried — no franchise-counting prose anywhere in the body-hero copy. Added an 8-case `describe` block pinning the tagline branch of the scan (closing-position end-of-clause, opener-position mid-sentence, `across <N> shows` form, the both-fields-emit-two-issues case, the named-shows + `<N>-seasons` carve-outs in tagline form, a live-catalog zero-offenders pin) — 92/92 content-check unit tests pass (+8). Updated `.claude/agents/content-curator.md` Description-discipline sub-rule + `skills/ship-content.md` Rule 3 to call out the widened scope (both fields, one helper, one strict floor). Verify green (leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 173 unit test files + 127 script tests + content:check ok 13 shows/298 seasons/13 canons/12 themes/3 legal, leg2 build, leg3 1348 e2e).

- [x] [MED] [anon] /themes/best-finales entry #04 (Survivor S40 Winners at War) blurb in `content/themes/best-finales.md` names two season-specific twist mechanics by name: `"The milestone framing earns itself in the closing run. Edge of Extinction reshapes the home stretch, the fire-token economy compresses into real currency, and the final tribal carries the weight of a roster that has played this game before."` Spoiler discipline is P0 (`CLAUDE.md` rules, brand promise "no spoilers" on home). The WaW premise (returning winners) is on the marketing box, but the twist mechanics — Edge of Extinction (a winners-return-from-elimination mechanic) and fire tokens (a season-specific in-game currency) — are exactly the kind of episode-by-episode reveal a viewer who hasn't watched is meant to discover. Cross-canon themed-list blurbs are public-list surfaces, not detail pages — they have to land without naming the twist set. Fix: rewrite the blurb to describe the closing-run quality without naming the twist mechanics — keep `"Twenty former champions taking the season to its full size"` and `"the final tribal carries the weight of a roster that has played this game before"`; drop the Edge-of-Extinction and fire-token clauses. Pair with a `scripts/content-check.ts` blocklist that flags the canonical-twist-name set (Edge of Extinction, fire token, redemption island, hidden immunity idol, etc.) on themed-list `entries[].blurb` fields so the spoiler invariant is mechanically enforced. (URL: /themes/best-finales, source: critique-pass-13) — 1241040
  - issue: #195
  - RESOLVED: WaW blurb rewritten in `content/themes/best-finales.md` to drop the Edge-of-Extinction + fire-token clauses while keeping the title's "Twenty former champions taking the season to its full size" anchor and the closing "the final tribal carries the weight of a roster that has played this game before" clause. New middle clause: "Every move lands heavier than it would in another season's room." Paired invariant `collectThemedEntrySpoilerIssues()` in `scripts/content-check.ts` flags the canonical-twist-name set (Edge of Extinction, Redemption Island, fire token/s/-token forms) on every themed-list `entries[].blurb`; ships strict at floor 0 (THEMED_ENTRY_SPOILER_STRICT = true) — the rewrite drained every offender in one tick. The invariant surfaced two latent offenders the critique row didn't catch — `best-comeback-seasons.md` entry #3 (also S40 WaW) and `best-returnees.md` entry #2 (also S40 WaW) both named Edge of Extinction + fire tokens; both rewrites land in the same tick. §5a: `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` gains a 9-case describe (flag EoE / fire-token / fire-tokens / Redemption Island / lowercased / clean-blurb / incidental-words / multi-entry-clean-theme / live-catalog-zero) — 84/84 content-check unit tests pass. Spoiler discipline P0 mechanically enforced going forward.

- [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/<show>/season/<slug> authed-but-not-yet-voted reader sees the VotePair head `YOUR VOTE / CAST YOURS THIS WEEK` paired with a numeric value `1 NET VOTE` — the head is a call to action and the numeric area is ambiguous: is "1" the global community net awaiting the reader's contribution, or confirmation that the reader already voted? Pass-12 #189 / commit 73c25eb fixed the inverse (the duplicated "YOU HAVEN'T VOTED" pill that nudged the same action twice), so the head now owns the no-vote channel — but the head is under-specified for the authed-not-yet-voted state because it doesn't disambiguate the numeric value's source (community-wide vs. personal). Fix: when authed-and-not-yet-voted, qualify the numeric area's k-label to make ownership explicit — render `COMMUNITY · 1 NET VOTE` (the `k` slot is already a span beside the number per `src/components/composition/SeasonInfoCard.tsx` styling). When authed-and-already-voted, the existing `YOUR VOTE` head + personal-confirmation styling already handles the reverse case; the gap is the in-between state. Don't reintroduce a "you haven't voted" pill — keep the channel on the head, just qualify the value's label. Unit + e2e: snapshot the authed-not-yet-voted state and assert the `k` label disambiguates. (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-13) — 1241040
  - issue: #198
  - RESOLVED this commit: `src/components/composition/VotePair.tsx` derives `authedNoVote = signedIn && state.value === 0` from the existing `/api/vote` read-back (no new fetch, no new state) and prefixes the pluralize-aware `displayLabel` with `community · ` only in that state. Anon viewers and authed-and-voted viewers keep the plain label — the former has no personal vote to confuse with, the latter has the existing `you voted higher/lower` cap above the buttons. SSR fallback (pre-hydrate) defaults to anon, so initial client render stays structurally identical to the server's. `src/components/composition/__tests__/VotePair.test.tsx` adds a 7-case `describe` block — plural prefix, singular prefix, anon-omitted, authed-voted-omitted, drops-on-click (signed-in-no-vote → signed-in-with-vote), custom-label honored, SSR fallback omitted. `apps/e2e/tests/vote-state-pill.spec.ts` adds two assertions — the authed walk asserts the label leads with `community · ` when `api.value === 0` and falls back to the plain label otherwise; the anon walk asserts the qualifier is absent (P0: surfacing the qualifier to an anon would imply a viewer-identity channel they don't have). Verify gate green across leg1 (unit + script + content-check) + leg2 (build) + leg3 (e2e).

- [x] [MED] [authed] /u/e2e owner-view renders identically to a stranger's view of the same profile — H1 `@e2e`, sub `Member since May 2026`, body `Nothing on the public record yet. Vote on a season pair, weigh in on a thread, and it will land here. Start with Survivor →` — with no second-person framing or ownership cue. The header chrome already shows `@e2e / Sign out`, but the page body never confirms "this is your page". The page already has the data: `src/app/(default)/u/[handle]/page.tsx:117` computes `isSelfView` from the session viewer's handle vs. the route handle. The branch just doesn't render anything owner-specific. Fix: when `isSelfView === true`, render an eyebrow above the H1 — `YOUR RECORD` or `YOU · @{handle}` — so the owner-view and the public-view are visually distinct without changing the public surface for strangers. Unit test the branch (`isSelfView=true` vs `false` from the existing helper), e2e walk the authed `/u/e2e` and assert the eyebrow is present + the anon `/u/e2e` walk asserts it is absent. (URL: /u/e2e, source: critique-pass-13) — 1241040
  - issue: #197
  - RESOLVED this commit: added optional `isSelfView` prop to `ProfileHeader` (default `false`); when `true` the header renders `Your record` as an uppercase mono eyebrow above the `@handle` H1 with `data-testid="profile-self-eyebrow"`. The page wires it from the existing `isSelfView` computation at `src/app/(default)/u/[handle]/page.tsx:117` — no new state, no new fetch. Public/stranger surface unchanged. Unit tests in `src/components/profile/__tests__/ProfileHeader.test.tsx` add 3 new cases pinning the branch: omitted-by-default, explicit `isSelfView={false}` omits, `isSelfView` renders the literal `Your record` eyebrow + document-order check that the eyebrow precedes the handle H1 (so screen readers announce ownership before the handle). E2E in `apps/e2e/tests/user-profile.spec.ts` adds two assertions: the existing stranger-view (post `clearCookies`) walk asserts the eyebrow has `count(0)`; a new authed test walks `/u/<handle>` with the storage state intact and asserts the eyebrow is visible with text `Your record`. Verify green: leg1 173 unit test files + 127 script tests + 13 shows/298 seasons/13 canons/12 themes/3 legal, leg2 build, leg3 1349 e2e (+1 new).

- [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/survivor + /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains disagree on the HvV community-vote state across three surfaces on adjacent hops. The show page's `What changed this week` ShiftCard renders `↑5 this week / Heroes vs. Villains / Climbed 5 spots since the last weekly update · 1 vote / was #07 / now #02` (post-#316 — the floor is `voteCount > 0`, so single-ballot movers admit). The same show page's canon-ladder COMMUNITY column for HvV renders `↑ 1 / #02`. The season page's `YOUR VOTE / CAST VOTE` block renders the vote-pair with the literal text `0 community · net votes` (SSR HTML: `data-testid="vote-pair" data-vote-value="0" data-voted="none"` with `<span class="vote-num">0</span><span class="vote-label">community · net votes</span>`). A first-time visitor who clicks from the show page into the season page sees `1 vote · climbed 5 to #02` on the parent hop, lands on the season page, and the vote-pair tells them the community position is unmoved (net 0). The three surfaces are technically describing three different mechanics — ShiftCard names the **vote count** (`1 vote`); canon-ladder COMMUNITY column names the **weekly rank delta** (`↑ 1`, which is a 7-day trend pill); season-page vote-pair names the **signed sum** (`0 community · net votes`, the net of upvotes minus downvotes) — but a reader doesn't carry that vocabulary across the click. The walk produces three different numeric framings of the same canonical fact (HvV's community state) in under two scrolls. Source: ShiftCard at `src/components/composition/ShiftCard.tsx:29` rendering `moverNote` from `src/lib/community/live.ts:74-87` (post-#316 includes `· N votes` suffix for `voteCount > 0`); canon-ladder COMMUNITY column at `src/components/canon/ShowRanking.tsx` rendering a 7d trend pill; vote-pair at `src/components/composition/VotePair.tsx:202-215` (the `you haven't voted yet` state-cap from pass-27 #284 hangs above this integer to reduce the framing ambiguity, but the bare `0 community · net votes` literal remains). Bearings voice cue is `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones` (`plan/bearings.md:370`); a peer doesn't publish three different numeric framings of the same fact across two adjacent surfaces and expect the reader to reconcile them. Same defect class as the resolved pass-32 #311 / pass-33 #318 cross-surface mechanic-drift chain, now at the **community-vote display** layer rather than the **vote-weighting policy** layer — and one tier deeper since the three surfaces describe genuinely-different mechanics (vote count, weekly rank delta, signed sum), not the same mechanic stated three ways. Fix options: (a) **primary path — align the three surfaces to cite the same canonical fact on every numeric framing**. The vote-pair's `community · net votes` is the most ambiguous label (a net-zero on one vote is a single split-the-difference signal, not the community-rank fact the reader expects); the cleanest alignment is to rename it to match the ShiftCard's framing — `1 community vote` (count of distinct voters, not the signed sum). The signed-sum is still useful internally but doesn't surface to the reader. The canon-ladder's `↑ 1 / #02` (weekly rank delta) reads cleanly already; it disambiguates by carrying the rank position the trend resolves to. (b) **alternative — keep the three mechanics distinct but add a one-line bridge near the season-page vote-pair** (e.g. `1 reader has voted on this season — net 0 says they're split on the top-10 question.`) so the reader can reconcile the numbers without leaving the page. Smaller blast radius (one prose line, no schema change) but doesn't address the cross-surface inconsistency itself. (c) **alternative — drop the vote-pair's numeric framing entirely** when `voteCount < some-threshold` so the season page reads `BE THE FIRST TO WEIGH IN` or `1 OTHER READER HAS VOTED` rather than `0 community · net votes`; loses the post-vote signal mechanism but resolves the cross-surface ambiguity at the lowest-signal end. Recommended (a) — single-label change on the vote-pair brings the three surfaces into agreement on what the number names (count of voters across all surfaces; the trend pill keeps its rank-delta framing since it disambiguates by carrying the rank). Pin: extend `src/components/composition/__tests__/VotePair.test.tsx` with a cross-surface parity case asserting the rendered label text matches `/community votes?$/` (NOT `/net votes?$/`) — bidirectional drift guard against a future refactor reverting to the signed-sum framing. Sibling pin: extend `src/lib/community/__tests__/live.test.ts` `moverNote` describe block with a positive case asserting the rendered note's vote count matches the same season's `voteCount` field surfaced to the vote-pair, so a regression where the two surfaces compute from different sources trips at unit time. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (UI-label edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the rotation preserves the count semantics without naming any vote's direction). (URL: /shows/survivor, /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-34) — b52ebba  RESOLVED bb2e314: re-pointed the VotePair main integer + label from signed-net → distinct-voter count so it cites the same canonical fact as the ShiftCards `vote_count`. Issue #361.
- [x] [LOW] [anon] /themes hero lede phrase `Some span the catalog, one lives inside one show.` trips a first-read parse — the doubled `one` tokens carry two distinct referents (one LIST lives inside one SHOW) but the sentence renders them as adjacent bare `one` tokens with no syntactic disambiguator. A reader has to back up and re-parse to recover the meaning (the first `one` refers to a list in the catalog, the second `one` refers to a show in the catalog; only context disambiguates). The lede sits two lines under the H1 (`Themed lists. / Cross-canon and single-show.`) and is the page's load-bearing summary sentence; a parse-stumble here taxes the most-read line on the most-trafficked themed-list catalog page. Source: `src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx:26` derives the dynamic numeric counts (`9 in the index, 3 featured this month — 12 we'd defend in a group chat`) but the editorial lede text `Some span the catalog, one lives inside one show. None of them spoil what they rank.` is the static frame around them. Bearings voice cue is `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones` (`plan/bearings.md:370`); the doubled bare `one` reads as clever-compression (using the same word to do two different jobs in the same clause) that costs comprehension on a load-bearing summary sentence. Same defect class as the pass-31 `lede shows the math inline when featured + index split` rotation (the count math was clarified by adding the prose split inline); this finding sits at the *referent-disambiguation* layer rather than the *math-transparency* layer — the counts read cleanly now; the trip is in the prose closer that names where the lists live. Fix options: (a) **primary path — disambiguate the referents with a syntactic marker on at least one of the bare `one` tokens**. Candidate rotation: `Some span the catalog, one stays inside a single show.` (swaps the second `one` clause for `one stays inside a single show` — drops the doubled `one`, replaces with `single show` which carries the same semantic but distinguishes lexically from the leading `one` subject). Alternative: `Most span the catalog; one is a single-show tier.` (compresses further, uses `single-show tier` to name the category — slightly more terminological but resolves the parse cleanly). Recommended: the first candidate — preserves the editorial cadence (`Some span ... , one stays ...`) while dropping the bare-`one` doubling. (b) **alternative — rephrase the whole sentence to lead with the category names** rather than the numeric `one`: `Most are cross-canon; one is a single-show tier.` Reads more declaratively; loses the gentler `Some / one` cadence the lede currently has. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — accept that the parse-stumble is small enough that most readers will recover on the second pass; the sentence's editorial work is still legible. Recommended (a) — single-clause edit, preserves the existing cadence, drops the bare-`one` doubling. Pin: extend `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListsHero.test.tsx` with a regression case asserting the rendered hero lede text does NOT match `/\bone\b\s+lives\s+inside\s+\bone\b/i` — bidirectional drift guard against a future authoring pass re-introducing the doubled-`one` clause. Sibling positive: assert the lede contains a disambiguating phrase matching `/\bsingle[- ]show\b|\bone[- ]show\b|\binside\s+a\s+show\b/i` so the rewrite doesn't drift to a different ambiguous form. Both pins live in the existing colocation; no e2e fixture row owed. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (lede prose edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-34) — b52ebba — issue: #324 — RESOLVED 6b463d9: took the row's recommended Option (a). `src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx` mixed-mode singular branch now reads `one stays inside a single show` instead of `one lives inside one show`; the second bare `one` is replaced with `a single show` — same semantic, lexically distinct from the leading `one` subject — so the doubled bare-`one` parse-stumble drops without touching the editorial cadence (`Some span ... , one stays ...`). Plural branch (`some live inside one show`) untouched — `some` + `one show` are different lexical roots, the parse-stumble does not apply there. Pinned per the row's spec (and bidirectionally): the existing pass-20 #133 case `singularizes "one lives inside one show" when singleShowCount is exactly 1` renamed to `singularizes "one stays inside a single show" when singleShowCount is exactly 1` and asserts the new positive text; the existing plural-branch case `keeps "some live inside one show" plural when singleShowCount > 1` asserts the new singular form is absent when singleShowCount > 1. New case `singular-branch lede drops the doubled bare-"one" parse-stumble (critique-pass-34 #324)` carries negative regex `/\bone\b\s+lives\s+inside\s+\bone\b/i` and positive regex `/\bsingle[- ]show\b|\bone[- ]show\b|\binside\s+a\s+show\b/i` — so a future authoring pass that re-introduces the doubled-bare-`one` clause fails at unit time, AND a rewrite that drifts to a different ambiguous form (neither `single show`, `one-show`, nor `inside a show`) also fails. No URL / schema / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — `apps/e2e/tests/themes.spec.ts:38` only pins `not.toContainText(/every show/i)` on the lede (the overclaim guard from a prior pass), unchanged by the new shape. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (lede prose edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2439 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1362 e2e (9.9m).
- [x] [LOW] [authed] /themes/best-finales leans on the `final X` syntactic frame across 7 of 7 entries — title nouns and body openers reach for `final leg / tribal / service / banishment / lip-sync / stretch` as the universal structural noun for naming each show's closing beat. Title-line `final X` instances: #01 `A final leg that proves the format's live sprint is built right.`; #02 `The first all-returnee season closing on a final tribal that reads like a verdict.`; #05 `The Traitors S02 tightening into the final banishment the format was built for.`; #06 `A final lip-sync that pays off a finale built on real artistry.` Body-line `final X` instances: #01 body `That final leg is where this season turns a cult favorite into a network anchor.`; #03 body `The final service plays like a high-end tasting menu`; #04 body `the final tribal carries the weight of a roster that has played this game before`; #07 body `The final stretch rewards the alliance play the summer spent building.` All seven entries carry `final X` framing at least once across title + body. The page is titled `Finales that stuck the landing` so `final` is on-topic and structurally honest — but turning it into the universal entry frame across 7 of 7 entries makes seven editorial gestures read as one template repeated seven times with a noun-substitution rather than seven angles into seven distinct shows' finales. Source: `content/themes/best-finales.md` — entries 1-7 title + blurb fields. Same within-list voice-template class as the pass-12 #187 `/shows` taglines (`Ranked without <verb> a single <noun>.` in 9 of 13 taglines, drained to 0-1) and pass-12 #191 `/themes` blurbs (`across <N> different franchises` / `<N> shows, <M> [thing-noun]` in 8 of 10 blurbs, drained to 0); the editorial-template-as-frame pattern keeps re-emerging at content-author time and gets drained via curator rotation. Bearings voice cue is `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken, never pretentious. Plain sentences over clever ones.` (`plan/bearings.md:370`); a peer naming seven different shows' closing beats reaches for seven different angles, not the same `final X` template across all of them. Fix options: (a) **primary path — curator-only edit rotating 3-4 of the seven entries off the `final X` template** by naming the closing beat through a different syntactic frame. Candidate rotations: #01 swap `A final leg that proves the format's live sprint is built right.` → `Season 7's last leg proves the format's live sprint is built right.` (drops `final`, replaces with `last`, swaps the deck shape to lead with the season number); #03 keep body's `The final service` since the body's first sentence post-#319 already opens with `Restaurant Wars takes the season into its endgame on a kitchen split, and the late-stage cooking never lets the level drop.` — the deck doesn't carry `final X` so the body can keep one occurrence (the body is the only `final X` in #03); #04 keep `final tribal` (the formal Tribal Council name — load-bearing show-vocabulary; the term IS the structural beat); #06 swap `A final lip-sync that pays off a finale built on real artistry.` → `The crown coronation pays off a finale built on craft.` (drops `final lip-sync`, swaps to the show's structural-beat name `crown coronation`; also drops `real` to honor the #304 in-card-repetition close); #07 swap `The final stretch rewards the alliance play the summer spent building.` → `Sue and Steph's closing run rewards the alliance play the summer spent building.` (drops `final stretch`, names the season's specific alliance — wait, naming the finalists is a spoiler; revise: `The closing run rewards the alliance play the summer spent building.` drops `final`, keeps the editorial point). After the rotation, the count drops from 7-of-7 to 3-of-7 carrying `final X` (#02 HvV `final tribal` retained as load-bearing returnee-final-tribal vocabulary; #03 Top Chef body `final service` retained as the show's structural-beat name; #04 Survivor S40 body `final tribal` retained as the formal Tribal Council name) — every retained instance is load-bearing show vocabulary, not template filler. (b) **alternative — extend the existing `collectThemeBodyPhraseRepetitionIssues` cliche-channel** in `scripts/content-check.ts` with a `final X` template pattern (similar to the existing `at full volume` and `freighted` patterns at #301 + #302) — would catch the cross-entry sweep at content-author time. Defer per the row's pin discussion below. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — accept that `final X` is on-topic for a `Finales that stuck the landing` list. Recommended (a) — single-tick content edit, drops the 7-of-7 template to 3-of-7 load-bearing usage, matches the editorial pattern the #187 + #191 closures established. The curator-only rotation is the right scope; the cliche-channel pin is the wrong layer because `final` is sometimes load-bearing (formal Tribal Council, formal Restaurant Wars finale service) and sometimes template filler — a regex can't tell which is which. Pin: defer the invariant per the row's analysis above (load-bearing vs template-filler can't be regex-distinguished). The next critique pass re-verifies the absence per the precedent on sibling content edits (#187, #191, #246, #248, #260, #301, #302). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (themed-list blurb + title edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the rotation preserves each entry's editorial framing without naming the finalists, winners, or final-vote outcomes). Retained as load-bearing show vocabulary: #02 (HvV) title `final tribal` (returnee verdict beat), #03 (top-chef S6) body `final service` (Restaurant Wars structural beat), #04 (Survivor S40) body `final tribal` (formal Tribal Council), #05 (Traitors S2) title `final banishment` (Round Table closing ritual), #01 body `final leg` (load-bearing The Race pit-stop vocab — not in the rotation list per the row's #01 candidate which only touched the title). Title-frame count drops from 4/7 to 1/7 (only #02 `final tribal` remains in a title, load-bearing); body-frame count holds at 3/7 (all load-bearing per the retention list). Same drainage mechanic as the pass-12 #187 `/shows` taglines (9/13→0–1) and pass-12 #191 `/themes` blurbs (8/10→0). `last_revised` bumped 2026-05-21 → 2026-06-06. **Spoiler discipline P0 held** — themed-list blurb + title edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface; the rotation preserves each entry's editorial framing without naming finalists, winners, or final-vote outcomes. Pin deferred per the row's analysis (load-bearing vs template-filler can't be regex-distinguished; the next critique pass re-verifies absence per the #187 / #191 / #246 / #248 / #260 / #301 / #302 precedent). - [x] [HIGH] [authed] /themes/best-finales #02 (Survivor S20 Heroes vs. Villains) title field carries a factually-wrong editorial claim. The rendered title reads `The first all-returnee season closing on a final tribal that reads like a verdict.` HvV is not the first all-returnee Survivor season — Survivor: All-Stars (Season 8, 2004) was the first all-returnee season; HvV (Season 20, 2010) was the second. The show's own canon at `content/shows/survivor/canon.md` confirms this on two adjacent surfaces a single click away. Entry `## 8. All-Stars` is tagged explicitly `tag: The first returnee season — historic, uneven, foundational to every returnee season after.` with `slot_argument: The franchise reaches for an all-star format too early, lands the concept, and writes the blueprint every returnee season since plays inside.` — All-Stars IS the format's origin. Entry `## 20. Heroes vs. Villains` body opens `Heroes vs. Villains is the all-star format running at its ceiling. Twenty returning players, two clean tribal premises, a decade of accumulated context — every move lands with the weight of seasons of prior text.` — frames HvV as the **ceiling** of an already-established returnee format, not its origin. A reader on /themes/best-finales who follows the natural canon-cross-reference (the same season-page link rendered on the entry) lands on /shows/survivor and reads the canon's tag for All-Stars naming it the first; the contradiction is one hop deep. Same defect class as pass-32 #311 / pass-33 #318 cross-surface mechanic-drift chain (which was about voting policy), now at the **editorial fact** layer — a content authoring error rather than a copy-divergence between two policy framings. Bearings voice cue is `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken, never pretentious` (`plan/bearings.md:370`); a peer doesn't publish a factual claim that contradicts the show's own canon on a flagship list with a tight click path to the source. Source: `content/themes/best-finales.md` entry rank=2 title field (`The first all-returnee season closing on a final tribal that reads like a verdict.`); cross-checked against `content/shows/survivor/canon.md` `## 8. All-Stars` tag/slot_argument and `## 20. Heroes vs. Villains` body. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rewrite #02's title to drop the `first all-returnee` claim and let the entry sit on its actual editorial argument** (HvV as the returnee-format ceiling, the body's existing argument). Candidate rotation: `The all-star format at its ceiling, closing on a final tribal that reads like a verdict on the returnee era.` (drops the factual error, preserves the load-bearing `final tribal that reads like a verdict` simile + `final tribal` retention per pass-34 #327's load-bearing-show-vocabulary carve-out, and brings the title's editorial argument into agreement with the body's `the all-star format running at its ceiling` frame from canon entry #20). Alternative title: `The returnee-era benchmark closing on a final tribal that reads like a verdict.` (keeps the `verdict` simile, names HvV as the **benchmark** rather than the **origin** of the returnee era — also accurate, slightly different angle). (b) **alternative — name HvV as the *second* all-returnee season** with a corrective edit: `The second all-returnee season closing on a final tribal that reads like a verdict.` Reads honestly but `second` doesn't carry the editorial load `first` was reaching for — and All-Stars then becomes the better candidate for that editorial claim on a future expansion of the list. (c) **alternative — leave as-is, accept the casual reader won't notice**. Rejected: brand-honesty is the project's whole posture; a factual error one click from the source canon that names the truth is the exact failure class brand promise `the seasons, ranked. no spoilers.` plus /about's `An experiment. Built and operated by one person. Free to use.` (`content/legal/about.md:78`) is committing the product against. Recommended (a) — single-line title swap, preserves the entry's editorial argument and the `verdict` simile, drops the factual error. Pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` with a `collectThemeFactualFirstClaimIssues` invariant for the returnee-specific case — for every themed-list entry whose `title` or `blurb` contains the literal `/\bfirst\b.{0,40}(all-returnee|all[ -]star|returnee\b)/i`, the named show + season must match a known-first-of-class allowlist. Initial allowlist: `{ slug: 'survivor', firstAllReturnee: 8 }` (Survivor S8 All-Stars). Strict at floor 0 since the rewrite drains the only known offender. Sibling test pin: extend `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` with a fixture case asserting any themed-list entry claiming `first all-returnee` for `show: survivor` fails unless the `season: 8`. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (themed-list title edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on either surface — the rotation preserves the editorial framing without naming the finalists, winner, or final-vote count). (URL: /themes/best-finales, /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-35) — e15f553 — issue: #328 — RESOLVED 4ae8c35: took the row's recommended Option (a). `content/themes/best-finales.md` entry rank=2 title swapped `The first all-returnee season closing on a final tribal that reads like a verdict.` → `The all-star format at its ceiling, closing on a final tribal that reads like a verdict on the returnee era.` Drops the factual error, preserves the load-bearing `final tribal that reads like a verdict` simile (and `final tribal` retention per pass-34 #327's load-bearing-show-vocabulary carve-out), and brings the title's editorial argument into agreement with the body's `the all-star format running at its ceiling` frame from canon entry #20. Pinned per the row's content-check invariant prescription: `scripts/content-check.ts` gained `collectThemeFactualFirstClaimIssues` — for every themed-list entry whose `title` or `blurb` matches `/\bfirst\b[^.]{0,40}?(all-returnee|all[ -]star|returnee)\b/i`, the named (show, season) tuple must be on the `FIRST_ALL_RETURNEE_ALLOWLIST`. Ships strict at floor 0 (`THEME_FIRST_CLAIM_STRICT = true`); the HvV rewrite drains the only known offender of the unqualified-first-class. The row's `{ slug: 'survivor', firstAllReturnee: 8 }` allowlist was expanded at fix-time to capture every existing in-corpus entry that makes a legitimate first-of-class claim (each verified against the show's own canon): `survivor: [8]` (S8 All-Stars — first all-returnee Survivor, 2004), `big-brother: [7, 22]` (S7 — first all-star BB, 2006; S22 — first all-star BB in fourteen years, qualified windowed first, 2020), `amazing-race: [11]` (first all-returnee Amazing Race, 2007). The header comment on the allowlist names the editorial scope (each tuple is verified against the show's own canon on the adjacent /shows/<show> surface) and the extension protocol (new "first <returnee>" claims must either match a row or rewrite the claim off the "first" frame). Nine colocated cases at `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` (new describe block `content-check — themed-list factual "first" returnee/all-star claim (critique pass-35, issue #328)`): (a) passes at the live catalog post-rewrite (no offending claims); (b) flags the historical defect — original HvV title trips the gate with allowlist-mismatch message; (c) passes for the allowlisted first-of-class entry (Survivor S8 All-Stars); (d) flags a "first all-star" claim on a non-allowlisted show with the not-allowlisted message; (e) flags the claim when it appears in the blurb rather than the title (asserts file column reads `entry #2 blurb` not `entry #2 title`); (f) passes for the rewritten HvV title (no "first" anywhere near returnee/all-star tokens); (g) is case-insensitive (catches Title Case future authoring); (h) does not flag a sentence where "first" is far from the returnee token (40-char-window boundary). The row's regex literal `/\bfirst\b.{0,40}(all-returnee|all[ -]star|returnee\b)/i` was tightened at fix-time to `/\bfirst\b[^.]{0,40}?(all-returnee|all[ -]star|returnee)\b/i` — the `[^.]` exclusion stops the match at sentence boundaries (so a "first" in one sentence and a "returnee" in the next can't conspire across a period), and the `?` makes the gap lazy so the engine never burns time backtracking on long blurbs. The row's allowlist shape `Record<string, number>` was widened to `Record<string, number[]>` to support shows with multiple legitimate first-of-class seasons (e.g. BB S7 first-ever + S22 first-in-window). No URL / schema / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — themed-list title edit + corpus invariant only; the smoke walker only pins 200/H1/no console errors on /themes/best-finales so it inherits the new copy cleanly. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (themed-list title edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on either surface — the rotation preserves the editorial framing without naming the finalists, winner, or final-vote count). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2455 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1362 e2e (8.1m).
- [x] [MED] [anon] Per-show canon-methodology cell `01 · WHO` body on 9 of 13 shows renders plural-collective editor voice while the component's own DEFAULT body in `src/components/canon/CanonMethodology.tsx:13-20` explicitly reads `One editor, named. Each show gets a single canon editor. The canon is one opinion, defended in public — we will tell you who, and we will not hide behind plural pronouns.` Every per-show `meth_who_p` override BREAKS that default's promise on the load-bearing methodology cell that explains the editorial model. Concrete carriers (9 of 13 show canon files): `content/shows/survivor/canon.md:6` (`tiered.tv's editors. We've watched Survivor since Pulau Tiga and we've replayed every season that lands on this list. The ranking is one editor's read first, calibrated against what reasonable Survivor fans agree on after a kitchen-table argument runs long enough. We aren't claiming to be objective. We are trying to be honest.`); `content/shows/dragrace/canon.md:6` (`tiered.tv's editors. We have watched Drag Race since the Logo years, and replayed every U.S.-flagship season that lands on this list. The ranking starts as one editor's read, then gets calibrated against what reasonable drag fans agree on after a long argument. We aren't claiming to be objective. We are trying to be honest.`); `content/shows/amazing-race/canon.md:6` (same shape, Keoghan summer in 2001); `content/shows/top-chef/canon.md:6` (same shape, Tom-Colicchio-era); `content/shows/project-runway/canon.md:6` (`tiered.tv's editors. The Project Runway canon is one editor's read, replayed against the agreed-upon shape of the show among fans who have watched every workroom cycle. ...`); `content/shows/the-challenge/canon.md:6` (same shape, MTV's first RV in 1998); `content/shows/love-island-us/canon.md:6` (`tiered.tv's editors, who have watched every American season from the Fiji inaugural through the Peacock-era breakout. We weigh production craft, format execution, and historical consequence — not whose couple lasted.`); `content/shows/love-island-uk/canon.md:6` (`tiered.tv's editors, who have watched every series from the 2015 Santanyí villa through the most recent Mallorca run. We rank as fans who follow the show closely, weighing each summer and winter edition against the full arc rather than judging it in isolation or by recency.`); `content/shows/traitors/canon.md:6` (`tiered.tv's editors, who have watched every American season from the mixed-cast original through the most recent all-celebrity run at Ardross Castle. We weigh production craft, format execution, and historical consequence — never who was lying.`). The 4 unaffected shows (Bachelor, Bachelorette, Big Brother, GBBO/Bake Off) carry no `meth_who_p` override and inherit the singular component default — they're correct. /about formally admits the singular form at two surfaces: `content/legal/about.md:29` (`The Editor's Canon is the editor's call — one person, one position per season, an 80–120 word reason.`) and `content/legal/about.md:78` (`An experiment. Built and operated by one person. Free to use.`). The pass-34 #322 closure (HIGH RESOLVED) addressed five visible-chrome surfaces of this same defect class — season-page byline (`Canon entry by tiered.tv Editors` → singular form), JSON-LD `author` field, theme `curator` default, featured-themes lowercase plural strip (`Cross-canon · curated by tiered.tv editors`), and ShowRanking literal (`One ranking, written by editors who have rewatched every season at least twice. We argue with the community out loud — but we ship one number.`). The canon `meth_who_p` field was not in pass-34's scope. The methodology cell is the surface where a reader scrolls to ask `who actually writes this?` — it's the editorial-model surface where the brand promise gets tested most directly. Bearings voice cue is `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken, never pretentious` (`plan/bearings.md:370`); the component default nails the voice (`One editor, named.`); the per-show overrides should mirror that, not fight it. Source: `src/components/canon/CanonMethodology.tsx:13-20` (DEFAULTS[0].body); 9 per-show canon.md files listed above (`grep -n "tiered.tv's editors" content/shows/*/canon.md`). Fix options: (a) **primary path — multi-show curator drain (one show per tick) rewriting each per-show meth_who_p override into singular voice that matches the component default + /about's promise**. Each rewrite recasts `tiered.tv's editors` → `tiered.tv's editor` (or `the editor running the X canon`); `we've watched` → `I've watched` (or `the editor has watched`); `we aren't claiming` → `the canon isn't objective. It's an honest argument.` The per-show editorial detail (Survivor since Pulau Tiga, Top Chef since the Tom-Colicchio-era, Amazing Race since Keoghan in 2001, etc.) is the editorial value of these blocks and gets preserved — only the voice shifts from plural-collective to singular-named. Same multi-tick drain mechanic as phase-26 / phase-31b / phase-34 / phase-41 / phase-43 that already drained five other defect classes; the `/ship-content` skill picks one show per tick until the corpus drains. (b) **alternative — drop the meth_who_p overrides entirely on all 9 shows** so they inherit the component default `One editor, named.` Saves the rewrite work but loses the show-specific watching credentials that carry the editorial weight (the per-show `since Pulau Tiga / since the Logo years / since the Tom-Colicchio-era` detail is what makes each block trustworthy to a returning fan; flat default loses it). Smaller blast radius but lower editorial signal. (c) **alternative — keep the plural form as a deliberate brand voice choice** and update /about + the component default to plural to match. Rejected: /about is the formal voting/editorial policy page and its singular admission has been the source of truth across pass-26 #282 + pass-27 #287 + pass-32 #311 + pass-34 #322 closure work. Reverting to plural would undo six passes of closure. Recommended (a) — multi-show drain preserves per-show editorial credentials in the singular voice that matches the default + /about. Pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` with a `collectCanonMethWhoPluralEditorIssues` invariant — for every `content/shows/<show>/canon.md`, scan the `meth_who_p` field for first-person-plural pronouns (regex `/\bwe(?:'ve|'re|'d|'ll| have| are| aren't| would)\b/i`) AND/OR the literal `/tiered\.tv's editors\b/i` (plural possessive form); if either appears, fail with the show slug + the offending substring. Initial floor: lax (`PLURAL_EDITOR_STRICT = false`) so the existing 9 offenders don't block the verify gate. Each drained show flips its row off the offender list at fix-time; the final tick of the drain flips `PLURAL_EDITOR_STRICT = true` (matches the phase-31b STRICT / phase-41 CROSS_SHOW_STRICT / phase-43 YEAR_TENURE_STRICT pattern, and the pass-32 #312 CANON_CLOSING_FORMULA_STRICT shape). Sibling test pin: extend `src/components/canon/__tests__/CanonMethodology.test.tsx` with a regression case asserting any fixture meth_who_p containing plural-collective pronouns is flagged at unit time, and a positive case asserting the component default body NEVER drifts off the singular shape (`/one editor, named/i` + `/we will not hide behind plural pronouns/i`). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (methodology-prose edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — singular-voice recasting preserves each per-show editorial credential without naming anyone). - [x] [LOW] [anon] Chrome metadata label drifts across surfaces for the canon-revision-date slot on the natural home → /shows → /shows/[show] click path. Home featured Survivor tile labels the slot `CANON REVISED` (verb-past). /shows/survivor hero stat strip also labels it `CANON REVISED`. /shows hero stat strip labels what reads visually as the same structural slot — third metadata tile under the hero, format `<number> <month-year> <label>` — as `LAST REVISION` (noun). A reader scanning the chrome across the click path reads two different label grammars in the same visual slot. The /shows referent IS technically different (the multi-show index's last revision, not a single canon's revision), but the chrome doesn't signal that semantic distinction — the two labels just disagree. The slot reads identically structurally: stat number + month-year + label, set in the same hero stat-strip pattern. Bearings voice cue is `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones` (`plan/bearings.md:370`); a peer commits to one label grammar and uses it across siblings. Same chrome-label-drift class as the pass-21 stat-strip captions sweep (RESOLVED, multi-tick), now at the revision-label sub-tile. Source: home featured tile renders `50 SEASONS RANKED / May 2026 / CANON REVISED`; /shows hero renders `13 SHOWS TRACKED / 298 SEASONS RANKED / May 2026 / LAST REVISION`; /shows/survivor hero renders `50 SEASONS RANKED / May 2026 / CANON REVISED`. Fix options: (a) **primary path — standardize on the verb-past form across all three surfaces**: `CANON REVISED` on show pages, `INDEX REVISED` on /shows. Single-component edit on the /shows hero stat-strip. Preserves the verb-past punch the show-page labels already carry; names the semantic referent (the index, not a canon) on the /shows surface. (b) **alternative — standardize on the noun form `LAST REVISION` across all three surfaces.** Loses the verb-past punch and the show-page-specific semantic (`CANON REVISED` names what's revised; `LAST REVISION` is generic). (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — accept that careful readers will reconcile two grammars. Recommended (a) — single-component edit on the /shows hero, drops the noun-vs-verb-past drift, names the index referent explicitly. Pin: extend the colocated test on `/shows`'s hero stat-strip component (likely `src/components/shows/ShowsHero.tsx` or equivalent — verify path at fix-time) with a regression case asserting the third tile's label matches `/^INDEX REVISED$/` (verb-past, indexed referent), NOT `/^LAST REVISION$/`. Sibling positive: extend the /shows/survivor hero stat-strip colocated test with a positive case asserting the third tile's label matches `/^CANON REVISED$/` so the show-page surface doesn't drift back to the noun form. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome label edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change). (URL: /shows, source: critique-pass-35) — e15f553 — issue: #336 — RESOLVED 6724a87: took the row's primary path (a). `src/components/shows/ShowsHero.tsx:47` `<div className="shows-stat-key">Last revision</div>` → `Index revised` — verb-past form, names the catalog-index referent the /shows surface actually labels (vs. the per-show `Canon revised` siblings on home + /shows/[show]). Pinned per the row's pin clause + sibling-positive clause: (1) `src/components/shows/__tests__/ShowsHero.test.tsx` gained a "labels the third stat cell as `Index revised` (not `Last revision`)" case asserting the canonical form is present at the DOM source, the rejected noun form is absent, and the regex `/^Index revised$/` holds on the `.shows-stat-key` element (CSS uppercases at render — the source-level regex is the durable pin); (2) `src/components/composition/__tests__/ShowHero.test.tsx` gained a sibling-positive "labels the canon-revised stat as `Canon revised` (not `Last revision`)" case so the show-page hero can't drift back to the noun form unobserved. - [x] [LOW] [anon] /themes featured-this-month section uses inconsistent CTA copy across three visually-identical sibling cards. Card 1 (Comebacks worth the swing) reads `read the list →`; cards 2 (Finales that stuck the landing) and 3 (Premieres that earned it) both read `read →`. The three cards sit in the same `src/components/lists/FeaturedCard.tsx` render via `ListsFeaturedRow.tsx`, frame identically (same bullet, same headings, same meta-strip, same border), so the first-card-different CTA reads as accident rather than editorial intent. The bare `read →` on cards 2 + 3 also leaves the verb dangling without a noun (read what? — the cards above carry a title but the CTA itself doesn't name its action target). Likely a per-card override (one of the three has a CTA-text frontmatter value the other two lack and fall back to a shorter default), or a render-side index condition. Source: /themes featured-this-month row, three card surfaces. Bearings voice cue is `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones` (`plan/bearings.md:370`); three identical-visual cards expect identical-form CTAs; a peer commits to one form and uses it across siblings. Fix options: (a) **primary path — standardize on `read the list →` for all three featured CTAs**. Preserves the verb + noun structure; reads as a deliberate editorial choice across the three cards; the noun gives the reader's eye a target. Single render-side change (the source of the per-card variance, fixed at the FeaturedCard render or the per-list frontmatter field). (b) **alternative — standardize on `read →`** — terser, but loses the noun. Three bare-verb CTAs read as a stylistic choice; one verb + noun CTA followed by two bare-verb CTAs reads as drift. Acceptable if the verb-only form is the deliberate brand convention, but the current state isn't that. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — accept the variance. Recommended (a) — single render-side change brings all three CTAs to the same form, matches the noun-bearing form already on card 1. Pin: extend the colocated test on `FeaturedCard.tsx` (or `ListsFeaturedRow.tsx` integration test) with a regression case asserting every rendered featured-card CTA matches the standardized form (`/^read the list →$/` after fix), bidirectional drift guard so a future authoring pass that drifts one card's CTA off the shared form fails at unit time. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (CTA-label edit only; no per-list verdict change, no canon position change). - [x] [MED] [anon] /themes/best-finales detail-page hero and the parent /themes index card (also surfaced on the / home card for the same list) describe the list with two unrelated editorial framings. The /themes index card + home featured-themes strip read `Closing runs that pay off the season they spent a dozen episodes building. The stakes feel earned, the last hour sits at the right altitude, and nothing gets handed over for free.` The /themes/best-finales hero lede reads `These finales land the season they were always making — texture intact, stakes earned, the last hour built at the size it needed.` Same list, two different promises about why it exists: card framing is `spent a dozen episodes building` + `the last hour sits at the right altitude`; detail framing is `the season they were always making` + `the last hour built at the size it needed`. The only structural overlap is the `last hour` clause, which lands at a different metaphor in each (`sits at the right altitude` vs `built at the size it needed`). A first-time reader clicking from /themes or the home featured-strip lands on a detail hero that rewrites the promise they just read, rather than expanding it — same defect class as pass-35 #324 cross-surface lede drift on /themes (`Some span the catalog, one lives inside one show.` resolved-to-`one stays inside a single show`), now at the list-vs-list-detail seam rather than within /themes itself. Source: `content/themes/best-finales.md` `description` field (card text) vs `tagline` field (or hero lede source — verify at fix-time, likely the long-form `tagline` field that the list-detail hero quotes per phase 19f schema). Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) is `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer commits to one framing per list and uses it across the click path, not two parallel editorials of the same idea on adjacent surfaces. Fix options: (a) **primary path — recast the detail hero as a deliberate expansion of the card description** so the click-through reads as one editor speaking once. Concrete shape: keep the card description as the establishing claim (`Closing runs that pay off the season they spent a dozen episodes building.`); rewrite the detail hero to open on the same nouns (`closing run`, `the last hour`) and extend the editorial point rather than restate it (e.g. `Closing runs that earn the season. The last hour sits at the altitude the show was building toward — texture intact, stakes earned, nothing handed over for free.`). Preserves the `last hour` clause both surfaces already share, drops the parallel-rewrite, keeps the editorial weight on the detail surface where the reader has the most time to read. Single content-file edit on `best-finales.md`. (b) alternative — recast the card description to match the detail hero. Loses the more verifiable claim the card already makes (the show spent a dozen episodes building toward this finale is a craft observation; the season `they were always making` is more abstract). (c) alternative — leave the two surfaces in parallel. Rejected — the click-path produces two different editorial promises for one list. Recommended (a) — single-source the framing on the card, expand on the detail page rather than rewrite. Pin: extend the colocated test on the themes-card / themes-hero components (likely `src/components/lists/ListTile.test.tsx` + `src/components/lists/ListHero.test.tsx`, verify paths at fix-time) with a cross-surface shared-noun assertion — for any list quoted by both the card and the detail hero, both surfaces must share at least one editorial noun-phrase (e.g. `last hour`, `closing run`); a regression where the two surfaces diverge on the load-bearing nouns fails at unit time. Sibling content-check invariant: in `scripts/content-check.ts`, scan each `content/themes/*.md` `description` + `tagline` field for a shared 2+ word noun-phrase between the two; lax-mode floor 1 initially, strict at floor 1 once the corpus drains the existing offenders. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial prose edit only; no per-season verdict, no canon position, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on either surface). - [x] [MED] [anon] /themes/best-finales body openers across the seven entries reach for `endgame` (3 of 7) and `closing run` (2 of 7) as the templated rewrite for `finale` — on a list whose subject IS finales. Verified shapes from the rendered detail page: #02 (Survivor S20 HvV) opens `The endgame compounds — every conversation freighted`; #03 (Top Chef Restaurant Wars) opens `Restaurant Wars takes the season into its endgame`; #04 (Survivor Winners at War) reads `pays back in the closing run`; #05 (Drag Race S2 Tyra-era — verify at fix-time) opens `The breakout season runs its endgame`; #07 (Big Brother Season X) reads `The closing run rewards the alliance play`. The hero lede itself also opens on `closing run` (`The closing run is where a season either pays off its promise...`). Five of seven entries (plus the hero) rotating between two synonyms for `finale` reads as the writer dodging the title noun rather than reaching for a fresh angle per entry. Same defect class as pass-34 LOW `final X` template (RESOLVED) — that finding addressed the title-noun template (`final tribal / leg / banishment / lip-sync`); this is the parallel defect at the body-opener layer the prior fix didn't sweep. The cross-entry voice elsewhere on the same list finds genuinely-different openers when the writer commits — #01 opens on Amazing Race's structural fact (`The Race always ends on a foot race...`); #06 opens on the season's pace (`Season 6 spends its run building toward a finale of working drag artists`). Those two prove the entries CAN open on something the synonym-rotation isn't covering. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer says `finale` in the body when the title is already framing the subject, then varies at the cast / staging / music / last-minute level rather than rotating between two stock substitutes. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rewrite 3 of the 5 offender openers** to vary at the sentence level (open on a structural fact, a cast beat, a staging beat, a music cue, a specific minute) instead of the noun level. Candidate rotations: #02 → `The endgame compounds` → `Eighteen returnees compress every old grudge into the last ninety minutes` (opens on cast scale + screen time, drops `endgame`); #04 → `pays back in the closing run` → `pays back in the last week of camp` (anchors to a verifiable structural beat, drops `closing run`); #05 → `runs its endgame the way the format always promised` → `runs the last four episodes the way the format always promised` (anchors to episode count, drops `endgame`). Preserves the editorial point per entry; drops the synonym-rotation tell. Each rewrite stays under the 50-80 word band per `themeEntryBody` in `src/content/schemas.ts:218-220`. Three lines touched in `content/themes/best-finales.md`. (b) alternative — let the body openers say `finale` directly when the entry warrants it. The title already framed the subject so the body can name it without redundancy. Smaller editorial change but reads as a deliberate plain-spoken choice. (c) alternative — leave as-is. Rejected — five of seven entries on a load-bearing themed list rotating between two substitutes reads as templated rather than authored. Recommended (a) — rewrites three of five offenders, leaves two intact (#03 `Restaurant Wars takes the season into its endgame` reads as deliberate `Restaurant Wars` callback + #07 `The closing run rewards the alliance play` reads as deliberate `closing run` rhythm with the alliance-play observation); drops the synonym-tic to a tolerable two-instance-per-list floor. Pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` `collectThemeBodyPhraseRepetitionIssues` (the within-list synonym-rotation invariant shipped at 1bc71ca for pass-28 #297) with a synonym-cluster check — for each theme, count occurrences of a known synonym-cluster (`{endgame, closing run, last act, final stretch}` for `finale`; cluster set extensible) across title + blurb + body fields; lax-mode warn at >= 4 occurrences across <= 7 entries (the current /themes/best-finales shape: 5 of 7 entries triggering); strict at floor 3. The cluster set lives next to the cross-page repetition floor the existing helper already maintains. Sibling colocated test extends `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` with three cases: clean list (no cluster hits), the current best-finales offender shape, and a multi-cluster fixture (proves the helper handles multiple clusters per pass). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial prose edit only; no per-season verdict, no canon position, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on either surface).
