- [x] [MED] [authed] /u/[handle] page carries a voice mismatch between the SSR meta description (third-person, written about a stranger) and the body copy (second-person, addressing the signed-in owner). Concrete observed shape on `/u/e2e` viewed as the signed-in owner: the `<head>` meta description reads `A reader on tiered.tv. Nothing on the public record yet.` while the body H1 + opening line read `Your record` + `New here. Cast one vote and your record starts writing itself.`. The two voices contradict on the user's own page: a viewer who shares their own profile URL (or sees a browser-tab preview / OG card preview to themselves) reads about themselves as if a third party is looking at them. The pass-22 #262 closure settled the H1 = handle question (the handle IS the chrome H1 anchor); the SSR meta description for `/u/[handle]` did not get the same self-vs-stranger branching pass. Same defect class as the pass-26 #282 + pass-27 #287 + pass-32 #311 + pass-34 #322 + pass-35 #329 cross-surface editorial-voice work — each prior closure brought one chrome surface into agreement with `/about`'s singular editorial promise; this is the chrome-vs-body axis at the same `/u/[handle]` surface, narrower scope. Source: SSR meta description for `/u/[handle]` (verify path at fix-time — likely `src/app/u/[handle]/page.tsx` `generateMetadata` returning a string-template description, no owner-vs-stranger branch). Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer doesn't speak about you to you in the same breath as speaking to you. Fix options: (a) **primary path — branch the `/u/[handle]` description on owner-vs-stranger viewer**. Owner view: mirror the body's second-person framing (`Your record on tiered.tv. Cast one vote to start writing it.`). Stranger view: keep the current third-person framing (`A reader on tiered.tv. Nothing on the public record yet.`). The branch reads off the request session's user-id vs the route's `[handle]` param — same shape as the chrome's `/api/auth/me` island already uses to render `Sign out` vs `Sign in`. (b) alternative — drop the per-state description entirely and use a single neutral form for both (`Reader profile on tiered.tv.`). Loses the editorial warmth `/about` and the body copy both commit to. (c) alternative — convert the body to third-person for self-view too. Loses the second-person warmth on the page where the reader sees themselves; reads as cold. Recommended (a) — mirrors the body voice when the viewer is the owner, preserves the current third-person framing for strangers (the path most readers will take); single `generateMetadata` branch. Pin: extend the colocated `src/app/u/[handle]/__tests__/page.test.tsx` (verify path) with two cases — (1) owner viewer: assert the rendered `<meta name="description">` content matches `/your record/i` AND the body content matches `/your record/i` (cross-surface positive parity); (2) stranger viewer: assert description matches `/a reader on tiered\.tv/i` AND body matches the third-person framing the page renders for non-owners (TBD at fix-time — `@e2e on tiered.tv` or similar). Bidirectional drift guard: a future refactor that re-converges the two views onto one description trips one of the two cases. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome-meta edit only; no per-season verdict, no canon position, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface). (URL: /u/e2e, /u/[handle] family, source: critique-pass-36) — 29baa5b — RESOLVED a76e6c0: branched `generateMetadata` in `src/app/(default)/u/[handle]/page.tsx` on owner-vs-stranger viewer using the same `auth0.getSession()` + `headerUserFromSession()` derivation the page body already runs for self-view. 2x2 description picker (populated/empty × owner/stranger): owner branches mirror the body's second-person voice (`Your public record on tiered.tv — votes, comments, the seasons you've weighed in on.` / `Your record on tiered.tv. Cast one vote to start writing it.`); stranger branches preserve the existing third-person framings verbatim (`<handle>'s public record on tiered.tv — votes, comments, the seasons they've weighed in on.` / `A reader on tiered.tv. Nothing on the public record yet.`). The auth0 boundary catch matches the page body's `.catch(() => null)` so a session failure falls through to the stranger branch — meta surface can't crash on auth degradation. The `noIndex` discipline is independent of viewer and unchanged. Pin: 5 new cases in `__tests__/page.test.tsx` `generateMetadata` describe block — OWNER + empty (`/^your record/i`), OWNER + populated (`/^your public record/i` + `/you've weighed in on/i`), STRANGER (different signed-in) + empty (`/a reader on tiered\.tv/i`), STRANGER (anon) + populated (`/'s public record/i` + `/they've weighed in on/i`), and getSession-throw → stranger fallback. Bidirectional drift guards: each branch's positive assertion is paired with a negative on the opposite voice (`.not.toMatch`), so a future re-convergence to one description trips here. Spoiler discipline P0 held — chrome-meta edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface. - [x] [LOW] [authed] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains comment thread stacks two affordances without a connecting line in the signed-in view. The live input prompts `Add a thought · no spoilers, please.` / `as @e2e` / `⏎`, then directly below — same column, no separator — the empty-state reads `No comments yet. Be the first to weigh in.` In the authed view the reader IS the would-be first commenter and the input is already in front of them; the empty-state instruction `Be the first to weigh in` is addressed at someone who hasn't yet been invited to comment, but the invitation just happened on the line above. Reads as two voices stacked: the input invites, the empty-state acts as if no invite was offered. The anon view (which renders `Sign in to comment` instead of a live input) needs the `No comments yet` line because there's no input to lean on — but the authed view already has the invitation built into the input affordance. Source: comment thread component at `/shows/[show]/season/[slug]` — likely `src/components/comments/Thread.tsx` or `CommentThreadEmpty.tsx` rendering an unconditional empty-state regardless of viewer auth-state. Phase 36 shipped the comment read path + the held-for-review affordance for the author's own pending comment, but the empty-state placement was not gated on viewer-can-post. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer doesn't invite you to comment in one breath then immediately tell you no one has commented in the next as if you weren't there. Fix options: (a) **primary path — gate the standalone empty-state on viewer auth-state** when the thread is empty AND the viewer can post (signed-in), suppress the standalone `No comments yet. Be the first to weigh in.` line. The input affordance already carries the invitation. When the thread is empty AND the viewer cannot post (anon), keep the empty-state — that's where it does work. Single conditional render in the thread component. (b) alternative — fold `Be the first to weigh in` into the input placeholder when the thread is empty AND signed-in (e.g. `Be the first thought · no spoilers, please.`). Carries the empty-state signal into the input but loses the cleaner default-state placeholder. (c) alternative — leave as-is. Rejected — the stacked affordances read as two voices not talking to each other. Recommended (a) — single conditional render, preserves both the input invitation in the authed view AND the empty-state nudge in the anon view, drops the doubled invitation. Pin: extend the colocated `src/components/comments/__tests__/Thread.test.tsx` (verify path) with two cases — (1) authed + empty: rendered DOM contains the input affordance and does NOT contain `/no comments yet/i`; (2) anon + empty: rendered DOM contains `/no comments yet/i` AND does NOT contain the live input. Sibling positive on non-empty case: when the thread has at least one published comment, neither empty-state nor input-invitation should render the `Be the first` framing on top of existing comments. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (empty-state gating only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no spoiler-text exposure — the gate runs on comment count, not comment content). Be the first to weigh in.` line drops when a signed-in viewer is looking at an empty thread (the live input affordance immediately above already carries the invitation `Add a thought · no spoilers, please.` / `as @e2e` / `⏎`). `CommentThreadLive.tsx` propagates `viewerCanPost={state.signedIn}` from the `/api/comments` read so the gate flips correctly after hydration. The pass-32 #314 closure rotated #02's body OFF the second `final tribal` to a `jury vote` framing — the cross-entry repetition with #04 wasn't in scope at the time. Same defect class as the pending pass-32 #313 / pass-33 / pass-34 within-entry phrase-repetition family, now at the cross-entry-within-show-within-list axis. The other five entries on the list (#01 Amazing Race, #03 Top Chef, #05 Drag Race, #06 Drag Race, #07 Big Brother) each terminate on a different structural beat — only the two Survivor entries share the `final tribal` close. Source: `content/themes/best-finales.md` entries #02 (Survivor S20 HvV) blurb second sentence and #04 (Survivor S40 Winners at War) blurb second sentence. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer doesn't reach for the same exact two-word verb-object pattern twice in the same list at two cards' distance, especially when both entries belong to the same show. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rotate #04 off the `final tribal` close** to a beat the all-winner endgame actually produces. Candidate rotations: `the fire-making round carries the weight of a roster that has played this game before.` (anchors to a verifiable structural beat the all-winner endgame is known for); or `the jury room carries the weight of a roster that has played this game before.` (anchors to the deliberation surface, drops the close-vote framing); or `the immunity table carries the weight of a roster that has played this game before.` (anchors to the physical end-game surface). Each preserves the entry's editorial point (the roster's prior-play history loading the endgame); drops the doubled `final tribal` with #02. Single content-file edit on `best-finales.md` entry #04 blurb. (b) alternative — rotate #02 instead of #04, but #02 has already been through two rotation passes (pass-32 #314 + pass-34 #322); rotating it a third time risks editorial fatigue on a single entry. (c) alternative — leave as-is. Rejected — two Survivor entries on the same list both terminating on `final tribal` is a craft-level slip even if each in isolation reads fine. Recommended (a) — single edit on #04, preserves the more-worked #02 rotation, drops the cross-entry close-beat repetition. Pin: extend the existing `scripts/content-check.ts` `collectThemeBodyPhraseRepetitionIssues` invariant with a cross-entry-within-show check — for each theme, group entries by their referenced `show` slug and assert no two same-show entries' blurb fields share a 2+ word verb-object phrase. Catches the next instance of this class at author-time; same shape as the existing within-entry stem-pair check, narrower scope (same-show grouping). Initial floor: lax (the current best-finales offender shape would warn, not fail); strict at floor 0 once the corpus drains. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial prose edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on either surface — `fire-making round` / `jury room` / `immunity table` are structural surfaces, not outcomes). - [x] [HIGH] [authed] /shows/survivor `What changed this week` Community Rank ticker surfaces 5- to 36-spot ordinal swings each driven by 1 or 2 votes, which loudly contradicts the home-page framing of Community Rank as `Plain. Restless. Honest.` and undermines the live half of the canon/community split phase 35 shipped. Verified observed shapes from the rendered show page (post-phase-35 live read path): `Heroes vs. Villains · Climbed 5 spots since the last weekly update · 1 vote / One World · Slid 36 spots since the last weekly update · 1 vote / The Australian Outback · Slid 9 spots since the last weekly update · 1 vote / New Era I · Slid 7 spots since the last weekly update · 2 votes`. Every entry in the four-card `ShiftsRow` ticker is driven by 1–2 ballots; the magnitudes are arithmetic artifacts of a thin vote pool, not signal. The home page explainer that runs alongside (`Signed-in voters self-attest they watched the season end to end; new accounts count at 0.25×, anonymous votes at 0.1×.`) makes the surfaced magnitudes *harder* to explain — a reader who reads both surfaces is told the system weights for confidence and then sees a 36-spot slide driven by one accounted ballot, and has no path to reconcile the two. The defect compounds against pass-34 #326 (already-pending HvV community-vote-state drift across ShiftCard / canon-ladder / vote-pair) by adding a fourth surface where the same low-N pool produces a different but equally noisy framing. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer doesn't publish a 36-spot weekly slide on one vote of leverage and call it `Restless. Honest.` Fix options: (a) **primary path — gate the `ShiftsRow` ticker on a minimum-vote-volume floor**: don't surface a `ShiftCard` for a season whose total `vote_count` is below a small threshold (initial floor: ≥ 5 distinct voters; tunable). The card row falls back to a small empty-state caption (`Early signal — not enough votes yet to show a weekly mover.`) until the floor is cleared. Preserves the surface's promise (live community signal) without publishing arithmetic noise as confident ordinal verdicts. (b) **alternative — keep the cards but rewrite the per-card line to expose the low-N caveat**: `Slid 36 spots · early signal · 1 vote` instead of asserting the swing as settled. Smaller blast radius (per-card label edit, no threshold logic) but the bare integer `36` still anchors the eye and the caveat is doing rhetorical work against the number. (c) **alternative — drop the integer magnitude entirely below the floor**: render `↑ early signal` or `↓ early signal` without the integer until the floor is cleared. Preserves the direction-of-travel cue (which IS honest at 1 vote) and drops the spurious magnitude. Recommended (a) — the threshold approach matches the `canon-mirror as always-working fallback` posture phase 35 named for the ranking surface, and the ticker has no editorial obligation to publish a card every week. Pin: extend `src/components/composition/__tests__/ShiftCard.test.tsx` (verify path) with two cases — (1) season with `vote_count < threshold` returns `null` / empty caption from the live pane; (2) season with `vote_count ≥ threshold` renders the integer magnitude as before. Sibling: extend `src/lib/community/__tests__/live.test.ts` `moverNote` describe block with a threshold-fixture case asserting low-N seasons are filtered before the row is computed (NOT after). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (gating + label edits only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure — the gate runs on vote count, not vote content). (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-37) — 5a650e3 — issue: #334 — RESOLVED this commit: took the row's recommended Option (a). Empty-state caption wired exactly as recommended: `ShiftsRow` gained an `emptyMessage` prop; when `community.source === 'votes'` and `shiftMovers.length === 0`, `src/app/shows/[show]/page.tsx` passes `Early signal — not enough votes yet to show a weekly mover.` and the surface renders the heading + editorial caption instead of bare cards (`.shifts-empty` CSS was already authored in `src/styles/screens.css:420` — anticipated styling, now consumed). Cards-only / caption-only / cards-and-caption (cards-win) branches all pinned in `ShiftsRow.test.tsx`. Picker floor pinned in `live.test.ts` `pickMovers` describe block: three new cases — (1) `MOVER_VOTE_FLOOR === 5` constant pin; (2) sub-floor exclusion (voteCount=1..4 returns []); (3) at-floor admission (voteCount=5 returns the mover). The pass-33 "keeps a one-ballot mover" case is superseded by these — a single ballot is no longer enough; the editorial framing now matches the surface's promise. Existing fixtures inherit the change cleanly: the `row()` factory defaults `voteCount: 10` which sits above the new floor, so every prior `pickMovers` case stays green. E2e pins widened in `community-page.spec.ts` (live mode, no baseline → empty-state caption asserted) and `show-home.spec.ts` (allows both canon-mirror absence AND live-mode empty-state caption; forbids bare cards — the surface invariant that pins the floor end-to-end). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (volume filter + editorial caption only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner/elimination/finale exposure — the floor runs on vote count, not vote content). - [x] [MED] [anon] /about and `/` describe the same vote-weighting system in different orders, with a different vocabulary, and disagree on whether the third (tenured) weight tier exists at all in the front-door framing. /about reads `Anonymous-guest votes count at 0.1×. Home reads `Signed-in voters self-attest they watched the season end to end; new accounts count at 0.25×, anonymous votes at 0.1×.` — two tiers, highest-to-lowest, no `7-day` boundary, no `1.0×` ceiling, and `new accounts` instead of `under 7 days old`. Loses the `7-day` boundary specificity /about currently names — drops information the existing surface carries. Rejected. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — accept that careful readers reconcile the two framings. Rejected — the tier-count drift is the load-bearing inconsistency, not the ordering. Recommended (a) — single home-page edit brings the front door into alignment with /about; the `7-day` boundary surfaces on the front door and the third tier stops being a /about-only disclosure. Sibling positive on /about: assert the same three-tier shape persists so the alignment doesn't drift in either direction. - [ ] [MED] [needs-user-call] [authed] /u/e2e own-profile `Your record` surface is structurally bare for an authed first-party view — total body chrome carries no record scaffold (no zeroed vote count, no zeroed lists-saved chip, no joined-cohort context) so the page reads more like a soft-404 than the record-you'll-grow-into framing the copy promises. Verified rendered shape: `Your record / @e2e / Member since May 2026 / New here. Cast one vote and your record starts writing itself. / Start with Survivor → / Browse all shows →` — body chrome is ~500 chars total, no stat strip, no metric chips. Compare to the rich stat strips every other major surface renders: /shows hero strip `13 SHOWS TRACKED · 298 SEASONS RANKED · May 2026 LAST REVISION`, /shows/survivor hero strip `50 SEASONS RANKED · May 2026 CANON REVISED`. The /u/[handle] surface is the one place a returning member sees their own name on the chrome, and it's also the surface that should make the record-system legible — what gets counted, what gets stored, what the cohort line means. The current empty state names the action (`Cast one vote`) but doesn't show the shape the record fills into. Same defect class as the pending pass-36 #335 [authed] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains stacked-empty-state-with-input finding (signed-in empty state reads as a voice not talking to the affordance), now at the own-profile rather than comment-thread layer. Phase 36 shipped the auth-aware chrome (header signed-in state on SSG/ISR, /api/auth/me read path) but the /u/[handle] empty record was not given a stat-chip scaffold during that ship. Source: `/u/[handle]` page route — likely `apps/web/src/app/u/[handle]/page.tsx` or equivalent (verify path at fix-time). Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer shows you what the record will look like, not just what you should do. Fix options: (a) **primary path — stub a zeroed stat-chip strip on /u/[handle]** in the same visual pattern as the /shows hero strip, matched to the record-shape an authed member will fill: `0 VOTES CAST · 0 LISTS SAVED · May 2026 JOINED`. Three tiles, font-size matched to existing hero stat-strip pattern (`src/components/shows/ShowsHero.tsx` or equivalent — verify path at fix-time), values pulled from the existing /api/auth/me + a small `/api/u/[handle]/record` aggregate (or computed at SSR if the existing auth-session payload already carries the counts). Preserves the empty-state copy AND demonstrates the shape the record fills into. (b) **alternative — keep the bare empty state but expand the copy** to name the three metrics in prose: `New here. Once you cast a vote, save a list, or jot a thought, this page starts filling in: votes cast, lists saved, comments left.` Smaller blast radius (copy-only) but doesn't show the shape, just lists it. (c) **alternative — leave as-is**. Rejected — the soft-404 reading is the defect, and the chrome pattern (stat strips on every other major surface) is the existing template. Recommended (a) — extends the existing chrome pattern to the one surface that currently breaks it, demonstrates the record-shape an authed first-party view should foreground. Pin: extend the colocated test on the /u/[handle] page component (verify path) with two cases — (1) zero-record authed view: rendered DOM contains the three stat chips with `0` values AND the joined-cohort tile; (2) non-zero-record authed view: rendered chips reflect the real `vote_count` / `list_count` aggregates. Sibling positive on the stranger-viewer pass (per pass-36 #336 closure): stranger-viewer of /u/[other-handle] sees the same chip pattern with the other user's record — confirms the chip scaffold is render-stable across viewer-vs-owner. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome scaffold only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no spoiler-text exposure — the chips count actions, not action content). (URL: /u/e2e, source: critique-pass-37) — 5a650e3 — [needs-user-call] flagged this iterate tick: recommended fix path (a) — `stub a zeroed stat-chip strip on /u/[handle]` `0 VOTES CAST · 0 LISTS SAVED · May 2026 JOINED` — directly contradicts the prior pass-28 #293 closure, which deliberately removed exactly this pattern. The closure is documented inline at `src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx:32-39` (the source-of-record comment): "the self-view used to stack a zeroed stat-tile row (0 / 0 / 0) above the prose, surfacing six pieces of zero/absence before the two CTA arrows — the surface read as an admin screen, not an editorial product. The stat tile row is now reserved for accounts with at least one vote so the structure earns its space; the self branch leads with a single warm editorial lede, the only surface that previously let the bearings voice (`knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken`) down. Reverts the pass-16 #217 zeroed skeleton." Two prior passes (#217 add, #293 remove) already adjudicated the zeroed-stat scaffold; this row's primary path re-opens that question without acknowledging the prior closure. The alternative (b) — copy-only expansion `Once you cast a vote, save a list, or jot a thought, this page starts filling in: votes cast, lists saved, comments left.` — is non-conflicting (no zero-tile chrome). The choice between (a) overriding pass-28 and (b) extending the prose is a genuine editorial call, not an auto-resolvable mechanical defect. Per `skills/iterate.md` §6.5, surfaced as `[needs-user-call]` and skipped this tick; the iterate drain picks the next-highest-scoring finding (combined pass-42/43 LOW HvV legacy-stinger cross-surface + within-page echoes).

- [ ] [LOW] [anon] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains spec-row metadata tile `EPISODE HEAT — peak run · the back-half` sits between concrete spec data (`FILMED · PREMIERED · EPISODES · FORMAT · CAST SIZE · HOST`) but is itself editorial-flavored rather than spec. The other six tiles answer where/when/how-many/what-shape (`Upolu, Samoa` / `Feb 11, 2010 CBS · Thursday 8/7c` / `14 / 39 days in country` / `Returnees · 2 tribes all-veteran cast` / `20 players · 10 heroes, 10 villains` / `Jeff Probst · twentieth season at the helm`); the `EPISODE HEAT` tile answers `where is the heat` with a vibe phrase (`peak run · the back-half`). A reader scanning the spec row for season facts hits a non-spec mid-strip and has to re-orient. The vibe phrase IS editorial work the page should do — but the spec row is the wrong tile to host it. Same defect class as the resolved pass-19 stat-strip-mixed-tile-categories work (RESOLVED, cross-surface chrome tile grammar discipline), now at the season-page spec row. Source: season-page spec row component — likely `src/components/season/SpecRow.tsx` or equivalent reading from `content/shows/survivor/seasons/heroes-vs-villains.md` frontmatter (verify component path + field name at fix-time). Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer keeps the spec row as a press-kit (facts) and the editorial sections as the editorial (vibes). Fix options: (a) **primary path — reshape the tile as a real stat**: `PEAK EPISODES — eps 9–13` or `RECOMMENDED START — ep 1, in order`. Anchors to a verifiable structural beat (episode range, recommended entry point) the way the other six tiles do. Single component + content edit. (b) **alternative — move the editorial framing out of the spec row** into a new `WHAT TO WATCH FOR` line in the existing `02 HOW` or `04 WHY` editorial block where vibe phrasing already lives. Drops the tile from the strip; preserves the editorial point. (c) **alternative — leave as-is**. Rejected — the spec-row vs editorial-section boundary is the chrome grammar; this tile crosses it. Recommended (a) — reshapes the tile to match its strip-mates without losing the editorial signal (the `eps 9–13` answer is the same fact the vibe was pointing at). Pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` with a `collectSeasonSpecRowGrammarIssues` invariant — for each season frontmatter, assert spec-row tile values match a small stat-shape regex set (numeric / date / proper-noun / quoted-range) and warn on free-form editorial-phrase values. Initial floor: lax (warn only); strict at floor 0 once the catalog drains. Sibling colocated test in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts`. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome label edit only; the `eps 9–13` framing names episode ranges, not outcomes — no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-37) — 5a650e3

- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/alone/season/vancouver-island-i (and other seasons without `watch_list`) — season page table-of-contents and inline section eyebrows skip ordinal 04 when the "What to watch for" section is absent, producing a visible gap: TOC renders "01 / 02 / 03 / 05 / 06" with no 04. A first-time reader sees the jump and either assumes the page is broken or that content is missing. Root: `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:386-388` conditionally spreads section 04 into the TOC array but sections 05 / 06 keep hardcoded `num: '05'` / `num: '06'`; the inline body eyebrows at lines 480 / 488 are also hardcoded, so both the sidebar TOC and the scroll-visible ordinals miscount. Verified: desktop + mobile captures of `/shows/alone/season/vancouver-island-i` render "01 The take / 02 The shape of the season / 03 Where it sits in the canon / 05 Adjacent in the canon / 06 Also appears in". Severity bumped LOW → MED at pass-68: the identical "01 / 02 / 03 / 06" gap (skipping both 04 and 05 this time, since these freshly-scaffolded shows also lack an "Adjacent in the canon" section) is now confirmed on two more shows, `/shows/jersey-shore/season/season-1` and `/shows/selling-sunset/season/season-1` — every single-season show currently in the catalog trips this same hardcoded-ordinal bug, not just seasons missing one optional section. Fix: derive each section's display ordinal from its index in the `sections` array (`(index + 1).toString().padStart(2, '0')`) instead of hardcoding; update both the TOC array and the inline article-eyebrow `<span className="num">` elements to use the same dynamic counter so the numbers always run consecutively regardless of which optional sections are present. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome numbering only). (URL: /shows/alone/season/vancouver-island-i, /shows/jersey-shore/season/season-1, /shows/selling-sunset/season/season-1, source: critique-pass-56, critique-pass-68) — 59ee5e4, 7173f3a — issue: #443 — RESOLVED f8c78cc: extracted pure `buildSections()` in `page.tsx` that filters the six section defs by visibility and assigns `num` from the filtered-array index; both the TOC and a new `numFor(id)` lookup for the inline eyebrows now read from that single array, so the two can never drift or skip independently. New colocated unit tests pin the reported gap shapes plus an exhaustive visibility-combination sweep (16 combinations, every one renumbers consecutively with no gap or duplicate). Verify gate green.
- [x] [LOW] [anon] /shows/below-deck — meta description opens with "No votes, no eliminations" which contradicts the page title's "community vote" promise in SERP context. Page title: "Below Deck — the canon + the community vote, no spoilers — tiered.tv"; meta description (derived from `tagline`): "No votes, no eliminations. Below Deck follows a working superyacht crew through the charter season — twelve seasons of captain authority, service pressure, and the friction of living where you work." In a search-result snippet, a user sees the title promise a community vote and the description deny votes in the opening clause. The intent — "No votes or eliminations" describes the show's docuseries format, not the site feature — is clear only after reading the full sentence; a searcher skimming the SERP scan reads it as a negation of the page's own feature. Verified: authed desktop capture title vs. description. Fix: reorder `content/shows/below-deck.md` `tagline` to front-load the show's format description before the contrast: e.g. "Twelve seasons of captain authority, service pressure, and the friction of living where you work — no game, no eliminations, nowhere to go below deck." The no-competition fact lands as a format clarifier, not a site-feature denial, and reads cleanly alongside the title's "community vote" mention. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (tagline edit only). (URL: /shows/below-deck, source: critique-pass-56) — 59ee5e4 — issue: #510 — RESOLVED 22c6248: reordered `tagline`/`card_tagline` to front-load the format description ("Below Deck follows a working superyacht crew through the charter season...") before the "No votes. No eliminations." contrast clause. Also rewrote `card_tagline` with fully divergent phrasing from `tagline` (was previously a near-verbatim subset), clearing the pre-existing #394 5-word-overlap content:check warning for this show as a side effect. Verify gate green: fast leg (2800+ unit tests, content:check 58 shows/717 seasons), build (958 pages), e2e 3231 passed (19.2m). Closes #510.
- [ ] [LOW] [anon] /shows/below-deck — era band chips "LEE ERA" and "TITHERADGE ERA" carry no parenthetical season ranges; same class as pending pass-53 finding on /shows/alone. A first-time visitor cannot map "TITHERADGE ERA" to a season range without domain knowledge of the Below Deck captaincy history. With only 5 of 12 seasons currently in the canon (all from the Lee era), the TITHERADGE ERA chip exists but produces an empty state ("No ranked seasons in this era yet.") with no guidance on which season numbers that era covers. The pending Alone finding (pass-53, row 20 in Pending) identified the same class: era band labels without `(S1–5)` / `(S6–12)` style ranges; this confirms the pattern is systemic across partially-seeded shows with captain-named eras. Fix: add season-number ranges to the era band labels in `content/shows/below-deck/canon.md` — e.g. `key: lee-era / label: "Lee era (S1–8)"` and `key: titheradge-era / label: "Titheradge era (S9–12)"` — same content-only repair as the Alone finding. - [x] [HIGH] [anon] /shows nine of thirteen show taglines close on the identical "Ranked without <verb> a single <noun>." template — read top-to-bottom on the tier-list page the repeating clause scans as fill-in-the-blank generation, not a knowledgeable peer writing per show. Verified on disk via grep: Drag Race ("Ranked without spoiling a single crowning."), Love Island UK ("...final couple."), GBBO/Bake Off ("...Star Baker."), Project Runway ("...finale collection."), Bachelorette ("...hometown."), Love Island US ("...recoupling."), The Challenge ("...elimination."), Bachelor ("Ranked without giving away a single final rose."), Big Brother ("Ranked without ruining a single eviction."). Top Chef has no such tag; Amazing Race uses a different shape ("We've ranked every leg of every one"); Survivor's tagline is longer-form. The inconsistency makes the repeating nine stand out more, not less. The site-wide "No spoilers" promise is already carried structurally by the chrome (header + footer `<ShieldBadge>` + the home-page brand promise) — every per-show repetition is editorial dead weight. Fix: drop the trailing "Ranked without <verb> a single <noun>." clause from at least six of the nine offenders and let each show's tagline close on its own editorial observation, the way Top Chef and Amazing Race already do. The clause's content is already covered site-wide; the tagline space is editorial real estate that should land an idea unique to that show. Pin with a `scripts/content-check.ts` invariant (lax→strict in the existing `STRICT` / `CROSS_SHOW_STRICT` / `YEAR_TENURE_STRICT` pattern) asserting at most N shows carry the templated trailing clause (initial floor: ≤ 3; final strict floor: ≤ 1) — same shape as the cross-show-coverage and year-tenure invariants already in place. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged — editorial-voice rewrite only. (URL: /shows, every show home-page card surface that quotes the tagline, source: critique-pass-12) — 60abb0d — issue #187 — RESOLVED at c4f0e6c: content-curator rewrote all ten offenders (the nine the critique named plus Traitors, which uses the same template — "Ranked without naming a single traitor."). Nine had the trailing clause dropped outright; each tagline now closes on the editorial observation already present in the penultimate sentence (Bachelorette → "warmer, sharper, and more willing to let the lead drive."; Bachelor → "running uninterrupted since 2002."; Love Island UK → "summer and winter editions included."; Project Runway → "still the one to beat."; Bake Off → "and somehow the most watchable."; The Challenge → "the genre's true combat league."; Traitors → "faster than anyone expected."; Love Island US → "finally found its footing on Peacock."). Drag Race was rebuilt as a two-sentence tagline matching siblings: "17 seasons of queens at a Los Angeles workroom, sewing through mini-challenges and lip-syncing for their lives on a pink-lit runway. The format that built a global drag economy and still sets the bar every other franchise chases." Big Brother extended the existing closer with a clause ("…and still the loudest room on summer television") rather than ending on the fragmentary "invented the genre.". Pinned with `scripts/content-check.ts` `collectTaglineTemplatedTailIssues()` + new `TAGLINE_TAIL_STRICT = true` toggle — strict floor 0, because the rewrite drained every offender in one tick (skipped the lax intermediate per the critique's "lax→strict in the STRICT / CROSS_SHOW_STRICT / YEAR_TENURE_STRICT pattern" guidance — no daylight to keep). Eight colocated tests in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` pin the regex shape across the four verb forms (spoiling / ruining / naming / giving away), the multi-word noun phrases (final couple, Star Baker, finale collection), the legal "Ranked by …" sibling (Top Chef), the legal "We have ranked every single one" sibling (Survivor / Amazing Race), and a live-catalog integration check reading zero offenders against the production content tree. Spoiler discipline P0 intact — editorial-voice rewrite only, zero spoiler reasoning revisited. Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + unit (incl. 8 new cases) + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 static pages), leg3 1348 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] /themes eight of ten themed-list blurbs close on the same count-of-shows tail ("across <N> different franchises" / "<N> shows, <M> [thing-noun]") — verified across `content/themes/best-*.md` blurb fields. Best premieres: "across six different franchises." Best finales: "six shows, seven landings." Best newbie casts: "six shows' worth of rookie rosters." Best returnee seasons: "five shows, one premise landed." Best post-merge runs: "Across five franchises." Best firsts/locations/comeback: same construction. The tail duplicates the structural "N SHOWS COVERED" / "N ENTRIES" stat strip that every /themes card AND every /themes/<theme> hero already renders — the blurb is paying twice for the same fact, and the parallel construction across eight of ten blurbs reads as one writer using one template (same class as the /shows tagline tail above). Fix: drop the count-of-shows tail from at least five of the eight offenders and let each blurb close on the editorial observation it actually has (already present in the prior sentences). Pin in the editorial drain via `.claude/agents/content-curator.md` + `skills/ship-content.md` Rule 3 — themed-list blurbs do not restate the show count; the chrome already renders it structurally. Optional lax-mode `content-check` invariant (floor: ≤ 3 blurbs carrying the count-of-shows tail) on the same pattern as the /shows tagline gate above; both can ship as one pure-script helper. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged. (URL: /themes, every /themes/<theme> card surface that quotes the list blurb, source: critique-pass-12, issue: #191) — 60abb0d — RESOLVED 9dc9418: rewrote ten themed-list `description` fields (best-comeback-seasons, best-finales, best-location-reveals, best-newbie-casts, best-non-winning-runs, best-post-merge, best-premieres, best-returnees, best-villain-editing, firsts) to drop the count construction outright; each blurb now closes on the editorial observation it already carried. best-reunion-specials (named-shows construction) + survivor-pillars (`category: single`, counts seasons) were already clean. Pinned with `collectThemeDescriptionCountTailIssues()` in `scripts/content-check.ts` + new `THEME_COUNT_TAIL_STRICT = true` toggle — strict floor 0, since the rewrite drained every offender in one tick (skipped the lax intermediate per the same lax→strict pattern STRICT / CROSS_SHOW_STRICT / YEAR_TENURE_STRICT / TAGLINE_TAIL_STRICT use; no daylight to keep). Two umbrella patterns covered: (A) `\bacross\s+<N>\s+(?:different\s+)?(?:franchises?|shows?)\b` catches `across six different franchises.`, `Across five franchises,`, `Seasons across six franchises`; (B) `\b<N>\s+shows[,'’]` catches the punch-list `six shows, seven landings.` and the possessive `six shows' worth`. Ten colocated tests in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` pin the regex shape across both umbrellas (5 offender shape cases incl. across-different / across-bare / across-shows / punch-list / possessive), the two intentional carve-outs (named-shows + `<N> seasons`), a 4-offender batch case, and a live-catalog integration check reading zero offenders against the production content tree. Brief updates: `.claude/agents/content-curator.md` (new "Description-discipline" sub-rule alongside the existing Title-discipline rule) + `skills/ship-content.md` Rule 3 (new bullet under Cross-canon coverage). Spoiler discipline P0 intact — editorial-voice rewrite only. Verify green: leg1 1948 unit (incl. 10 new cases) + 127 script + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build, leg3 1348 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] /themes all ten themed-list TITLES on disk share one syntactic mold — "<plural noun> that <past-tense verb> <optional object>" (verified via `grep -h "^title:" content/themes/best-*.md`): *Location reveals that announced themselves / Finales that stuck the landing / Premieres that earned it / Comeback seasons that landed / Non-winning runs that defined the season / Post-merge runs that delivered / Newbie casts that arrived ready / Villain edits that ran the season / Returnee seasons that paid off / Reunion specials that closed the loop*. On `/themes` itself the ten titles sit in a category-grouped list and the parallelism is visible top-to-bottom; on the home page's `HomeListsStack` four titles stack adjacent in immediate succession and the syntactic uniformity reads as templated rather than authored. The bearings voice law says plain sentences over clever ones and the design law warns against AI-generated tells; ten-for-ten on one syntactic shape is the canonical example. Fix: re-author three to four titles to break the mold — drop the relative clause for at least two (e.g. *Newbie casts that arrived ready* → *Rookie casts that showed up ready*, *Location reveals that announced themselves* → *Locations that did the talking*) and rewrite one or two into a different shape entirely (a noun-only or imperative title — *The premieres that earned the rest of their seasons* / *Comebacks the franchises actually needed*). Author into `.claude/agents/content-curator.md` Title-discipline block: themed-list titles vary syntactically across the catalog; no more than N of M may share the "X that Y-ed Z" relative-clause shape (initial floor: ≤ 5/10; strict floor: ≤ 4/10). Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged. (URL: /themes, /, every surface that quotes themed-list titles, source: critique-pass-12, issue: #190) — 60abb0d — addressed 361a89b (5/10 retitled to non-clause shapes; Title-discipline block pinned in content-curator brief)

- [x] [MED] [anon] /themes H1 "Themed lists. / Cross-canon." overclaims against the body — the H1 sub-line frames every list as cross-show, but the same page surfaces a `SINGLE-SHOW` filter chip, a per-card `SINGLE-SHOW LIST` eyebrow on `survivor-pillars`, and the body sentence directly underneath the H1 already admits the mix ("Some span the catalog, some live inside one show. None of them spoil what they rank."). A first-time visitor reading only the H1 expects every list to span the catalog. The same pattern appears on the home page's `HomeListsStack` heading ("Themed lists, cross-canon.") which `iterate` already addressed (#129 made it data-derived from `showsCovered > 1`) — but that fix only checks the *overall* cross-canon threshold (today 10/12 lists cross-canon → still reads "cross-canon."). The H1 on `/themes` doesn't even derive — it's a hardcoded literal in `src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx` (or wherever the H1 ships). Two clean shapes for the fix: (a) drop the "Cross-canon." sub-line entirely and let the per-card `CROSS-CANON / SINGLE-SHOW` eyebrow chips do the typing, OR (b) make the sub-line data-derived in the same shape as #129 — show "Cross-canon and single-show." when both populations exist, "Cross-canon." only when 100% of lists are cross-canon, "Single-show." only when 100% are single-show. The body's "Some span the catalog, some live inside one show." already does the work; the sub-line is editorial dead weight when it disagrees with the body two lines later. Same `getThemeStats()` helper #129 introduced is the source-of-truth. Pin with a `ListsHero.test.tsx` case asserting the sub-line never claims a coverage shape that disagrees with `showsCovered > 1` + `singleShowCount > 0`. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged. (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-12) — 60abb0d — issue #188 — RESOLVED at 6b87f06: option (b) from the row — `getThemeStats` (`src/content/loaders.ts:301-336`) gains `crossCanonCount` + `singleShowCount` derived from each theme's distinct-show count (theme is cross-canon when its `entries` cover >1 shows, single-show when exactly 1). `<ListsHero>` (`src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx`) picks one of three accents from the mix: both > 0 → "Cross-canon and single-show.", cross-canon only → "Cross-canon.", single only → "Inside one canon." `data-coverage` carries a new `mixed` value alongside `cross-canon` / `single-canon`. Today's catalog (11 cross-canon + 1 single-show after phase 41) lands on the mixed accent — the H1 finally tells the same truth the body and the per-card eyebrow chips already do. §5a: `ListsHero.test.tsx` gains the row's requested pin (mixed-mode must contain "single-show" and must not render the bare "Cross-canon." literal) + a positive mixed-mode render; `loaders.test.ts` `getThemeStats` test covers both new counts; two new shape cases pin the pure-cross and pure-single extremes (the home consumer `src/app/(default)/page.tsx` only reads `showsCovered` — additive change, unaffected). Spoiler discipline P0 intact. Verify green: leg1 1927 unit / 127 script / content:check 13 shows-298 seasons-13 canons-12 themes-3 legal, leg2 build, leg3 1348 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] /themes/<theme> `LAST REVISED` stamp renders relative-time ("this week" on best-finales today) while every other revised-stamp surface across the site renders calendar month + year ("May 2026" on the home `CANON REVISED` block, "May 2026" on /shows `LAST REVISION`, "2026" on the /themes `INDEX LAST REVISED` strip). Three different revision-timestamp formats across the same crawl is a chrome-consistency drift, and "this week" is the only format that will silently rot — by next week the stamp will be a lie no editor remembers to fix. The `content/themes/<theme>.md` frontmatter carries a `last_revised: <ISO date>` field (added in phase 19f); the page can derive a calendar-month stamp from that field with the same `formatLastRevised(iso)` helper the show-page hero presumably uses today (or, if no shared helper exists, the same shape /themes' overview stat strip already uses). Fix: render the list's `LAST REVISED` stamp from the source-of-truth field, formatted as calendar month + year (e.g. `May 2026`), matching the show-page convention. Drop any "this week" / "today" / "yesterday" branch — relative-time stamps require a freshness gate that the static site can't enforce. Pin with a colocated unit case asserting the stamp matches `MMM YYYY` shape, and with an e2e assertion on `/themes/best-finales` that `LAST REVISED` does not literally read "this week" (or any other relative-time token). Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged. (URL: /themes/best-finales, every /themes/<theme> URL, source: critique-pass-12) — 60abb0d — RESOLVED fcb98ae: `formatRevisedRelative` in `src/lib/themes-format.ts` collapsed to a one-liner delegating to the existing `canonRevisedLabelFromIso(iso)` helper at `src/lib/canon/last-revised.ts:39` — the same helper the home `CANON REVISED` block, the /shows `LAST REVISION` stat, and the show-page hero already use, so every revised-stamp surface across the site now ships from one source of truth. The "this week" / "this month" / "YYYY-MM" branches are gone; the stamp renders calendar "Month YYYY" UTC ("May 2026") on best-finales today and on every other `/themes/<theme>`. The unused `today` prop dropped from `<ListDetailHero>` and its callers (no other component referenced it). Colocated tests rewritten — `themes-format.test.ts` `formatRevisedRelative` now covers the calendar shape across May / April / January / 2024-August + asserts the regex `/^[A-Z][a-z]+ \d{4}$/` shape + an explicit `not.toMatch(/this week|this month|this year|today|yesterday/i)` guard + graceful drops on empty + unparseable ISO; `ListDetailHero.test.tsx` updated the meta-cell expectation `'this week'` → `'May 2026'` for the existing date and added a dedicated case asserting "January 2026" rendering with no relative-time tokens. New e2e `apps/e2e/tests/themes.spec.ts:248` walks `/themes/best-finales`, strips the `Last revised` dt label, and pins the `MMM YYYY` shape + the absence of relative-time tokens — a future regression to a relative-time formatter fails verify, not the next reader pass three weeks later when "this week" has rotted to a lie no one notices. Verify green: leg1 (1915 unit tests across 172 files, 127 script tests, content:check 13 shows/298 seasons/13 canons/12 themes/3 legal docs), leg2 build (1.02 KB themes/[theme] route unchanged), leg3 1348 e2e (+1 — the new LAST-REVISED pin). Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged — pure chrome-format fix. Issue #186 auto-closed via the fix commit's `Closes #186` trailer.

- [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/<show>/season/<slug> signed-in-no-vote viewer reads `YOUR VOTE · CAST YOURS THIS WEEK` (the head) and `YOU HAVEN'T VOTED` (the VotePair state pill) within four lines of each other — both nudging the same action against the same `1 NET VOTE`. The head meta `cast yours this week` (pass-11 #182, `src/components/composition/VoteRowHead.tsx:30`) is the imperative; the pill `you haven't voted` (pass-6 #160, `src/components/composition/VotePair.tsx`) is the diagnostic — two channels carrying the same job for the same viewer state. This is the same redundancy class pass-7 fixed for the comment composer (composer placeholder + empty-state line both told signed-in members the thread was empty). The two fixes shipped independently (#160 in pass-6, #182 in pass-11) without considering the overlap because pass-11 only tightened the head's grammar — not its overlap with the pill that already lived below it. Fix: pick one channel per state and let the other go silent. Two clean shapes: (a) when `vote-row-head` resolves to `signed-in-no-vote`, suppress the `VotePair` `you haven't voted` pill — the head already says it; or (b) on `signed-in-no-vote` swap the head meta to a non-redundant frame (drop `cast yours this week`, render `Your vote · weekly recompute` as a time-frame note and let the pill carry the action). Either shape works; (a) is the smaller surgery (one prop / one render gate in `<VotePair>`) and preserves the head's imperative voice; (b) keeps the pill the source-of-truth for state and rewrites the head into pure metadata. Pin with a unit case in `VotePair.test.tsx` OR `VoteRowHead.test.tsx` asserting the two strings (`cast yours this week` + `you haven't voted`) never both surface in the no-vote state. Anon viewers (no head, no pill) and signed-in-with-vote viewers (`change within 72h` head + `you voted higher/lower` pill — two different facts, not redundant) are unchanged. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged. (URL: every /shows/<show>/season/<slug> URL, source: critique-pass-12) — 60abb0d — issue #189 — RESOLVED 73c25eb: adopted option (a) per the row's preference (smaller surgery, preserves head's imperative voice). `VotePair.tsx` gates `vote-state-cap` on `signedIn && (votedUp || votedDown)` so the cap surfaces only as post-action confirmation ("you voted higher" / "you voted lower"); the no-vote channel is owned by `<VoteRowHead>`'s head meta. The unit pin (`VotePair.test.tsx`, new "haven't voted never surfaces" case) sweeps the signed-in no-vote state across 5 count values and asserts `stack.textContent` never contains `haven't voted`; the prior `signedIn:true + value:0 → "you haven't voted"` case inverted to assert no pill renders. e2e `vote-state-pill.spec.ts` reworked — the with-vote/no-vote branches assert pill is present with the appropriate copy OR absent, and pin the redundant copy never surfaces on the stack. `screens.css` + `api/vote/route.ts` doc-comments refreshed to reference #189. Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 1928 unit tests (incl. updated VotePair cases) + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 static pages), leg3 1348 e2e (incl. updated vote-state-pill spec).
  - issue: #189

- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows generateMetadata announces a B tier the page does not render — `src/app/(default)/shows/page.tsx:20` ships description "Reality-TV canons, sorted by how settled the ranking is. S tier is format-defining, A tier has the deep canon, B tier is in review." A reader clicking the SEO snippet expects a B tier section, but `content/shows/*.md` carries `tier: S` on 2 shows + `tier: A` on 11 shows + `tier: B` on zero. The /shows page itself renders only the S and A tier-head blocks (the tier rail is gated on `tier-head` rows with members > 0), so the description overclaims against its own rendered content. Fix: in `src/app/(default)/shows/page.tsx` generateMetadata, derive the description from the tiers actually present — iterate `getAllShows()` and only mention tiers with `length > 0` (e.g. "S tier is format-defining, A tier has the deep canon"). Same shape as the existing tier-head rendering rule. Pin with a colocated unit case asserting the description does not name a tier that has zero members. (URL: /shows, source: critique-pass-11) — bbd3082 — issue #181 — RESOLVED this commit (7d86e6a): new pure helper `buildShowsMetaDescription(populatedTiers)` (`src/components/shows/tierMetaDescription.ts`) composes the SEO description from the same TIER_ORDER + members-gt-0 filter the page uses to gate `<TierSection>` — the snippet can never name a tier the page does not render. Pattern mirrors the existing `tierLedeSentences` helper (one fragment per populated tier, joined in S → A → B order). `src/app/(default)/shows/page.tsx` `generateMetadata` now derives `populatedTiers` from `getAllShows()` + `groupShowsByTier` + the existing `showsForTier(...).length > 0` predicate and hands them to `buildShowsMetaDescription`. With today's catalog (zero B-tier shows) the snippet now reads "Reality-TV canons, sorted by how settled the ranking is. S tier is format-defining, A tier has the deep canon." — the B-tier overclaim is gone, but if a future `content-curator` tick lands a B-tier show the snippet drains the new fragment automatically, no second edit needed. Colocated unit tests for the helper (8 cases — empty / S-only / S+A / S+A+B / B+S ordering / dedup / A+B / always-ends-in-single-period) and for the page (9 cases — title + canonical pin, every catalog shape pins the description literal, plus the "never names a tier with zero members" invariant swept across every (S, A, B) presence combination, plus the empty-catalog opener-only path). `@/components/shows` barrel + barrel-test updated for the new export (documented runtime-key count 15 → 16). No new URL, no schema, no content edit. Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 171 unit test files (incl. 2 new, 17 cases) + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 static pages), leg3 1346 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains returns 404 — the natural-form slug a reader would type (mirroring the editorial display "Heroes vs. Villains" and the existing `27-blood-vs-water` / `33-millennials-vs-gen-x` / `35-heroes-vs-healers-vs-hustlers` / `37-david-vs-goliath` slug pattern) dead-ends because the source file is `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-villains.md` (no `vs-`). Every other season-name with "vs." on Survivor follows the `-vs-` slug convention; S20 is the one exception. External links typed by an unfamiliar reader or a clever-search guess therefore hard-404 instead of landing on the canonical page. Fix: either (a) rename the file to `20-heroes-vs-villains.md` and update internal references (themed-list entries, canon `season:` keys, JSON-LD), then ship a 308 redirect from the legacy `heroes-villains` slug for any in-the-wild links, OR (b) keep `heroes-villains` canonical and add a route-level 308 alias from `heroes-vs-villains` (and any other "natural-form" → canonical mapping the editorial display implies). Option (a) is preferable — it brings S20 in line with the other 4 `-vs-` siblings and removes the structural exception. Pin with a colocated route-redirect test (mirrors `/shows/[show]/canon` redirect contract). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-11) — bbd3082 — issue #185 — RESOLVED f3d039e: adopted option (a) per the row's preference — `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-villains.md` renamed to `20-heroes-vs-villains.md` so the canonical URL `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains` now serves the page (`generateStaticParams` walks the filename per 31a's filename-as-slug rule; no frontmatter slug-override owed). New helper `src/lib/season/slug-aliases.ts` exports `SEASON_SLUG_ALIASES` (one entry: `survivor: { 'heroes-villains': 'heroes-vs-villains' }`) + `resolveSeasonSlugAlias(show, slug)`; the season page handler calls it before `getSeasonBySlug` and `permanentRedirect`s on a hit, 308'ing any in-the-wild links to the new canonical. The same map is inlined in `apps/e2e/src/fixtures/redirect-fixtures.ts` (e2e package has no `@/` alias into `src/`) and feeds the existing `seasonRedirects` array, so `redirects.spec.ts` walks the 308 + Location + `rel=canonical` contract end-to-end with no spec edit (+1 test pass vs prior 1346). 9 test/fixture references that pinned the old slug updated to the new canonical (`ProfileComments`, `routes`, `rss`, `content-check`, `vote-state-pill`, `comment-read`, `user-profile`, `season-page` specs + `sample-urls` + `loaders` comment examples; synthetic content-check tmp-dir fixtures realigned for consistency). Colocated unit `src/lib/season/__tests__/slug-aliases.test.ts` pins the helper (8 cases — registered alias maps to canonical; every target is a valid kebab-case slug; no self-loops; resolver returns null for unregistered shows / canonical slugs / empty inputs; no two-hop chains). Historical CRITIQUE / plan entries that quote the old slug left intact as write-once snapshots. Spoiler discipline P0 intact — slug rename + redirect plumbing only, no editorial content change. Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 1912 unit tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 static pages — one-for-one rename), leg3 1347 e2e (+1 — new alias row picked up by `redirects.spec.ts`).

- [x] [MED] [anon] /themes/best-finales S20 entry blurb closes with "every conversation freighted with two decades of history before two decades had passed" — clever but mathematically wrong: Survivor S1 (Borneo) premiered 2000-05-31, S20 (Heroes vs. Villains) premiered 2010-02-11, so ~10 years (one decade) of franchise history existed at filming, not two. A Survivor reader scanning the editorial blurb registers the inversion and the count immediately. Source: `content/themes/best-finales.md:27` (the entry blurb). The conceit is reachable both via /themes/best-finales and via any rendering surface that pulls the entry. Fix: rewrite the closing clause in `content/themes/best-finales.md:27` to either drop the inverted-time conceit ("every conversation freighted with the show's first decade of history") or recast it without the count ("every conversation freighted with everything Survivor had been"). Plain sentences over clever ones per bearings. The Phase 43 year-tenure invariant doesn't cover this surface (no `est_year` substitution, no anniversary anchor) — this is hand-authored editorial copy that drifted, addressable as a one-line content edit. (URL: /themes/best-finales, source: critique-pass-11) — bbd3082 — issue #183 — RESOLVED this commit: adopted the row's second suggested rewrite — `content/themes/best-finales.md:27` blurb closing clause rewrites "every conversation freighted with two decades of history before two decades had passed" → "every conversation freighted with everything Survivor had been". The math conceit is gone (no decade-count literal at all — the rewrite is plural-stable, will never rot, and never re-invites the inversion); the rhetorical weight stays (the franchise-history beat is preserved as "everything Survivor had been"); the second sentence is untouched (the final-tribal-as-summary-statement coda still lands). Plain sentence over a clever one, per bearings. Self-check across `content/`, `apps/`, `src/`: the literal "two decades of history" appears once more at `content/shows/survivor/seasons/40-winners-at-war.md:23` — that one is mathematically accurate (S40 premiered 2020-02-12, twenty years after S1's 2000-05-31) and stays. One-line content edit; no schema field, route, page family, helper, or test surface owed. Spoiler discipline P0 intact — editorial-voice rewrite only, no per-season verdict surfaces. - [x] [MED] [anon] /themes/best-finales BB10 entry uses "S10 · Renegades Era" as the structural header slot — every other entry in the same list uses the season's marketed identity in that slot (HEROES VS. VILLAINS, WINNERS AT WAR, LAS VEGAS) or a bare season number (S07, S06). "Renegades" is the in-season alliance nickname (Dan Gheesling + Memphis Garrett), not a Big-Brother-marketed season title — naming it in the chrome header (vs. the body prose where the era frame can be set up) anoints an alliance for a reader who hasn't watched BB10. Spoiler discipline is P0 and the soft-leak class is exactly what the bearings preserve. Source: `content/themes/best-finales.md:54` `season_label: "S10 · Renegades Era"`. Fix: drop the alliance-nickname suffix from the header slot — set `season_label: "S10"` like the AR S07 / Drag Race S06 entries do — and either remove the "Renegades Era" framing from the blurb prose or reframe it without naming the alliance ("the season that taught Big Brother its strategic vocabulary"). Standing rule to author into `ship-content` Rule 3 / `content-curator`: header-slot `season_label` quotes the season's *marketed* title, never an alliance / arc / outcome nickname authored by fans inside the season. The blurb at line 57 rewrites "Renegades Era closes on the season that taught Big Brother its strategic vocabulary" → "The season that taught Big Brother its strategic vocabulary closes on a finale built for it" — the alliance-nickname leak is gone from both the chrome-header slot AND the opening blurb clause, while the in-season editorial framing (strategic-vocabulary beat) is preserved in body prose where the bearings allow it. Standing-rule landings: `.claude/agents/content-curator.md` gains a "Header-slot marketed-title rule" bullet in the Entry-title discipline block (alongside the existing exact-match rule), and `skills/ship-content.md` Rule 3 section gains the same bullet — future themed-list authoring drops the suffix and uses bare `S<N>` whenever the season's editorial `title` is an alliance/arc/outcome nickname (Big Brother's `Renegades Era`, `The Brigade`, `MVP Summer` flagged as the canonical examples). `content:check` already permits the bare form (verified: `scripts/content-check.ts:202` only enforces the suffix-vs-title match when ` · ` is present); no schema change owed. Spoiler discipline P0 intact — the fix removes a soft-leak, the blurb's strategic-era framing stays in body prose. - [x] [MED] [authed] VoteRowHead signed-in-no-vote meta "cast within the week" reads ambiguously next to its sibling state "change within 72h" — `src/components/composition/VoteRowHead.tsx:30` ships `{ title: 'Your vote', meta: 'cast within the week' }` for the `signed-in-no-vote` state. The 72h sibling at line 31 is unambiguous (verb + object + window: "change [your vote] within 72h"); "cast within the week" can parse as imperative ("cast [your vote] within the week") OR descriptive ("votes are cast within the week, then tallied"), and the surrounding panel already announces the weekly cadence elsewhere ("canon position recomputes weekly") so the reader sees the weekly window stated twice without the action becoming the obvious read. Phase 36 shipped the three-state head as #177 (e2c732d / VoteRowHead.tsx); pass-11 found the no-vote state's meta needs a second pass. Fix: rewrite the `signed-in-no-vote` meta to match the 72h sibling's verb-first imperative shape — e.g. `'cast yours this week'` or `'cast before the recompute'`. One-line change in the `COPY` map; the 8 colocated unit cases already pin the resolver behavior, only the literal copy assertion (if any) needs updating. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged. (URL: every /shows/<show>/season/<slug> URL, source: critique-pass-11) — bbd3082 — issue #182 — RESOLVED this commit: `src/components/composition/VoteRowHead.tsx:30` `COPY['signed-in-no-vote'].meta` rewrites `'cast within the week'` → `'cast yours this week'` (verb + pronoun-object + window), structurally mirroring the unambiguous sibling `'change within 72h'` (verb + object + window). The descriptive parse ("votes are cast within the week, then tallied") is closed off — `yours` forces the pronoun object onto the verb, making the imperative the only valid read. The "this week" window stays the floor the period-cadence panel already announces, so the panel's weekly statement still parses as one repeated framing, not two competing ones. The 9-line file header comment example block updated alongside the literal so the docstring stays accurate. Colocated unit test at `src/components/composition/__tests__/VoteRowHead.test.tsx:68` updated `toHaveTextContent('cast within the week')` → `toHaveTextContent('cast yours this week')` (one literal swap, same describe block, same 8-case branch coverage — anon default before hydration / signedIn=false keeps anon / signedIn=true value=0 → signed-in-no-vote / signedIn=true value=1 OR value=-1 → signed-in-with-vote / fetch-failure keeps anon / ok=false response keeps anon / correct query params). No other surface holds the string (grep across `src/` + `apps/e2e/` returned only the three resolved sites). Spoiler discipline P0 intact — copy gates on auth/vote state, never on editorial verdict. The fix is one-line per the suggested-fix spec; the resolver behavior + SSR-anon default + fetch-failure fallback shipped at #177 hold unchanged. - [x] [LOW] [anon] "We're not pretending to be objective; we are pretending to be honest" — a clever-template flourish replicated as the closing sentence of `meth_who_p` across at least 5 canon files (`content/shows/survivor/canon.md:6`, `content/shows/amazing-race/canon.md:6`, `content/shows/top-chef/canon.md:6`, `content/shows/the-challenge/canon.md:6`, `content/shows/dragrace/canon.md:6`). The construction (semicolon, parallel paradox, deliberate cleverness) is exactly the form `plan/bearings.md` declares against ("Plain sentences over clever ones … never pretentious"). The surrounding sentences are in-voice; only the closing flourish drifts, and the drift compounds because the same line appears across every canonical-show methodology block — a reader hitting more than one show canon reads the same clever beat twice. Fix: rewrite the closing line uniformly to a plain form — e.g. "We aren't claiming to be objective. We are trying to be honest." — and update `.claude/agents/content-curator.md` so future canon `meth_who_p` blocks are born without the parallel-paradox flourish. Eight or so files; small content edit + agent-brief update. Survivor uniquely used the contracted "We're not pretending…"; the other four used the uncontracted "We are not pretending…"; both forms collapse to the same plain pair so a reader hitting more than one canon now lands on the same in-voice close rather than reading the parallel-paradox beat twice. `.claude/agents/content-curator.md` updated in two places so future canons are born without the flourish: the Voice section's "Plain sentences over clever ones" bullet (lines 49–60) now documents the `meth_who_p` closing-sentence trap explicitly with the offending construction and the in-voice replacement as before/after examples; the canon frontmatter YAML reference (line 369) gains a comment block above `meth_who_h` pinning the rule for an author following the schema scaffold. Grep across `content/`, `src/`, `apps/`, `scripts/`, `design/` confirms zero live occurrences of the prior closing line; the remaining matches in `plan/CRITIQUE.md` + `plan/PHASE_CANDIDATES.md` are inside the audit-log entries that quote the original finding (correct — those are records, not rendered copy). No URL, schema field, helper module, or page family change; no test surface owed (the `meth_who_p` field is content frontmatter consumed by the existing canon page renderer the show-home/canon e2e walks already exercise). Spoiler discipline P0 intact — voice rephrase only, no per-season editorial verdict surfaces revisited. - [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/<show> meta description bolts an SEO prefix in front of the full tagline and overshoots Google's truncation point — `<meta name="description">` on /shows/survivor measures 273 chars ("Survivor, every season ranked: the Editor's Canon and the live community vote on one page. 50 seasons of strangers on a beach. The genre that invented itself in episode one, and has spent twenty-five years rediscovering what it is. We've ranked every single one."). Google snippets clip around 155 chars, so a search result lands on "Survivor, every season ranked: the Editor's Canon and the live community vote on one page. 50 seasons of strangers on a beach. The genre that invented itse..." — the editorial half (the part a reader clicks on) sits behind the boilerplate prefix and gets truncated. The boilerplate prefix is the same string on every show, so the variable / editorial half is the part that gets cut. Pattern applies to all 13 show pages. Fix: drop the "every season ranked: the Editor's Canon and the live community vote on one page" prefix; lead the description with the editorial `tagline` (the long form — the show page is the one surface CLAUDE.md reserves for the full tagline, and it's the one a search reader is most likely to land on). If the resulting description still runs over 155 chars on any show, switch to `card_tagline` for that show (Survivor's card_tagline is 110 chars and reads cleanly as a stand-alone first sentence). Pin the contract with a colocated test asserting the meta description equals `card_tagline ?? tagline` and is ≤ 160 chars on every /shows/<show>. (URL: /shows/survivor, every /shows/<show> URL, source: critique-pass-10) — 1941e89

- [x] [MED] [anon|authed] /shows/<show>/season/<slug> renders the vote-row eyebrow "Your vote / change within 72h" unconditionally on every season page — anon viewers (no account) read copy that implies an account context they don't have, and authed-no-vote viewers (signed in, never voted on this season) read "change within 72h" implying a vote-to-change they haven't cast. Issue #160 fix (27e732d) added the "you haven't voted / you voted higher / you voted lower" pill *inside* the vote-pair for signed-in viewers (good), but the surrounding `<div className="info-row-head">` at `src/components/composition/SeasonInfoCard.tsx:106-108` still ships `<span>Your vote</span><span className="meta">change within 72h</span>` with no `signedIn` or `hasVote` gate, so the eyebrow's premise is wrong in two of the three viewer states (anon, signed-in-no-vote). The fix shape mirrors the existing pill plumbing: thread `signedIn` (and ideally `hasVote`) through `SeasonInfoCard` to the row head, then render three states — anon ("Cast a vote · sign in to weigh in"), signed-in-no-vote ("Your vote · cast within the week"), signed-in-with-vote (current copy "Your vote · change within 72h"). Same `info-row-vote` testid stays; only the head copy switches by state. (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-villains, every /shows/<show>/season/<slug> URL, source: critique-pass-10) — 1941e89 — issue #177 — RESOLVED this commit: new `<VoteRowHead>` client component (`src/components/composition/VoteRowHead.tsx`) reuses the same `/api/vote` GET that `<VotePair>` already fetches on mount (the route was extended at #160 to return `signedIn` alongside `value`/`count`) and renders one of three head copies based on `{ signedIn, value }` — anon ("Cast a vote · sign in to weigh in"), signed-in-no-vote ("Your vote · cast within the week"), signed-in-with-vote ("Your vote · change within 72h"). The SSR fallback is the anon copy (most viewers of a static season page are unauthenticated; the same `useState('anon')` initial keeps the hydration markup identical to SSR — no mismatch). On a fetch failure the component holds the anon default rather than rendering an error eyebrow. `SeasonInfoCard` gains an optional `voteRowHead?: ReactNode` slot that replaces the prior static `<div className="info-row-head"><span>Your vote</span><span className="meta">change within 72h</span></div>`; when the slot is absent the static fallback still renders (back-compat for any non-season call site). `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx` wires `<VoteRowHead targetType="season" targetId={seasonTargetId} />` into the `<SeasonInfoCard voteRowHead>` slot so all 298 SSG season URLs adopt the three-state head automatically. Tests pin the contract end-to-end: 8 colocated unit cases for `<VoteRowHead>` (anon default before hydration; signedIn=false-after-fetch keeps anon; signedIn=true value=0 → signed-in-no-vote; signedIn=true value=1 OR value=-1 → signed-in-with-vote; fetch-failure keeps anon; ok=false response keeps anon; correct `/api/vote?targetType=...&targetId=...` query params). `<SeasonInfoCard>` gains 2 colocated cases (static fallback when `voteRowHead` is absent; slot replaces the default head when provided). Barrel test bumps the documented runtime-keys count 27 → 28 and adds the `VoteRowHead` identity-equality check. Same `info-row-vote` testid stays — the e2e `expectVisible` walk across every season URL keeps passing without fixture changes. Spoiler discipline P0 intact — copy gates on auth/vote state, never on a season's editorial verdict. Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 1825 unit tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 static pages), leg3 1346 e2e (all prior cases + the season-page smoke walk re-rendering the new client component across every URL).

- [x] [LOW] [authed] /u/[handle] self-view is indistinguishable from a stranger viewing the same profile AND offers no next-action affordance on the empty state — the page renders `@<handle>`, `Member since <month> <year>`, the empty-state sentence ("Nothing on the public record yet. Vote on a season pair, weigh in on a thread, and it will land here."), and the chrome — but no "this is your profile" marker, no "Edit display name", no "Start with Survivor" link, no link to a season pair to act on the empty-state prompt. A signed-in member landing on their own empty profile has zero next-action surface from the page itself; the prompt to act is rhetorical. The signed-in viewer also can't visually distinguish their own profile from a stranger's coincidentally-handled one. Fix: on self-view (viewer == owner), append one inline CTA inside `<ProfileEmpty>` — e.g. "Start with Survivor →" linking to `/shows/survivor` (the featured show) — so the prompt to act is one click, not a memory test. Optionally, add a small "Your profile" eyebrow adjacent to the handle (or surface the Sign out affordance from chrome). Empty-branch only — the populated branch (post-vote) is already the right summary per #164. (URL: /u/e2e, every /u/[handle] self-view of an empty profile, source: critique-pass-10) — 1941e89 — issue #178 — RESOLVED this commit: `<ProfileEmpty>` (`src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx`) gains an optional `selfView?: { showName, showHref }` prop. When passed, the component appends a single `<Link data-testid="profile-empty-cta">Start with {showName} →</Link>` below the canonical sentence; the sentence + testid + muted-ink class contract are unchanged for the stranger-view path (the existing 3 unit cases stay green). `src/app/(default)/u/[handle]/page.tsx` reads `auth0.getSession()`, derives the viewer handle via the existing `headerUserFromSession()`, compares to `profile.handle`, and on the empty-branch + self-view path resolves `getFeaturedShow()` (Survivor today, follows the home hero's `featured: true` source-of-truth so a future re-feature drains automatically — no hardcoded show slug). Stranger viewing an empty profile sees the unchanged sentence-only state. The populated branch is untouched (per #164 the stat strip + Recent comments already carry the summary). Tests pin both branches: 4 new colocated unit cases in `ProfileEmpty.test.tsx` (no CTA when `selfView` absent; CTA links to featured show with verbatim "Start with {name} →" copy; show name + href flow verbatim — no hardcoded "Survivor"; sentence + CTA coexist on self-view). The existing `apps/e2e/tests/user-profile.spec.ts` populated path stays green (no empty-branch e2e fixture today — the e2e DB seeds populated profiles by design). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (CTA links to a public show page, no per-season verdict, no held-comment leakage). Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 1827 unit tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 static pages), leg3 1346 e2e.
