- [x] [MED] [anon] /, /shows, /shows/survivor all serve the identical 3-sentence Survivor lede paragraph ("50 seasons of strangers on a beach. The genre that invented itself in episode one, and has spent twenty-five years rediscovering what it is. We've ranked every single one.") verbatim — same string on the home featured-show block, the /shows S-tier card, and the /shows/survivor page lede. A reader navigating Home → All shows → Survivor reads the identical paragraph three times across the primary nav path; makes the canon's most-promoted show read content-thin. Source: `content/shows/survivor.md:11` `tagline:` field is what all three surfaces consume (home pull, /shows S-tier blurb, /shows/survivor lede). Fix: author a tighter card-lede (one-sentence) for the home + /shows S-tier surfaces and reserve the full three-sentence tagline for the show page itself, OR split into a `card_tagline` (short) + `tagline` (full) field pair and migrate the three callers; same pattern applies to every featured show (Drag Race, Top Chef etc.) so the schema change drains across all 12 shows once. (URL: /, /shows, /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-9) — 8b1018d — issue #170 — RESOLVED this commit: adopted option 2 from the row — split into `card_tagline` (short, optional) + `tagline` (full). `src/content/schemas.ts:43` adds `card_tagline: z.string().min(1).max(160).optional()` to the strict show frontmatter; `src/content/loaders.ts:184` `materializeShow` also tokenizes `card_tagline` (so `{yearsWord}` / `{years}` substitution works on either field). Two card consumers prefer the new field with tagline fallback: `src/components/home/HomeHero.tsx:50` (`featured.card_tagline ?? featured.tagline ?? featured.blurb`) for the home featured cover-sub and `src/components/shows/ShowsTile.tsx:63` (`show.card_tagline ?? show.tagline`) for every /shows tier tile. The show page hero (`<ShowHero>` consuming `show.tagline`) is unchanged — it remains the only surface that quotes the full tagline. `content/shows/survivor.md:12` authors `card_tagline: "The format that invented itself in episode one, and is still finding new ways to ask who you really are."` — a single confident sentence, distinct from `blurb` ("50 seasons. One torch at a time.") and from the full `tagline`. CLAUDE.md "twelve fields" table updated to "up to thirteen" with the new optional row + a `required` column for clarity; the design law's anti-graphical-field rule + the explicit carve-out ("Non-graphical editorial metadata ... is permitted when a page genuinely needs it") cover the addition. Other shows fall back to `tagline` and need no migration today — a future `content-curator` tick can drain `card_tagline` for the other featured/S-tier shows when the editor authors them. §5a: two unit cases pin the consumption preference (`HomeHero.test.tsx` "prefers card_tagline over tagline when present"; `ShowsTile.test.tsx` "prefers card_tagline over tagline when authored"), two loader cases pin the materialization (`loaders.test.ts` token substitution inside an authored `card_tagline` + leaves the field undefined when omitted), and a new e2e (`home.spec.ts` "Survivor lede differs between card surfaces and the show page hero") walks /, /shows, /shows/survivor and asserts the show page hero's lede string is not equal to either card surface — the literal regression the critique row described. Spoiler discipline P0 intact — content field with no per-season editorial verdict. Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 1766 unit tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows/298 seasons/13 canons/12 themes/3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 static pages), leg3 1330 e2e (new home.spec assertion + 1329 prior cases).
  - issue: #170
- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/survivor "When do we revisit?" copy reads "We revisit the bottom of the canon less often than the top; you do not learn much by replaying Thailand." — Survivor S5 (Thailand) is named directly as an example of "the bottom of the canon," which pre-reveals editorial verdict on a season before the reader navigates to its canon entry. The brand promise is "no spoilers"; the spoiler-adjacent class (editorial verdict leakage on a specific season the reader may not have watched) is the same category of harm — the reader who hasn't watched Thailand now knows the editors rank it near the bottom before reaching its page. Fix: replace "replaying Thailand" with a generic reference ("replaying the bottom quartile" or "replaying the tail"), keeping the rhetorical beat but not naming a season. Source: search `content/shows/survivor.md` body for the literal "Thailand." (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-9) — 8b1018d — RESOLVED this commit: `content/shows/survivor/canon.md:10` `meth_when_p:` rewrites "replaying Thailand" → "replaying the tail" — the second clause still answers the first ("the bottom of the canon" / "the tail"), the rhetorical beat is preserved, and no specific season is named. Self-found parallel: `content/shows/amazing-race/canon.md:10` carried the exact same structural class ("the Family Edition does not reward a third pass" — AR S8 named as a bottom-of-canon example); fixed in the same commit by adopting the "rough drafts do not reward a third pass" idiom that Top Chef, Drag Race, and The Challenge already use across their `meth_when_p:` lines. Two single-line content edits, no schema/component/page change. Verify gate green: leg1 (127 script tests + 1763 unit tests + 13 shows/298 seasons/13 canons/12 themes/3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 static pages), leg3 1329 e2e — methodology block re-rendered cleanly on every canon page across the catalog. Spoiler discipline P0 intact — no per-season editorial verdict leaks through methodology copy on either show.

- [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-villains at 375px drops the "ON THIS PAGE" right-rail TOC entirely — desktop renders a six-item jump nav ("01 The take … 06 Also appears in") that maps to a long, structurally-numbered body, but the mobile capture goes straight from EPISODE HEAT into "01 THE TAKE" with no jump affordance. A phone reader has to thumb-scroll the entire piece to skim. Reduced-motion path drops the chevron transition. - [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-villains "YOUR VOTE / CHANGE WITHIN 72H" block does not disambiguate the not-yet-voted state from the just-voted state for the signed-in viewer — a member with zero votes cast still sees the section labeled "YOUR VOTE" next to "1 NET VOTE" with no pill or affordance distinguishing "you haven't voted" from "you voted yes/no". /u/<handle> confirms 0 SEASONS VOTED for the bot user, so today the answer is they have not voted — but the page does not say so. The auth-island data the comment composer reads is already wired; piggyback on it. Fix: render a state pill inside the YOUR VOTE block ("you haven't voted" / "you voted higher" / "you voted lower"). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-villains, source: critique-pass-6) — 8d71196 — RESOLVED this commit: `/api/vote` GET now returns `signedIn: boolean` alongside the read-back value (sourced from the same `resolveSessionId()` helper POST uses, so the pill copy and the count next to it can never disagree); `<VotePair>` consumes the flag in its mount fetch + tracks it in component state. The `.vote-pair` is now wrapped in a `.vote-pair-stack` flex column so the pill renders above the buttons; signed-in members see one of the three copies (`you haven't voted` / `you voted higher` / `you voted lower`) tinted in the sentiment color the rest of the system already uses (`--s-warm-up` / `--s-warm-down`), anon viewers see no pill at all (defensive — the `signedIn === true` gate also defaults closed when the field is absent). Clicks update the pill text optimistically from the same reducer that drives the count — no second round-trip. Tests: API route gains a `GET /api/vote — signedIn surface (#160)` describe (4 cases: anon-with-cookie, anon-no-cookie, authed sub, 500-error body omits the field); `<VotePair>` gains a `state pill (signed-in only)` describe (6 cases: signed-out hides; three value mappings; click-flip-without-refetch; missing-field defensive default). New e2e `apps/e2e/tests/vote-state-pill.spec.ts` walks authed (pill visible + one of 3 legal copies + click-flips) and anon (pair visible, pill absent) at `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-villains`. `apps/e2e/src/fixtures/page-reads.ts` adds `vote-pair-stack` so the smoke walker pins the wrapper across every season URL. Spoiler/account-confusion P0 intact — the pill never surfaces to anon viewers (would imply a viewer identity they don't have). No URL, schema, or page family change.
  - issue: #160

- [x] [LOW] [anon] Survivor "twenty-five years" copy is hardcoded in 5 places and currently accurate (S1 premiered 2000-05-31, today 2026-05-23, so 25y 11mo) but unpinned — it silently rots on 2026-05-31 when Survivor's 26th year begins. Phase 43 editorial-honesty class: derive from `show.est_year` and `Date.now()` where the surface can, OR pin with a `content-check` invariant that fails when the spelled-out years count drifts from the real value. Occurrences: `content/shows/survivor.md:11` (tagline "spent twenty-five years rediscovering what it is"), `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-villains.md:14` (pull), `content/shows/survivor/seasons/50-survivor-50.md:11` (body, twice including "twenty-fifth anniversary"), `content/shows/survivor/canon.md:919`, plus the parallel "After twenty-five years" line in `content/shows/bachelor/seasons/26-clayton-echard.md:12`. (URL: /, /shows, /shows/survivor, /shows/survivor/season/heroes-villains, source: critique-pass-6) — 8d71196 — issue #163 — RESOLVED at 0061177: four of the five listed surfaces had already shipped through earlier Phase 43 ticks — `survivor.md` tagline carries the `{yearsWord}` token (tick 1), the bachelor S26 pull was disambiguated to "twenty-five seasons under one voice" (tick 3, a season count not a year count), and `survivor/seasons/50-survivor-50.md` "twenty-fifth anniversary" + `survivor/canon.md` "quarter-century" both anchor to S50's airing-as-25-year-celebration (a historical milestone reference, not "today's count" — stable forever). The remaining live offender was `survivor/seasons/20-heroes-villains.md:14` pull "Twenty-five years in, ..." — which slipped past the `collectYearTenureIssues` invariant because `YEAR_TENURE_RE` was lowercase-only (no `i` flag) and the comparison against `expectedPhrase` was a strict `===`. A sentence-leading capital-T form was invisible to the gate. Fix tightens the regex to `gi`, normalizes both the `expectedPhrase` and `isAnchorAllowed` comparisons to lowercase, and rephrases the H&V pull "Twenty-five years in" → "Decades in" (plural-stable, no rotting numeric literal, same rhetorical beat — preserves "even after all this time, this is the season" reading; the second sentence and the defense-without-footnotes coda are untouched). §5a: a new unit case in `content-check.test.ts` catches a capitalized-T "Twenty-five years" against an est_year that puts the helper at "twenty-six" — pins the case-sensitivity catch against any future regex regression. No URL, schema field, page family, or component change — script invariant + content edit. Spoiler-safe — voice rephrase only. Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 1763 unit tests + script tests + content:check (13 shows/298 seasons/13 canons/12 themes/3 legal docs), leg2 build, leg3 1329 e2e.

- [x] [LOW] [authed] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-villains header chip reads "1 MIN READ" on a page with a lede, 6 numbered sections, and 4 watch-for callouts — capture body length 3632 chars, closer to 3–4 minutes at a typical read rate. The number reads suspiciously round and is likely hardcoded. Phase 43 editorial-honesty class: derive read-time from rendered word count (a small helper, ~half the work of `tierLede.ts`), or drop the chip if it isn't load-bearing. Source: search the canon-entry header component for the "MIN READ" literal. (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-villains, source: critique-pass-6) — 8d71196 — RESOLVED at c4ab814 (phase 43 tick 4): `src/lib/season/read-minutes.ts` added — `computeReadMinutes` walks every editorial surface that renders on the season page (lede, body, pull, "Where it sits" copy, watch-list items) and returns `Math.max(1, Math.round(words / 220))`. The season page wires it at `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:300` (`computeReadMinutes({ lede, body, pull, whereItSits, watchList })`) and renders the result in the byline at line 351 (`{readMinutes} min read`). Heroes vs. Villains now reads ≥3 min as the row predicted. Pass-6 filed this at 8d71196 (13:30 UTC); the deriver shipped at c4ab814 (19:22 UTC) six hours later, but pass-7 didn't re-walk it — this tick is housekeeping only (no source change).

- [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/<show> at 375px drops the CURATED / LIVE state badges from the ranking tabs — desktop renders "01 Editor's CanonCURATED / 02 CommunityLIVE", mobile renders the bare "01 Editor's Canon / 02 Community". The CURATED / LIVE chips disambiguate the central product premise (editor-curated stable canon vs. live community vote) and the mobile reader loses the only on-page differentiator between the two ranking systems. Source: `src/styles/canon.css:1091` hides `.cp-tab-cap { display: none }` inside the `@media (max-width: <mobile-breakpoint>)` block. Fix: keep the CURATED / LIVE chips visible at 375px. If horizontal room is the concern, stack the chip under the tab name (so each tab becomes a two-line cell) or shorten to "STABLE" / "LIVE" rather than dropping the affordance entirely. Sample URL: /shows/survivor — same shape applies to every /shows/<show> page since the rule is global CSS. (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-8) — 6b81691 — RESOLVED this commit: flipped `.cp-tab-cap` inside `@media (max-width: 560px)` from `display: none` to `display: block; margin-left: 0; margin-top: 3px` so the CURATED / LIVE caption falls under the tab name as a two-line cell (option 1 of the suggested fix — preserves the existing CURATED/LIVE wording rather than re-coining STABLE/LIVE). Also slightly relaxed `.cp-tab` mobile padding (14→12 vertical) to keep the two-line cell vertically balanced. e2e (`show-home.spec.ts` mobile @375 block) asserts both `.cp-tab-cap` nodes are visible at 375px on /shows/survivor and carry the expected /curated/i + /live/i text. Unit test (`CanonTabSwitch.test.tsx` "emits .cp-tab-cap spans") pins the DOM contract so the breakpoint always has a node to stack. Markup unchanged.
  - issue: #158

- [x] [LOW] [anon] /themes lede opens "12 pieces of editorial opinion, organized by the part of the craft they admire." — "pieces of editorial opinion" reads as a CMS-field noun phrase, not the knowledgeable-peer voice the rest of the site holds. The very next sentence proves the voice is available ("Some span the catalog. Some live inside one. None of them spoil what they rank.") — the first half just doesn't reach for it. Fix: rework the first sentence into the same register as the second. Something like "12 lists we'd defend in a group chat. Some span the catalog, some live inside one show. None of them spoil what they rank." Keeps the count, keeps the structure, drops the noun phrase. (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-8) — 6b81691 — RESOLVED at 4f8215c: both `<ListsHero>` lede branches in `src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx:11-13` (cross-canon + single-canon) swapped the opening sentence `"${total} ${piece|pieces} of editorial opinion, organized by the part of the craft they admire."` for the suggested in-voice rewrite `"${total} ${list|lists} we'd defend in a group chat."`; pluralizer flips from `piece/pieces` to `list/lists`; count stays parameterized; second-sentence structure preserved on both branches (cross-canon "Some span the catalog, some live inside one show. None of them spoil what they rank.", single-canon "Every list lives inside one canon today — cross-canon entries arrive as more catalogues fill in. None of them spoil what they rank."). Unit tests pin both directions: `ListsHero.test.tsx` cross-canon + single-canon branches each gain a negative assertion (`pieces of editorial opinion` must not appear) AND a positive assertion (`lists we'd defend in a group chat` must appear), defending against any future regression on either branch. The /themes index e2e (`apps/e2e/tests/themes.spec.ts:20-22`) already pins the lede against the false "every show" overclaim; the rewrite preserves that contract (no occurrence of "every show" in either branch). No URL, schema, page family, or component shape change.
  - issue: #162

- [x] [LOW] [authed] /u/<handle> empty profile stacks three zero-valued stat blocks (0 COMMENTS / 0 SEASONS VOTED / 0 SHOWS FOLLOWED) directly above the empty-state sentence "Nothing on the public record yet. Vote on a season pair, weigh in on a thread, and it will land here." — the sentence already says what the three zeros say, so the empty state is announced twice in the same column (roughly half of the page's 524 bodyText chars). Fix: hide the stat strip until at least one counter is non-zero, and let the sentence carry the empty state alone. Once the reader has any activity, the stat strip becomes the right summary and the sentence can go. Source likely `src/components/profile/` — paired with the existing `ProfileEmpty` component the #153 pass-6 row already rewrote. (URL: /u/e2e, source: critique-pass-8) — 6b81691 — issue #164 — RESOLVED at b1e9a4d: `src/app/(default)/u/[handle]/page.tsx` rendered `<ProfileStats>` unconditionally at line 136-140 then split `<ProfileComments>` vs `<ProfileEmpty>` on `profile.populated` immediately below — so an empty profile got the three zero-valued stat cells AND the empty-state sentence stacked in the same column. Fix folds the stat strip into the `profile.populated` branch alongside Recent comments: empty branch now renders `<ProfileEmpty>` alone (sentence carries the empty state, as the row requested); populated branch renders `<ProfileStats>` + Recent comments (the stat strip is the right summary once the reader has activity, and the sentence is gone). The page has one branch, not three independent toggles a future regression could desync. The colocated `ProfileStats` unit tests (`src/components/profile/__tests__/ProfileStats.test.tsx`) keep covering the component itself (including the all-zero render path — defensive in case some future caller renders zeros directly); the populated e2e (`apps/e2e/tests/user-profile.spec.ts:75` "known handle renders activity to a logged-out visitor; pending never leaks") continues to assert `profile-stats visible` + `profile-stat-seasons` contains "1" against a real vote (populated=true), so the populated branch is pinned by e2e and the empty branch is pinned by the conditional render alongside `<ProfileEmpty>`. Spoiler/mod P0 intact — the stat strip surfaces participation counts only (never per-ballot detail), and the gate only changes when those counts surface, not how. Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 163 unit test files (1763 passed) + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows/298 seasons/13 canons/12 themes/3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 static pages), leg3 1329 e2e.

- [x] [MED] [anon] All pages (chrome) — footer `SHOWS` column renders the same three shows on every captured page in the walk: `The Amazing Race / The Bachelor / The Bachelorette`. Survivor never appears in any footer column on any page in the walk, including `/shows/survivor` and `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains` (Survivor-scoped pages whose own footer column promotes three other shows and a generic `ALL SHOWS →` link). Source: `src/components/chrome/footer/FooterTiersCol.tsx:6` calls `getAllShows().slice(0, 3)`; `getAllShows` (`src/content/loaders.ts:207`) returns the catalog sorted by `slug.localeCompare`, so the alphabetical-first three are picked deterministically — `amazing-race` / `bachelor` / `bachelorette` — and Survivor (slug `survivor`) lives at the alphabetical tail of a 13-show catalog. But every other site signal places Survivor as the flagship: home `CURRENTLY FEATURED` (`HomeHero`), /shows S-tier #1 of 2 (`ShowsTiers`), 50-season `getCanon('survivor')` (the deepest canon in the catalog), the only show with multi-era band navigation. A first-time visitor scrolling to the footer of a Survivor season page sees a footer column promoting three other shows — the flagship the rest of the page is selling is absent from chrome navigation. The shortlist composition undercuts the editorial hierarchy every other surface declares. Fix options: (a) **primary path** — replace the alphabetical slice with a tier-aware slice that surfaces the flagship + the next two by tier. Add a tier-rank helper (`S→0, A→1, B→2, C→3`) and sort: `[...getAllShows()].sort((a, b) => tierRank(a.tier) - tierRank(b.tier) || a.slug.localeCompare(b.slug)).slice(0, 3)` — produces `Survivor / The Amazing Race / RuPaul's Drag Race` against today's catalog (S-tier #1 / S-tier #2 / A-tier alphabetical-first); declarative against existing show frontmatter, no special-cased slug. (b) thread the home `featured` show into the slice unconditionally then fill from tier-sorted remaining (`featured.slug` first, then top-2 by tier excluding featured) — guarantees the home-featured show is reachable from chrome on every page. (c) hardcode `survivor` first then slice the rest alphabetically — rejected, flagship status will drift as the catalog grows and a non-Survivor show graduates to S-tier #1. Recommended (a) — extends the editorial hierarchy from page bodies into chrome, no special-cased slug, the slice stays declarative against tier metadata already in show frontmatter. Pin: extend `src/components/chrome/footer/__tests__/FooterTiersCol.test.tsx` with (1) a positive case asserting the first rendered show is the highest-tier alphabetical-first show (Survivor against today's catalog: `getAllByText(/Survivor/).length >= 1` within the `site-footer-tiers-col` testid) and (2) a regression pin that the column does NOT render the alphabetical-first-3 against a fixture catalog where the highest-tier show is alphabetically last. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (chrome navigation only). (URL: all pages — site chrome, source: critique-pass-29) — 54a4170 — issue: #298 — RESOLVED: took option (a) — `src/components/chrome/footer/FooterTiersCol.tsx` now sorts `getAllShows()` by `TIER_ORDER.indexOf(tier)` first and `slug.localeCompare` as the within-tier tiebreaker, then slices to 3. Against today's 13-show catalog (S: `dragrace`, `survivor`; A: 11 shows; B: none) chrome navigation now leads `RuPaul's Drag Race / Survivor / The Amazing Race` — the flagship plus the next S-tier show plus the first A-tier show by slug — declarative against show frontmatter `tier`, no special-cased slug, no per-page override. The alphabetical-first three (`amazing-race / bachelor / bachelorette`) no longer ship as chrome by accident of slug order, and a future S-tier graduation flows into the column automatically the next time the catalog rebuilds. Tests: `FooterTiersCol.test.tsx` gains (1) a tier-first positive case against a synthetic 5-show catalog ([S: amazing-race, survivor; A: drag-race; B: bachelor, bachelorette]) asserting the rendered hrefs are `[/shows/amazing-race, /shows/survivor, /shows/drag-race]` — slug-last flagship still surfaces; (2) a regression pin against a synthetic [B: aardvark, badger; A: cobra; S: zebra] catalog asserting the rendered hrefs are NOT `[/shows/aardvark, /shows/badger, /shows/cobra]` and that `/shows/zebra` leads — locks down the bug class the alphabetical slice produced; (3) a within-tier ordering test (`zulu / alpha / mike` all tier-S → `Alpha, Mike, Zulu`) so the tiebreaker never drifts. The `makeShow` test helper gains an explicit `tier` argument so fixtures can opt into varied tiers; the existing 8px-bullet test was updated to use the new positional signature (`tier='S'` + explicit primary). Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (chrome navigation only — no spoiler-bearing copy touched). Verify green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit test files (2362 passed) + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 static pages), leg3 1361 e2e.

The sibling [LOW] pass-29 `had been` row (line 1521 in this file) closes alongside this MED drain — the rewrite of the HvV body opener anchors `had been` at `had been by 2010` per the row's prescribed edit, as the row itself anticipated ("this finding closes alongside the [MED] `at full volume` cliche drain"). - [x] [LOW] [anon] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains section 05 `ADJACENT IN THE CANON` subhead reads `Read next.` and renders two cards: `← #01 IN CANON / Cagayan` and `#03 IN CANON → / Borneo`. The parent header `Adjacent in the canon` is correct — these are the canon-adjacent seasons. But the `Read next.` subhead between header and cards frames both cards as the reader's forward path, when one of them (#01 Cagayan) is structurally *higher* in the canon than the season being viewed (#02 HvV). For a reader on canon slot #02, Cagayan is `read above` / `read previous`, not `read next`. The label fits Borneo (#03, one slot below) but contradicts Cagayan's direction; the cards' own arrows (`←` / `→`) plus position eyebrows (`#01 IN CANON` / `#03 IN CANON`) already declare bidirectional adjacency, so the `Read next.` subhead is the misframing layer between honest header and honest cards. Source: `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx:449-450` renders the section header (`<div class="article-eyebrow"><span class="num">05</span><span>Adjacent in the canon</span></div>`) immediately followed by `<h2>Read next.</h2>` — the `<h2>` is the misframing token. Fix options: (a) **primary path** — drop the `<h2>Read next.</h2>` line entirely so `Adjacent in the canon` carries the framing alone; the cards' arrows + position eyebrows already declare direction. Smallest blast radius (one-line removal in `page.tsx:450`). (b) rename the subhead to `Either direction.` to acknowledge the bidirectional pairing — preserves the visual rhythm of header + subhead used elsewhere on the page (`01 / The take.`, `04 / What to watch for.`) but reads honestly against a canon-above + canon-below pair. (c) split per-card with directional micro-labels (`One slot higher` / `One slot lower`) — accurate but adds chrome above each card and requires a component refactor. Recommended (b) — keeps the page's eyebrow + subhead rhythm consistent (every section 01–05 carries a one-sentence h2 cap), is canonically true for any pair the section ever renders, and resolves at one literal swap. Pin: extend `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/__tests__/page.test.tsx` with a positive case asserting the section-05 h2 reads `Either direction.` (or whichever literal lands) — and the existing assertion that `Adjacent in the canon` renders stays green. Spoiler P0 unchanged (eyebrow text only, no per-season position leak). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-29) — 4e4ba8a — issue: #299 — RESOLVED 4e4ba8a: took the row's recommended Option (b) — `src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx` section-05 h2 literal flipped from `Read next.` to `Either direction.`, extracted as a new pure export `ADJACENT_SECTION_H2 = 'Either direction.' as const` so the colocated `page.test.tsx` (which scopes to pure exports per its file header) can pin the literal without forcing a full-page React render. Pin landed: new describe block `ADJACENT_SECTION_H2 — Section 05 "Adjacent in the canon" subhead` with (1) positive case asserting the literal equals `Either direction.`; (2) bidirectional regression pin asserting it is NOT `Read next.` AND does not match `/\bnext\b/i` — so a future authoring pass that flips back to a forward-only subhead (or a near-synonym carrying the same unidirectional framing) trips at unit time. The page's eyebrow + h2 rhythm now reads canonically against any pair the section can render (canon-above + canon-below, two canon-below, or two canon-above). Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged — subhead text only, no per-season verdict / position leak / finale beat exposed. Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit test files (2364 passed, +1 describe block, +2 cases) + 127 script tests + content-check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs), leg2 build (374 static pages), leg3 1361 e2e.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] /themes/best-finales entry #02 (Survivor S20 Heroes vs. Villains) body opens `The endgame plays at full volume — every conversation freighted with everything Survivor had been. (Note: this finding closes alongside the [MED] `at full volume` cliche drain — the proposed `at full volume` rewrite of the same sentence may also resolve the `had been` framing in a single content edit; if the curator picks an alternate opener the past-perfect tense issue may dissolve. Single-pattern check, lifts at content-author time, sibling to the existing `CLICHE_PATTERNS` and `collectYearTenureIssues` invariants. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (typography hygiene only). (URL: /themes, /themes/best-finales, /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, /about, source: critique-pass-29) — b061d5f — issue: #305 — RESOLVED b061d5f: took the row's full path with a scoped invariant. The best-finales.md:26 entry #02 title had already been rewritten in an intermediate pass (no `back half` remains in best-finales.md), so the 5 listed sites collapsed to 4 actionable surfaces: (1) `content/themes/best-post-merge.md:33` Big Brother S07 All-Stars entry blurb `runs on its back half` → `runs on its back-half`; (2) same file `:39` Top Chef S06 entry blurb `the back half plays as chef against chef` → `the back-half plays as chef against chef`; (3) same file `:45` Drag Race S06 entry blurb `the sixth season's back half` → `the sixth season's back-half`; (4) `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-vs-villains.md:24` episode_heat_caption `peak run · the back half` → `peak run · the back-half`. The invariant's source scope (every themed-list editorial field + season episode_heat_caption) sweeps in 9 sibling episode_heat_captions that were unhyphenated outside the row's enumeration (the row spotted HvV but not the parallel cases): challenge S21/S29/S31/S33/S34/S38/S39 + survivor S27/S47 — all rewritten to the hyphenated form for the same brand-consistent noun-phrase shorthand. Pin landed: `scripts/content-check.ts` `collectBackHalfHyphenIssues` walks all theme description/tagline/body_md/entry title/blurb + season episode_heat_caption; the regex `/\bback half\b/gi` catches case-insensitive + possessive forms; strict toggle ships at floor 0 (`BACK_HALF_HYPHEN_STRICT = true`) since the rewrite drained every offender in this commit. Scope deliberately narrower than the row's "every editorial field" prescription — canon prose and season ledes/bodies/pulls sit outside the scanner so the broader corpus (~20 `back half` occurrences in canon entries + season prose) stays untouched until a future critique flags a wider drift visible on a single reader scroll. The narrower scope matches the row's evidence (themed-list cross-references + HvV episode_heat_caption are the surfaces a reader sees together) without forcing a corpus-wide rewrite that exceeds the row's "5 content sites listed" mandate. Test pins in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` (new describe block `content-check — back-half hyphenation drift (critique pass-30, issue #305)`): (a) live-catalog passes post-rewrite (asserts `setContentRoot(null)` reads zero issues); (b) themed-list entry blurb regression flags the unhyphenated form with the expected file path + message + issue-id reference; (c) season episode_heat_caption regression flags the unhyphenated form with the expected file path; (d) case-insensitive coverage (`Back Half` + `season's back half`); (e) negative pin that the canonical hyphenated form (`back-half`) does not trigger. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (typography hygiene only; no per-season verdict, canon position, finale beat, or winner exposed). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 187 unit files / 2385 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1361 e2e (8.4m).

- [x] [LOW] [anon] /themes/best-finales entry #06 (RuPaul's Drag Race S6) uses `real` twice in adjacent clauses inside the entry's title + body opener — within one card's worth of editorial copy. Title (`content/themes/best-finales.md:64`): `A final lip-sync that pays off a finale built on real artistry.` Body opener (`:65`): `Season 6 spends its run building toward a finale of real artists, and the last episodes deliver — runway moments still replayed, a lip-sync stretch with genuine stakes.` Two adjacent phrasings `real artistry` / `real artists` read as the same word doing the work of two different intensifiers; the body's own `genuine stakes` a half-clause later proves the author has the broader vocabulary available. The two instances also fall inside the entry-title vs entry-blurb seam — the same noun is intensified the same way in the louder rank label and the quieter editorial copy below it, which makes the repetition louder than the count alone suggests. Fix: curator-only edit, smallest blast radius — drop `real` from the body and keep the title. Candidate body opener: `Season 6 spends its run building toward a finale of working drag artists, and the last episodes deliver — runway moments still replayed, a lip-sync stretch with genuine stakes.` Or invert: keep the body verbatim, retitle to `A final lip-sync that pays off a finale built on genuine artistry.` Either preserves the entry's editorial point. Pin: no new invariant — this is a one-off adjacent-repetition, not a recurring class; if `real` recurs across future content the existing `CLICHE_PATTERNS` channel at `scripts/content-check.ts:598` is the seam to extend. Spoiler discipline P0 unchanged (voice edit only). (URL: /themes/best-finales, source: critique-pass-30) — issue: #304 — RESOLVED this commit: took the row's smaller-blast-radius candidate. `content/themes/best-finales.md:51` Drag Race S06 entry blurb's opener swapped `real artists` → `working drag artists`, keeping the title (`built on real artistry`) intact. The body's later `genuine stakes` clause was intentionally left untouched — the alternative retitle to `genuine artistry` would have created a fresh `genuine/genuine` echo inside the same card, swapping one in-card repetition for another. `working drag artists` introduces no new repetition: the title's `real artistry` survives as the one occurrence, and `working` / `drag` / `artists` do not appear adjacent on the entry's siblings (Traitors S2 #05 post-#302, Big Brother S10 #07 — checked across the immediate before/after entries on the list). No pin owed per the row — the prescription was explicit that this is a one-off adjacent repetition, not a recurring class; if `real` ever recurs across future content the existing `CLICHE_PATTERNS` channel at `scripts/content-check.ts:598` is the documented seam to extend. No URL / schema / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — content edit only; the smoke walker only pins 200 / H1 / no console errors on `/themes/best-finales` so it inherits the new copy cleanly. - [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/survivor canon-section lede sentence reads `One ranking, written by an editor who has rewatched every season at least twice. The community argues back; this is one number.` (`src/components/canon/ShowRanking.tsx:98`) — the very next sibling DOM element is the two-tab UI labeled `Editor's Canon CURATED` / `Community LIVE`. Prose says `One ranking` and the UI immediately renders two clearly-labeled ranking surfaces. A cold reader hears the editor's voice committing to a single number and at first paint sees two tab triggers offering two; the prose and the UI disagree on shape at the moment of first impression. Concrete observed sequence from the rendered captures (both desktop and mobile, anon walk): `The canon, top to bottom. / One ranking, written by an editor who has rewatched every season at least twice. The community argues back; this is one number. / Editor's Canon CURATED / Community LIVE / #01 / Cagayan / ...`. The intended editorial point is plausibly that `one ranking` refers to the editor's column only (with the community column being the argued-back-against second voice) — but the sentence makes that referent implicit, and the next sentence's `this is one number` doubles down on the singular framing the tab UI immediately undermines. Same defect class as the resolved pass-32 #311 (cross-surface /home Community Rank card vs /about voting policy attestation-mechanic drift) — that closure addressed a cross-surface trust-mechanic mismatch; this row addresses a within-page prose-vs-UI shape mismatch on the most-visited show's canon module. Source: `src/components/canon/ShowRanking.tsx:97-99` lede literal `<p>One ranking, written by an editor who has rewatched every season at least twice. The community argues back; this is one number.</p>` followed by the tab-switcher render. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer doesn't say `one ranking` while presenting two ranking surfaces in the same module head without making explicit which shape the sentence is naming. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rephrase the lede so `one ranking` explicitly names the editor's column** and the community column is named as the second voice, not collapsed into `one number`. Candidate rewrite: `One editor's ranking, top to bottom — and the community argues back in the next tab.` (drops the implicit-referent `one ranking` for `One editor's ranking`, keeps the argued-back framing, drops the contradictory `this is one number` clause). Single-string edit at `src/components/canon/ShowRanking.tsx:98`. Brings the prose and the two-tab UI into agreement on shape. (b) **alternative — drop the two-tab UI's `Editor's Canon CURATED` / `Community LIVE` labels** when the lede commits to one-ranking framing — rejected; the tabs are the load-bearing affordance for the canon/community split phase 35 shipped (`Editor's Canon` + `Community` tabs are the contract), and the labels are how a reader knows what tab they're on. The prose should bend to the UI, not the UI to the prose. (c) **alternative — keep both as-is and accept that readers reconcile the shape after a beat** — rejected; the prose-vs-UI mismatch at first paint is the defect, and reconciliation-by-context is what the editorial copy is supposed to forestall. Recommended (a) — single-string edit, preserves the editorial point (an editor's curated ranking, with community as the argued-back second voice), brings prose and UI into agreement at first paint. Pin: extend the colocated test on `src/components/canon/__tests__/ShowRanking.test.tsx` with two cases — (1) positive pin asserting the rendered lede explicitly contains either `editor's ranking` OR `the editor's column` OR another single-column-naming phrase (regex `/editor'?s (ranking|column|canon)/i`); (2) bidirectional drift guard asserting the rendered lede does NOT match `/^[^.]*\bone ranking\b[^.]*\./i` standalone (i.e. a future refactor that reintroduces a bare `One ranking` framing without naming the editor's column trips at unit time). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome prose edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface). (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-39) — 9c486a9 — issue: #345 — RESOLVED this commit: took the row's recommended path (a) with a slight refinement to preserve the pass-31 #305 pin literal exactly. `src/components/canon/ShowRanking.tsx:97-100` lede swapped from `One ranking, written by an editor who has rewatched every season at least twice. The community argues back; this is one number.` to `One editor's ranking, written by an editor who has rewatched every season at least twice. The community argues back in the next tab.` — explicitly names the editor's column at sentence-start (closes the prose-vs-UI mismatch from one side), preserves the singular-editor admission verbatim (`written by an editor who has rewatched every season at least twice` — the pass-31 #305 pin literal is unchanged), names where the community lives (`in the next tab` — closes from the other side), drops the contradictory `this is one number` coda. `src/components/canon/__tests__/ShowRanking.test.tsx` gained a new pass-39 #345 case after the pass-31 #305 case: (1) positive pin asserting the rendered canon lede matches `/editor'?s (ranking|column|canon)/i`; (2) bidirectional drift guard asserting the lede does NOT match `/^[^.]*\bone ranking\b[^.]*\./i` (standalone `One ranking` sentence — guards against a future refactor that drops the editor's-column qualifier) AND does NOT contain `/this is one number/i` (the contradictory coda). - [x] [LOW] [anon] / home + footer use the eyebrow `featured` for two different things on the same page load — a within-scroll cross-surface label collision. Concrete observed shapes from the rendered home capture (both desktop and mobile, anon walk): home hero opens with the eyebrow `CURRENTLY FEATURED` above a single show (`Survivor`), framing `featured` as the one anchor show the editorial team picks per month; the footer rendered on the same page carries the eyebrow `FEATURED SHOWS` above three show names (`RuPaul's Drag Race / Survivor / The Amazing Race`), framing `featured` as the three top-billed shows in the catalog. A cold reader reads the hero `CURRENTLY FEATURED` and forms the model that `featured` means the one anchor show this month; one scroll later the footer says `FEATURED SHOWS` and renders three, and the reader either reconciles by treating `currently` as the discriminator (this month's pick vs the standing top-billed three) or treats one of the two labels as imprecise. Same defect class as the resolved pass-38 #336 / pass-39 (i) cross-surface label-grammar drift family (`INDEX LAST REVISED` vs `INDEX REVISED`, same stat-strip slot, two grammatical registers across analogous surfaces), now at the home-hero vs home-footer eyebrow layer — same page, same word, two meanings. Source: home hero eyebrow at the `HomeHero` or `HomeFeaturedRow` component (verify component path — likely `src/components/home/` family) rendering `CURRENTLY FEATURED` above the single anchor-show card; footer eyebrow at the `FooterTiersCol` (`src/components/chrome/footer/FooterTiersCol.tsx`, verify path) rendering `FEATURED SHOWS` above the three top-billed names. Both occupy chrome eyebrow-level micro-labels. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer commits to one meaning for the same word across analogous surfaces on the same page, or differentiates the labels enough that the reader doesn't have to do reconciliation work mid-scroll. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rename the footer column from `FEATURED SHOWS` to a label the editor would actually say** for the three top-billed names — candidate forms: `START HERE` (names the function: these are the three the editor would point a newcomer at first), `TOP-BILLED` (names the catalog state without using the contested word), `THE BIG THREE` (names the cohort with editorial voice), or simply `SHOWS` with the three names sitting under the brand line as a flat nav row. Keeps `CURRENTLY FEATURED` in the hero as the single-anchor framing it already carries; resolves the cross-surface collision by differentiating the two labels. Single component edit on the footer column. (b) **alternative — rename the hero eyebrow from `CURRENTLY FEATURED` to a single-anchor-specific label** like `THIS MONTH` or `THIS MONTH'S PICK` — preserves the footer's `FEATURED SHOWS` framing for the standing three. Acceptable but moves the meaning shift onto the hero where the editorial weight sits; the hero is the louder surface and changing its label has a higher reader-impact than changing the footer's. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; the cross-surface label collision within one scroll reads as drift, even if each label in isolation is defensible. Recommended (a) — smaller blast radius (footer label edit, not hero), preserves the hero's editorial framing, differentiates the two labels enough that a reader doesn't have to reconcile mid-scroll. Pin: extend the colocated test on `src/components/chrome/footer/__tests__/FooterTiersCol.test.tsx` (verify path — create if absent) with a regression case asserting the column heading literal matches the post-rewrite form (e.g. `/^SHOWS$/i` or `/^START HERE$/i` depending on the chosen rotation) AND does NOT match `/featured/i` (bidirectional drift guard against a future refactor re-introducing the contested word). Sibling positive on `HomeHero.test.tsx` (or equivalent) asserting the hero eyebrow continues to render `CURRENTLY FEATURED` (unchanged). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome label edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the label edit runs on the eyebrow grammar, not the underlying show selection). Pinned per the row's prescription + bidirectional drift guard, mirroring the pass-33 #321 closure pattern: `src/components/chrome/footer/__tests__/FooterTiersCol.test.tsx` positive pins flipped to `Start here` (h2 textContent, aria-label, graceful-counts head), and the existing `toMatch(/featured/i)` assertions flipped to `not.toMatch(/featured/i)` + `toMatch(/^start here$/i)` on both the h2 and the aria-label — so a future refactor that reaches for `Featured shows` (either form) trips the unit gate. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome label edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the label edit moves a footer column head literal, doesn't change the underlying show selection). - [x] [LOW] [anon] /shows hero stat-strip last cell reads `May 2026 INDEX REVISED` while /themes hero same-cell reads `June 2026 INDEX REVISED` — same label, two freshness claims, captured on the same anon walk (today is 2026-06-07). Concrete observed shapes from the rendered captures: /shows top-stat strip `13 SHOWS TRACKED · 298 SEASONS RANKED · May 2026 INDEX REVISED`; /themes top-stat strip `3 FEATURED · 9 IN THE INDEX · 10 SHOWS COVERED · June 2026 INDEX REVISED`. The pass-38 #338 / pass-39 (i)'s closure (`f339b1c`) aligned the /themes label from `Index last revised` to `Index revised` (matching the /shows verb-past form) — that closure addressed grammatical-register drift. The two surfaces now agree on grammar but disagree on the date the same global timestamp resolves to: /shows says May, /themes says June. A cold reader who reads both pages back-to-back on the same walk is told one global catalog is a full month staler than another, with no editorial explanation for the gap. The data flows are probably distinct (/shows freshness reads the most-recent canon-mtime across all shows; /themes freshness reads the most-recent theme-content-mtime across all lists) and the May-vs-June difference reflects actual recent content drains — but the reader doesn't carry that vocabulary across the click. Same defect class as the resolved pass-38 #338 (cross-surface label-grammar drift on the same stat-strip slot) — that closure addressed grammar; this row addresses the *value* the slot resolves to. Source: /shows freshness computed at `src/components/shows/ShowsHero.tsx` (verify path) — likely from `getAllShows()` + `canon.revised_at` field max; /themes freshness computed at `src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx:102` (post-`f339b1c` literal `Index revised`) — likely from `getAllThemes()` + `theme.revised_at` max. Both labels read `INDEX REVISED` post-#338 but resolve to different months because they aggregate over different content corpora. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer who uses the same label for two stats either makes the labels distinguishable or makes the resolved values converge. Fix options: (a) **primary path — distinguish the two labels so the reader doesn't read them as the same global timestamp drifting**. Candidate forms: /shows says `CATALOG REVISED` (names the catalog as the referent) and /themes says `LISTS REVISED` (names the lists corpus); or /shows says `SHOWS REVISED` and /themes says `LISTS REVISED` (parallel construction, each names its own corpus). Two-component edit on `ShowsHero.tsx` + `ListsHero.tsx` (one literal each). Preserves the verb-past framing the post-#338 closure committed to; differentiates the referent so the May-vs-June values stop reading as the same drifting clock. (b) **alternative — converge the two values onto a single global timestamp** — compute a single `MAX(shows.canon.revised_at, themes.revised_at)` across both corpora and surface that on both heroes. Larger blast radius (new shared helper + caller changes on both pages) and loses the per-corpus freshness signal a reader uses to gauge how recently each index was touched. Rejected unless future readers ask for a global timestamp. (c) **alternative — rename only /shows** to a corpus-specific label, leaving /themes as-is — keeps `INDEX REVISED` on the /themes hero but renames the /shows hero to `CATALOG REVISED`; differentiates the two but asymmetric. Acceptable but parallel-construction option (a) is cleaner. (d) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; the cross-surface value drift on the same label reads as a single global timestamp drifting by a month, which is misleading. Recommended (a) — symmetric, single-literal-each edit on both components, preserves the verb-past framing, differentiates the two corpora at the chrome-eyebrow layer. Pin: extend the colocated tests on `src/components/shows/__tests__/ShowsHero.test.tsx` (already pins `Index revised` per the #336 close at lines 128-141) and `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListsHero.test.tsx` (pins `Index revised` per the f339b1c close) with rewritten regression cases asserting the new literals (e.g. `/^SHOWS REVISED$/` on ShowsHero and `/^LISTS REVISED$/` on ListsHero) AND bidirectional drift guards asserting neither label re-matches `/^INDEX REVISED$/` (which would re-introduce the cross-surface label collision the row addresses). Sibling positive on `Footer.test.tsx` / `HomeHero.test.tsx` asserting no other surface uses the bare `INDEX REVISED` literal. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome label edits only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface). Two-component edit (one literal each); preserves the verb-past framing the post-#338 closure committed to; differentiates the referent so the May-vs-June values stop reading as the same global timestamp drifting. The sibling `Canon revised` labels on the home featured tile + /shows/[show] hero are untouched — they name a per-show canon, distinct from the per-corpus catalog freshness on the index landings. - [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/survivor canon methodology block stacks three cells in immediate succession that swap narrators mid-block. Cell 01 `01 · WHO Who ranks?` body (`content/shows/survivor/canon.md` `meth_who_p`, post-pass-35 #329 drain) reads `tiered.tv's editor. I've watched Survivor since Pulau Tiga and I've replayed every season that lands on this list... I'm not claiming to be objective. I'm trying to be honest.` — first-person-singular throughout. Cell 02 head reads `02 · HOW How do we weigh it?` (plural-collective `do we`). Cell 03 head + body reads `03 · WHEN When do we revisit? / After every season finale... can shift our read on a player's original season... We revisit the bottom of the canon less often than the top.` (plural-collective `we revisit`, `our read`). A reader scrolling the three methodology cells in order reads `I` (cell 01) → `we` (cell 02) → `we` (cell 03) — the editorial voice swaps narrators inside one module. The pass-35 #329 closure (drained 9 per-show `meth_who_p` overrides from plural to singular voice, flipped `PLURAL_EDITOR_STRICT = true`) committed the site to singular-editor voice on editorial attribution at the canon-who cell layer; the sibling `meth_how_h` / `meth_how_p` / `meth_when_h` / `meth_when_p` fields were not in scope and still carry plural-collective voice across all 13 canons (verified: every `content/shows/*/canon.md` carries the four methodology fields; all 13 use `we` / `our` / `do we` in the heads + paragraphs — 10 use the longer `How do we weigh it? / When do we revisit?` head form, 3 [love-island-uk, love-island-us, traitors] use the shorter `How we weigh it / When we revisit` form). The drift recurs on every show whose canon renders this module. Concrete observed shape on the rendered /shows/survivor canon module (verified across both desktop + mobile anon walks): `THE METHODOLOGY · CANON / 01 · WHO / Who ranks? / tiered.tv's editor. I've watched Survivor since Pulau Tiga... I'm not claiming to be objective. I'm trying to be honest. / 02 · HOW / How do we weigh it? / Four lenses... / 03 · WHEN / When do we revisit? / After every season finale... We revisit the bottom of the canon less often than the top.` Same defect class as the resolved pass-35 #329 plural→singular drain at the `meth_who_p` field layer (`9 carrier canons drained`); this row asks for the sibling sweep at the four `meth_how_*` / `meth_when_*` fields the #329 closure did not touch. Same defect class also as the resolved pass-34 #322 (5 visible-chrome surfaces from plural to singular) — both committed the site to singular voice on editorial attribution; this row extends to the within-canon methodology layer. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken`; a peer doesn't change their narrator pronoun mid-paragraph on the same activity. Fix options: (a) **primary path — drain `meth_how_h` / `meth_how_p` / `meth_when_h` / `meth_when_p` across all 13 `content/shows/*/canon.md` files to first-person singular** matching the pass-35-drained `meth_who_p`. Heads become `How do I weigh it?` / `When do I revisit?` (10 carriers) or `How I weigh it` / `When I revisit` (3 carriers — love-island-uk, love-island-us, traitors). Paragraph bodies rewrite the `we` / `our` references to `I` / `my` — e.g. `Returnee seasons in particular can shift our read on a player's original season` → `Returnee seasons in particular can shift my read on a player's original season`; `We revisit the bottom of the canon less often than the top` → `I revisit the bottom of the canon less often than the top`. Content-curator drain; each canon file gets a minor edit on the four fields, no schema change, no rationale change. (b) **alternative — drain `meth_who_p` BACK to plural-collective** to match the sibling fields — rejected, contradicts the pass-35 #329 + pass-34 #322 closures committing the site to singular-editor voice on editorial attribution. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; same defect class as #329 / #322 at the sibling-field layer; the methodology block stacks three cells on the same scroll region and the voice swap is visible to any reader who reads more than one cell. Recommended (a) — content-curator drain across the four sibling methodology fields in all 13 canon files, mirroring the pass-35 mechanic. Sibling positive in the existing `content-check.test.ts` block — assert each of the four fields renders no plural-we / our literal post-drain. Bidirectional drift guard: future canon authoring that re-introduces plural-collective voice at the methodology layer trips at content-check time. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial voice edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the methodology fields describe the ranking method, not any specific season's outcome). - [x] [MED] [authed] /shows A-tier band runs all 10 blurbs through the same `{N} seasons of {plural-noun} {-ing verb}` templated opener — `40 seasons of reality veterans hurling themselves off cliffs...` (The Challenge), `38 seasons of strangers running through airports with backpacks...` (Amazing Race), `28 seasons of one man, twenty-something women, and a mansion...` (Bachelor), `26 seasons of houseguests locked in a wired-up soundstage...` (Big Brother), `22 seasons of professional cooks in unfamiliar kitchens...` (Top Chef), `21 seasons of one woman with the calendar...` (Bachelorette), `20 seasons of designers at sewing machines in a Manhattan workroom...` (Project Runway), `15 seasons of amateur bakers in a white tent on a country estate...` (Bake Off), `11 seasons of singles in a Mallorca villa, recoupling at the fire pit...` (Love Island UK), `6 seasons of singles in a Fiji villa, voting each other into and out of couples...` (Love Island US). 10/10 A-tier `tagline` fields in `content/shows/*.md` open on the same five-word grammar — `{seasons-count} seasons of {plural-noun} {-ing verb-phrase}` — verified via `grep -h '^tagline:' content/shows/*.md`. The S-tier above (Survivor + Drag Race) deliberately opens differently (`Strangers on a beach...` / `17 seasons of queens at a Los Angeles workroom...`); the template uniformity is specific to A-tier, not site-wide. Each individual blurb is in voice on its own; 10 in a row reads as a fill-in-the-blank exercise rather than a knowledgeable peer with 10 different things to say about 10 different shows. Same defect class as the resolved pass-32 #312 canon-rationale closing-formula adjacency closure (`tiered.tv's canon places it [N] because no other [X]` repeating across Survivor #01/#02), now at the catalog-page A-tier band layer rather than the within-canon adjacent-rationale layer. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer ... plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer varies the door they open with when the subject genuinely differs (this is 10 shows across 10 networks across 25 years of TV). The /shows page is the second-loudest editorial surface on the site (catalog entry-point following the home pitch), and the template tic is visible to any reader scrolling the full A-tier band. Concrete observed shape on the rendered /shows page (verified across desktop + mobile authed captures): under the band head `A SPINE OF THE GENRE / The deep canons. Years on the air and the seasons to defend a real ranking.`, the 10 tile-blurbs each lead on the seasons-count opener (mobile renders the tiles stacked; desktop renders them in a grid; the opener echo lands identically across both viewports). Fix options: (a) **primary path — curator-rewrite 3-5 of the 10 A-tier tagline openers** in `content/shows/*.md` so the strip varies its first three words across the band, leaving the strongest count-openers intact so the survivors read as deliberate-editorial rather than templated. Candidate rewrites: Bachelor → `One man, twenty-something women, a mansion in Agoura Hills, 28 seasons of it. The franchise that turned the rose ceremony into a national vocabulary...`; Bake Off → `Amateur bakers in a white tent, signature, technical, showstopper. The gentlest competition on television, and somehow the most watchable across 15 seasons.`; Top Chef → `Professional cooks in unfamiliar kitchens, racing Quickfires and Eliminations toward the dining-room reckoning. The format that took food seriously first, 22 seasons in.`; Bachelorette → `One woman with the calendar, the keys, and the final say. 21 seasons of the flip-the-script sibling that turned out to be its own show...`; Love Island UK → `Singles in a Mallorca villa, recoupling at the fire pit and reading text messages aloud. The 11-season original that the rest of the world has been adapting since 2015...`. Each candidate moves the seasons-count off the opener but preserves it in the body so the catalog still surfaces the count. Exact replacement copy is content-curator's call — the row's contract is "vary the first three words of the A-tier band so ≤5 of the 10 share the same opener lemma." (b) **alternative — sweep the whole `content/shows/*.md` corpus to a fixed catalog-band template** (S-tier ⇒ `{format-frame} ...`, A-tier ⇒ `{N} seasons of ...`) and accept the template as deliberate. Rejected — the defect is exactly that the template is *visible to a scrolling reader as a template*; codifying it doesn't address the editorial-voice critique. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; the 10-of-10 echo is the loudest editorial-voice tic on the second-loudest surface, same defect-class as the pass-32 #312 adjacency closure resolved. Recommended (a) — content-curator drain across 3-5 carriers. Pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` with a `collectShowTaglineTemplateEchoIssues` invariant — for every band (S / A) in the /shows ladder, compute the first-two-word lemma of each show's `tagline` field; if ≥7 of N shows in a band share the same first-two-word lemma, fail with the band-name + the carrier slugs + the shared lemma. Ships lax during the curator drain then flips strict-at-floor-7 (the post-drain max-allowed) after carriers settle. Sibling positive in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` — assert the live catalog reads zero band-template issues post-drain. Bidirectional drift guard: future authoring that re-templates the band trips at content-check time. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (tagline editorial-prose edit only; no per-show verdict change, no tier-band assignment change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the rewrites move the seasons-count from opener to body but preserve every concrete editorial fact). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (tagline editorial-prose addition only; no per-show verdict change, no tier-band assignment change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the new card_taglines move the seasons-count from opener to body but preserve every concrete editorial fact). - [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains section 01 (`THE TAKE`) and section 02 (`THE SHAPE OF THE SEASON`) open with verbatim-near-identical structural sentences within ~80 words of each other. Section 01 (THE TAKE) opens: `Twenty returnees split into a heroes tribe and a villains tribe, filmed on the Samoan coast at the close of the format's first decade.` Section 02 (THE SHAPE OF THE SEASON) opens: `Twenty returnees split into two tribes by reputation, shot in Samoa, treated by the show as a celebration of its first decade.` Both sections lead on `Twenty returnees split into [...] tribe[s]`, both name `Samoa`, both name the show's `first decade`, both then pivot to talk about casting. The TAKE section is the editorial verdict (the show's own canon read); the SHAPE section is the rhythm-and-pacing read (`This is what the season *does* across its run`). They are meant to do different work, but their opening sentences restate the same three facts (cast size, location, anniversary framing) in different word orders before they diverge. A reader scrolling past 01 into 02 reads the same setup twice without progress — the SHAPE section should expand on what TAKE established, not paraphrase its opener. Same defect class as the resolved pass-38 #05/#06 within-entry headline-to-body phrase echoes on /themes/best-finales (Round Table tightening + crown coronation restated verbatim across headline + body of the same entries, closed a5c1de9) and the pending pass-40 LOW within-entry echo on /themes/best-finales #02 (`verdict on the returnee era` repeated across title + blurb of one short entry, Pending); both prior rows address echoes inside ONE entry. This row is the next layer — adjacent within-page section openers restating the same three facts across two sections. Source: `content/shows/survivor/seasons/heroes-vs-villains.md` `take` field opener + `shape` field opener (verify field names at fix-time; the season-frontmatter schema is the gold-standard reference at `phase_26a_season_data_shape.md`). The pull-quote (TAKE intro) and the pacing read (SHAPE intro) both currently lead on the same setup-tax of who-where-when before the verdict / pacing read begins. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer ... plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer who reads two sections in sequence expects them to do different work. Fix options: (a) **primary path — curator-rewrite SHAPE's opening sentence to skip restating the cast-size + Samoa + decade-anniversary framing and lead with the editorial point SHAPE actually owns** — the pacing observation TAKE doesn't reach. Candidate: `The pace is unrelenting from the marooning, and the post-merge stretch is dense with moves the format usually staggers across two seasons.` (Concrete pacing beat preserved; the setup-tax stays in TAKE alone.) Single-content-edit on the season frontmatter `shape` field. Exact replacement copy is content-curator's call — the row's contract is "rewrite SHAPE's opening sentence so the cast-size + Samoa + first-decade facts appear once, in TAKE, not twice." (b) **alternative — rewrite TAKE's opener** to a different framing so SHAPE keeps its setup. Rejected — TAKE is the verdict section and earns the setup-tax first; SHAPE should defer to it, not the other way around. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; same defect class as pass-38 #05/#06 closure at the within-page cross-section opener layer. Recommended (a) — single content-edit on the SHAPE field opener, preserves the editorial verdict TAKE carries, brings SHAPE to its own pacing read. Pin: extend the existing within-entry phrase-echo invariant in `scripts/content-check.ts` (the one the pending pass-40 LOW row above adds for `themes/*.md` title/blurb 5-grams) to also scan adjacent within-page sections of season frontmatter (TAKE / SHAPE) for shared 5-grams between their opening 30 tokens — fails if any 5-gram present in the first 30 tokens of TAKE also appears in the first 30 tokens of SHAPE on the same season. Bidirectional drift guard: future season authoring that re-introduces a same-three-facts opener across TAKE / SHAPE trips at content-check time. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial-prose edit only on the SHAPE opener; the cast-size + Samoa + first-decade facts are documented production metadata; the rewrite shifts to a pacing beat without exposing the winner / elimination order / final tribal / vote outcome — same discipline the existing TAKE / SHAPE bodies hold). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-41) — f9ff581 — issue: #358 — RESOLVED 2ff9006: took the row's primary path (a) — content-curator rewrote the SHAPE body's opening sentence at `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-vs-villains.md` body markdown (lines after closing frontmatter `---`) to lead with the rhythm/pacing read SHAPE actually owns: `The episodes land in a different rhythm than the format's fresher casts can carry. Scenes cut tight, alliances pivot inside single confessionals, and immunity nights stack moves the editor must trust the audience to follow. Pair that velocity with twenty recognizable players placed opposite each other — prior memory doing half the storytelling work — and you get a decade-memory hour that every all-returnee run since has lived in the shadow of.` Zero 5-gram overlap with the `pull` (TAKE) field anywhere in the body. Substance preserved: casting-as-format observation ("recognizable players placed opposite each other — prior memory doing half the storytelling work"), long-shadow framing ("every all-returnee run since has lived in the shadow of"), decade-memory hour closer ("decade-memory hour"). Word count 70 (within the 50-80 SHAPE budget); ~63-char line wrap matches the file's existing body rhythm. Pinned per the row's recommendation + the #325 closure pattern (bidirectional drift guard): new colocated `src/content/__tests__/hvv-take-shape-opener-echo.test.ts` with five cases — (1) extracts both editorial fields from the live content file; (2) positive pin asserting SHAPE body opens on `/^The episodes land in a different rhythm/`; (3) bidirectional negative pin against the prior duplicative opener literals (`Twenty returnees split`, `two tribes by reputation`, `shot in Samoa`, `celebration of its first decade`); (4) generic 5-gram parity check — token-walks both `pull` and body fields, builds a 5-gram set from pull, asserts zero overlap from body; (5) substance-preservation pin (`recognizable players placed` / `all-returnee run since` / `decade-memory hour`). Together they trip at unit time on a future authoring drift, not the next reader pass. Note: a v1 curator draft introduced a NEW echo (verbatim 7-word "the back half rarely lets the editor breathe" lifted from TAKE's sentence 3); a pushback prompt to content-curator with the full TAKE text + explicit banned-phrase list produced this v2 draft with zero overlap. The 5-gram parity case in the pin catches that class of regression generally — any future verbatim 5-gram lifted from any TAKE sentence to anywhere in SHAPE trips at unit time. No URL / schema field / e2e fixture row owed — body markdown edit on a long-shipped season file; no e2e spec asserts on the prior body opener literal (`grep "Twenty returnees split into two tribes by reputation" apps/e2e/` returns empty); the smoke walker pins `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains` URL + the H1 (unchanged) + scrollWidth at 375px (unaffected). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial-prose edit on the SHAPE body opener only; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / final tribal / vote outcome exposure on any surface — the rewrite shifts the opener from setup-tax restatement to a pacing/rhythm beat using documented production observations). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 189 unit files / 2577 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1362 e2e (7.9m).

The pass-35 #329 closure solved the plural-vs-singular voice drift at the `meth_who_p` field layer (the visible defect was 13 canons rendering plural-collective voice on the canon-who cell; the drain rewrote 9 of them to a singular-editor admission ending on this 12-word personal-creed). The intent was right; the unintended side effect is a NEW shared corpus literal at the closing position — a reader scrolling more than one show's canon reads the same 12-word personal-creed twice (or 9 times across the catalog). Same defect class as the resolved pass-11 closure that addressed the parallel-paradox flourish recurring across rationales — different literal, same defect shape (a shared closing-pair authored by a single curator pass landing verbatim across multiple carrier shows). Distinct from pass-35 #329's resolved class (that was voice — plural vs singular; this row is verbatim corpus echo on the closing pair). Concrete observed shape on the rendered canon methodology blocks (verified across multiple shows in the anon walk's /shows/survivor capture + cross-referenced against `content/shows/dragrace/canon.md` `meth_who_p` field): every canon-who cell closes on the same 12-word sentence-pair. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken`; a peer commits to one editorial creed across their work, but doesn't deliver it verbatim across 9 show pages. Candidate rotations (each one captures the singular-editor admission the pass-35 closure committed to, in a different voice register): `It's a read, not a verdict.` / `The point isn't to be right; it's to be honest.` / `Honest about the show, calibrated against the room.` / `Not the only ranking — the one I'd defend in a group chat.` / `Closer to a read than to the truth, but honest enough to argue.` Each carrier picks one rotation; the 9-of-9 literal collapses to 3-4-of-9 voice variants. Same drain mechanic as the pass-35 #329 multi-tick content-curator pass that authored the original closer. (b) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; the cross-show verbatim 12-word echo is the same defect-class as the pass-11 closure addressed at a different layer (the corpus shouldn't carry a 12-word shared literal at the closing position of an identity-bearing field), and the literal is visible to any reader scrolling more than one show-canon. (c) **alternative — drop the closing creed entirely** — rejected; the pass-35 closure committed the site to a singular-editor admission at this position; dropping the creed regresses to the bare paragraph the closure was rewriting away from. Recommended (a) — content-curator drain across the 9 carriers, mirroring the pass-35 mechanic. Pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` with a `collectCanonMethWhoClosingPairEchoIssues` invariant — for every `content/shows/*/canon.md`, scan `meth_who_p`'s last two sentences (the closing pair); if any closing-pair appears verbatim in ≥3 of N carrier canons, fail with the carrier-slugs + the offending sentence-pair. Ships lax during the curator drain then flips strict-at-floor-2 (max 2 carriers may share a closing-pair) after the rotation settles. Sibling positive in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` — assert the live catalog reads zero closing-pair echo issues post-drain. Bidirectional drift guard: future authoring passes that re-introduce a shared closing-pair across ≥3 canons trips at content-check time. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial creed edit only at the canon-who methodology cell; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the rotations rephrase the same singular-editor admission). A reader who hops /themes/best-finales → /shows/survivor (or in either direction via the cross-surface canon-list pairing) reads the same `all-star format ... at its ceiling` frame on both surfaces, framing the same season on the same editorial verdict twice. Same defect class as the pending pass-40 LOW within-entry echo on this same #02 entry (`verdict on the returnee era` repeating between title + blurb of one short entry, Pending) but at the cross-surface layer rather than the within-entry layer. The two rows together represent the #02 HvV editorial corpus carrying two distinct echo classes: one within-entry, one cross-surface. The within-entry echo is the simpler fix (rewrite the blurb close to drop the verdict-frame restatement); this row is the cross-surface fix (rotate one of the two surfaces off the `all-star format ... at its ceiling` frame). Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer ... plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer commits to one editorial verdict per season across the corpus rather than rendering the same six-word frame on two pages a reader hops between. Candidate replacement at `content/themes/best-finales.md:26` (the row's headline `title:` field — content-curator's exact copy call): `An all-star return season that closes on the final tribal the format was building toward.` (Concrete finale-specific frame preserved; the `at its ceiling` editorial verdict stays at /shows/survivor as the canon-anchored read.) (b) **alternative — rewrite the /shows/survivor canon #02 rationale opener** to drop `all-star format running at its ceiling`. Rejected — the canon-rationale is the more editorially-weighted surface; the themed-list entry should defer to the canon read, not the other way around. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; same defect class as the pending #02 within-entry echo at the cross-surface layer. Recommended (a) — single content-edit on the themed-list headline, preserves the canon-rationale's editorial frame, drops the cross-surface verbatim echo. Pin: the existing within-entry phrase-echo invariant the pending pass-40 LOW row above proposes (`content-check` scanning `content/themes/*.md` title/blurb 5-grams) can be extended at the same authoring tick to also scan title 5-grams against `content/shows/<show>/canon.md` rationale 5-grams when the themed-list entry's `show_slug` field matches — fails if any 5-gram in the themed-list `title` field of an entry also appears in the matching canon-rationale `rationale` field. Bidirectional drift guard against future authoring that re-introduces a cross-surface echo on the same season. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (themed-list headline edit only; the headline frames the season's finale shape without naming the winner / elimination / vote outcome — same discipline the original headline holds).
