- [x] [LOW] [anon] /shows/[show]/season/[slug] `CommentInputStub` renders both the action-verb span `Sign in to comment.` and the brand-tagline span `No plot, no winners, no twists →` inside a single `Link` to `/sign-in`, but the arrow `→` sits at the end of the tagline span (not the action span). Source at `src/components/composition/CommentInputStub.tsx:7-20`: the link's body is `<span>Sign in to comment.</span><span className="comment-stub-mono">No plot, no winners, no twists →</span>` — the two spans render as adjacent siblings, with the arrow ligature trailing the second (tagline) span only. Visual signal in the rendered surface: the action verb has no arrow; the brand tagline carries the arrow. A reader scanning the affordance reads the arrow as the destination cue for the tagline (which makes `No plot, no winners, no twists →` look like a link to the spoilers policy or similar) rather than as the destination cue for the action verb (`Sign in to comment. →`). The whole block IS one link to `/sign-in`, so clicking either span goes to the same place — but the visual hierarchy puts the arrow on the wrong span. Same defect class as the pending pass-36 #335 stacked-affordance (HvV comment-thread `Sign in to comment.` + `No comments yet. Be the first to weigh in.` two-voices finding) — that row addresses two-voice stacking on the empty-state; this row addresses arrow-placement-vs-action-verb on the input-stub. Both at the comment-affordance layer. Source: `src/components/composition/CommentInputStub.tsx:16-17` — the arrow ligature is concatenated to the tagline literal, not the action literal. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer puts the directional cue on the verb that takes the reader somewhere, not on the brand-tagline reassurance below it. Fix options: (a) **primary path — move the arrow ligature from the tagline span to the action span**. Single-component edit at `CommentInputStub.tsx:16-17` swapping `<span>Sign in to comment.</span><span className="comment-stub-mono">No plot, no winners, no twists →</span>` → `<span>Sign in to comment. →</span><span className="comment-stub-mono">No plot, no winners, no twists</span>`. The destination cue lands on the action verb; the brand tagline drops to plain prose. Preserves the two-line affordance; preserves the whole-block-is-one-link contract. (b) **alternative — drop the arrow entirely** — rejected; the arrow is the visual signal that this affordance is a link, and removing it leaves the block reading as a plain two-line text block. (c) **alternative — render the two spans as visually-separate items** (e.g. the brand tagline on its own line below the link with no link styling) — larger blast radius (new component / CSS structure) and changes the affordance pattern across every season page; the editorial intent of the brand-tagline-as-reassurance below the link is preserved by option (a) without restructuring. (d) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; the arrow-on-wrong-span reads as either accident or as the tagline being a separate link to an undeclared destination. Recommended (a) — smallest blast radius (one literal swap per span, no component-structure change), brings the directional cue onto the action verb where it does its work. Pin: extend the colocated test on `src/components/composition/__tests__/CommentInputStub.test.tsx` (verify path — create if absent) with two cases — (1) positive pin asserting the rendered action span (the one containing `Sign in to comment`) ends with the arrow ligature (regex `/Sign in to comment\.?\s*→\s*$/`); (2) negative pin asserting the rendered tagline span (the one containing `No plot, no winners, no twists`) does NOT end with the arrow (regex `→` does not appear inside the `.comment-stub-mono` span). Sibling positive on `Thread.test.tsx` asserting the empty-state's stacked-affordance pattern (pass-36 #335 finding above) continues to render independently of the arrow-placement edit. Bidirectional drift guard against a future refactor reverting the arrow position. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome affordance edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the edit moves a directional cue between two spans inside an existing Link, doesn't change the destination or the comment-thread content). The two-span flex layout under `justify-content: space-between` is unchanged; the arrow ligature now lands on the action span (left side, immediately after the verb that takes the reader to `/sign-in`) instead of the tagline (right side, brand reassurance). - [x] [MED] [anon] /shows A-tier band — within the eleven A-tier blurbs the rendered surface stacks together (The Challenge, The Amazing Race, The Bachelor, Big Brother, Top Chef, The Bachelorette, Project Runway, GBBO, Love Island UK, Love Island US, The Traitors), The Amazing Race is the only one that closes in first person — its `tagline` at `content/shows/amazing-race.md:11` ends `I've ranked every leg of every one.` while the other ten close in third-person editorial (`...spends a season chasing.` / `...the format that took food seriously first.` / `...a roster that has played this game before.` and so on). On the /shows tile the field that renders is `card_tagline` when present, else `tagline` (per `CLAUDE.md` show-identity table); for Amazing Race the field rendered is the same first-person tagline. A reader scanning the A-tier band from top to bottom reads ten third-person editorial cards and one first-person beat in the middle. Same defect class as the resolved pass-41 #ii closure (`We've ranked every single one.` plural-collective on Survivor + Amazing Race show-page hero taglines vs same-page canon first-person), but at the /shows tier-tile cross-card layer rather than the same-page intra-page layer — that closure rewired the show-page heroes to the singular-editor voice; this row addresses the inverse defect (the singular-editor `I've` voice landing on a tier-tile band where every other card holds the third-person editorial register). Source: `content/shows/amazing-race.md:11` is the offending field — the closing sentence `I've ranked every leg of every one.` is the literal under fix. The first-person beat belongs on the /shows/the-amazing-race show-page hero where /shows/survivor uses the analogous `I've ranked every single one.` Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; the tier-tile band wants one editorial register so the eye doesn't catch on one card switching pronoun. Fix options: (a) **primary path — author a `card_tagline` field on `content/shows/amazing-race.md` matching the show's A-tier band register**, e.g. `The longest-running travel competition on television — a cross-continent route mechanic that has held its shape across twenty-five years of starting lines.` (drops the first-person close; keeps the show-specific concrete detail). Per `CLAUDE.md` show-identity table, `card_tagline` is the one-sentence form rendered on the /shows tier tile, with the show-page hero quoting the full `tagline`. Single content-file edit; preserves the editor voice on /shows/the-amazing-race hero (the full `tagline` still ships) while shipping the third-person register on the tier tile. (b) **alternative — rewrite the `tagline` field directly** to drop `I've ranked every leg of every one.` Rejected — the show-page hero is the surface that should carry the editor voice; rewriting the full `tagline` regresses the voice the show-page hero needs. (c) **alternative — re-author the other ten A-tier `card_tagline`/`tagline` closes to first person** matching Amazing Race. Rejected — same defect class as pass-41 #ii closure but in reverse; the band's documented voice is third-person editorial on the tile, first-person on the show-page hero, and rewriting ten cards regresses that contract. (d) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; cross-card voice register drift on the tier band is the defect, and the fix is single-file. Recommended (a) — author a `card_tagline` for Amazing Race, brings the A-tier band to one editorial register on the tile, preserves the show-page hero's first-person beat. Pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` with a `collectShowCardTaglineVoiceConsistencyIssues` invariant — for each tier (S/A/B) group of `content/shows/*.md`, tokenize each show's tile-rendered field (`card_tagline` if present, else `tagline`) and flag any tier-group whose tile fields mix first-person markers (`I`, `I've`, `my`) with third-person editorial register. Initial floor: lax (warn only) during catalog drain; strict at floor 0 once the catalog is one register per tier. Sibling colocated test in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts`. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome editorial register edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the rewrite is about pronoun + closing-sentence register, not show content). (URL: /shows, source: critique-pass-42) — e2b1618 — issue: #363 — RESOLVED 56b05f1: took the row's primary path (a). Content-curator authored a `card_tagline` on `content/shows/amazing-race.md` — `Airports and backpacks, route markers and taxi queues across six continents — {yearsWord} years of travel competition that still shapes the genre.` — third-person register matching the rest of the A-tier band tiles; uses the `{yearsWord}` token so the count rolls over without a manual revision (same substitution pattern as the show's full `tagline`). The full `tagline` field stays untouched — `/shows/the-amazing-race` show-page hero still ships the first-person editor voice (`I've ranked every leg of every one.`). Pinned per the row's spec: new `collectShowCardTaglineVoiceConsistencyIssues` invariant in `scripts/content-check.ts` (sibling to `collectShowTaglinePluralEditorIssues`) — checks the same field the renderer reads (`card_tagline ?? tagline` per `ShowsTile.tsx:63`) for first-person-singular markers via `/\b(I'd|I'll|I'm|I've|I|myself|mine|my)\b/i` (case-insensitive so sentence-initial `My` also trips). Tile-only scope — the invariant exempts a show whose `card_tagline` is authored from a `tagline`-field check (that's the whole point of having a tile-only field; the hero keeps the editor voice, the tile takes the band register). Wired into `main()` strict-day-one (`SHOW_TILE_FIRST_PERSON_STRICT = true`) since the Amazing Race fix takes A-tier carrier count from 1 to 0, S-tier carrier count is 0 (Survivor + DragRace both ship third-person card_taglines), B-tier is 0. Nine new colocated cases at `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` (new describe block `content-check — show tile-rendered field first-person voice (critique pass-42, issue #363)`): (a) reads zero issues at the live catalog post-drain; (b) passes for a third-person tile-rendered field; (c) flags the historical Amazing-Race-shaped `I've` close on a bare tagline; (d) reads the rendered tile field — `card_tagline` overrides the `tagline` check (Survivor pattern); (e) flags a `card_tagline` that re-introduces a first-person marker (regression pin against future drift); (f) catches every first-person marker variant (`I` / `I've` / `I'm` / `my`); (g) case-insensitive on the markers — catches sentence-initial `My` and uppercase `I` alike; (h) does not false-positive on substrings (`myth` / `Iliad` / `mind` stay clean); (i) flags each carrier independently when more than one show drifts. Bidirectional drift guard: any future authoring pass slipping a first-person marker back onto a tile-rendered field trips at the verify gate. No URL / schema field / e2e fixture row owed — frontmatter addition on a long-shipped show file; the existing /shows e2e (`apps/e2e/tests/shows.spec.ts`) pins the tier-band shape + tile presence (unaffected); the smoke walker pins `/shows` URL + the H1 (unchanged) + scrollWidth at 375px (unaffected). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (tile-only editorial register edit + invariant addition; no per-season verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the rewrite is about pronoun + closing-sentence register on the tile, not show content). - [x] [MED] [anon] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains anon comment-thread empty-state stacks two arguing CTAs: the `CommentInputStub` link reads `Sign in to comment. →` (the page's only posting affordance for anon visitors) and the `CommentThread` empty-state below renders `No comments yet. Be the first to weigh in.` Two-line message, two competing reads — the input-stub tells the reader the only path forward is signing in, and the empty-state immediately below invites them to weigh in as if a posting affordance is present. For an anon stranger, `Be the first to weigh in` reads as a broken CTA: the eye looks for an input box, finds none, then notices the sign-in line above and has to re-resolve which affordance is real. Same defect class as the pending pass-36 #335 [authed] `signed-in empty-state stacks input + empty-state` finding (RESOLVED c746098 — gated on `viewerCanPost` so the empty-state copy only renders when the input is present), now at the **anonymous** rather than authed layer. Pass-36 closed the authed-side double-render; the anon-side carries the inverse defect (the empty-state copy renders when there is NO input). Verified rendered shape on the pass-42 anon walk capture of `/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains`: thread block text reads `Sign in to comment. → / No plot, no winners, no twists / / No comments yet. Be the first to weigh in.` Two CTAs, no input. Source: `CommentThread` (verify exact path at fix-time — likely `src/components/composition/CommentThread.tsx` or similar) currently renders `No comments yet. Be the first to weigh in.` unconditionally on an empty thread; the pass-36 closure for authed-side double-render gated the empty-state on `viewerCanPost`, but anon's `viewerCanPost === false` should likewise suppress the `Be the first to weigh in.` half (anon visitors can't be first to weigh in; they can only sign in to weigh in). Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer offers one ask per affordance, not two arguing ones. Fix options: (a) **primary path — gate the `Be the first to weigh in.` half of the empty-state on `viewerCanPost`** (anon: hide the second sentence; authed: show it). Mirror of the pass-36 closure at `viewerCanPost` but applied to the empty-state copy rather than the input. Single component edit on the `CommentThread` component (verify path) — wrap the `Be the first to weigh in.` literal in a `{viewerCanPost && (...)}` branch. Anon visitors now see only `No comments yet.` as the empty-state, and the `Sign in to comment. →` input-stub above remains the only posting CTA — the affordance and the invitation align. (b) **alternative — drop the `No comments yet.` literal entirely for anon viewers** — rejected; the empty-state signal that the thread is empty (vs not-yet-loaded) is valuable for anon viewers too; only the posting-invitation half is the offending text. (c) **alternative — rewrite the anon-side empty-state literal** to `No comments yet. Sign in to be the first to weigh in.` — rejected; duplicates the `Sign in to comment. →` ask one line above, doubling the sign-in CTA on the page instead of resolving it. (d) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; same defect class as the resolved pass-36 #335 at the anon-side layer; the empty-state copy should match the affordance the page offers. Recommended (a) — minimal-edit closure mirroring the pass-36 #335 closure mechanic at the anon-side. Pin: extend the colocated test on `src/components/composition/__tests__/CommentThread.test.tsx` (verify exact filename) with two cases — (1) anon empty-state: rendered DOM contains `No comments yet.` but does NOT contain `Be the first to weigh in.`; (2) authed empty-state with `viewerCanPost`: rendered DOM contains both halves. Bidirectional drift guard catches a future refactor that re-introduces the second half on anon. Sibling smoke pin on the season-page e2e at `apps/e2e/src/fixtures/page-reads.ts:?` — anon assertion negates `Be the first to weigh in.`. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (empty-state copy gating only; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the edit removes a CTA literal for anon, doesn't change comment content). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-42) — e2b1618 — issue: #362 — RESOLVED 1ebeb7a: rather than the row's literal primary path (a) — which would have regressed the pass-36 #335 closure by restoring the full "No comments yet. Be the first to weigh in." empty-state for authed viewers — the fix dropped the "Be the first to weigh in." literal entirely from `src/components/composition/CommentThread.tsx:62`. The empty-state is now a pure state signal `No comments yet.` rendered for anon-only (per pass-36's existing `showEmpty = !hasBody && !viewerCanPost` gate); authed viewers continue to see no empty-state line because the input affordance above already invites them. This satisfies both findings' underlying spirit: pass-36 #335 (authed shouldn't see a redundant CTA when the input invites them) and pass-42 #362 (anon shouldn't see a broken CTA when they can't actually weigh in without first signing in). The `Sign in to comment. →` `CommentInputStub` above remains the only posting CTA on the anon-side surface — affordance and invitation align. Component docstring + `viewerCanPost` prop comment rewritten to cite both pass-36 #335 and pass-42 #362 closures so the next maintainer reading the surface understands the two-finding contract. Pin landed per the row's spec at `src/components/composition/__tests__/CommentThread.test.tsx`: three existing pinned text assertions (`No comments yet. Be the first to weigh in.`) swapped to `No comments yet.` across the baseline empty-state case (line 16-18), the anon `viewerCanPost={false}` case (line 111-113), and the SSR-default-to-anon case (line 144-146). A new describe block `anon-side CTA drop (critique pass-42 #362)` added with two regression pins: (1) anon empty-state never contains `be the first` or `weigh in` (rendered DOM contains `No comments yet` only); (2) authed empty thread renders no `be the first` or `weigh in` text anywhere in the container (sibling positive — pass-36 hides the whole line for authed, so neither half of the prior literal should appear). Bidirectional drift guard against a future refactor re-introducing the CTA half at either auth state. No URL / schema / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — the literal `Be the first to weigh in` was not referenced in any e2e fixture or smoke walker assertion. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (empty-state CTA-literal removal only; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the edit removes a broken CTA literal for anon, doesn't change comment content or expose any season's outcome). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 189 unit files / 2581 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1362 e2e (8.0m). Closes #362.

- [x] [MED] [anon|authed] /shows/[show]/season/[slug] comment-stub on mobile renders an unlabeled `⏎` glyph as the right-rail signal on the collapsed CTA (`CommentInputStub` for anon at `src/components/composition/CommentInputStub.tsx`, `CommentInput` collapsed-state for authed at `src/components/composition/CommentInput.tsx:130-142`). Verified rendered shape from the pass-42 walker captures at 375×800: authed-side collapsed CTA reads `Add a thought · no spoilers, please. / as @e2e / ⏎`. The `⏎` is a hint that the keyboard return key triggers the collapsed→open transition (visible per `CommentInput.tsx:130-142` — the collapsed button responds to keyboard activation). On mobile (375×800) the device-level keyboard is not present until the input is focused, so the `⏎` glyph has no first-paint affordance reference — a stranger on mobile reads a single decorative glyph with no plain-English signal of what it does. On desktop the glyph reads naturally (the return key is present on the physical keyboard). Same defect class as the pending pass-37 #336 [authed] /u/e2e own-profile bare-scaffold finding at the affordance-discoverability layer — both surfaces ship an affordance that doesn't read on first paint for the device class viewing it, and both want the affordance signal to land in plain English. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; the `⏎` glyph is clever where plain wins, especially on the device class where the keyboard isn't present. Source: `CommentInputStub.tsx` for anon (verify exact line at fix-time; the brand-tagline span the pass-39 closure adjusted is in this component) and `CommentInput.tsx:140` for authed (the `<span className="comment-stub-mono">{'⏎'}</span>` literal — the glyph the closure should re-shape). Fix options: (a) **primary path — pair the `⏎` glyph with a plain-English label on mobile only** via a CSS media query at the `.comment-stub-mono` class or a JSX-level viewport branch. On mobile (`max-width: 640px` or the existing season-page mobile breakpoint), render `Tap to add a thought` or `Tap to write` in place of (or alongside) the glyph; on desktop, keep the glyph alone. Single CSS rule + (optionally) a JSX viewport class — no schema or content change. (b) **alternative — drop the `⏎` glyph entirely across viewports** and replace with a plain-English label on both. Rejected — the glyph reads naturally on desktop where the keyboard is present; dropping it regresses the desktop affordance. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; mobile-side stranger affordance discoverability is the defect, and the keyboard-shortcut affordance is the surface that wants a plain-English label on touch devices. (d) **alternative — rewrite the entire collapsed CTA** to a different affordance shape (e.g. a single full-width button with `Add a thought →`). Larger blast radius; the existing collapsed-CTA pattern is fine on desktop. Rejected on blast-radius grounds. Recommended (a) — minimal-edit mobile-only label pairing with the glyph; preserves the desktop affordance and brings the mobile affordance to plain English. Pin: extend the colocated tests on `src/components/composition/__tests__/CommentInput.test.tsx` + `src/components/composition/__tests__/CommentInputStub.test.tsx` with one case each — (1) at the mobile viewport breakpoint (mocked via the existing matchMedia or container-class branch), rendered DOM contains a plain-English label (`Tap` or `Add` or `Write` keyword sufficient); (2) at the desktop viewport, the glyph remains alone. Sibling smoke pin on the season-page mobile e2e at `apps/e2e/tests/season-page.spec.ts` (verify exact spec) asserts the mobile composer-stub renders a non-glyph label. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome affordance label edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure — the edit adds a plain-English label on the collapsed-CTA, doesn't change the composer destination or the thread content). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-42) — e2b1618 — issue: #364 — RESOLVED: took the row's primary path (a). The authed `CommentInput` collapsed stub now ships two sibling spans inside `.comment-stub-right` — `comment-stub-mono-desktop` (the `⏎` glyph, `aria-hidden`) and `comment-stub-mono-mobile` (literal `Tap to write`, `data-testid="comment-stub-mobile-label"`). Both nodes ship in the DOM unconditionally; visibility is CSS-only via `src/styles/screens.css` — a new `@media (max-width: 560px)` block flips `display: none` on the desktop glyph and `display: inline` on the mobile label, matching the existing season-page mobile breakpoint convention (`screens.css:484` `.season-page` `--s-gutter` reflow at the same 560px). The desktop affordance (keyboard `↵` cue alongside the physical return key) is preserved; the mobile affordance reads in plain English on a viewport where the device keyboard is not present until focus. The anon `CommentInputStub` was inspected and left untouched — its `comment-stub-mono` span already renders prose (`No plot, no winners, no twists`, the brand-tagline span the pass-39 closure adjusted), so the `[anon|authed]` row scope reduces to the authed surface where the `⏎` literal lives. Pin landed per the row's spec at `src/components/composition/__tests__/CommentInput.test.tsx`: new case `renders a plain-English mobile label alongside the ⏎ glyph on the collapsed stub` — asserts (1) the mobile label is in the DOM via `data-testid="comment-stub-mobile-label"`, (2) it contains a `tap` or `write` keyword (regex `/tap|write/i`), and (3) the `⏎` glyph still ships on the collapsed stub. DOM-presence check pins the wiring; the viewport-conditional paint is css-only and validated by the e2e mobile reflow walker. No `CommentInputStub.test.tsx` extension owed — the anon surface already passes prose. No URL / schema / e2e fixture row owed — the literal `⏎` was not referenced in any e2e fixture or smoke walker assertion; the existing season-page mobile reflow assertion (375×800, no horizontal scroll) covers the new span layout. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome affordance label edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the edit adds a plain-English label on the collapsed-CTA, doesn't change the composer destination or the thread content). Closes #364.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] /themes — every featured card and every list-index card carries the eyebrow `STABLE LIST` with no first-paint definition. Verified rendered shape from the pass-42 walker captures at /themes: the three featured-this-month cards (Comebacks worth the swing, Finales that stuck the landing, Premieres that earned it) each render `STABLE LIST` above the `read the list →` CTA, and the twelve all-lists index cards each render `STABLE LIST` above their `→` CTA. Twelve+ cards on one page stamped with the same two-word eyebrow with no glossary, tooltip, or hero-band explainer. The home page does the analogous job for rankings via the `01 · CURATED Editor's Canon` / `02 · LIVE Community Rank` explainer band — /themes ships the label without the explainer. A first-time visitor sees `STABLE LIST` on every card and has to guess: stable as in not-yet-revised? stable as in vote-locked? stable as in `we stand by it`? The most likely intended meaning is `signed editorial list, not vote-driven` (the analog of the home page's `CURATED` band), but the reader has to infer. Source: `src/components/lists/FeaturedCard.tsx` + `src/components/lists/ListRow.tsx` (the two surfaces that render the `STABLE LIST` eyebrow; verify exact line + class at fix-time — the `.feat-foot` + `.list-row-meta` styles the pass-38 #342 casing closure addressed). Same defect class as the resolved pass-30 #310 /themes hero comprehension closure (the hero band gained a peer-voice intro after a similar `what does this index mean?` reader question) but at the per-card eyebrow layer rather than the hero band. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer who calls a list `stable` either tells you what stable means or doesn't reach for the label. Fix options: (a) **primary path — add a one-line explainer near the `Featured this month` header** at the top of /themes, in the same shape the home page renders for `01 · CURATED Editor's Canon`. Candidate copy at the `ListsHero` component (`src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx` — verify path): below the existing hero subtitle, a 1-line gloss reading `Every list here is editor-signed and stable — written once, revised when we change our mind, not vote-driven.` (or the curator's preferred phrasing). Drops the eyebrow ambiguity by giving `STABLE` a definition the reader carries through the rest of the page. Single component + content edit. (b) **alternative — drop the `STABLE LIST` eyebrow entirely** until a `LIVE LIST` variant exists to contrast against. Rejected — the eyebrow does editorial work (signaling list-lifecycle) even when there's only one variant; dropping it regresses the signal. (c) **alternative — gloss the eyebrow inline** (e.g. `STABLE LIST · editor-signed`) on every card. Rejected — twelve+ cards on the page would double-stamp the gloss; the hero-band explainer carries the gloss once for the whole page. (d) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; first-paint reader comprehension on the page's primary editorial signal is the defect. Recommended (a) — single hero-band explainer near `Featured this month`, defines `STABLE` once for the whole page. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome explainer addition only; no per-list verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the line defines a list-lifecycle label, not list content). The jury room is stacked with players who know exactly what they're hearing, and the vote pulls from twenty years of receipts.` Two Survivor returnee/champion finale entries, two cards apart on one ranked list, both terminating on the same `<duration> of receipts` noun-metaphor framing for the same dynamic — jury weighing accumulated history. (b) **alternative — rotate #02 instead of #04** — rejected; #02 has already absorbed pass-32 #314 + pass-34 #322 + pass-42 closure rotations on adjacent fields; rotating #02 a fourth time risks editorial fatigue on a single entry. The pass-36 LOW row pending on #02 + #04 (`final tribal` close repetition) is the same shape and already proposes #04 as the rotation target for that pair — keeping consistency on which entry absorbs cross-entry rotations. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; cross-entry within-list noun-metaphor echo on two same-show entries is the defect, same defect class as the pending pass-36 LOW already-flagged repetition. Recommended (a) — single content-edit on the #04 blurb, preserves the more-worked #02 rotation, drops the cross-entry close-beat noun-metaphor echo. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial prose edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on either surface — the rotations rephrase the same jury-weight admiring beat). - [x] [MED] [authed] /u/e2e own-profile empty-state CTA verb and destination disagree by one click. Verified rendered shape from the pass-43 authed walk capture at both desktop + mobile: `Your record / @e2e / Member since May 2026 / No votes yet. Cast one and your record starts writing itself. / Start with Survivor → / Browse all shows →`. The promise verb is `Cast one` (sourced from `src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx:54` `'No votes yet. Cast one and your record starts writing itself.'`); the CTA underneath reads `Start with Survivor →` (sourced from `src/components/profile/ProfileEmpty.tsx:64` `Start with {selfView.showName} →` with `href={selfView.showHref}`). The featured-show `showHref` resolves to `/shows/survivor` (the canon index) per the resolved pass-26 LOW closure (CRITIQUE.md done block line 962 referenced in the pass-26 #285 rationale — the featured-show CTA was kept as the first-move pointer to the canon index rather than a specific season). `/shows/survivor` renders the Editor's Canon tier ladder + per-show palette chrome; no vote-pair affordance is visible on that page. The vote-pair widget lives at `/shows/[show]/season/[slug]` — the season detail page, one click further than the canon index. The signed-in member who took the CTA at its word (`cast one`) must scroll the canon, pick a season tile (e.g. Cagayan at #01), and only on the season detail page does the vote-pair widget appear. Promise verb and link destination are one click out of phase. Distinct from the pending pass-37 #336 own-profile bare-scaffold finding — that row is about the profile chrome carrying no zeroed stat-strip scaffold (a structural defect on the empty-state shape); this row is about the one CTA the empty state DOES carry pointing one hop short of the action the verb names (a promise/delivery defect on the affordance). Same defect class as the resolved pass-19 #240 home `pairwise` claim vs single-binary vote mechanic (closed 5c07076) — both rows are promise/delivery mismatches between an editorial line and the affordance the page actually ships, at different surface layers. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer points you at the thing you can do, not the place you have to look to find it. Fix options: (a) **primary path — point the empty-state CTA at the featured show's #01 canon-slot season page** so the verb (`cast one`) and the destination (a page with the vote-pair widget) line up in one tap. Concrete: extend `selfView`'s shape with a `featuredSeasonHref` field plumbed from the same featured-show loader that already supplies `showName` + `showHref` — the loader picks the show's canon #01 season slug at server-render time (e.g. `/shows/survivor/season/cagayan` for Survivor). The CTA literal can stay `Start with {seasonLabel} →` (where seasonLabel is the season's display name) or shift to `Cast your first vote →` paired with the deep link. Keep `Browse all shows →` as the secondary catalog-explore alternative. Single component edit + loader plumb. (b) **alternative — change the empty-state verb to match the current destination** so the promise becomes `Pick a season and cast your first vote.` followed by the canon-index CTA. Smaller blast radius (single literal edit at `ProfileEmpty.tsx:54`) but trades the verb-tightness the pass-26 #285 closure committed to (`Cast one and your record starts writing itself.` is the in-voice line; weakening it to `Pick a season and cast your first vote` loses the rhythmic confident verb). The pass-19 #240 / pass-22 closures consistently chose the verb-tightening path; (a) preserves the verb. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; promise/delivery defect on the one affordance the signed-in member sees on first paint at their own profile is the defect. Recommended (a) — single CTA-destination edit + loader plumb, preserves the in-voice verb (`Cast one`), brings the destination into phase with the verb. Pin: extend `src/components/profile/__tests__/ProfileEmpty.test.tsx` with two cases — (1) positive — the rendered CTA's `href` attribute matches the `/shows/<show>/season/<slug>` shape (regex `/^\/shows\/[a-z-]+\/season\/[a-z0-9-]+/`) AND the `Cast one` empty-state copy is still present; (2) negative — the rendered CTA's `href` is NOT the bare `/shows/<show>` index path while the empty-state copy reads `Cast one and your record starts writing itself.` (bidirectional drift guard against a future authoring pass silently regressing the destination back to the canon-index path). Sibling positive on the loader: `src/content/__tests__/loaders.test.ts` (or wherever `getFeaturedShow` lives — verify path at fix-time) gains one case asserting the loader returns a `featuredSeasonHref` matching the canon #01 slug. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome destination edit only; the season landing page is the existing canon #01 season detail which already carries spoiler-shield + vote-pair affordance; no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure introduced — the CTA just points one click deeper into the existing nav graph). The row's static-code reasoning cited a stale closure: the pass-26 #285 rationale at CRITIQUE.md done line 962 was superseded by c62c4e3 (2026-05-27 `fix: route /u/<self> empty-state CTA to a season page (#203)`), which plumbed `pickFeaturedSeason` through `src/lib/profile/context.ts` and rewired `src/app/(default)/u/[handle]/page.tsx:140-153` so `selfView.showHref` is computed as ``/shows/${featuredShow.slug}/season/${selfViewSeason.slug}`` whenever the canon's top entry resolves to a real season file — falling back to `/shows/${featuredShow.slug}` only when `pickFeaturedSeason` returns null (no canon, or top entry's season missing). Verification this tick: (1) `pnpm tsx` probe of `getFeaturedShow()` + `pickFeaturedSeason(getCanon(slug), n => getSeason(slug, n))` returned `{ slug: 'cagayan', number: 28 }`; (2) the 28-test `src/app/(default)/u/[handle]/__tests__/page.test.tsx` suite is green and locks the season-URL contract at line 434-437 (`expect data-cta-href = '/shows/survivor/season/<top-canon-slug>'`) plus the show-home fallback at line 446-449; (3) a minted-cookie HTTPS GET of `https://tiered.tv/u/e2e` returned `<a ... data-testid="profile-empty-cta" href="/shows/survivor/season/cagayan">Start with <!-- -->Survivor<!-- --> →</a>` (raw HTML capture verified). - [x] [LOW] [anon] /themes/best-finales #03 (Top Chef) title reads as a stumbled compound modifier: `The deepest knife-skill cast carries the kitchen all the way home.` Verified rendered shape on the pass-43 walker capture at both desktop + mobile (text matches the source literal at `content/themes/best-finales.md:32` `title: "The deepest knife-skill cast carries the kitchen all the way home."`). The singular noun `knife-skill` is being asked to act as a compound modifier on `cast`, but it does not parse cleanly on first read — cooks have knife skills (plural). The eye stumbles between `knife-skill cast` (compound-modifier read) and `knife, skill cast` (apposition misread) before it resolves. The other six entries on the list each carry plain-grammar titles without compound-modifier stumbles — only entry #03 reaches for the singular-noun-as-adjective construction. Same defect class as the resolved pass-28 #292 Amazing Race tagline `twenty-five of starting lines` grammar finding (closed ac2dafe) at the catalogue editorial-byline-grammar layer; both rows are single-content-file grammar slips on a brand-central editorial surface that a peer voice should not ship. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer who calls a cast `deep` names the skill in its natural plural. Fix options: (a) **primary path — pluralize the noun head** at `content/themes/best-finales.md:32` `title:` to read `The deepest knife-skills cast carries the kitchen all the way home.` Single one-character content edit; preserves the entry's editorial point (cast depth in technique) and resolves the compound-modifier stumble. (b) **alternative — restructure the title** to drop the compound modifier entirely, e.g. `The deepest-skilled kitchen carries the season home.` or `A cast deep enough on knife work to carry the finale.` Both read cleaner but trade the entry's emphasis on `cast` (a Top Chef finale's key variable) for `kitchen` or `finale`. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; first-read grammar stumble on a list-index title is the defect, same defect class as the resolved pass-28 #292. Recommended (a) — single-character pluralization edit, preserves the entry's editorial frame, resolves the compound-modifier read. Pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts`'s existing entry-title grammar pass (verify the helper name — likely the title sweep added by an earlier closure) with a regex pass against `content/themes/*.md` entry `title:` fields flagging singular-noun-as-adjective constructions when paired with a head noun the singular-noun's typical plural form occupies (e.g. `knife-skill` + cast/kitchen/cook → flag; `knife-skills` + same → pass). Initial floor: lax (warn only); strict at floor 0 once the corpus drains. Sibling colocated test in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts`. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (title prose edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure — Top Chef finalist names / elimination order / cast composition unchanged). (URL: /themes/best-finales, source: critique-pass-43) — e795a43 — issue: #370 — RESOLVED 1ff632a: took the row's recommended primary path (a). Single one-character pluralization at `content/themes/best-finales.md:32` `title:` — `The deepest knife-skill cast carries the kitchen all the way home.` → `The deepest knife-skills cast carries the kitchen all the way home.` Preserves the entry's editorial point (cast depth in technique) and resolves the compound-modifier stumble. The other six entries on the list each carry plain-grammar titles without compound-modifier stumbles; #03 now joins them. Pinned per the pass-36 #325 closure pattern (bidirectional drift guard): new colocated regression test `src/content/__tests__/best-finales-top-chef-title-grammar.test.ts` reads the live `content/themes/best-finales.md` Top Chef entry block and asserts (1) the title quotes the plural `knife-skills cast` form AND (2) the title does NOT match the prior singular `knife-skill cast` form — a future authoring pass that regresses to the singular-noun-as-adjective construction trips at unit time. The proposed broader pin (extending `scripts/content-check.ts` with a regex pass against `content/themes/*.md` entry `title:` fields flagging singular-noun-as-adjective constructions paired with a head noun the typical plural form occupies) is **deferred** — the catch-everything heuristic requires a fuzzy POS-tagging layer the corpus doesn't justify yet at corpus N=12 themes / ~80 entries; the colocated regression-guard catches this row's specific defect class at unit time without the false-positive burden of a corpus-wide singular-noun detector. No URL / schema / page-family / e2e fixture row owed — title prose edit only. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure — Top Chef finalist names / elimination order / cast composition unchanged). - [ ] [LOW] [anon+authed] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains mobile — the canon-position chrome under the season hero surfaces the rank label `#02` twice in adjacent stacked rows. Verified rendered shape from the pass-43 walker capture at 375×800: `EDITOR'S CANON / 50 SEASONS / #02 / of 50 / #02 / #01 · CANON PEAK / #50 · THE TAIL` — the first `#02` is the slot indicator (`<this season>'s rank in the canon` paired with `50 SEASONS / of 50`), the second `#02` is the canon-meter pip marking the season's position on the meter range (paired with `#01 · CANON PEAK / #50 · THE TAIL`). At first paint on mobile, where the rows stack vertically with minimal visual separation, the duplicated `#02` token reads as a duplicate render before the eye picks up the meter context. On desktop the two `#02` tokens sit on different visual columns (slot card on the left, canon meter on the right) so the duplication does not stack visually. Same defect class as the resolved pass-39 #346 `STABLE LIST` per-card eyebrow duplication at the chrome-token-repeat layer, now at the mobile-stack canon-meter layer. Source: the canon-position chrome on the season page — likely `src/components/season/SeasonInfoCard.tsx` for the slot indicator (`50 SEASONS / #02 / of 50` shape) + `src/components/canon/CanonMeter.tsx` or equivalent for the meter pip (verify exact component paths at fix-time). Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer marks `you are here` without restating the rank number adjacent to itself. Fix options: (a) **primary path — replace the canon-meter pip's `#02` label with a non-numeric `YOU ARE HERE` marker** (or a filled bullet in `var(--show-primary)` per the `design/CLAUDE.md` 5-rule discipline that filled-circle bullets are the only show-specific graphic permitted). The slot indicator keeps the `#02` numeric label (it's the primary rank statement); the meter pip becomes a positional marker rather than a second numeric statement. Single component edit at the canon-meter component. (b) **alternative — label the meter row `CANON METER` (eyebrow above the row)** so the second `#02` reads as `where on the meter` rather than `rank again`. Preserves the numeric label but adds chrome to disambiguate. Smaller blast radius (single eyebrow addition) but doesn't drop the duplicated numeric token. (c) **alternative — drop the meter pip's `#02` label entirely and rely on positional placement alone** (the pip sits on the meter row at the correct position). Cleanest but loses the rank-confirm signal for users who scroll past the slot indicator. (d) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; mobile-stack duplicate-token first-paint reading is the defect. Recommended (a) — drops the duplicated numeric token, preserves the meter pip's positional signal, brings the chrome in line with the `design/CLAUDE.md` filled-bullet discipline. Pin: extend the colocated test on the canon-meter component (verify path at fix-time) with one case — at the mobile viewport (375×800), the rendered DOM under the season hero contains the rank label `#02` AT MOST ONCE in any contiguous stack-row window (computed via DOM order + viewport-row computation, similar to the pass-22 #261 rendered-row arithmetic). Bidirectional drift guard against a future refactor reintroducing the duplicated numeric label. Sibling positive on the desktop viewport — the two-column layout preserves both labels in their respective columns. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome marker edit only; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure — the marker swap is positional, not editorial). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-43) — e795a43 — **Severity bumped LOW → MED at pass-74** and corroborating URL added: `/shows/american-ninja-warrior/season/the-proving-ground` (a freshly-seeded single-season show) reproduces the same root component with a worse symptom — confirmed independently by both the pass-74 anon and authed reader walks, desktop + mobile: `EDITOR'S CANON / 1 SEASON / #01 / of 1 / #01 / #01 · CANON PEAK / #01 · THE TAIL`, i.e. the rank token `#01` appears three times because `total === 1` makes the peak endpoint, the tail endpoint, and the "here" marker all resolve to the same number. Confirmed at the source: `src/components/composition/RankScale.tsx:57-60` renders the `.scale-marks` div's `#01 · canon peak` / `#{pad2(total)} · the tail` endpoint labels unconditionally, with no guard for `total === 1` (where both endpoints and the rank pip collapse to the same value) or for `rank === 1`/`rank === total` (where the pip already sits on one of the endpoints). Every newly-seeded show enters the catalog with exactly one canon.md entry under the site's standing drain-style content mandate (`plan/bearings.md` "Content velocity"), so this triple-render fires on every fresh show until its second season drains in — a recurring, systemic instance, not a one-off. Fix (extends the row's existing option (a)): in `RankScale.tsx`, when `total === 1`, suppress the `.scale-marks` endpoint row entirely (there is no peak-vs-tail range to describe with one entry) in addition to the original fix (replacing the meter pip's numeric label with a non-numeric marker on multi-season shows). Pin: extend `RankScale.test.tsx` with a `total === 1` case asserting `.scale-marks` does not render, alongside the original row's multi-season pin. - [x] [MED] [anon] /themes hero copy uses first-person plural for the editorial voice twice in the same hero block: `12 lists we'd defend in a group chat — 3 featured this month.` (`src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx:28-29`) and `Every list here is editor-signed and stable — written once, revised when we change our mind, not vote-driven.` (`src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx:87`). A reader hopping /shows/survivor (singular `I've ranked every single one.` body, singular `the tiered.tv editor` byline post-#306 closure) → /themes meets a plural-we voice for the same editor function — the editorial bylines have committed to singular across the catalog via the pass-31 #306 cross-surface drain (closed 63a711d) and the recent `meth_who_p` canon-cell rotation across 9 canon files (e795a43, pass-41 LOW closure), but the /themes hero lede + the per-list editor-signed gloss both kept their plural-we voice. The post-#306 invariant (`collectEditorialBylineSingularIssues` in `scripts/content-check.ts`) catches `tiered.tv editors` byline literals but does not scan first-person plural pronouns in editor-voice prose. Same defect class as the resolved pass-31 #306 cross-surface plural-vs-singular drift, now at the within-hero editor-voice-pronoun layer. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken, never pretentious`; the same brand commits to singular admission on /about (`Built and operated by one person`) and on every editorial byline post-#306, so the /themes hero is the last brand-central surface still using plural-we for the editor function. Single component edit at `src/components/lists/ListsHero.tsx:28-29,87`. Candidate copy: `12 lists I'd defend in a group chat — 3 featured this month.` (line 28) + `Every list here is editor-signed and stable — written once, revised when I change my mind, not vote-driven.` (line 87). Preserves the editorial frame, brings the hero into agreement with /shows/[show] singular body + the singular post-#306 byline; the `editor-signed` noun-phrase already implies singular authorship so the verb-pronoun pair matching strengthens the read. (b) **alternative — rotate to nominal-singular** that drops the first-person entirely: `12 lists worth defending in a group chat — 3 featured this month.` + `…revised when the editor changes their mind, not vote-driven.` Cleaner but loses the first-person editorial warmth the /shows/survivor body voice carries. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; the brand has committed to singular on every other editorial surface; /themes hero is the holdout. Recommended (a) — preserves the first-person editorial register, brings the singular into the last plural-we holdout. Sibling content-check extension: extend `collectEditorialBylineSingularIssues` in `scripts/content-check.ts` to also scan source-file editor-voice prose (the `ListsHero.tsx` literals + the `ShowRanking.tsx` gloss + any future hero literal) for first-person plural pronouns (`/\b(we|our|us)\b/i`) while `/about` carries the singular-admission anchors — bidirectional with the existing byline-plural guard, same anchor-trigger. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (hero prose edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface). Closes #375.

Signed-in viewers (verified on the pass-44 authed walk, signed in as @e2e) see `CommentInput` stub render `Add a thought · no spoilers, please. / as @e2e / ⏎` (`src/components/composition/CommentInput.tsx:133`) — adding a softening `please` to the spoiler clause. `Please` is the only please-flavored polite-customer-service softener anywhere in the chrome pre-action invite; a sibling use exists in the rate-limit error `Easy — please wait before posting again.` at `CommentInput.tsx:89` but that's situational error-handling (the editor stepping in when the user has done something), not a pre-action invite. The two stubs render in the same surface slot on the same page — only the auth-state branch picks which copy lands — and the anon copy carries the editorial baseline (three-noun rule, no please) while the authed copy softens with a please that breaks register. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — confident, warm, plain-spoken, never pretentious`; the peer doesn't say `please` to other peers about spoilers, and the same affordance on the same page should not shift register between viewers. Single-literal edit; preserves the `as @e2e / ⏎` attribution + submit hint. (b) **alternative — add a softening to the anon stub** to match the authed softener: `Sign in to comment, please. → / No plot, no winners, no twists, please.` Rejected — adds politeness softeners to two more surfaces, runs further from the bearings voice cue. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; same-affordance register split is the defect. Recommended (a) — single-character-cluster edit, brings the authed branch into agreement with the anon branch's editorial register. - [x] [MED] [authed] /themes/[theme] `Save list` pre-click button literal reads as account-backed for a signed-in member but writes to localStorage (`src/components/lists/ListDetailTools.tsx:5` `tiered_saved_lists` key; `:87` `{saved ? 'Saved' : 'Save list'}` button literal). The button renders identically for anon and authed viewers — same literal, same backing store, no auth-aware copy. For a signed-in member whose `/u/[handle]` own-profile already carries the `your record starts writing itself` framing (the only persisted-state promise visible elsewhere in the authed session is the vote read-back via the post-#240 single-binary mechanic + canon position), the pre-click `Save list` button reads as account-backed (the natural authed-state assumption). The pass-24 #272 closure landed a `saved on this device` caption that appears AFTER click (`ListDetailTools.tsx:90-93`), but the PRE-click literal still reads `Save list` with no `on this device` cue. A signed-in user clicks the button with the implicit account-backed promise, gets `Saved + saved on this device` — a two-step walk-back of an implied promise on the first interaction. Honest from-the-start version would scope the affordance up-front. Same defect class as the resolved pass-19 #240 home `pairwise` claim vs single-binary mechanic (closed 5c07076 — promise/delivery defect at the editorial line vs the affordance the page ships) and the pass-43 LOW /u/e2e CTA verb-vs-destination row (closed-as-no-defect ea4dafa, with the destination already pre-shipped). The pass-24 #272 closure explicitly punted on the member-record wiring (`bonus Saved lists → row on ProfileStats is deliberately deferred — it owes a member-record wire-up that belongs in a phase, not an iterate tick`) — this row is the residual pre-click promise mismatch that the device-cap closure didn't cover, at the pre-click layer the post-click caption can't reach. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer says what the button does up front, not after the action. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rescope the pre-click literal to acknowledge local-only storage up-front**: change the unsaved label at `ListDetailTools.tsx:87` from `Save list` → `Save (this device)`. Single-literal edit; the post-click `Saved` literal stays as the success state; the existing `saved on this device` caption at `:90-93` reads as the per-event confirmation. (b) **alternative — keep `Save list` but always render the `local to this device` caption beside the button regardless of saved state** (currently only shown post-click). Smaller pre-click footprint, but doubles the caption text on the saved state. (c) **alternative — implement member-record sync** — rejected per the pass-24 #272 closure's explicit deferral (a phase-scale change, not an iterate-tick shape). (d) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; pre-click promise mismatch on a signed-in member's first interaction is the defect. Recommended (a) — single-literal edit, brings the pre-click button into the same scope honesty the post-click caption already carries. Pin: extend `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListDetailTools.test.tsx` (verify the colocated test exists) with two cases — (1) positive — the rendered unsaved button text matches `/Save \(this device\)/` (or `/this device/i` if the parenthetical doesn't survive CSS uppercase); (2) negative — the rendered unsaved button text does NOT match `/^Save list$/` (bidirectional drift guard against a future refactor regressing the bare literal). Sibling positive on the post-click `Saved` literal and the `saved on this device` caption stay unchanged. Member-record wire-up (Save list → /u/[handle] LISTS SAVED row on ProfileStats) remains deferred as pass-24 #272 noted. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome button literal edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the literal frames the storage scope, not the list's content). (URL: /themes/best-finales, source: critique-pass-44) — b52875a — closed 7c51c88 (recommended path (a) — pre-click literal at `ListDetailTools.tsx:87` rescoped `Save list` → `Save (this device)`; the post-click `Saved` literal + `saved on this device` caption stay unchanged so the success state is unchanged. Colocated test in `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListDetailTools.test.tsx` extended per the pin: positive matches `/Save \(this device\)/`; bidirectional negative pin asserts `/^Save list$/` does NOT match — drift guard against a future refactor regressing the bare literal. Pre-click promise now scoped honestly from the start; the first-interaction walk-back closes. issue #377)
- issue: #377

- [x] [MED] [authed] Mobile (≤720px) header account-menu trigger is visually indistinguishable from a static label. The desktop branch renders `@e2e / Sign out` as two visible links; mobile collapses to a single `<button aria-haspopup="menu">` whose contents is just the handle `@e2e` (`src/components/chrome/HeaderView.tsx:143-155`). CSS at `src/styles/chrome.css:161-176` styles it as plain mono text — same 12px mono, same color, same letter-spacing as static chrome eyebrows — with NO chevron, NO caret, NO underline, NO disclosure glyph. The hover→opacity 1.0 cue has no mobile equivalent (mobile has no hover). Screen-reader users get `aria-haspopup` + `aria-expanded` semantics; sighted mobile users get nothing telling them the handle opens a menu. A fresh-eyes mobile peer who wants to sign out (or navigate to their profile) reads `@e2e` as a label and looks elsewhere for the affordance — the pass-23 #268 closure that moved sign-out behind the trigger to prevent mis-taps relied on the trigger being discoverable; without a visual disclosure indicator the discovery cost is high on mobile specifically. Verified across every pass-44 authed mobile capture (7 URLs × 1 viewport at 375×800) — the only text in the auth-state slot is `@e2e` with no visible affordance signal. Same defect class as the resolved pass-23 #268 mis-tap-guard closure (header chrome affordance shape vs viewer expectation), now at the disclosure-glyph layer the #268 closure didn't cover. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer points at the menu by showing it's a menu, not by hiding behind aria. Fix options: (a) **primary path — add a tiny disclosure indicator next to the handle on mobile only** via a `::after` pseudo-element scoped to the existing `@media (max-width: 720px)` block at `src/styles/chrome.css`. Smallest change: `.site-header-user-trigger::after { content: ' ▾'; opacity: 0.7; margin-left: 0.25em }` inside the mobile media query. Single CSS rule, no markup change, no aria change needed (`aria-haspopup` already pins screen-reader semantics; the chevron supplements the sighted-mobile cue). (b) **alternative — render the `Sign out` link inline on mobile** matching the desktop branch, dropping the menu disclosure entirely. Rejected — the pass-23 #268 closure deliberately moved sign-out behind a tap to prevent mis-taps on mobile; reverting that defeats the prior closure. (c) **alternative — replace `@e2e` with a hamburger icon** — rejected; loses the editorial choice the pass-23 closure made to surface the user's handle as the primary auth-state signal. (d) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; mobile-only disclosure-signal gap is the defect. Recommended (a) — single CSS rule edit, preserves the trigger's editorial form (handle as primary label) AND the pass-23 closure's mis-tap-guard (menu behind tap) AND adds the missing visual disclosure for sighted mobile viewers. Pin: extend `src/components/chrome/__tests__/HeaderView.test.tsx` (verify the colocated test exists) with one case — at the mobile viewport (`@media (max-width: 720px)` via jsdom or fixture), the rendered `.site-header-user-trigger::after` computed `content` is NOT `none` (asserts the chevron pseudo-element renders); negative pin asserts the desktop viewport's `::after` is `none` (chevron is mobile-only, doesn't double up on desktop where `Sign out` already renders inline). Sibling positive on the existing `aria-haspopup="menu"` semantic pin stays unchanged. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome CSS pseudo-element edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the indicator points at a menu, not at any season's outcome). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains (mobile only), source: critique-pass-44) — b52875a — issue: #378 — RESOLVED dbd84bf: took the row's recommended primary path (a). Added a `.site-header-user-trigger::after` rule inside the existing `@media (max-width: 720px)` block at `src/styles/chrome.css:184-188` — `content: ' \25BE';` (U+25BE BLACK DOWN-POINTING SMALL TRIANGLE, the same chevron the design's mobile disclosure pattern uses), `opacity: 0.7;`, `margin-left: 0.25em;`. The Unicode escape sidesteps a literal `▾` glyph in the source CSS (keeps the file ASCII-safe; the browser renders the codepoint identically). Desktop (>720px) does not declare the pseudo-element — the inline `Sign out` link continues to serve as that branch's interactive-surface signal, so the chevron doesn't double up. No markup change to `HeaderView.tsx`, no aria change (`aria-haspopup="menu"` + `aria-expanded` already pin the screen-reader contract). Pinned at source via two new cases in `src/components/chrome/__tests__/HeaderView.test.tsx` in a fresh describe block, modeled on the `FeaturedCard.test.tsx` `.feat-foot` casing pin pattern (`src/components/lists/__tests__/FeaturedCard.test.tsx:166`): (1) the header mobile media block (`headerMobileMediaBounds()` locates the right `@media (max-width: 720px)` by finding the opener nearest-before the `.site-header-user-trigger::after` rule — chrome.css carries three separate `(max-width: 720px)` blocks for wrap padding / header chrome / footer cols, so a naive `indexOf` picks the wrong one) declares the rule with a non-`none` `content:` value; (2) bidirectional negative — the rule does NOT appear outside the mobile block, AND the sibling positive that the rule's one source of truth IS the mobile slice. A future refactor moving the chevron to desktop or dropping it from mobile cannot regress without tripping the unit gate. The pass-23 #268 mis-tap-guard closure remains intact (sign-out still behind a tap); the chevron only changes the trigger's affordance shape, not its behavior. Spoiler discipline P0 held — chrome pseudo-element edit only, no per-show verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface. Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 191 unit files / 2620 tests (+2 new HeaderView chevron-pin cases) + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1362 e2e.

- [ ] [LOW] [anon] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains within-page hyphenation drift on the compound noun `back-half`. Verified shapes from the pass-44 walker captures at both desktop + mobile: EPISODE HEAT spec strip reads `peak run · the back-half` (hyphenated — the form the pass-29 #305 closure committed to as canonical at `episode_heat_caption` and across themed-list editorial fields); THE TAKE body section two scroll-units below reads `The post-merge stretch runs denser than the format usually permits, and the back half rarely lets the editor breathe.` (unhyphenated). Same page, same compound noun, two forms one scroll apart. This is exactly the broader-corpus drift the pass-29 #305 closure deliberately deferred — that closure's scope was `themed-list editorial fields + season episode_heat_caption` only, with the row's own note inviting `a future critique [to flag] wider drift visible on a single reader scroll`. HvV TAKE body is that wider drift case: the body prose at `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-vs-villains.md` (verify the field name at fix-time — likely `take` or `body_md` block) carries the unhyphenated form roughly 100 characters below the chrome's hyphenated form, and both render in the same reader scroll. Same defect class as the resolved pass-29 #305 themed-list `back-half` drift (closed by hyphenating in the `episode_heat_caption` + themed-list field surface), now at the season-page-body layer the prior closure left out of scope. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer holds one hyphenation for the same compound noun on one scroll. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rewrite `the back half` → `the back-half` in the HvV TAKE body** at `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-vs-villains.md` (verify the field name — likely `take` per the section's editorial slot; the body prose carrying `The post-merge stretch runs denser than the format usually permits, and the back half rarely lets the editor breathe.`). Then extend `collectBackHalfHyphenIssues` in `scripts/content-check.ts` to scan season body fields (`take` / `shape` / `body_md` / `watch_for` per the season frontmatter schema at `src/content/schemas.ts`) so the same drift can't reappear on adjacent season-page surfaces unobserved. The prior pass-29 #305 helper's scope was deliberately narrow (`episode_heat_caption` + themed-list fields); the expansion-trigger is now met. (b) **alternative — rewrite the chrome `peak run · the back-half` to the unhyphenated form** and propagate to every other surface the pass-29 closure committed. Rejected — the pass-29 closure deliberately picked hyphenation as the canonical form (matches the `back-half` noun pattern in editorial style guides; the unhyphenated form parses as `back` + `half` as separate nouns, a slip a peer doesn't ship). (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; within-page same-noun two-form on one scroll is the defect class the pass-29 closure deferred. Recommended (a) — content rewrite + helper-scope expansion, brings the season-body field into the same hyphenation discipline as the chrome + themed-list surfaces. - [x] [LOW] [anon] Cross-surface label-scope ambiguity on the chrome label `SEASONS RANKED`: home hero featured-show stat-strip reads `50 / SEASONS RANKED / May 2026 / CANON REVISED` (Survivor-scoped — the featured show's per-show seasons count); /shows hero stat-strip reads `13 SHOWS TRACKED / 298 SEASONS RANKED / May 2026 / SHOWS REVISED` (catalog-scoped — every show's seasons summed). Same chrome label slot, two different scoped facts on the natural home → /shows click path. Both surfaces use the identical typography pattern (large number, small-caps mono label, identical hero-stat-strip slot) so a reader scanning the chrome reads the same label as if it were the same fact updating between surfaces. The within-page corroboration is visible on home alone: the home brand-block subhead reads `13 shows tracked. Every season reviewed.` (catalog-scope claim in prose) while the home featured-tile stat reads `50 SEASONS RANKED` (one-show-scope claim in chrome) — even within the home page the `SEASONS RANKED` label slot's scope sits one section apart from a different-scoped sibling fact. Same defect class as the resolved pass-39 #347 corpus-label rotation (`SHOWS REVISED` vs `LISTS REVISED` rotation on the revision-date stat slot to disambiguate /shows vs /themes), now at the count-label slot the #347 closure didn't reach. The #347 closure proved the chrome's `<NOUN> <STATE>` label pattern is the right axis to carry scope — applying the same rotation discipline to the count label closes this row. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer names the scope on the label, not via context the reader has to compute. Fix options: (a) **primary path — differentiate the count label by scope**, parallel to the #347 corpus-label rotation: home featured tile → rotate `SEASONS RANKED` → `SEASONS IN CANON` (names the per-show count anchored to that show's canon length, the editorial unit the home featured tile is sized to); /shows hero → keep `SEASONS RANKED` (the catalog-aggregate, the fact /shows is editorially about). Single-literal edit on the home featured stat-strip (verify path — likely `src/components/home/HomeHero.tsx` or `HomeFeaturedTile.tsx`); /shows hero stays unchanged. (b) **alternative — rotate /shows hero instead**: `SEASONS RANKED` → `SEASONS ACROSS THE CATALOG`. Acceptable but the catalog-aggregate is the more abstract scope and the rotation reads as more verbose; (a) puts the rotation on the per-show surface where the scope is more concrete. (c) **alternative — rotate both** — rejected; the catalog-aggregate is the editorially-canonical `SEASONS RANKED` claim (matches the `the seasons, ranked.` brand promise), so the home featured tile is the slot that should absorb the rotation. (d) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; same chrome label, two scopes on the natural click path is the defect class the #347 closure proved the rotation discipline closes. Recommended (a) — single-literal edit, brings the home featured tile's scope into agreement with the editorial unit (the show's canon) while preserving /shows hero as the catalog-aggregate carrier. Pin: extend the colocated tests at both surfaces — (1) `src/components/home/__tests__/HomeHero.test.tsx` (or equivalent): positive — rendered home featured stat-strip contains `SEASONS IN CANON`; negative — does NOT contain `SEASONS RANKED` on the home featured slot; (2) `src/components/shows/__tests__/ShowsHero.test.tsx`: positive — rendered /shows hero stat-strip contains `SEASONS RANKED` (catalog-aggregate); negative — does NOT contain `SEASONS IN CANON` (ensures the two labels remain disjoint across surfaces). Bidirectional drift guard against a future regression converging them back. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome label edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the label frames the count's scope, not any season's outcome). - [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/survivor hero stat-strip still reads `50 / SEASONS RANKED` while the freshly-rotated home featured tile (pass-44 #379 closure at a33cede) now reads `50 / SEASONS IN CANON` for the same show. Same fact, two labels, one click apart on the natural home → /shows/survivor click path. The pass-44 #379 closure rotated the home featured tile's per-show count label off the catalog-aggregate `SEASONS RANKED` literal to disambiguate from /shows hero's catalog-aggregate `298 SEASONS RANKED`; that closure intentionally LEFT the /shows/survivor show-page hero out of scope (per the closure's own note: `the show-page hero label was intentionally left out of scope — src/lib/canon/seasons-stat-label.ts still produces seasons ranked for fully-drained shows, matching /shows hero's catalog-aggregate slot rather than the rotated home featured tile`). But the show-page hero is editorially a per-show surface, not a catalog-aggregate carrier — Survivor's hero stat IS the per-show count (50 seasons IN this show's canon), with the catalog aggregate (`298 SEASONS RANKED across 13 shows`) living one click up at /shows. The pass-44 closure aligned home and /shows by their editorial scope (home featured = per-show, /shows = catalog) but stopped one surface short — the /shows/survivor show page is also a per-show editorial surface and should carry the per-show label. A reader who hops home → /shows/survivor reads the same fact under two labels (`50 / SEASONS IN CANON` → `50 / SEASONS RANKED`); a reader who hops home → /shows → /shows/survivor reads three slots (`50 / SEASONS IN CANON` → `298 / SEASONS RANKED` → `50 / SEASONS RANKED`) where slot 1 and slot 3 carry the same number under different labels and slot 3 carries a different number from slot 2 under the same label. Same defect class as the resolved pass-44 #379 — chrome label slot scope drift — at the show-page layer the #379 closure named-and-deferred. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer carries the editorial scope on the label across all the per-show surfaces the reader hops through, not just the home featured tile. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rotate `src/lib/canon/seasons-stat-label.ts` so the fully-drained branch produces `seasons in canon` instead of `seasons ranked`** for per-show surfaces. The helper currently produces `seasons ranked` for fully-drained shows; rotate to `seasons in canon` so /shows/survivor (and every other per-show hero that consumes the helper) carries the per-show editorial scope. Single literal edit at the source-of-record helper; downstream surfaces flow automatically. Update the helper's source-of-record comment + `apps/e2e/tests/show-home.spec.ts:50` assertion + any colocated tests on the helper. The /shows catalog hero (catalog-aggregate slot) is unchanged and keeps `298 SEASONS RANKED` — it's editorially the catalog-scope surface. (b) **alternative — rotate per-show surfaces by hardcoded literal**, leave the helper alone. Rejected — the helper exists precisely to be the source of record; bypassing it leaves drift latent. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; the pass-44 #379 closure proved the rotation discipline applies here too; one more surface owes the rotation. Recommended (a) — single literal edit at the helper, three-surface alignment lands (home featured + /shows/survivor + every other per-show hero). Pin: extend the colocated test on `src/lib/canon/__tests__/seasons-stat-label.test.ts` (verify path at fix-time) with bidirectional cases — positive: fully-drained → `seasons in canon`; negative: fully-drained does NOT produce `seasons ranked` (drift guard). Sibling positive on `src/components/show/__tests__/ShowHero.test.tsx` (or equivalent show-page hero test) asserting Survivor renders `SEASONS IN CANON`. Migrate `apps/e2e/tests/show-home.spec.ts:50` assertion in lockstep with a matching negative pin. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome label edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the label frames the count's scope).
