- [x] [MED] [authed] Auth-menu disclosure pattern split across breakpoints: mobile chrome (≤720px) wraps the `@e2e` handle behind a chevron menu trigger (pass-44 #378 closure at dbd84bf) but desktop chrome (>720px) renders `@e2e` (handle link) + `Sign out` (terminal action) as two flat sibling actions in the header. Same chrome, two different disclosure models. The wrong CTA is foregrounded on the larger surface — `Sign out` is the most prominent member-facing action on the signed-in home for desktop readers, with no `My profile` / `My votes` / `Account` peer to balance it. The pass-44 #378 closure already shipped the mobile chevron + disclosure menu component to gather member actions behind a single trigger (closes #378 explicitly cites the chevron as the progressive-disclosure pattern for the auth menu); the desktop breakpoint never adopted it. A signed-in member who hops desktop → mobile (or vice-versa) meets two different account-menu shapes; a desktop signed-in member's chrome is a `Sign out` button-bar by default. Same defect class as the resolved pass-39 #346 cross-breakpoint chrome drift (`STABLE LIST` casing) and the pass-44 #378 mobile-only fix — both at the chrome-parity layer; this row carries the parity to the desktop breakpoint the #378 closure didn't reach. Concrete observed shape on the rendered page (verified across both desktop + mobile authed captures at fed0402): desktop chrome → `tiered.tv / Shows / Lists / About / ⌕ Search⌘K / @e2e / Sign out` (two flat actions); mobile chrome → `tiered.tv / Shows / Lists / About / ⌕ Search / @e2e` with `Sign out` tucked behind the chevron menu. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer collapses the menu the same way at both viewports rather than foreground a logout button on the larger one. Fix options: (a) **primary path — mirror the mobile disclosure pattern on desktop** so the pass-44 #378 chevron component drives both breakpoints. The chevron menu collapses `@e2e` (handle link) + `Sign out` (terminal action) behind one trigger; future additions (`My profile`, `My votes`, settings) slot into the same menu without re-architecting the header. Single CSS media-query change + likely one component restructure removing the desktop-only flat-render branch (the chevron component already renders on mobile — extend its breakpoint to all viewports). Verify path at fix-time: likely `src/components/chrome/HeaderAuthMenu.tsx` or `AccountMenuTrigger.tsx`. (b) **alternative — leave desktop flat, fold the chevron's mobile content into a desktop flat-shape too** (so mobile gets a flat row matching desktop). Rejected — the pass-44 #378 closure deliberately chose disclosure as the progressive-reveal pattern; reverting mobile to flat undoes that work and the chevron is the right pattern for a growing menu. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; the pass-44 #378 closure proved the disclosure pattern is the right shape; one more breakpoint owes the rotation. Recommended (a) — single CSS / component edit, the chevron menu carries both breakpoints. Pin: extend the colocated test at `src/components/chrome/__tests__/HeaderAuthMenu.test.tsx` (verify path at fix-time) with bidirectional cases — positive: the chevron trigger renders at all viewports (mock the matchMedia / viewport at desktop and assert the trigger is present); negative: at desktop viewport, the `Sign out` action is NOT a flat sibling of `@e2e` in the header DOM (asserts the desktop-flat-render path is gone). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome disclosure pattern edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the auth menu carries account actions, not editorial content). (URL: /, source: critique-pass-45) — fed0402 — issue: #384 — RESOLVED f2f53dd: took the row's recommended primary path (a). `src/components/chrome/HeaderView.tsx` no longer renders the flat desktop `@handle` link + `Sign out` link — the chevron trigger button is now the only authed-handle surface, and the disclosed menu carries `Your record` + `Sign out` for every viewport. CSS rules for `.site-header-user-trigger` + `.site-header-user-menu` + the `::after` chevron pseudo-element moved out of `@media (max-width: 720px)` into default scope in `src/styles/chrome.css`; the mobile media block keeps only non-account chrome rules (header padding/min-height/flex-wrap, nav-row reflow). The trigger button gains a `data-profile-href` attribute so e2e specs that previously discovered the profile URL via the gone `site-header-user-link.href` keep working without opening the menu first. Tests: `HeaderView.test.tsx` replaces the pass-23 desktop-pair assertions with the all-viewports chevron contract (handle on the trigger, menu-signout has the absolute returnTo, signed-out branch absent on the trigger, signed-in branch absent on the signin link) and rewrites the chrome.css source-pin to assert the chevron `::after` rule appears exactly once AND sits OUTSIDE every `@media (max-width: 720px)` block (bidirectional guard against either a regression that re-traps it in the mobile block, or a duplicate that doubles the cue). E2E `authed-example.spec.ts` desktop walk now opens the trigger and asserts the menu's `Sign out` href; the 375px walk is preserved as a cross-breakpoint parity pin. `user-profile.spec.ts` switches four `site-header-user-link.href` reads to `site-header-user-trigger.data-profile-href`. `comment-read.spec.ts` swaps its signed-in header probe to the trigger. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome composition only — no per-show/per-season/per-user surface change). Closes #384.

- [x] [MED] [anon] /about narrator drift: the editor function is narrated in plural-we (`For every show we cover`, `Editor's Canon — our editorial ranking`, `We err on the side of redacting`) and the same surface is internally inconsistent two paragraphs later — `What this is / An experiment. Built and operated by one person.` Plural masthead voice + singular operator voice on one page. Same surface drifts from the singular-I editor narrator now established across the rest of the editorial chrome — /themes hero post-pass-44 #341 closure reads `12 lists I'd defend in a group chat` + `revised when I change my mind`; /shows/survivor canon-methodology (`01 · WHO`, `02 · HOW`, `03 · WHEN`) reads `I've ranked every single one` / `How do I weigh it?` / `When do I revisit?`. On the natural home → footer → /about click path a reader meets the answer to "who is behind this?" twice in one viewport — once as `our editorial ranking` (plural masthead), once as `one person` (singular operator). Distinct from the resolved pass-44 #341 /themes hero plural→singular closure — that closure rotated /themes hero only and explicitly left /about (and other surfaces) out of scope; this row carries the same rotation discipline to /about. Same defect class as #341 — narrator voice plural→singular drain — at the surface the #341 closure didn't reach. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer's `who-is-this` page carries one narrator. Concrete observed shape on the rendered page (verified across both desktop + mobile anon captures at fed0402): `For every show we cover, two rankings live side by side: Editor's Canon — our editorial ranking, with a short reason per position.` then `Every comment passes through an AI pre-filter before it posts; flagged comments enter a human-reviewed mod queue. We err on the side of redacting.` then `What this is / An experiment. Built and operated by one person.` Fix options: (a) **primary path — rotate the plural-we instances on /about to singular-I** so the surface matches the singular-I editor narrator established on /themes hero + /shows/[show] canon-methodology. Suggested rewrites on `content/legal/about.md` (verify path at fix-time): `For every show we cover` → `For every show I cover`; `Editor's Canon — our editorial ranking` → `Editor's Canon — the editorial ranking` (or `my editorial ranking`); `We err on the side of redacting` → `I err on the side of redacting`. Drops the plural masthead persona that the `one person` closer already contradicts; brings /about into voice-alignment with the editorial surfaces a first-time reader walks before footer-clicking About. (b) **alternative — rotate /about to plural-we throughout AND rotate the singular `one person` closer to a plural framing** (`built and operated by a small team`, etc.). Rejected — the `one person` framing is editorially load-bearing (the same beat appears in the home footer copyright `est. as a quiet rebellion against ranked lists that ruin the show` and elsewhere); the singular-I narrator is the catalogue's established voice post-pass-44 #341. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; same defect class as the resolved pass-44 #341; the catalog's narrator should be one narrator. Recommended (a) — single content edit on `content/legal/about.md`, brings /about into the singular-I voice the catalog established. Sibling positive on the rendered /about test (verify path at fix-time — likely `src/app/about/__tests__/page.test.tsx`) asserting the rendered body contains `I cover` (positive on the rotated narrator). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial voice rewrite of a legal-doc page; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure — the legal-doc copy frames the editor function, not any specific show or season). - [x] [MED] [authed] /themes/best-finales action row carries three peers whose verb-objects target two different audiences: `SAVE (THIS DEVICE) / SHARE / SUGGEST AN ENTRY`. `SAVE` and `SHARE` act on the list-as-object (the reader's relationship to it — save it for later, share the URL); `SUGGEST AN ENTRY` is a meta-action that flows backward to the editor (the reader signals an editorial change). A signed-in member reading the row scans verb / verb / verb but the third verb's object is the editor, not the list. The pass-44 #377 closure tightened `SAVE` → `SAVE (THIS DEVICE)` to clarify device-scope (the reader's relationship to the save state) but didn't address the third action's category drift. Concrete observed shape on the rendered page (verified at fed0402 across desktop + mobile authed captures): the three-button row sits directly under the meta-strip with identical button-bar typography for all three actions, no visual category separator between `SHARE` and `SUGGEST AN ENTRY`. Same defect class as the resolved pass-19 /themes featured-tile affordance grammar split (closed by /themes reading-order alignment, pass-38 close) — both at the action-row scope-uniformity layer; this row carries the discipline to the list-detail page's action row. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer's action row carries one verb-object class. Fix options: (a) **primary path — move `SUGGEST AN ENTRY` out of the primary action row** into a secondary editorial-footer slot (alongside the curator byline at the page footer, or as a verb-arrow CTA next to the `MORE LISTS IN THIS VEIN` rail). The primary action row keeps `SAVE (THIS DEVICE)` + `SHARE` (both reader-scope). The editorial-feedback action moves to its own slot framed as participation (`SUGGEST AN ENTRY →`) rather than as a button-bar peer. Verify path at fix-time: likely `src/components/lists/ListDetailActions.tsx` or `ListDetailHero.tsx`. (b) **alternative — rephrase `SUGGEST AN ENTRY` to a reader-scope frame** so the verb-object stays reader-scoped (`SUBMIT A NOMINATION` is no better; `WHAT'S MISSING? →` reads more like a reader-action). Acceptable — single literal edit; smaller blast radius than (a). Worth doing alongside (a) if the editorial-footer slot is non-trivial to add. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; the verb-object scope drift is the defect class. Recommended (a) — component restructure separating reader-scope action row from editorial-feedback slot. Pin: extend the colocated test on `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListDetailActions.test.tsx` (verify path at fix-time) with two cases — (1) the primary action row contains exactly `SAVE` + `SHARE` (no `SUGGEST` in the row); (2) the `SUGGEST AN ENTRY` action exists on the page but is rendered outside the action row (`closest([data-testid=list-detail-actions]) === null` from the suggest-an-entry button's perspective). Bidirectional drift guard. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome action-row edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface). (URL: /themes/best-finales, source: critique-pass-45) — fed0402 — issue: #385 — RESOLVED this commit: took the row's recommended primary path (a). New component `src/components/lists/SuggestEntryCTA.tsx` (data-testid `list-suggest-cta` wrapping the existing `list-suggest` mailto link) renders a participation-framed `Suggest an entry →` verb-arrow CTA. The link itself keeps its `data-testid=list-suggest`, aria-label (`Suggest an entry for {themeTitle}`), and the same `mailto:editors@tiered.tv?subject=Suggest entry: {themeTitle}` href that previously lived in `ListDetailTools` — the e2e page-reads visibility selector continues to pass without modification. `src/components/lists/ListDetailTools.tsx` drops the `<a>` block + the unused `mailto` const; the primary action row now carries only the reader-scope `Save (this device)` + `Share` + the spoiler shield. The page route at `src/app/(default)/themes/[theme]/page.tsx` imports the new component and renders `<SuggestEntryCTA themeTitle={theme.title} />` directly below `<AdjacentLists>`, putting participation in an editorial-footer slot adjacent to the `MORE LISTS IN THIS VEIN` rail (the recommended slot named in the row's primary path). New CSS rule `.list-suggest-cta` in `src/styles/lists.css` styles the slot — centered, monospace eyebrow-typography that mirrors `.adj-tag` so the CTA reads as a sibling of the adjacent-lists rail rather than a button-bar peer of Save/Share. Pinned per the row's bidirectional drift-guard spec: (1) `ListDetailTools.test.tsx` gained `primary action row contains only reader-scope Save + Share — no Suggest in the row (pass-45 #384)` — positive on `list-save` + `list-share`, negative on `list-suggest` inside `list-tools` and on the literal `/suggest an entry/i` anywhere in the component's render; (2) new `src/components/lists/__tests__/SuggestEntryCTA.test.tsx` carries three cases — renders the CTA with verb-arrow glyph + aria-label, mailto href with encoded theme-title subject (replaces the previous tools-host test, same assertions migrated to the new component), and the bidirectional pin asserting the link's `closest('[data-testid=list-suggest-cta]')` is non-null AND `closest('[data-testid=list-tools]')` is null (so a future regression cannot satisfy both halves without re-introducing the defect). `ListDetailHero.test.tsx` flipped its "shows tools row" case to assert `list-suggest` is NOT in the hero anymore — positive on Save/Share/shield, negative on the Suggest testid. `apps/e2e/src/fixtures/page-reads.ts` adds the `list-suggest-cta` wrapper selector alongside the existing `list-suggest` selector so the e2e walker pins both the slot AND the link — a regression that drops the slot OR puts Suggest back inside the tools row trips this fixture. `apps/e2e/tests/a11y.spec.ts` desktop + mobile comments updated in lockstep (Save/Share/Suggest → Save/Share + the post pass-45 #384 editorial-footer CTA). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome action-row split + new CTA wrapper only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the action row carries the same Save/Share interactions and the Suggest mailto opens the same editor inbox). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 193 unit files / 2632 tests (+1 file / +3 cases) + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1362 e2e (7.9m).

- [x] [MED] [authed] /u/[handle] meta description is written in second-person addressed to the profile owner: `Your record on tiered.tv. Cast one vote to start writing it.` The page is publicly indexable (canonical = `https://tiered.tv/u/e2e`, no `noindex` directive verified at fed0402) so any visitor's social-card preview, search-result snippet, or link unfurl will read the description as if the page is addressing them when it's actually about user `e2e`. A profile page's meta-description should describe the profile from a reader's perspective, not coach the owner. Concrete observed shape on the rendered page (verified at fed0402 across both desktop + mobile authed captures): `<meta name="description" content="Your record on tiered.tv. Cast one vote to start writing it.">` with canonical `https://tiered.tv/u/e2e`. The page `<title>` is correctly third-person: `@e2e — tiered.tv`. Only the description regresses to second-person. Same defect class as the resolved pass-39 #345 cross-audience meta-description voice drift (page titles correct, meta descriptions inconsistent) — at the /u/[handle] surface the #345 closure didn't reach. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer's profile-page meta-description introduces the profile to anyone unfurling the link, not to the owner who clicked through. The signed-in-owner empty-state CTA copy `New here. Cast one vote and your record starts writing itself.` is the place the second-person voice belongs — in the rendered body, not in `<meta>`. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rewrite the meta description to third-person, profile-about voice** so social-card / search-snippet readers meet a description of the profile rather than a coaching instruction. Suggested rewrite at the meta-description generator (verify path — likely `src/app/u/[handle]/page.tsx` or `src/lib/seo/profile-meta.ts`): `@{handle}'s record on tiered.tv — the seasons they've voted into the community rank.` (or similar — third-person `they`, profile-scope frame). The empty-state CTA copy `Cast one vote and your record starts writing itself.` stays in-page as the owner-only signed-in message, not in `<meta>`. (b) **alternative — render two distinct meta-descriptions branched on owner-vs-visitor view** so the owner sees a coaching frame and visitors see a third-person frame. Rejected — meta-descriptions are page-level metadata, not viewer-conditional; SSG/ISR can't branch on viewer identity at the `<head>` layer, and Next's static metadata API doesn't support per-request meta. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; the second-person voice in a public meta-description misframes the page to its non-owner readers. Recommended (a) — single literal edit at the meta-description generator. Pin: extend the colocated test on the profile page meta-description (verify path — likely `src/app/u/[handle]/__tests__/page.test.tsx` or `profile-meta.test.ts`) with bidirectional cases — positive: rendered `<meta name="description">` matches `/'s record on tiered.tv/` (third-person possessive frame); negative: rendered description does NOT match `/^Your record/` (drift guard against reverting to second-person). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome meta-description edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the meta-description frames the profile page, not any specific season). (URL: /u/e2e, source: critique-pass-45) — fed0402 — issue: #382 — RESOLVED 55151e2: took the row's recommended primary path (a). `src/app/(default)/u/[handle]/page.tsx:88-101` removed the pass-36 #331 isOwner branching + the `auth0.getSession()` call inside `generateMetadata`; the description picker is now a single `profile.populated` ternary returning the existing stranger-branch literals — third-person possessive `${profile.handle}'s public record on tiered.tv — votes, comments, the seasons they've weighed in on.` for populated profiles, and `A reader on tiered.tv. Nothing on the public record yet.` for empty profiles. The comment header was rewritten to record the pass-45 #1139 supersedes of pass-36 #331 with the full reasoning (meta is for crawlers / social-card unfurlers / search snippets — all anonymous fetches; the owner reads `<title>` in the tab, not the description; a self-shared URL is unfurled anonymously by the social platform; the body-level second-person CTA lives in `<ProfileEmpty>`'s rendered chrome). Pinned per the row's prescription: `src/app/(default)/u/[handle]/__tests__/page.test.tsx:221-285` migrated five generateMetadata cases for the rotation — OWNER + empty now asserts `/a reader on tiered\.tv/i` + `/nothing on the public record/i` with negative pin `.not.toMatch(/^your record/i)`; OWNER + populated asserts `/e2e's public record on tiered\.tv/i` + `/they've weighed in on/i` with negative pin `.not.toMatch(/^your public record/i)`; the two STRANGER cases stayed unchanged (already third-person); the previous auth0-down test was rewritten to assert `generateMetadata` never reads the session at all (`expect(getSessionMock).not.toHaveBeenCalled()`) — meta is viewer-agnostic, no auth0 crash surface. Bidirectional drift guards on both OWNER cases — a future refactor re-introducing the owner branch trips the negative `.not.toMatch` lines; a re-converge onto a different third-person frame trips the positive matches. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome meta-description edit only; no per-show verdict / canon position / finale beat exposure on any surface). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 191 unit files / 2625 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1362 e2e (8.3m). Issue #382 auto-closed via the fix commit's `Closes #382` trailer.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains section 04 ("What to watch for") — three of four moment bodies open with the same noun-phrase frame `The [first/opening] [unit-of-time]`: OPENER · COLD OPEN opens `The opening shot — boat, water, twenty people who already know the camera is watching — does in 90 seconds...`; MID · MERGE opens `The first ten minutes after the merge are the cleanest piece of reality-TV staging the show has ever shot.`; LATE · THIRD ACT opens `The first minute of the late-game stretch is doing real work — staging, blocking, who's eating where. Don't blink.` The fourth (EARLY · LONG TAKE) breaks the pattern (`An unbroken three-minute confessional that the show normally would have cut into ribbons. Watch the editor's patience.`). Three of four sibling beats on one section's stacked list share the same opening grammatical shape — the kind of echo a reader picks up scanning the section's four bullet bodies in sequence. Same defect class as the resolved pass-41 MED HvV TAKE+SHAPE opener echo (closed 5c17357 — adjacent sections restating the same opener frame) and the pass-37 #338 watch-for OPENER/EARLY/MID/LATE rotation discipline — at the within-section watch-for body-opener layer. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer breaks the opener frame after using it once or twice, not three of four. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rotate one of the three echoing openers off the `The [first/opening] [unit]` frame**. Recommended target: rewrite LATE · THIRD ACT opener `The first minute of the late-game stretch is doing real work — staging, blocking, who's eating where. Don't blink.` → `Late-game staging earns its keep — blocking, blocking, who's eating where. Don't blink.` (drops the leading `The first [unit]` shape, keeps the editorial beat about staging). Content edit on `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-vs-villains.md` (verify field — likely `watch_for[3].body` or equivalent). Single-entry edit drops the echo from three-of-four to two-of-four. (b) **alternative — rotate both MID and LATE off the frame**, leaving only OPENER on the `The opening shot...` opener (the OPENER section earns its opener literal because the section name IS `OPENER · COLD OPEN`). More thorough; small additional edit. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; three-of-four sibling-body opener-echo is the defect. Recommended (a) — single content edit on LATE body, brings the echo from 3/4 to 2/4. Pin: extend the existing colocated test on the watch-for section (verify path — likely `src/content/__tests__/hvv-watch-for.test.ts` or `season-shape.test.ts`) with a positive case asserting LATE body opens on a non-`The [first/opening]` frame; negative case asserting LATE body does NOT open with `The first` (drift guard). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial rewrite of a watch-for body; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure — the rewrite shifts the opener clause to a staging-beat frame without naming any moment's resolution). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-45) — fed0402 — issue: #383 — RESOLVED this commit: took the row's recommended primary path (a). `content/shows/survivor/seasons/20-heroes-vs-villains.md:33` LATE · THIRD ACT body rotated `The first minute of the late-game stretch is doing real work — staging, blocking, who's eating where. Don't blink.` → `Late-game scenes do real work — staging, blocking, who's eating where. Don't blink.` — drops the leading `The first minute of the late-game stretch is` (the `The [first|opening] [unit-of-time]` opener frame the row called out), preserves the staging-beat noun list (`staging, blocking, who's eating where`) and the `Don't blink.` close. The row's literal candidate (`Late-game staging earns its keep — blocking, blocking, who's eating where.`) was rejected at fix-time because it duplicates the word `blocking` (a `blocking, blocking` typo in the row's draft); the chosen rotation keeps the row's `Late-game` lead-noun + non-`The first` frame intent while preserving the original noun list integrity (`staging, blocking, who's eating where`). Brings the section's opener-frame echo from three-of-four to two-of-four (OPENER + MID still earn the `The [opening|first] [unit]` frame — OPENER because the section name IS `OPENER · COLD OPEN`; MID because the post-merge minute count is a structural fact the body anchors to). New colocated `src/content/__tests__/hvv-watch-list-opener-echo.test.ts` (sibling of `hvv-take-shape-opener-echo.test.ts`, mirrors its bidirectional discipline) carries four cases: (1) extracts all four `watch_list` bodies from the live content file (sanity); (2) positive — LATE body opens on `^Late-game scenes do real work`; (3) bidirectional negative — LATE body does NOT open with `^The (first|opening)\b` (drift guard against a future authoring pass reverting to the rejected frame); (4) at-most-two — at most two of the four watch_list bodies share the `^The (first|opening)\b` opener frame (broader drift guard — catches a future fourth body drifting into the frame even if LATE stays clean). No URL / schema field / e2e fixture row owed — `grep -rn "The first minute\|late-game stretch\|Don't blink\|first ten minutes after the merge" apps/e2e/ src/` returned only synthetic test fixtures in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` (unrelated invariant fixtures, not pinning this literal). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial rewrite of a watch-list body; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure — the rewrite shifts the opener clause to a staging-beat frame without naming any moment's resolution). - [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/survivor canon HvV slot copy carries a dangling-possessive typo at `content/shows/survivor/canon.md:64-65`: the slot-2 body ends `Episodes run dense in a way the merge alone cannot deliver. The second slot is its by default. No other all-star attempt sustains this much pressure across this many recognizable faces, and decades on, the rest of the format is still measured against this stretch of TV.` — the sentence `The second slot is its by default.` carries a possessive `its` with no antecedent noun; the implied antecedent (`Heroes vs. Villains`) is the paragraph subject several sentences back, leaving the possessive dangling. A reader scanning the canon body stops mid-paragraph to re-parse the sentence. This sits inside the canonical second-most-prominent canon slot on the flagship show page — the same /shows/survivor canon surface a first-time reader hits via home → /shows/survivor click path; verified rendered shape at 45457ae across both anon + authed walks (the body literal renders identically because the slot body is SSG-shared across all viewers). Same defect class as the resolved pass-32 #313 HvV body within-entry phrase-repetition family and the pass-19 #244 canon-entry voice repairs — at the within-sentence grammar layer the prior closures didn't reach. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer's flagship canon doesn't ship a dangling possessive in the second-rank slot. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rewrite the sentence so the possessive resolves to an explicit antecedent**. Suggested: `The second slot belongs to it by default.` (drops the dangling possessive entirely; `it` carries the antecedent Heroes vs. Villains established in the paragraph's opening sentences). Alternative literals: `The second slot is its own by default.` (adds the missing noun the possessive needs) or `The slot is its by default.` (rejected — same dangling shape, just shorter). Single-line content edit on `content/shows/survivor/canon.md:65`. (b) **alternative — rewrite the whole sentence** to a different grammatical shape: `No other all-star season earns the second slot.` — preserves the editorial point (HvV owns the slot uncontested) but recasts the agent. Acceptable, larger blast radius. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; the dangling-possessive read-stop is the defect. Recommended (a) with the `belongs to it by default` literal — single-line content edit, drops the dangling possessive, preserves the slot-owns-the-rank editorial point. Pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` with a new `collectDanglingPossessiveIssues` invariant scanning every show's `canon.md` body fields for the regex `/\bis its\b(?!\s+(?:own|by\s+default\s+\w))/i` (catches the literal pattern `is its` not followed by `own` or `by default <noun>`); initial floor lax, strict at floor 0 once the live corpus drains. Sibling colocated test case in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` against synthetic fixtures (the bare-possessive form flags, the `is its own` form passes, the `belongs to it by default` rewrite passes). Bidirectional drift guard. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (single-sentence grammar edit on a canon body; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure — the sentence is about slot ownership, not season outcome). (URL: /shows/survivor, source: critique-pass-46) — 45457ae — issue: #389 — RESOLVED a2b9903: took the row's recommended primary path (a). `content/shows/survivor/canon.md:65` rotated `The second slot is its by default.` → `The second slot belongs to it by default.` — drops the dangling possessive entirely; `it` carries the antecedent Heroes vs. Villains established in the paragraph's opening sentences. Word count stays within the 80–120 rationale window. Pinned with a new `collectCanonRationaleDanglingPossessiveIssues` invariant in `scripts/content-check.ts` scanning every canon entry's `rationale` + `tag` + `slot_argument` for the literal pattern `\bis its\b` not followed by `own` (the grammatical `is its own X` form passes). Ships strict at floor 0 — the rewrite drains the only offender in the corpus. Simplified the suggested regex from the row's `/\bis its\b(?!\s+(?:own|by\s+default\s+\w))/i` to `/\bis its\b(?!\s+own\b)/i` — the `by default <noun>` carve-out is unnecessary since the recommended rewrite drops `is its` entirely and the only grammatical carve-out needed is `is its own`. Colocated test cases in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts` inside a new describe block: live-catalog pass; rationale-bare-form flag (pinning file path + literal + issue #389); `is its own` passes; `belongs to it by default` rewrite passes; case-insensitive match; sibling `tag` flag; sibling `slot_argument` flag; no-canon no-op. Bidirectional drift guard. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (single-sentence grammar edit on a canon body; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure). Issue #389 auto-closed via the fix commit's `Closes #389` trailer.

Fix options: (a) **primary path — rotate the plural-we lead to singular-I across all 5 shows' `tier_s_blurb` field**. Suggested rewrite: `Format-defining or unrepeatable. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; the rest of the catalogue settled on singular-I post-#306 + #341 + #381; the tier-blurb pocket should follow. - [x] [MED] [anon] /about Become-an-editor section breaks the page's first-person-singular narrator with plural-voice `tell us`. Verified rendered shape on pass-46 anon walker at 45457ae: /about reads in singular-I throughout (`For every show I cover, two rankings live side by side` / `I err on the side of redacting` / `Built and operated by one person`) — the result of the resolved pass-45 #381 plural→singular drain — but the Become-an-editor section (anchor `#editors`) at `content/legal/about.md:67-78` reads `80–120 words per ranked season, spoiler-safe — open an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/daretodave/tiered) and tell us which show and which season.` (line 72-73). The page's narrator is one editor (`one person`), so the plural `tell us` either refers to a fictional team (contradicting the `one person` closer two paragraphs below) or to the editor + reader as `us` (awkward for an editorial invitation — the editor is the addressee, not part of the `us`). Same defect class as the resolved pass-45 #381 narrator drift at /about — the #381 closure rotated the masthead `we cover` / `our editorial ranking` / `we err on the side` lines to singular but didn't reach the Become-an-editor body (verify: `git show 5e82a3f -- content/legal/about.md` confirms #381's edits stop at the masthead paragraph; the Become-an-editor `tell us` survived the rotation). A reader who hops home → /about reads `For every show I cover` then `Built and operated by one person` then `tell us which show and which season` — the same page, two narrators one viewport apart. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer's contact-the-editor copy carries one editor. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rotate `tell us` to `tell me`** at `content/legal/about.md:73`. The full sentence becomes `…open an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/daretodave/tiered) and tell me which show and which season.` Matches the singular-I narrator established by the rest of the page post-#381 and post-#306. Single-line content edit. (b) **alternative — recast the sentence in neutral imperative**: `…open an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/daretodave/tiered) naming the show and the season.` (drops the editor as addressee; both editor and reader read as third parties). Acceptable but loses the personal invitation tone the page otherwise carries. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; same defect class as the resolved pass-45 #381 narrator drift; the Become-an-editor copy is the next pocket owing the rotation. Recommended (a) — single-line edit, brings the section into voice-alignment with the rest of /about. Pin: extend the colocated `editorial narrator voice on /about` describe block at `src/app/(default)/about/__tests__/page.test.tsx` (landed at #381 closure 5e82a3f) with one bidirectional pair — positive on `/tell me which show and which season/i`; negative on `/tell us which show/i` (drift guard against a future authoring pass reverting). Sibling positive in the existing pass-45 #381 cases continues to pin the masthead literals. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (legal-doc voice edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the editorial invitation prose frames the editor function, not any specific show). Pinned per the pass-45 #381 closure pattern: extended the colocated `editorial narrator voice on /about (critique pass-45, issue #381)` describe block at `src/app/(default)/about/__tests__/page.test.tsx` with one bidirectional case — positive on `/tell\s+me\s+which\s+show\s+and\s+which\s+season/`; negative on `/tell\s+us\s+which\s+show/`. - [x] [LOW] [authed] /themes featured-tile + index-card render the same long body tagline verbatim within a single page scroll. The Featured-this-month rail at `src/components/lists/FeaturedCard.tsx:61` renders `theme.description` inside `.feat-blurb` for each of the 3 featured tiles; the all-lists index further down the page at `src/components/lists/ListRow.tsx:40` renders the same `theme.description` inside `.list-row-blurb` for each list — including the same 3 featured tiles that appear again in their categorized sub-bands (BY CRAFT / BY TONE / BY STRUCTURE). Verified rendered shape on pass-46 authed walker capture at 45457ae: `Finales that stuck the landing` body `Closing runs that pay off the season they spent a dozen episodes building. The stakes feel earned, the last hour sits at the right altitude, and nothing gets handed over for free.` appears twice on /themes — once under the Featured rail tile, once under the BY CRAFT index sub-band tile, with identical text. Same pattern repeats for `Comebacks worth the swing` (Featured + BY TONE) and `Premieres that earned it` (Featured + BY CRAFT). A reader scanning /themes top-to-bottom reads the same 35-word paragraph twice per featured list within one viewport — three duplicate paragraphs total on a single page. Same defect class as the resolved pass-38 #340 mispositioned-label finding (`SHOWING · ALL 9 IN THE INDEX` over the Featured rail) and the resolved pass-22 #261 split-reveal closure — both at the Featured-vs-Index relationship layer; this row carries the discipline to the body-tagline repetition that the prior closures didn't address. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer doesn't repeat the same paragraph twice within one viewport. Fix options: (a) **primary path — render featured tiles with a shorter pull-quote so the full tagline appears only once per scroll**. `FeaturedCard.tsx:61` swaps the `.feat-blurb` from `theme.description` to a new optional frontmatter field `theme.featured_pull` (15-20 word single-sentence form, content-curator authors at promotion time) with a fallback to the first sentence of `theme.description` when `featured_pull` is absent. Single literal edit at the source-of-record component + new optional content field across 3 currently-featured theme files. The index card continues to render the full `theme.description` since it's the canonical home for the long tagline. (b) **alternative — dedupe by suppressing featured-tier entries from the index sub-bands** so each list appears in exactly one section per page. Smaller blast radius (single filter at the page-level `ListsAllSection` aggregator dropping `category: featured` entries) but loses the index's "every list is accounted for in its category" guarantee — a reader scanning BY CRAFT for a finales-themed list would now miss `Finales that stuck the landing` because it's promoted to Featured this month. Rejected — the index's category coverage is editorially load-bearing. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; the single-scroll paragraph echo is the defect. Recommended (a) — new optional `featured_pull` frontmatter field + first-sentence fallback at the FeaturedCard render path, preserves the index as the canonical long-form home + introduces a featured-rail-specific shorter form. Pin: extend `src/components/lists/__tests__/FeaturedCard.test.tsx` with a case asserting (1) when `featured_pull` is present, the `.feat-blurb` renders `featured_pull` and does NOT render the full `theme.description`; (2) when `featured_pull` is absent, the `.feat-blurb` renders only the first sentence of `theme.description` and does NOT render the full body. Sibling regression case in `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListRow.test.tsx` asserts the index card continues to render the full `theme.description` regardless of the `featured_pull` field. New schema case in `src/content/__tests__/schemas.test.ts` adds the optional `featured_pull` field to the theme schema with a `nonempty string when present` Zod constraint. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome composition edit only + optional content field; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the body tagline frames the list's editorial premise, not any specific entry's outcome). (URL: /themes, source: critique-pass-46) — 45457ae — issue: #397 — RESOLVED ef4998a: took the row's recommended primary path (a). `src/content/schemas.ts` themeFrontmatterObject gained the optional `featured_pull: z.string().min(1).max(180).optional()` field with an inline comment naming the pass-46 #397 rationale + the fallback contract. `src/lib/themes-format.ts` added two new helpers — `firstSentence(text)` (sentence boundary regex `/^([^.!?]*[.!?])(?:\s|$)/` trimming whitespace and preserving trailing punctuation) and `featuredPullText(theme)` (`theme.featured_pull ?? firstSentence(theme.description)`), the single source of truth so any future surface using a featured-rail tile inherits the same discipline. `src/components/lists/FeaturedCard.tsx:73` flipped from `<p className="feat-blurb">{theme.description}</p>` to `<p className="feat-blurb">{featuredPullText(theme)}</p>` with the inline rationale comment cross-referencing the index-card canonical-long-form contract. Pinned per the row's spec: (i) `FeaturedCard.test.tsx` new `blurb pull (critique pass-46 #397)` describe block with two bidirectional cases — when `featured_pull` is present the `.feat-blurb` renders it and the full description's second-sentence anchor (`the last hour sits at the right altitude`) is absent from the rendered card; when `featured_pull` is absent the `.feat-blurb` renders only the first sentence and the second-sentence anchor (`The stakes feel earned`) is absent. (ii) `ListRow.test.tsx` sibling pin asserts the full `theme.description` continues to render in the index regardless of `featured_pull` — drift guard against a future refactor flipping the index to also render the pull. (iii) `schemas.test.ts` new `featured_pull (pass-46 #397)` describe block with four cases — accepts a short pull string, treats absence as `undefined` (optionality), rejects empty string (`min(1)`), rejects over-180-char string (`max(180)`). (iv) `themes-format.test.ts` gained `firstSentence (pass-46 #397)` describe block (5 cases — two-sentence boundary, single-sentence fall-through, `?`/`!` terminators, whitespace trim, no-terminator pass-through) and `featuredPullText (pass-46 #397)` describe block (3 cases — featured_pull present, fallback path, bidirectional negative pin). No content authoring required this tick — the fallback path covers every currently-featured theme out of the box (all four `featured: true` themes have a two-sentence description whose first sentence reads as a clean rail-tile pull); `content-curator` may author dedicated `featured_pull` literals at future promotion time. No URL / schema-shape break (the field is purely additive). No e2e fixture row owed — `grep -rn "feat-blurb\|stakes feel earned" apps/e2e/` confirmed no e2e pinned the prior body literal; the existing themes.spec.ts tests on the featured rail (count cap, slug overlap with the grid) keep passing unchanged. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome composition + optional content field only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface). Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + 193 unit files / 2684 tests + 127 script tests + content:check (13 shows / 298 seasons / 13 canons / 12 themes / 3 legal docs); leg2 build (374 pages); leg3 1362 e2e (8.1m). Closes #397.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] /themes index sub-band eyebrow `ORGANIZED BY WHAT THEY'RE ADMIRING` personifies the lists as the agent doing the admiring. Verified rendered shape on pass-46 anon walker capture at 45457ae: /themes index reads `SHOWING · ALL 12 IN THE INDEX / All lists. / ORGANIZED BY WHAT THEY'RE ADMIRING / BY TONE · 3 / [tiles] / BY STRUCTURE · 4 / [tiles] / BY CRAFT · 4 / [tiles] / SINGLE-SHOW · 1 / [tile]`. Source literal at `src/components/lists/ListsAllSection.tsx:39` `Organized by what they're admiring` (CSS uppercase via `.lists-section-eyebrow`). The phrasing makes `they` (the lists) the agent doing the admiring — but lists don't admire; the editor admires through the lists. Reads as ad-copy personification rather than the bearings voice's `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`. A reader scanning the page reads `BY TONE · 3` / `BY STRUCTURE · 4` / `BY CRAFT · 4` as four organizing dimensions of the editor's lens, not four things the lists themselves are doing. Same defect class as the resolved pass-19 #244 lede voice repairs and the pass-44 #341 plural→singular drain at the eyebrow layer — at the lists-personification sub-band the prior closures didn't address. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer's catalogue eyebrow names the editorial lens, not the lists' interior life. Fix options: (a) **primary path — reframe the eyebrow editor-first**. Suggested literal at `src/components/lists/ListsAllSection.tsx:39`: `Organized by what they look at` (drops the personification — `look at` is a neutral structural verb; the lists are sorted by their subject-matter scope, not by their feelings). Alternative literals: `Grouped by angle` (shorter, drops the editorial frame entirely), `Organized by their lens` (preserves the editorial frame; the lens is the editor's). The sub-band names (BY TONE / BY STRUCTURE / BY CRAFT / SINGLE-SHOW) stay intact — each names what the editor is looking at, not what the lists admire. Single literal edit at the source-of-record component. (b) **alternative — drop the eyebrow** so the BY TONE / BY STRUCTURE / BY CRAFT / SINGLE-SHOW sub-bands stand alone as their own organizing dimensions. Smaller blast radius but loses the meta-explanation that helps a first-time visitor understand the index isn't sorted by show or chronology. (c) **alternative — leave as-is** — rejected; the personification reads as ad-copy voice on a brand-central editorial surface. Recommended (a) with the `Organized by what they look at` literal — single literal edit, drops the personification, preserves the editorial-lens frame. Pin: extend the colocated test at `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListsAllSection.test.tsx` (verify path at fix-time) with bidirectional cases — positive: rendered eyebrow matches `/organized by what they look at/i`; negative: rendered eyebrow does NOT match `/admiring/i` (drift guard against a future authoring pass reverting). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome eyebrow edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the eyebrow frames the index sub-band organization, not any list's content). Drops the personification — `look at` is a neutral structural verb; the lists are sorted by their subject-matter scope, not by their feelings. The sub-band names (BY TONE / BY STRUCTURE / BY CRAFT / SINGLE-SHOW) stay intact — each names what the editor is looking at. Pinned per the row's spec at `src/components/lists/__tests__/ListsAllSection.test.tsx:137-160` by extending the existing pass-24 #275 apostrophe-discipline case in lockstep — case header rewritten to `lists-section-meta renders the editor-first subhead (critique pass-46 #391)`, body now carries three pins: (1) positive verbatim `toBe('Organized by what they look at')`; (2) positive regex on `/organized by what they look at/i` (the bidirectional drift-guard the row spec calls for); (3) bidirectional negative on `/admiring/i` (drift guard against a future authoring pass reverting to the personification form); plus the retained pass-24 #275 noise-floor guard on `/’/` (the new literal has no apostrophe, but a future authoring pass that reintroduces one must keep the straight ASCII form). (c) **alternative — leave as-is**. Rejected — the within-scroll first-sentence echo is the defect class pass-46 #397's structural fix targeted; this is its residual. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer's catalog blurb and on-page lede aren't two parallel takes on the same sentence. Fix options: (a) **primary path — align the on-page lede to the catalog `description` as the canonical voice** so the lede *extends* the catalog blurb rather than rewriting it. Candidate shape (lifts the description verbatim as the first sentence of the lede, then adds the editorial reward the page click earns): `Closing runs that pay off the season they spent a dozen episodes building. The stakes feel earned, the last hour sits at the right altitude, and nothing gets handed over for free. The seven entries below all do that; some by tightening the format's existing template, some by changing what 'finale' means.` Single content-file edit at `content/themes/best-finales.md` `intro` field. (b) **alternative — keep the on-page lede as canonical and rewrite the `description` to a contracted form of it** (catalog cards quote a shorter clip of the page lede rather than carrying their own parallel voice). Risk: the `description` field also drives `<meta name="description">` + OG card preview, which would change SERP voice — larger blast radius. (c) **alternative — leave as-is**. Rejected — three parallel rewrites of the same list in two clicks is the defect class. Recommended (a) — single content edit, drops the parallel-rewrite axis, anchors the click reward to extending (not restating) the catalog voice. Pin: extend `scripts/content-check.ts` with a `collectThemeIntroDescriptionPrefixAlignmentIssues` invariant — for each theme, assert the `intro` body field's first sentence (per `firstSentence(theme.intro)`) tokenizes to a 5-or-more-word verbatim prefix overlap with `firstSentence(theme.description)` (the catalog-card sentence). Initial floor: lax (warn on parallel-rewrite shapes); strict at floor 0 once the catalog drains. Sibling colocated test in `src/content/__tests__/content-check.test.ts`. Same shape as the existing prefix-alignment helpers. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (editorial intro rewrite only; the rewrite lifts the description's existing language and extends it with a structural framing of *how* the seven entries do that — no per-season verdict change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure). (URL: /themes/best-finales, source: critique-pass-48) — f303ca3

- RESOLVED 4853e10: took the row's primary path (a). `best-finales` tagline updated so its first sentence now opens verbatim with the catalog description ("Closing runs that pay off the season they spent a dozen episodes building. The stakes feel earned, the last hour sits at the right altitude, and nothing gets handed over for free.") and then extends with the editorial reward the page click earns ("<b>The seven entries below all clear that bar</b> — some by tightening the format's existing template, some by changing what 'finale' means."). Also fixed `survivor-pillars.md` tagline which had the same 3-shared-words parallel-rewrite pattern ("Four seasons that hold the show up" vs description "Four seasons that define the show's eras") — updated to open with the description verbatim ("Four seasons that define the show's eras — the original experiment, the tactical era's apex, the post-pandemic reset, and the steady-state new normal.") then extend with the existing second-sentence editorial punchline. Added `collectThemeTaglineDescriptionAlignmentIssues` invariant to `scripts/content-check.ts` (parallel-rewrite zone: 2–4 shared opening words then diverge); ships strict at floor 0 with 4 colocated tests. Verify gate green: leg1 tokens + typecheck + no-raw-img + test-colocation + 2753 unit tests (4 new) + 79 script tests + content:check (22 shows/326 seasons/22 canons/12 themes/3 legal docs ok), leg2 build 438 SSG pages, leg3 1562 e2e. Spoiler discipline P0 intact.

- [x] [MED] [authed] Cross-surface `revised`-date drift on the chrome timestamps a returning member uses to judge what's new — home featured callout reads `CANON REVISED · May 2026`, `/shows` index stat strip reads `SHOWS REVISED · May 2026`, but `/themes` index stat strip reads `LISTS REVISED · June 2026`. Two of the three chromes pin to May; the third advances to June. A returning member scanning the chrome for "what's new since last visit" cannot tell which surface is canonical-fresh. Verified rendered shapes on pass-48 authed walker captures at f303ca3: home `/` `Currently featured` callout strip reads `50 / SEASONS IN CANON / May 2026 / CANON REVISED`; `/shows` hero stat strip reads `13 / SHOWS TRACKED · 298 / SEASONS RANKED · May 2026 / LAST REVISION`; `/themes` hero stat strip reads `12 / THEMED LISTS · 3 / FEATURED THIS MONTH · June 2026 / LISTS REVISED`. Three surfaces, three independently-computed timestamps, two months out of sync. The defect is bidirectional: either home + /shows are stale (the catalog actually revised in June but the older timestamp is hard-coded to the last "human-authored" canon update); or /themes is overstating freshness (its `LISTS REVISED` field counts any frontmatter mtime touch, including a typo fix). Either way the chrome lies about the same fact in two directions. Same defect class as the resolved pass-37 `last revision` cross-surface alignment work (chrome-timestamp discipline at the show-page + index seam, now at the cross-section home + /shows + /themes seam the prior closure didn't reach). Source: home `/` `CANON REVISED` value — likely a derived constant from `content/shows/` mtime aggregate (verify at fix-time); `/shows` `LAST REVISION` value — likely the same aggregate; `/themes` `LISTS REVISED` value — likely a separate `content/themes/` mtime aggregate. The three surfaces use two different timestamp sources without a single source-of-record. Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer's three chromes don't disagree by a month about the same fact. Fix options: (a) **primary path — define a single source-of-record `lastRevised(scope)` helper** at e.g. `src/lib/content-revisions.ts` (verify at fix-time) that the three surfaces consume with explicit scope arguments: `lastRevised('canon')` (canon files), `lastRevised('shows')` (show frontmatter), `lastRevised('themes')` (theme frontmatter). The home `CANON REVISED` consumes `lastRevised('canon')`; `/shows` `LAST REVISION` consumes `lastRevised('shows')`; `/themes` `LISTS REVISED` consumes `lastRevised('themes')`. Each chrome label names exactly what it's measuring (canon vs shows vs themes) and the underlying mtime is computed from a single helper so the months align with the truth. (b) **alternative — collapse all three chromes to a single "site revised" date** sourced from `max(content/**/*)` mtime. Smaller blast radius (one helper, one constant exported to all three surfaces) but loses the per-section editorial signal (a `themes` content drop should advance the /themes chrome without falsely advancing the /shows chrome). (c) **alternative — leave as-is**. Rejected — three independently-computed timestamps disagreeing on the same chrome family is the defect class. Recommended (a) — single helper with explicit per-scope arguments, preserves the per-section editorial signal, drops the cross-surface drift. Pin: add a sibling colocated test on the new helper (`src/lib/__tests__/content-revisions.test.ts`) asserting (1) `lastRevised('canon')` returns the max mtime across `content/shows/*/canon.md`; (2) `lastRevised('shows')` returns the max mtime across `content/shows/*.md`; (3) `lastRevised('themes')` returns the max mtime across `content/themes/*.md`. Sibling positive: add a `scripts/content-check.ts` invariant `collectCrossSurfaceRevisedDateConsistencyIssues` — for each currently-rendered chrome timestamp (verify the rendered DOM at fix-time), assert the timestamp matches the corresponding `lastRevised(scope)` value. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome timestamp source-of-record refactor only; no per-show verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the timestamps name *when* the content last changed, not *what* changed). (URL: /, /shows, /themes, source: critique-pass-48) — f303ca3
  - STALE — verified 2026-07-05 (iterate): the ambiguous-label half of this finding was already fixed before it was even filed. Commit `0c06a69` ("voice: rename /shows + /themes hero freshness slot from `INDEX REVISED` to per-corpus `SHOWS REVISED` / `LISTS REVISED`") predates the filing commit `f303ca3` in history (`0c06a69` is the older commit). Current live labels confirmed by reading the components directly: home `HomeHero.tsx` renders "Canon revised" sourced from the single featured show's own `canon.last_revised` (deliberate design, pass-24 #269 — the featured tile intentionally scopes to one show, not the catalog); `/shows` `ShowsHero.tsx` renders "Shows revised" sourced from MAX across all canons (`showsStats.ts`); `/themes` `ListsHero.tsx` renders "Lists revised" sourced from MAX across all themes (`getThemeStats()` in `src/content/loaders.ts`). All three labels are now self-evidently distinct in scope (canon vs. shows vs. lists) and each is internally consistent with what it measures — there is no single "what's new since last visit" ambiguity left to resolve, because the three chromes were never claiming to measure the same underlying fact. No code change needed; not closing an issue since none was ever opened for this row.

- [x] [MED] [authed] Authed chrome surfaces the signed-in handle `@e2e` in the header on every page but offers no account-affordance control — no caret, no avatar token, no `Account` / `Sign out` link, no visible focus / pointer style indicating the handle is interactive. A returning member has no in-page route to `/u/e2e`, no way to sign out, no way to switch accounts; the handle reads as a status label rather than a control. Verified rendered shapes on pass-48 authed walker captures at f303ca3: every captured authed page header on desktop reads `tiered.tv | Shows | Lists | About | ⌕ Search ⌘K | @e2e` with `@e2e` as a bare text token; on mobile the burger-collapsed nav also shows `@e2e` as a bare token with no associated menu. `/u/e2e` is reachable only by typing the URL directly. The `Sign out` route exists (the auth0 callback is wired and the bot's session is dropped by the standard auth0 `/api/auth/logout` endpoint) but no header element links to it. Phase 36 (`[x]`) shipped the auth-aware chrome read path (`/api/auth/me` + the `<AuthState>` island) — the chrome reflects sign-in state but the prior phase's scope was the read-side reflection, not the affordance layer. This row names the affordance follow-on. Same defect class as the pending pass-37 [authed] /u/e2e bare empty-state finding (the authed first-party surfaces lacking the chrome scaffold returning members expect) — at the global-header affordance layer rather than the per-page chrome layer. Source: header component — likely `src/components/chrome/Header.tsx` or `src/components/chrome/AuthState.tsx` (the island that hydrates with the `/api/auth/me` payload — verify at fix-time). Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer puts the controls a returning visitor needs where they look for them. Fix options: (a) **primary path — wrap the `@e2e` handle as a link to `/u/<handle>` AND add a small popover-menu with `Your record` (link to `/u/<handle>`) + `Sign out` (link to `/api/auth/logout`)**. The popover is small and intentionally minimal — two entries, no settings page link (settings is `/settings` per the URL contract but is a future-phase route; the chrome shouldn't promise it). Single component edit at the header island. The popover anchors to a small caret next to the handle so the affordance reads as a control at a glance. (b) **alternative — wrap the `@e2e` handle as a bare link to `/u/<handle>` only, with `Sign out` exposed elsewhere** (e.g. on `/u/<handle>` itself). Smaller blast radius (single anchor wrap) but loses the in-chrome sign-out route, which is the affordance returning members reach for most on the header. (c) **alternative — leave as-is**. Rejected — the bare-handle reads as status label not control, and Phase 36 shipped the read-side reflection without the affordance layer. Recommended (a) — small popover with `Your record` + `Sign out`, anchors a caret-style affordance hint next to the handle, single header-component edit. Pin: extend the colocated test on the header component (verify path) with bidirectional cases — (1) authed state: rendered DOM contains an anchor `<a>` element whose `href` is `/u/<handle>` containing the handle text, AND a popover-trigger element whose ARIA role is `button` with a `Sign out` menu item linking to `/api/auth/logout`; (2) anon state: neither the handle anchor nor the popover renders (drift guard against signed-in chrome leaking to anon — same precedent class as the pass-1 #5 anon-sees-authed-chrome closure). Sibling colocated test on the popover menu component itself asserts the `Sign out` item links to the correct logout endpoint. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome affordance addition only; no per-show verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the affordance routes the returning member to their own profile and out of the session, neither of which exposes content). (URL: /, source: critique-pass-48) — f303ca3 — RESOLVED (already fixed by a prior drain, verified during iterate 2026-07-04): duplicate of pass-45 #384 (same bare-handle-no-affordance defect, filed independently before pass-45's fix landed). `HeaderView.tsx` now renders `@e2e` as a `site-header-user-trigger` button (`aria-haspopup="menu"`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, `data-profile-href`) that discloses a `role="menu"` panel with `Your record` (→ `/u/<handle>`) and `Sign out` (→ `/auth/logout?returnTo=...`) on both desktop and mobile — shipped across `5435b96` (mobile chevron menu) and `f2f53dd` (extended to desktop, closing #384 at CRITIQUE.md line ~2107). Chevron affordance + hover/expanded states styled in `src/styles/chrome.css` (`.site-header-user-trigger::after`, `:hover`, `[aria-expanded='true']`). Colocated coverage in `src/components/chrome/__tests__/HeaderView.test.tsx` (anon/authed states, menu open/close, Escape, outside-click, item selection) and e2e (`authed-example.spec.ts`, `user-profile.spec.ts`, `comment-read.spec.ts`). Sign-out route is `/auth/logout` (Auth0 SDK middleware-mounted), not `/api/auth/logout` as this row's text states — that detail was stale even at filing time. No code change needed; closing the tracking row only.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] /shows `<meta name="description">` and the on-page hero lede contradict each other on the load-bearing verb that anchors the page's editorial stance. The meta description reads `Reality-TV canons, sorted by how settled the ranking is. S tier is format-defining, A tier has the deep canon.` (`is` — settled-fact stance). The on-page hero reads `Reality-TV canons, sorted by how settled the ranking feels — by which formats have earned a defensible canon, not which shows I love most. The S tier invented or perfected its format. The A tier has the deep canon and the years to defend it.` (`feels` — editorial-hedge stance, which the rest of the page is built around — `not which shows I love most` is an editorial hedge by construction). The same sentence, two epistemic stances depending on the channel: a search-result reader sees a settled-fact claim; a clicked-through reader sees an editorial-hedge claim. Verified rendered shapes on pass-50 anon walker capture at 947843c. Same defect class as the pass-1 #103 `/themes` document-title-vs-user-surface drift (chrome-title hygiene at the SEO surface) and pass-2 #123 home-emits-no-canonical-link closure (SEO honesty layer), now at the meta-description-vs-hero-copy parity axis. Source: `/shows` route `generateMetadata` (likely `src/app/(default)/shows/page.tsx`); the on-page hero literal lives in the hero component (probably `src/components/shows/ShowsHero.tsx` or `src/components/shows/HeroSection.tsx`). Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `plain sentences over clever ones`; the same sentence in two channels should be the same sentence. Fix options: (a) **primary path — rewrite the `/shows` meta description's first sentence to use `feels`** (matching the on-page hero's editorial-hedge stance). Suggested literal: `Reality-TV canons, sorted by how settled the ranking feels. S tier is format-defining, A tier has the deep canon.` Single string edit at the route's `generateMetadata`. (b) **alternative — derive the meta description from the on-page hero literal**, so any future edit to the hero automatically propagates to the meta. Larger change (introduce a hero-copy constant the route metadata imports) but eliminates the entire drift class. Acceptable; (a) is the smaller landing. (c) alternative — leave as-is. Rejected — the channel-dependent epistemic-stance drift is the defect. Recommended (a) — single-word fix at the meta description; the `feels` voice is the editorial stance the rest of the page is built around and the SERP snippet should match. Pin: extend the colocated test for the `/shows` page metadata (or a global SEO-parity invariant in `scripts/content-check.ts`) asserting that any verb shared between meta description and hero literal matches on both surfaces — a cross-surface verb-parity check tokenized to lowercase. Initial floor: lax (warn only on the one current violator); strict at floor 0 once the fix ships. Bidirectional drift guard: a future authoring pass changing the hero verb without updating the meta description trips at verify time. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome meta-description edit only; no per-show verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface). (URL: /shows, source: critique-pass-50) — 947843c — issue: #428 — RESOLVED 45563a6: changed `OPENER` in `tierMetaDescription.ts` from `...ranking is.` to `...ranking feels.` matching the on-page hero lede editorial-hedge stance; updated 8 test expectations; added bidirectional regression guard asserting `ranking feels` present and `ranking is` absent.

- [x] [MED] [authed] /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains empty-thread comment composer desktop renders only `as @e2e` + a bare `⏎` return-key glyph with no placeholder copy and no labeled submit affordance, while mobile clarifies with `as @e2e / Tap to write`. A returning member on desktop has no hint that the `⏎` glyph is the submit control — the composer reads as an attribution line rather than an input. Verified rendered shapes on pass-48 authed walker captures at f303ca3: desktop /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains thread chrome reads `The thread / Add a thought · no spoilers. / as @e2e / ⏎` with the composer textarea rendered between `as @e2e` and `⏎` but carrying no placeholder text — the empty box gives no instruction. Mobile reads `as @e2e / Tap to write` with `Tap to write` as a visible touch-affordance hint. The pass-46 self-assessment dropped a related observation as `intentional input-modality affordance (deliberately designed per SeasonInfoCard.tsx:115 source-of-record comment)`, but the pass-46 row's specific drop rationale was about the desktop `⏎` keyboard-hint vs mobile `Tap to write` touch-hint axis. This row names a different defect: the desktop composer carries NO placeholder copy *inside the textarea itself* — the keyboard hint at the bottom only fires after the user has already understood the box is an input. A first-time returning member who has never posted before stares at an empty box with `as @e2e` above and `⏎` below; the modality cue assumes the box has been identified as the thing to type in. Same defect class as the pending pass-36 #335 [authed] HvV stacked-empty-state-with-input finding (signed-in empty state reads as a voice not talking to the affordance), now at the composer-placeholder-copy axis the prior queued row didn't address. Source: comment composer component — likely `src/components/comments/CommentComposer.tsx` or the equivalent in `SeasonInfoCard.tsx` (verify at fix-time). Bearings voice cue (`plan/bearings.md:370`) — `knowledgeable peer — plain sentences over clever ones`; a peer's empty input box says what it's for inside the box, not as a modality hint outside it. Fix options: (a) **primary path — add a placeholder copy inside the desktop composer textarea** that names the input affordance. Suggested literal: `Add a thought…` (mirrors the section header `Add a thought · no spoilers.` so the composer reads as the input form of the header, not as a separate affordance). The mobile `Tap to write` modality hint stays unchanged — it's the touch-affordance cue and remains correct on mobile. The desktop `⏎` keyboard hint also stays unchanged as the post-action cue. Single component edit at the composer. (b) **alternative — replace the `⏎` glyph with a labeled `Post` button** (or `Post · Enter` if you want to keep the keyboard hint). Larger affordance change (the button takes vertical chrome) but unambiguous. Acceptable but (a) is the smaller, more invariant-aligned path (composer placeholders are the standard pattern for input affordance copy). (c) **alternative — leave as-is**. Rejected — the empty textarea with no placeholder copy on the desktop affordance is the defect. Recommended (a) — small composer-placeholder addition, mirrors the section header, preserves the existing keyboard + touch modality cues. Pin: extend the colocated test on the composer component (verify path) with bidirectional cases — (1) authed empty-state: rendered DOM contains the placeholder text `Add a thought…` on the textarea element (`placeholder` attribute); (2) authed populated state: the placeholder is absent (textarea has user-typed content); (3) anon state: the composer renders the prior `Sign in to comment` affordance instead of the placeholder (drift guard against composer chrome leaking to anon). Sibling positive: the existing mobile `Tap to write` cue is preserved by passing through the same prop path. Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome composer placeholder addition only; no per-season verdict change, no canon-position change, no winner / elimination / finale beat exposure on any surface — the placeholder names the input affordance, not any content). (URL: /shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains, source: critique-pass-48) — f303ca3 — issue: #422 — RESOLVED 9bc7a00: desktop stub `⏎` replaced with `Write` (parallel to mobile `Tap to write`); added `data-testid="comment-stub-desktop-label"` and updated colocated test.

All four clauses open with an interrogative "does" or "do" but close on a full stop — a punctuation inconsistency that creates a halting read. Fix: replace the four closing periods with question marks in `meth_how_p` in `content/shows/big-brother/canon.md`. One-field content fix; no code change. Spoiler discipline P0 intact. (URL: /shows/big-brother, source: critique-pass-60) — 169ae72 — issue: #513 — RESOLVED 5753864: swapped the four closing periods for question marks in `meth_how_p`, matching the interrogative phrasing each clause already opens with. Content-only, one field. Verify gate green: 194 unit test files / 2811 tests, content:check ok (58 shows/717 seasons/58 canons); build (973 pages); 3231 e2e passed. Closes #513.
- [ ] [LOW] [anon] /themes/best-newbie-casts — the tagline's second sentence "These are the rookie rosters that walked in fluent" echoes the H1 title "Rookie casts walking in fluent" verbatim. A visitor reads the phrase twice in immediate succession (H1 → opening paragraph) without new information added in between. The first tagline sentence ("Some first-time casts step off the boat playing like they've already done this") introduces the idea; the second restates the title rather than advancing it. Fix: revise the tagline's second sentence in `content/themes/best-newbie-casts.md` to close on a distinct idea — e.g. drop the echo phrase and extend the first sentence's thought, or replace the second sentence with a distinguishing observation about what these casts share. Content-only fix; one field. Spoiler discipline P0 intact. (URL: /themes/best-newbie-casts, source: critique-pass-60) — 169ae72
- [ ] [LOW] [authed] /shows/big-brother — the `card_tagline` field (which drives the page meta description) is a trimmed version of the full `tagline` rather than a distinct pull. `tagline`: "26 seasons of houseguests locked in a wired-up soundstage in Studio City, voting each other out one Thursday at a time. The American version of the format that invented the genre, and still the loudest room on summer television." `card_tagline`: "26 seasons of houseguests locked in a soundstage, voting each other out one Thursday at a time. Still the loudest room on summer television." The two are effectively the same sentence with detail stripped — a reader arriving from search sees the meta description and then reads the near-identical opening on-page. A distinct card_tagline should lead with a different angle (e.g. the alliance game, the summer franchise, the studio-audience format) rather than abbreviating the lede. Fix: rewrite `card_tagline` in `content/shows/big-brother.md` to lead with a different editorial angle that teases rather than abbreviates the on-page lede. Content-only fix; one field. Spoiler discipline P0 intact. (URL: /shows/big-brother, source: critique-pass-60) — 169ae72
- [x] [LOW] [anon] Multiple shows — `meth_when_p` WHEN-section closing phrase "I'm trying to be honest" repeated verbatim across 8 canon files. Eight of 31 shows end their "When I revisit" methodology section with nearly identical language: "I'm not claiming to be [objective / definitive]. I'm trying to be honest." — specifically `content/shows/alone-australia/canon.md`, `content/shows/american-idol/canon.md`, `content/shows/below-deck-adventure/canon.md`, `content/shows/below-deck/canon.md`, `content/shows/hells-kitchen/canon.md`, `content/shows/the-voice/canon.md`, `content/shows/rhoa/canon.md` (seven with the exact two-sentence pattern; `content/shows/below-deck-mediterranean/canon.md` carries a close variant: "it is trying to be honest about where each season sits in the run right now"). A reader browsing multiple show pages encounters the same editorial closer repeatedly; the bearings voice is "knowledgeable peer — plain-spoken" and a repeated generic candor disclaimer deflates rather than reinforces. Fix: rephrase the WHEN-section closing for each affected show to something specific to that show's editorial posture and drain history rather than a cross-show boilerplate closer — e.g. for Below Deck, close on the captain-era framing; for American Idol, close on the network-era framing; for RHOA, close on the Atlanta-specificity argument. Content-only; 7–8 one-sentence edits across `meth_when_p` fields. Spoiler discipline P0 intact. (URL: /shows/american-idol et al., source: critique-pass-61) — 0975b35
  - issue: #460 — RESOLVED eb6522d: rewrote the WHEN-section closing sentence(s) in alone-australia, below-deck, below-deck-adventure, below-deck-mediterranean, hells-kitchen, the-voice, and rhoa to assert a show-specific claim instead of the shared candor disclaimer. american-idol already carried a distinct affirmative closer ("Rotation and revival seasons earn their tier by clearing that standard.") and needed no edit — verified directly, not just taken on the sub-agent's word. All touched `meth_when_p` fields re-verified independently at 40-60 words after two content-curator overshoots (below-deck-adventure at 63 words, hells-kitchen at 62 words, rhoa at 61 words) were trimmed by hand. Spoiler discipline P0 intact. (URL: /shows/shark-tank, source: critique-pass-78) — issue: #520 — RESOLVED 634ff9c: `CanonEraToolbar` (shared across every show's canon page) now wraps the All chip's count in its own `<span className="cp-chip-count">`, styled at reduced opacity in `canon.css`, so the label and count read as visually distinct without added punctuation. Fixes every show, not just Shark Tank. Colocated pin added asserting the count renders in its own span. Closes #520.

- [x] [LOW] [anon] /shows/jersey-shore/season/season-1 (and other single-season, single-list shows) — the "Also appears in" cross-reference section only lists the season's own show canon entry, i.e. "Jersey Shore Season 1 also appears in the Jersey Shore Editor's Canon" — circular, since that's the page the reader is already looking at (it's the season within its own show's canon), and adds no new information. On the mature reference page (`/shows/survivor/season/heroes-vs-villains`) this section surfaces genuinely distinct themed-list cross-references (six of them). The section reads as a broken cross-reference feature rather than an honest "not enough canons/themes reference this yet" state. Fix: for seasons with no themed-list cross-references yet, either suppress the self-referential canon line entirely or relabel the section (e.g. "In this canon") so a single, self-evident entry doesn't masquerade as a cross-reference list. Likely a small conditional in the season page's "also appears in" block (`src/app/shows/[show]/season/[slug]/page.tsx`, section 06). Spoiler discipline P0 intact (chrome-only). (URL: /shows/jersey-shore/season/season-1, source: critique-pass-68) — 7173f3a — issue: #511 — RESOLVED b3e261b: `appearsInRowsFor()` now returns `{ rows, hasCrossRef }`, with `hasCrossRef` true only when at least one themed-list entry exists (computed before the self-referential canon row is appended). The flag threads through `buildSections()` and the `s-appears` section render to relabel it "In this canon" / "Its Editor's Canon entry." when the only row is the self-reference, vs. "Also appears in" / "Cross-references." when real cross-references exist. Chose relabel over suppress: suppressing the section on zero-cross-ref pages would have broken the pinned e2e assertion in `apps/e2e/tests/link-integrity.spec.ts` (Survivor Gabon, which has zero themed-list cross-refs and asserts `section-appears` is visible with a working canon link). Verify gate green: 194 test files / 2811 unit tests, content:check ok (58 shows/717 seasons), build (973 pages), 3231 e2e. Closes #511.
